/1.1.1.1/setup/.title: 设置
/1.1.1.1/setup/.description: Learn how to 设置 Cloudflare's 1.1.1.1 DNS 解析器 for enhanced 安全 and privacy. Protect against malware and adult content with easy 配置.
paragraphs: By 默认, the DNS server your devices use is provided by your Internet service provider (ISP). Some ISPs and network equipment providers partner with Cloudflare to add safer browsing to their offerings.
If your providers are not currently using Cloudflare, you can change the DNS 设置 on your device or 路由r as detailed in the following instructions.

You can also 设置 1.1.1.1 for Families for an added layer of protection on your home network against malware and adult content. 1.1.1.1 for Families leverages Cloudflare's 全球网络 to ensure that it is fast and secure around the world, and includes the same strong privacy guarantees that Cloudflare committed to when launching 1.1.1.1.
1.1.1.1 for Families categorizes destinations on the Internet based on the potential threat they pose regarding malware, phishing, or other 类型s of 安全 risks.
1.1.1.1 for Families has two 默认 options:
Use the following DNS 解析器s to block malicious content:
Use the following DNS 解析器s to block malware and adult content:
Cloudflare returns  if the fully qualified 域名 name (FQDN) or IP in a DNS 查询 is classified as malicious.
:::注意[域名 miscategorization]
If you are using 1.1.1.1 for Families and see a 域名 that you believe is miscategorized, fill in this form to bring it to our attention. Your submission will remain anonymous.
We review these submissions to improve Cloudflare’s categorization.
After configuring 1.1.1.1 for Families, you can 测试 if it is working as intended with the following URLs:
https://malware.测试category.com/: Use this to 测试 if 1.1.1.1 for Families is blocking known malware addresses correctly.
https://nudity.测试category.com/: Use this to 测试 if 1.1.1.1 for Families is blocking known adult content and malware addresses correctly.

If you have a DoH-compliant client, such as a compatible 路由r, you can 设置 1.1.1.1 for Families to encrypt your DNS queries over HTTPS. This pr事件 spoofing and tracking by malicious actors, advertisers, ISPs, and others. For more 信息rmation on DoH, refer to the Learning Center article on DNS 加密.
To configure an encrypted DoH connection to 1.1.1.1 for Families, 类型 one of the following URLs into the appropriate 字段 of your DoH-compliant client:

1.1.1.1 for Families also 支持s DoT if you have a compliant client, such as a compatible DoT 路由r. DoT allows you to encrypt your DNS queries, protecting you from spoofing, malicious actors, and others. You can 了解更多 about DoT in the Learning Center article on DNS 加密.
To configure an encrypted DoT connection to 1.1.1.1 for Families, 类型 one of the following URLs into the appropriate 字段 of your DoT-compliant client:

/1.1.1.1/additional-options/dns-over-tor.title: DNS over Tor
headings: Setting up a local DNS 代理 using socat
DNS over TCP, TLS, and HTTPS
paragraphs: :::注意
The hidden 解析器 is still an 实验性 service and should not be used in production or for other critical uses.

If you do not want to disclose your IP address to the 解析器, you can use our Tor onion service. Resolving DNS queries through the Tor network guarantees a significantly higher level of anonymity than making the 请求s directly. Not only does doing so pr事件 the 解析器 from ever seeing your IP address, but it also pr事件 your ISP from knowing that you attempted to resolve a 域名 name.
阅读更多 about this service in this blog post.
The 重要 difference between using all other modes of DNS and this one is that packet 路由 no longer uses IP addresses, and therefore all connections must be 路由d through a Tor client.
Before you start, head to the Tor Project website to download and 安装 a Tor client. If you use the Tor Browser, it will automatically start a SOCKS 代理 at .
If you use Tor from the command line, 创建 the following 配置 file:
Then you can run tor with:
Also, if you use the Tor Browser, you can head to the 解析器's address to see the usual 1.1.1.1 page:
:::注意[注意]
The HTTPS 证书 indicator should say "Cloudflare, Inc. (US)."
If you ever for获取 1.1.1.1's address, use cURL to retrieve it:
Of course, not all DNS clients 支持 connecting to the Tor client, so the easiest way to connect any DNS-speaking software to the hidden 解析器 is by forwarding ports locally, for instance using .
The hidden 解析器 is 设置 to 列表en on TCP ports 53 and 853 for DNS over TCP and TLS. After setting up a Tor 代理, run the following  command as a privileged user, replacing the port 数字 appropriately:

From here, you can follow the regular 指南 for setting up 1.1.1.1, except you should always use  instead of . If you need to access the 代理 from another device, simply replace  in  commands with your local IP address.
As explained in the blog post, our favorite way of using the hidden 解析器 is using DNS over HTTPS (DoH). To set it up:
Download  by following the 指南 for connecting to 1.1.1.1 using DNS over HTTPS clients.
Start a Tor SOCKS 代理 and use  to forward port TCP:443 to localhost:
Instruct your machine to treat the  address as localhost:
Finally, start a local DNS over UDP daemon:

/1.1.1.1/.title: 1.1.1.1 (DNS 解析器)
paragraphs: Speed up your online experience with Cloudflare's public DNS 解析器.
1.1.1.1 is Cloudflare’s public DNS 解析器. It offers a fast and private way to browse the Internet. DNS 解析器s translate 域名s like  into the IP addresses necessary to reach the website (like ).

Unlike most DNS 解析器s, 1.1.1.1 does not sell user data to advertisers. 1.1.1.1 has also been measured to be the fas测试 DNS 解析器 available — it is 部署ed in hundreds of cities worldwide, and has access to the addresses of millions of 域名 names on the same servers it runs on.
1.1.1.1 is completely free. Setting it up takes minutes and requires no special software.
1.1.1.1 for Families has additional protection against malware and adult content.
1.1.1.1 offers an encrypted service through DNS over HTTPS (DoH) or DNS over TLS (DoT) for increased 安全 and privacy. You can also access 1.1.1.1 as a Tor hidden service.
Access the Internet in a more secure and private way.
Cloudflare's global DNS 平台 provides speed and resilience. DNS customers also benefit from free DNSSEC, and protection against 路由 leaks and hijacking.
Secure and accelerate your TCP or UDP based applications.

/1.1.1.1/encryption/.title: 加密
paragraphs: Traditionally, DNS queries and replies are performed over plaintext. They are sent over the Internet without any kind of 加密 or protection, even when you are accessing a secured website. This has a great impact on 安全 and privacy, as these queries might be subject to surveillance, spoofing and tracking by malicious actors, advertisers, ISPs, and others.
To pr事件 untrustworthy entities from interpreting and manipulating your queries, 1.1.1.1 支持s different standards to encrypt plaintext DNS traffic and improve DNS privacy:
DNS over TLS (DoT)
DNS over HTTPS (DoH)
Oblivious DNS over HTTPS (ODoH)
You can also configure your browser to secure your DNS queries.
If you need to secure connections in your smartphone, refer to 1.1.1.1 iOS or Android apps.

paragraphs: When you 构建 MCP Servers on Cloudflare, you extend the  class, from the Agents SDK:
This means that each instance of your MCP server has its own durable state, backed by a Durable 对象, with its own SQL 数据库.
Your MCP server doesn't necessarily have to be an Agent. You can 构建 MCP servers that are stateless, and just add 工具 to your MCP server using the  package.
But if you want your MCP server to:
remember previous tool calls, and 响应s it provided
provide a game to the MCP client, remembering the state of the game board, previous moves, and the score
cache the state of a previous external API call, so that subsequent tool calls can reuse it
do anything that an Agent can do, but allow MCP clients to communicate with it
You can use the APIs below in order to do so.
The  static method 创建s a Worker 处理程序 that 路由 请求s to your MCP server:
This is the simplest way to 部署 an MCP server — about 15 lines of code. The  method handles 流able HTTP transport automatically.
When using the OAuth Provider Library, pass your MCP server to :
For GDPR and data residency compliance, specify a jurisdiction to ensure your MCP server instances run in specific regions:
With OAuth:
When you specify :
All MCP 会话 data stays within the EU
User data processed by your 工具 remains in the EU
State stored in the Durable 对象 stays in the EU
Available jurisdictions include  (European Union) and  (FedRAMP compliant locations). Refer to Durable Objects data location for more options.

instances automatically 支持 WebSockets Hibernation, allowing stateful MCP servers to sleep during 不活跃 periods while preserving their state. This means your agents only consume compute 资源 when 活跃ly processing 请求s, optimizing costs while maintaining the full context and conversation history.
Hibernation is 已启用 by 默认 and requires no additional 配置.
The McpAgent class provides seamless integration with the OAuth Provider Library for 身份验证 and 授权.
When a user authenticates to your MCP server, their identity 信息rmation and 令牌s are made available through the  parameter, allowing you to:
access user-specific data
check user 权限 before performing operations
customize 响应s based on user attributes

paragraphs: Schedule 任务s to run in the future — whether that is seconds from now, at a specific date/time, or on a recurring cron schedule. 定时 任务s survive agent restarts and are persisted to SQLite.
定时 任务s can do anything a 请求 or 消息 from a user can: make 请求s, 查询 数据库s, send emails, 读取 and write state. 定时 任务s can invoke any regular method on your Agent.
The scheduling system 支持s four modes:
Under the hood, scheduling uses Durable 对象 alarms to wake the agent at the right time. 任务s are stored in a SQLite 表 and executed in order.

Durable Objects are evicted after a period of inactivity (typically 70-140 seconds with no incoming 请求s, WebSocket 消息s, or alarms). During long-运行中 operations — 流ing LLM 响应s, waiting on external APIs, 运行中 multi-步骤 computations — the agent can be evicted mid-flight.
pr事件 this by creating a 30-second heartbeat schedule. The internal heartbeat callback is a no-op — the alarm firing itself is what resets the inactivity timer. Because it uses the scheduling system:
The heartbeat does not conflict with your own schedules (the scheduling system mul提示lexes through a single alarm slot)
The heartbeat shows up in  if you need to inspect it

Each  call returns an independent disposer:

**任务 size:** Each 任务 (including payload) can be up to 2MB.

**Cron precision:** Minute-level (not seconds)
**Interval precision:** Second-level
**Cron 作业s:** After 执行, automatically re定时 for the next occurrence
**Interval 作业s:** After 执行, re定时 for ; skipped if still 运行中

/agents/api-reference/store-and-sync-sta.title: Store and sync state
headings: Use Agent state as model context
工作流 步骤 方法
paragraphs: Agents provide built-in state management with automatic persistence and real-time synchronization across all connected clients.
State within an Agent is:
**Persistent** - Automatically saves to SQLite, survives restarts and hibernation
**Synchronized** - Changes are broadcast to all connected WebSocket clients instantly
**Bidirectional** - Both server and clients can 更新 state
**类型-safe** - Full TypeScript 支持 with generics
**Immediately consistent** - 读取 your own writes
**Th读取-safe** - Safe for concurrent 更新s
**Fast** - State is colocated wherever the Agent is 运行中

Agent state is stored in a SQL 数据库 embedded within each individual Agent instance. You can interact with it using the higher-level  API (recommended), which allows you to sync state and 触发器 事件 on state changes, or by directly 查询ing the 数据库 with .
:::注意[State vs Props]

State is persistent data that survives restarts and syncs across clients. Props are one-time initialization arguments passed when an agent is instantiated - use props for 配置 that does not need to persist.
Use the  property to define 默认 值s for 新功能 agent instances:
The second generic parameter to  defines your state 类型:
Initial state is applied lazily on first access, not on every wake:
**新功能 agent** -  is used and persisted
**Existing agent** - Persisted state is loaded from SQLite
**No  defined** -  is
Access the current state via the  获取ter:
If you do not define ,  returns :
Use  to 更新 state. This:
Saves to SQLite (persistent)
Broadcasts to all connected clients (excluding connections where [](/agents/api-参考/协议-消息s/) returned )
触发器s  (after broadcast; best-effort)
State is stored as JSON, so it must be serializable:
Override  to react when state changes (通知/side-effects):
The  shows who 触发器ed the 更新:
This is useful for:
Avoiding infinite loops (do not react to your own 更新s)
Validating client input
触发器ing side effects only on client 操作s
If you want to validate or reject state 更新s, override :
Runs before persistence and broadcast
Must be synchronous
Throwing aborts the 更新
:::注意
is not intended for validation; it is a notification hook and should not block broadcasts. Use  for validation.
State synchronizes automatically with connected clients.
When using 工作流s, you can 更新 agent state from 工作流 步骤s:
These are durable operations - they persist even if the 工作流 重试.

Every individual Agent instance has its own SQL (SQLite) 数据库 that runs within the same context as the Agent itself. This means that inserting or 查询ing data within your Agent is effectively zero-延迟: the Agent does not have to round-trip across a continent or the world to access its own data.
You can access the SQL API within any method on an Agent via . The SQL API accepts template literals:
You can also supply a TypeScript 类型 argument to the 查询, which will be used to infer the 类型 of the result:
You do not need to specify an 数组 类型 ( or ), as  will always return an 数组 of the specified 类型.
Providing a 类型 parameter does not validate that the result matches your 类型 definition. If you need to validate incoming 事件, we recommend a library such as zod or your own validator logic.
The SQL API exposed to an Agent is similar to the one within Durable Objects. You can use the same SQL queries with the Agent's 数据库. 创建 表s and 查询 data, just as you would with Durable Objects or D1.
State is broadcast to all clients on every change. For large data:
For responsive UIs, 更新 client state immediately:
Be careful not to 触发器 state 更新s in 响应 to your own 更新s:

You can combine the state and SQL APIs in your Agent with its ability to call AI models to include historical context within your prompts to a model. Modern Large Language Models (LLMs) often have very large context windows (up to millions of 令牌s), which allows you to pull relevant context into your prompt directly.
For 示例, you can use an Agent's built-in SQL 数据库 to pull history, 查询 a model with it, and append to that history ahead of the next call to the model:

This works because each instance of an Agent has its own 数据库, and the state stored in that 数据库 is private to that Agent: whether it is acting on behalf of a single user, a room or channel, or a deep re搜索 tool. By 默认, you do not have to manage contention or reach out over the network to a centralized 数据库 to retrieve and store state.

/agents/api-reference/using-ai-models/.title: Using AI Models
headings: 流ing over WebSockets {/* long-运行中-model-请求s */}
paragraphs: AnchorHeading,
Meta信息,
Render,
类型,

WranglerConfig,

} from "~/components";
Agents can call AI models from any provider. Workers AI is built in and requires no API 键s. You can also use OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, or any service that exposes an OpenAI-compatible API.

The AI SDK provides a unified interface across all of these providers, and is what  and the starter template use under the hood. You can also use the model 路由 特性 in AI Gateway to 路由 across providers, eval 响应s, and manage rate 限制.

You can call models from any method within an Agent, including from HTTP 请求s using the [](/agents/api-参考/agents-api/) 处理程序, when a 定时 任务 runs, when handling a WebSocket 消息 in the [](/agents/api-参考/websockets/) 处理程序, or from any of your own 方法.

Agents can call AI models on their own — autonomously — and can handle long-运行中 响应s that take minutes (or longer) to respond in full. If a client disconnects mid-stream, the Agent keeps 运行中 and can catch the client up when it reconnects.
Modern reasoning models can take some time to both generate a 响应 _and_ stream the 响应 back to the client. Instead of buffering the entire 响应, you can stream it back over WebSockets.
You can also persist AI model 响应s back to Agent state using . If a user disconnects, 读取 the 消息 history back and send it to the user when they reconnect.
You can use any of the models available in Workers AI within your Agent by configuring a 绑定. No API 键s are 必需.
Workers AI 支持s 流ing 响应s by setting . Use 流ing to avoid buffering and delaying 响应s, especially for larger models or reasoning models.
Your Wrangler 配置 needs an  绑定:
You can use AI Gateway directly from an Agent by specifying a  配置 when calling the AI 绑定. Model 路由 lets you 路由 请求s across providers based on 可用性, rate 限制, or cost bud获取s.
The  绑定 in your Wrangler 配置 is shared across both Workers AI and AI Gateway.
Visit the AI Gateway 文档 to learn how to configure a gateway and retrieve a gateway ID.

The AI SDK provides a unified API for text generation, tool calling, structured 响应s, and more. It works with any provider that has an AI SDK adapter, including Workers AI via [](https://www.npmjs.com/package/workers-ai-provider).
You can swap the provider to use OpenAI, Anthropic, or any other AI SDK-compatible adapter:
Agents can call models across any service that 支持s the OpenAI API. For 示例, you can use the OpenAI SDK to call one of Google's Gemini models directly from your Agent.
Agents can stream 响应s back over HTTP using Server-Sent 事件 (SSE) from within an  处理程序, or by using the native WebSocket API to stream 响应s back to a client.

/agents/concepts/human-in-the-loop/.title: Human in the Loop
paragraphs: Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) 工作流s integrate human judgment and oversight into automated processes. These 工作流s pause at critical points for human review, validation, or decision-making before proceeding.
**Compliance**: Regulatory 要求 may mandate human approval for certain 操作s.
**Safety**: High-stakes operations (payments, deletions, external communications) need oversight.
**Quality**: Human review catches 错误 AI might miss.
**Trust**: Users feel more confident when they can approve critical 操作s.
The Agents SDK provides five patterns for human-in-the-loop. Choose based on your architecture:
For durable, multi-步骤 processes with approval gates that can wait hours, days, or weeks. Use Cloudflare 工作流s with the  method.
键 APIs:
— Pause 工作流 until approved
— Approve a waiting 工作流
— Reject a waiting 工作流
Best for: Expense approvals, content publishing 管道s, data export 请求s
For  工具 that should pause for user confirmation before executing. Define  on the tool — it can be a 布尔值 or an async predicate based on the tool arguments:
On the client, render 待处理 approvals from 消息 parts and call :
For custom denial 消息s, use  with  instead of :
For 工具 that need browser APIs (geolocation, clipboard, camera) or user inter操作 before returning a result. Define the tool on the server without , then handle it on the client:
When  is  (the 默认), the conversation automatically continues after the client provides the tool output.
For MCP servers that need to 请求 additional structured input from users during tool 执行. The MCP client renders a form based on your JSON 模式:
Best for: Inter活跃 tool confirmations, gathering additional 参数 mid-执行
In a 工作流-based approval:
The 工作流 reaches an approval 步骤 and calls
The 工作流 pauses and 报告 progress to the agent
The agent 更新s its state with the 待处理 approval
Connected clients see the 待处理 approval and can approve or reject
When approved, the 工作流 resumes with the approval metadata
If rejected or timed out, the 工作流 handles the rejection appropriately
Set 超时s to pr事件 工作流s from waiting indefinitely:
Use scheduling for escalation:
Maintain immu表 audit 日志 of all approval decisions using the SQL API. 记录:
Who made the decision
When the decision was made
The reason or justification
Any relevant metadata

Human review processes do not operate on predic表 timelines. A reviewer might need days or weeks to make a decision. Your system needs to maintain state 一致性 throughout this period — the 源al 请求, intermediate decisions, partial progress, and review history.
:::注意[提示]
Durable Objects provide persistent compute instances that maintain state for hours, weeks, or months — ideal for long-lived approval flows.
Human reviewers play a crucial role in evaluating and improving LLM 性能:
**Decision quality assessment**: Have reviewers evaluate the LLM's reasoning process and decision points.
**Edge case identification**: Use human expertise to identify scenarios where 性能 could be improved.
**反馈 collection**: Gather structured 反馈 that can be used to fine-tune the LLM. AI Gateway can help 设置 an LLM 反馈 loop.

Your system should gracefully handle reviewer un可用性, system outages, conflicting reviews, and 超时 expiration. Implement clear escalation paths for exceptional cases and automatic checkpointing that allows 工作流s to resume from the last s表 state after any interruption.

Handle connection 错误 and disconnections. The  method has two overloads — one for WebSocket connection 错误 and one for server-level 错误:
The 默认  implementation 日志 the 错误 and rethrows it. Override it to add custom 错误处理, reporting, or recovery logic.
Agents 支持 hibernation — they can sleep when 不活跃 and wake when needed. This saves 资源 while maintaining WebSocket connections.
Hibernation is 已启用 by 默认. To disable:
Agent is 活跃, handling connections
After a period of inactivity with no 消息s, the agent hibernates (sleeps)

When a 消息 arrives, the agent wakes up
is called as normal
Store 重要 data in  or SQLite, not in class properties:
Track who is online using per-connection state. Connection state is automatically cleaned up when users disconnect:

Most AI applications today are stateless — they process a 请求, return a 响应, and for获取 everything. Real agents need more. They need to remember conversations, act on schedules, call 工具, coordinate with other agents, and stay connected to users in real-time. The Agents SDK gives you all of this as a TypeScript class.

Each agent runs on a Durable 对象 — a stateful micro-server with its own SQL 数据库, WebSocket connections, and scheduling. 部署 once and Cloudflare runs your agents across its 全球网络, scaling to tens of millions of instances. No infrastructure to manage, no 会话s to reconstruct, no state to externalize.
Three commands to a 运行中 agent. No API 键s 必需 — the starter uses Workers AI by 默认.

The starter includes 流ing AI chat, server-side and client-side 工具, human-in-the-loop approval, and 任务 scheduling — a foundation you can 构建 on or tear apart. You can also swap in OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, or any other provider.
**Remember everything** — Every agent has a built-in SQL 数据库 and 键-值 state that syncs to connected clients in real-time. State survives restarts, 部署s, and hibernation.

**构建 AI chat** — [](/agents/api-参考/chat-agents/) gives you 流ing AI chat with automatic 消息 persistence, resumable 流s, and tool 支持. 对 it with the [](/agents/api-参考/chat-agents/) React hook to 构建 chat UIs in minutes.

**Think with any model** — Call any AI model — Workers AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini — and stream 响应s over WebSockets or Server-Sent 事件. Long-运行中 reasoning models that take minutes to respond work out of the box.
**Use and serve 工具** — Define server-side 工具, client-side 工具 that run in the browser, and human-in-the-loop approval flows. Expose your agent's 工具 to other agents and LLMs via MCP.
**Act on their own** — Schedule 任务s on a delay, at a specific time, or on a cron. Agents can wake themselves up, do work, and go back to sleep — without a user present.
**Browse the web** — Spin up headless browsers to scrape, screenshot, and interact with web pages.
**Orchestrate work** — Run multi-步骤 工作流s with automatic 重试, or coordinate across mul提示le agents.
**React to 事件** — Handle inbound email, HTTP 请求s, WebSocket 消息s, and state changes — all from the same class.
An agent is a TypeScript class. 方法 marked with  become 类型d RPC that clients can call directly over WebSocket.
For AI chat, extend  instead. 消息s are persisted automatically, 流s resume on disconnect, and the React hook handles the UI.
Refer to the 快速开始 for a full walkthrough, the chat agents 指南 for the full chat API, or the Agents API 参考 for the complete SDK.
Run machine learning models, powered by 无服务器 GPUs, on Cloudflare's 全球网络. No API 键s 必需.
构建 无服务器 applications and 部署 instantly across the globe for exceptional 性能, reliability, and scale.
观察 and control your AI applications with 缓存, 速率限制, 请求 重试, model 回退, and more.
构建 full-stack AI applications with Vectorize, Cloudflare's vector 数据库 for semantic 搜索, recommendations, and providing context to LLMs.
构建 stateful agents that guarantee 执行s, including automatic 重试, persistent state that runs for minutes, hours, days, or weeks.

/ai-crawl-control/features/analyze-ai-tr.title: Analyze AI traffic
headings: Analyze site areas with pattern grouping
paragraphs: AI Crawl Control 指标 provide you with insight on how AI crawlers are interacting with your website (Cloudflare 区域).
To view AI Crawl Control 指标:
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **AI Crawl Control**.
You can find meaningful 信息rmation across the 概述, Crawlers, and 指标 tabs.
The 概述 tab provides a 快照 of AI crawler activity:
The Crawlers tab provides detailed 信息rmation about individual AI crawlers and allows you to set Block or Allow controls:
For more 信息rmation on managing crawler access, refer to Manage AI crawlers.
The 指标 tab provides detailed 分析 and charts to help you understand how AI crawlers are interacting with your website.
Visualize crawler activity patterns over the selected time period. Use the view selector to switch between 指标:
You can group the data by different dimensions:
:::注意
The Data transfer view shows . Some crawlers may show higher data transfer per 请求 than others, de待处理 on the content they 请求.
The Status code distribution chart shows HTTP 响应 codes for AI crawler 请求s over time:
**2xx** — 成功ful 响应s
**3xx** — 重定向s
**4xx** — Client 错误 (including 403 blocked and 402 payment 必需)
**5xx** — Server 错误
Use this chart to identify patterns in how your site responds to AI crawlers, including the distribution of 错误 codes and the volume of 重定向s.
This 特性 is available for customers on a paid plan.
Identify traffic sources with referrer 分析 to understand discovery patterns and content popularity from AI operators.
Toggle between views using the tabs in the Top referrers section.
Track referral traffic trends over your selected time period. Group by:
The Most popular paths 表 shows which pages on your site are most frequently 请求ed by AI crawlers.
To see a breakdown by crawler per path, use the top-level 过滤器s.
Select the Patterns tab to see 请求s grouped by URI pattern (such as , , or ). This helps you quickly identify which areas of your site AI crawlers tar获取 most.
Pattern grouping aggregates individual paths into site areas:
Patterns include up to 2 levels of depth (for 示例, , )
The root pattern  shows total traffic across your site
Patterns are not aggregated across 主机名s
Single-path patterns display without 通配符s (for 示例,  instead of )
Additional tabs include:
**All** — Combined content and media 请求s (individual paths, not patterns)
**Content** — HTML, JSON, and text content
**Media** — Images, videos, and other media files
Use the 过滤器 bar to narrow your analysis by mul提示le criteria:
Combine mul提示le 过滤器s for complex analysis. All 指标 and charts 更新 dynamically based on your 过滤器 selection.
To export your data, select Download CSV or Download image. Downloads include all applied 过滤器s and groupings.
The Crawlers tab includes an 操作s menu for each crawler with the following options:
For programmatic access to AI Crawl Control 分析, use the GraphQL 分析 API. The API provides access to the same data available in the 控制面板, including detection IDs, referrer data, and data transfer 指标.

监控 and control how AI services access your website content.

AI companies use web content to train their models and power AI applications. AI Crawl Control (formerly AI Audit) gives you visibility into which AI services are accessing your content, and provides 工具 to manage access according to your p参考s.
With AI Crawl Control, you can:

**Control access with granular policies** - Set allow or block 规则 for individual crawlers
**监控 robots.txt compliance** - Track which crawlers follow your directives and 创建 enforcement 规则
**Explore monetization options** - 设置 pay per crawl 定价 for content access (private 测试版)
**部署 with zero 配置** - Works automatically on all Cloudflare plans

/ai-crawl-control/features/manage-ai-cra.title: Manage AI crawlers
headings: Take 操作 for each AI crawler
Edit the 响应 正文
paragraphs: AI Crawl Control enables you to take specific 操作 for each AI crawler.
To manage AI crawlers:
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **AI Crawl Control**.
Go to the **Crawlers** tab.
The Crawlers tab displays a 表 of AI crawlers that are 请求ing access to your content, and how they interact with your pages. The 表 provides the following 信息rmation.
:::注意[Quality of AI crawler detection]
On the 免费计划, AI Crawl Control identifies AI crawlers based on their user agent 字符串s. This enables AI Crawl Control to detect well-known, self-identifying AI crawlers.
Upgrade your plan to enable a more thorough detection using Cloudflare's Bot Management detection ID 字段.
You can use 过滤器s to narrow the scope of your result:
**Name:** 搜索 the name of the AI crawler.
**Operator:** 过滤器 by the AI crawler operator.
**Category:** 过滤器 by the category of the AI crawler (for 示例, AI crawler, AI assistant, or archiver).
The 值s of the 表 will 更新 according to your 过滤器.
For each AI crawler, you can choose to allow or block access.
**Summary:** You can allow an AI crawler to scrape your content.
**When to use:** Allow AI crawlers that offer services which provide 值 through citations, referrals, or existing agreements.
**Implementation:** From the **操作s** column, select **Allow**.
注意 that you can still choose to Enforce .
**Summary:** You can block an AI crawler to completely stop the AI crawler from scraping your webpage.
**When to use:** Block AI crawlers when their behavior do not align with your content strategy, or violate your policies.
**Implementation:** From the **操作s** column, select **Block**.
注意 that you can configure the 响应 that 获取s returned when blocking an AI crawler. Refer to Configure block 响应.
:::注意[Pay per crawl closed 测试版]
Pay per crawl is currently in closed 测试版.
To find out how to join the 测试版 program, reach out to us at Pay per crawl signup, or contact your 账户 executive if you are an existing Enterprise customer.
To 了解更多 about pay per crawl, refer to Cloudflare blog: Introducing pay per crawl: enabling content owners to charge AI crawlers for access.
For each AI crawler, you can take one of three 操作s: allow, charge, or block.
For more details on how this 规则 interacts with other Cloudflare 设置, refer to 工作原理.
**Summary:** You can charge the owner of the AI crawler for each 成功ful crawl 请求.
**When to use:** Charge AI crawlers when your content has training 值, and you want to explore monetization options.
**Implementation:** From the **操作s** column, select **Charge**.
For more 信息rmation, refer to What is Pay Per Crawl?.
:::提示[Need more advanced control?]
You can also 创建 more complex 规则 when taking 操作 on AI crawlers, using Cloudflare WAF. For more 信息rmation on creating more specific 规则, refer to 创建 a custom 规则 in the 控制面板.

When you block a crawler in AI Crawl Control, the system 创建s or 更新s a WAF custom 规则 on your 区域 to enforce that block. For advanced scenarios such as adding path-based exceptions or extra user agents, you can extend this 规则 directly in WAF.
For more 信息rmation, refer to AI Crawl Control with Cloudflare WAF.
When blocking an AI crawler, you can configure the details of the 响应 that 获取s returned to the AI crawler. Specifically, you can configure:
The 响应 code
The 响应 正文

This provides you with a channel to open dialogue with the AI crawler owner, and to 信息rm the AI crawler how to properly license their content, thereby creating a direct path from crawling attempt to commercial agreement.
To edit these 值s:
Go to the **设置** tab.
Under **Block 响应**, select **Edit**.
Once you have edited the 值s, select **Save**.
:::注意
You must have opted to block at least one AI crawler to configure a custom block 响应.
You can choose which HTTP 响应 code to return when blocking an AI crawler.
Use the dropdown menu to select the desired 响应 code. You can choose from:
: Use this option if you wish to indicate that you do not want the AI crawler to access your content.
: Use this option if you wish to indicate that the AI crawler must pay to access your content.

AI Crawl Control uses Cloudflare WAF to return custom block 响应s. If you have manually configured the AI Crawl Control WAF 规则 to return a 响应 code other than  or , AI Crawl Control will not be able to enforce the 响应 code you have selected, and the dropdown will appear blank. Ensure you have selected either  or .
Refer to Configure a custom 响应 for blocked 请求s and AI Crawl Control with Cloudflare WAF for more 信息rmation.
You can write a custom 消息 (HTTP 响应 正文) to return when blocking an AI crawler.
In the 响应 正文 text 字段, enter the 响应 you wish to display for the AI crawler in plain text.
Use pay per crawl to charge AI crawlers every time they access your content.
Learn how AI Crawl Control interacts with WAF, including advanced 规则 customization, in AI Crawl Control with Cloudflare WAF.

/ai-crawl-control/features/pay-per-crawl.title: What is Pay Per Crawl?
paragraphs: :::注意[Pay per crawl closed 测试版]
Pay per crawl is currently in closed 测试版.
To find out how to join the 测试版 program, reach out to us at Pay per crawl signup, or contact your 账户 executive if you are an existing Enterprise customer.
To 了解更多 about pay per crawl, refer to Cloudflare blog: Introducing pay per crawl: enabling content owners to charge AI crawlers for access.

AI crawlers often consume vast amounts of web content. Some provide mutual benefit to content owners by 索引ing content for 搜索 engines, but others engage in activities such as content scraping without permission.
The resulting landscape leaves content owners with limited options for managing AI crawlers or receiving compensation for automated access to their intellectual property.
Pay per crawl is a 特性 of AI Crawl Control that enables site owners to control and monetize AI crawler access to content by setting a price per 区域.

Each time an AI crawler 请求s content, they either present payment intent via 请求 头部 for 成功ful  access, or receive an  响应 with 定价. Cloudflare acts as the  Merchant of 记录  for pay per crawl and also provides the underlying technical infrastructure.
:::注意

If you block an AI crawler from a 区域 via either of Cloudflare's WAF or Bot Management products, those products' 规则ets will override pay per crawl's "charge" 特性, and the blocked crawler will not have access to the 区域.
Ultimately, pay per crawl enables:
Site owners to take control of their content, and charge a fee every time an AI crawler accesses a page in their Cloudflare 区域. For more details, refer to use pay per crawl as a site owner.
AI crawler owners to pay to access content on sites protected by pay per crawl. For more details, refer to use pay per crawl as an AI owner.
Refer to the following 资源.
Use pay per crawl as a site owner.
Use pay per crawl as an AI owner.
AI Crawl Control with Cloudflare WAF.
AI Crawl Control with Cloudflare Bots.

/ai-crawl-control/features/track-robots-.title: Track robots.txt
headings: 过滤器 robots.txt 请求 data
paragraphs: The Robots.txt tab in AI Crawl Control provide insights into how AI crawlers interact with your    files across your 主机名s. You can 监控 请求 patterns, verify file 可用性, and identify crawlers that violate your directives.
To access robots.txt insights:
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **AI Crawl Control**.
Go to the **Robots.txt** tab.
The status card at the top of the tab shows whether Cloudflare is managing your  file.

When 已启用, Cloudflare will include directives to block common AI crawlers used for training and include its Content Signals Policy in your . For more details on how Cloudflare manages your  file, refer to Managed .
You can apply 过滤器s at the top of the tab to narrow your analysis of robots.txt 请求s:
过滤器 by specific crawler name (for 示例, Googlebot or specific AI bots).
过滤器 by the entity 运行中 the crawler to understand direct licensing opportunities or existing agreements.
过滤器 by general 使用场景 (for 示例, AI training, general 搜索, or AI assistant).
Select a custom time frame for historical analysis.
The 值s in all 表s and 指标 will 更新 according to your 过滤器s.
The 可用性 表 shows the historical 请求 frequency and health status of  files across your 主机名s over the selected time frame.
From this 表, you can take the following 操作s:
监控 for a high 数字 of un成功ful 请求s, which suggests that crawlers are having trouble accessing your  file.
If the **Status** is , 创建 a  file to provide clear directives.
If the file exists, check for 上游 WAF 规则 or other 安全 设置 that may be blocking access.

If the **Content Signals** column indicates that signals are missing, add them to your  file. You can do this by following the Content Signals instructions or by enabling Managed  to have Cloudflare manage them for you.
The Violations 表 identifies AI crawlers that have 请求ed paths explicitly disallowed by your  file. This helps you identify non-compliant crawlers and take appropriate 操作.
:::注意[How violations are calculated]

The Violations 表 identifies mismatches between your current  directives and past crawler 请求s. Because violations are not logged in real-time, recently added or changed 规则 may cause previously legitimate 请求s to be flagged as violations.
For 示例, if you add a 新功能  规则, all past 请求s to that path will appear as violations, even though they were not violations at the time of the 请求.
When you identify crawlers violating your  directives, you have several options:
Navigate to the **Crawlers** tab to permanently block the non-compliant crawler.
Use Cloudflare WAF to 创建 a path-specific 安全 规则 for the violating crawler.
Use 重定向 规则 to 指南 violating crawlers to an appropriate area of your site.

观察 and control your AI applications.

Cloudflare's AI Gateway allows you to gain visibility and control over your AI apps. By connecting your apps to AI Gateway, you can gather insights on how people are using your application with 分析 and logging and then control how your application scales with 特性 such as 缓存, 速率限制, as well as 请求 重试, model 回退, and more. Better yet - it only takes one line of code to 快速开始.
Check out the 快速开始 指南 to learn how to configure your applications with AI Gateway.
View 指标 such as the 数字 of 请求s, 令牌s, and the cost it takes to run your application.
Gain insight on 请求s and 错误.
Serve 请求s directly from Cloudflare's cache instead of the 源al model provider for faster 请求s and cost savings.
Control how your application scales by limiting the 数字 of 请求s your application receives.
Improve resilience by defining 请求 重试 and model 回退s in case of an 错误.
Workers AI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, OpenAI, Replicate, and more work with AI Gateway.
Run machine learning models, powered by 无服务器 GPUs, on Cloudflare’s 全球网络.

构建 full-stack AI applications with Vectorize, Cloudflare's vector 数据库. Adding Vectorize enables you to perform 任务s such as semantic 搜索, recommendations, anomaly detection or can be used to provide context and memory to an LLM.

are 构建ing, and discuss the 平台 with other developers.

全球网络.

what is 新功能 in Cloudflare Workers.

/ai-gateway/features/caching/.title: 缓存
/ai-gateway/features/caching/.description: Override 缓存 设置 on a per-请求 basis.
headings: 优势 of Using 缓存
paragraphs: AI Gateway can cache 响应s from your AI model providers, serving them directly from Cloudflare's cache for identical 请求s.
**Reduced 延迟:** Serve 响应s faster to your users by avoiding a round trip to the 源 AI provider for repeated 请求s.
**Cost Savings:** Minimize the 数字 of paid 请求s made to your AI provider, especially for frequently accessed or non-dynamic content.
**Increased 吞吐量:** Offload repetitive 请求s from your AI provider, allowing it to handle unique 请求s more efficiently.
:::注意
Currently 缓存 is 支持的 only for text and image 响应s, and it applies only to identical 请求s.
This 配置 优势 使用场景 with limited prompt options. For 示例, a 支持 bot that asks "How can I help you?" and lets the user select an answer from a limited set of options works well with the current 缓存 配置.
We plan on adding semantic 搜索 for 缓存 in the future to improve cache hit rates.
To set the 默认 缓存 配置 in the 控制面板:
登录to the Cloudflare 控制面板 and select your 账户.
Select **AI** > **AI Gateway**.
Select **设置**.
Enable **Cache 响应s**.
Change the 默认 缓存 to whatever 值 you prefer.
To set the 默认 缓存 配置 using the API:
创建 an API 令牌 with the following 权限:
获取 your 账户 ID.
Using that API 令牌 and 账户 ID, send a  请求 to 创建 a 新功能 Gateway and include a 值 for the .
This 缓存 behavior will be uniformly applied to all 请求s that 支持 缓存. If you need to modify the cache 设置 for specific 请求s, you have the flexibility to override this setting on a per-请求 basis.
To check whether a 响应 comes from cache or not, cf-aig-cache-status will be designated as  or .

While your gateway's 默认 cache 设置 provide a good baseline, you might need more granular control. These situations could include data freshness, content with varying lifespans, or dynamic or personalized 响应s.
To address these needs, AI Gateway allows you to override 默认 cache behaviors on a per-请求 basis using specific HTTP 头部. This gives you the precision to optimize 缓存 for individual API calls.
The following 头部 allow you to define this per-请求 cache behavior:
The following 头部 have been 已更新 to 新功能 names, though the old 头部 will still 函数. We recommend updating to the 新功能 头部 to ensure future 兼容性:
is now
Skip cache refers to bypassing the cache and fetching the 请求 directly from the 源al provider, without utilizing any cached copy.
You can use the 头部 cf-aig-skip-cache to bypass the cached version of the 请求.
As an 示例, when submitting a 请求 to OpenAI, include the 头部 in the following manner:

Cache TTL, or Time To Live, is the duration a cached 请求 remains valid before it expires and is refreshed from the 源al source. You can use cf-aig-cache-ttl to set the desired 缓存 duration in seconds. The minimum TTL is 60 seconds and the maximum TTL is one month.

For 示例, if you set a TTL of one hour, it means that a 请求 is kept in the cache for an hour. Within that hour, an identical 请求 will be served from the cache instead of the 源al API. After an hour, the cache expires and the 请求 will go to the 源al API for a fresh 响应, and that 响应 will repopulate the cache for the next hour.
Custom cache 键s let you override the 默认 cache 键 in order to precisely set the cacheability setting for any resource. To override the 默认 cache 键, you can use the 头部 cf-aig-cache-键.

When you use the cf-aig-cache-键 头部 for the first time, you will receive a 响应 from the provider. Subsequent 请求s with the same 头部 will return the cached 响应. If the cf-aig-cache-ttl 头部 is used, 响应s will be cached according to the specified Cache Time To Live. Otherwise, 响应s will be cached according to the cache 设置 in the 控制面板. If 缓存 is not 已启用 for the gateway, 响应s will be cached for 5 minutes by 默认.
:::注意[AI Gateway 缓存 behavior]

Cache in AI Gateway is volatile. If two identical 请求s are sent simultaneously, the first 请求 may not cache in time for the second 请求 to use it, which may result in the second 请求 retrieving data from the 源al source.

/ai-gateway/observability/analytics/.title: 分析
paragraphs: Your AI Gateway 控制面板 shows 指标 on 请求s, 令牌s, 缓存, 错误, and cost. You can 过滤器 these 指标 by time.
These 分析 help you understand traffic patterns, 令牌 consumption, and
potential issues across AI providers. You can
view the following 分析:
**请求s**: Track the total 数字 of 请求s processed by AI Gateway.
**令牌 Usage**: Analyze 令牌 consumption across 请求s, giving insight into usage patterns.
**Costs**: Gain visibility into the costs associated with using different AI providers, allowing you to track s待处理, manage bud获取s, and optimize 资源.
**错误**: 监控 the 数字 of 错误 across the gateway, helping to identify and troubleshoot issues.
**Cached 响应s**: View the percentage of 响应s served from cache, which can help reduce costs and improve speed.
You can use GraphQL to 查询 your usage data outside of the AI Gateway 控制面板. See the 示例 查询 below. You will need to use your Cloudflare 令牌 when making the 请求, and change  to match your 账户 tag.

/ai-gateway/features/dynamic-routing/.title: Dynamic 路由
paragraphs: Dynamic 路由 enables you to 创建 请求 路由 flows through a visual interface or a JSON-based 配置. Instead of hard-coding a single model, with Dynamic 路由 you compose a small flow that evaluates 条件s, enforces quotas, and chooses models with 回退s. You can iterate without touching application code—publish a 新功能 路由 version and you’re done. With dynamic 路由, you can easily implement advanced 使用场景 such as:
Directing different segments (paid/not-paid user) to different models
Restricting each user/project/team with bud获取/rate 限制
A/B and gradual rollouts
while making it accessible to both developers and non-technical team members.
**路由**: A named, versioned flow (for 示例, dynamic/支持) that you can use as instead of the model name in your 请求s.
**Nodes**
**Start**: Entry point for the 路由.
**条件al**: If/Else branch based on 表达式s that 参考 请求 正文, 头部, or metadata (for 示例, user_plan == "paid").
**Percentage**: 路由 请求s probabi列表ically across mul提示le outputs, useful for A/B 测试 and gradual rollouts.
**Model**: Calls a provider/model with the 请求 参数
**Rate Limit**: Enforces 数字 of 请求s quotas (per your 键, per period) and switches to 回退 when exceeded.
**Bud获取 Limit**: Enforces cost quotas (per your 键, per period) and switches to 回退 when exceeded.
**End**: Terminates the flow and returns the final model 响应.
**Metadata**: Arbitrary 键-值 context attached to the 请求 (for 示例, userId, orgId, plan). You can pass this from your app so 规则 can 参考 it.
**Versions**: Each change produces a 新功能 draft. 部署 to make it live with instant rollback.
:::注意
Ensure your gateway has 身份验证 turned on, and you have your 上游 providers 键s stored with BYOK.
创建 a 路由.
Go to **(Select your gateway)** > **Dynamic 路由** > **Add 路由**, and name it (for 示例, ).
Open **Editor**.
Define 条件als, 限制 and other 设置.
You can use Custom Metadata in your 条件als.
Configure model nodes.
示例:
Node A: Provider OpenAI, Model
Node B: Provider OpenAI, Model
Save a version.
Click **Save** to save the state. You can always roll back to earlier versions from **Versions**.
部署 the version to make it live.
Call the 路由 from your code.
Use the OpenAI compatible 端点, and use the 路由 name in place of the model, for 示例, .

/ai-gateway/observability/logging/.title: Logging
headings: DLP 字段s in 日志
How to 删除 日志
paragraphs: Logging is a fundamental 构建ing block for application development. 日志 provide insights during the early stages of development and are often critical to understanding issues occurring in production.

Your AI Gateway 控制面板 shows 日志 of individual 请求s, including the user prompt, model 响应, provider, timestamp, 请求 status, 令牌 usage, cost, and duration. When DLP policies are configured, 日志 for 请求s that 触发器 a DLP match also include the DLP 操作 taken (Flag or Block), matched policy IDs, matched profile IDs, and the specific detection entries that were 触发器ed. These 日志 persist, giving you the flexibility to store them for your preferred duration and do more with valuable 请求 data.

Each gateway has a storage limit based on your plan. You can customize this limit per gateway in your gateway 设置. If your storage limit is reached, 新功能 日志 will stop being saved. To continue saving 日志, you must 删除 older 日志 to free up space for 新功能 日志.
To 了解更多 about your plan 限制, refer to 限制.

We recommend using an authenticated gateway when storing 日志 to pr事件 unauthorized access and protects against invalid 请求s that can inflate log storage usage and make it harder to find the data you need. 了解更多 about setting up an authenticated gateway.

日志, which include 指标 as well as 请求 and 响应 data, are 已启用 by 默认 for each gateway. This logging behavior will be uniformly applied to all 请求s in the gateway. If you are concerned about privacy or compliance and want to turn log collection off, you can go to 设置 and opt out of 日志. If you need to modify the log 设置 for specific 请求s, you can override this setting on a per-请求 basis.
To override the 默认 logging behavior set in the 设置 tab, you can define 头部 on a per-请求 basis.

The  头部 allows you to bypass the 默认 log setting for the gateway. If the gateway is configured to save 日志, the 头部 will exclude the log for that specific 请求. Conversely, if logging is 已禁用 at the gateway level, this 头部 will save the log for that 请求.
In the 示例 below, we use  to bypass the 默认 setting to avoid saving the log.

The  头部 allows you to control whether the raw 请求 and 响应 bodies (payloads) are stored for a given 请求. Unlike , which controls the entire log entry, this 头部 only affects payload storage — metadata such as 令牌 counts, model, provider, status code, cost, and duration will still be logged.
This is useful when you want to maintain visibility into usage 指标 and 请求 metadata without persisting sensitive prompt or completion data.
In the 示例 below, we use  to skip storing the 请求 and 响应 bodies while keeping the metadata log.
:::注意
If  is set to , the entire log entry (including metadata) is skipped regardless of the  值. Use  on its own if you only want to suppress payload storage while retaining metadata 日志.
When Data Loss Pr事件ion (DLP) policies are 已启用 on a gateway, log entries for 请求s that 触发器 a DLP policy match include additional 字段s:
These 字段s are available both in the 控制面板 log viewer and through the 日志 API. You can 过滤器 日志 by DLP 操作 in the 控制面板 to view only flagged or blocked 请求s. For more details on DLP 监控, refer to 监控 DLP 事件.
To manage your log storage effectively, you can:
Set Storage 限制: Configure a limit on the 数字 of 日志 stored per gateway in your gateway 设置 to ensure you only pay for what you need.

Enable Automatic Log Deletion: Activate the Automatic Log Deletion 特性 in your gateway 设置 to automatically 删除 the oldest 日志 once the storage limit for your 账户 is reached. This ensures 新功能 日志 are always saved without manual intervention.
To manage your log storage effectively and ensure continuous logging, you can 删除 日志 using the following 方法:

​To maintain continuous logging within your gateway's storage constraints, enable Automatic Log Deletion in your Gateway 设置. This 特性 automatically 删除s the oldest 日志 once the storage limit for your 账户 is reached, ensuring 新功能 日志 are saved without manual intervention.

To manually 删除 日志 through the 控制面板, navigate to the 日志 tab in the 控制面板. Use the available 过滤器s such as status, cache, provider, cost, or any other options in the dropdown to refine the 日志 you wish to 删除. Once 过滤器ed, select 删除 日志 to complete the 操作.
See full 列表 of available 过滤器s and their descriptions below:
You can programmatically 删除 日志 using the AI Gateway API. For more comprehensive 信息rmation on the  日志 端点, check out the Cloudflare API 文档.

/ai-gateway/features/rate-limiting/.title: 速率限制
paragraphs: 速率限制 controls the traffic that reaches your application, which pr事件 expensive bills and suspicious activity.
You can define rate 限制 as the 数字 of 请求s that 获取 sent in a specific time frame. For 示例, you can limit your application to 100 请求s per 60 seconds.

You can also select if you would like a fixed or sliding 速率限制 technique. With 速率限制, we allow a certain 数字 of 请求s within a window of time. For 示例, if it is a fixed rate, the window is based on time, so there would be no more than  请求s in a ten minute window. If it is a sliding rate, there would be no more than  请求s in the last ten minutes.

To illustrate this, let us say you had a limit of ten 请求s per ten minutes, starting at 12:00. So the fixed window is 12:00-12:10, 12:10-12:20, and so on. If you sent ten 请求s at 12:09 and ten 请求s at 12:11, all 20 请求s would be 成功ful in a fixed window strategy. However, they would fail in a sliding window strategy since there were more than ten 请求s in the last ten minutes.
When your 请求s exceed the allowed rate, you will encounter 速率限制. This means the server will respond with a  status code and your 请求 will not be processed.
To set the 默认 速率限制 配置 in the 控制面板:
登录to the Cloudflare 控制面板 and select your 账户.
Go to **AI** > **AI Gateway**.
Go to **设置**.
Enable **Rate-limiting**.
Adjust the rate, time period, and 速率限制 method as desired.
To set the 默认 速率限制 配置 using the API:
创建 an API 令牌 with the following 权限:
获取 your 账户 ID.
Using that API 令牌 and 账户 ID, send a  请求 to 创建 a 新功能 Gateway and include a 值 for the , , and .
This 速率限制 behavior will be uniformly applied to all 请求s for that gateway.

/ai-search/configuration/indexing/.title: 索引ing
paragraphs: AI 搜索 automatically 索引es your data into vector embeddings optimized for semantic 搜索. Once a data source is connected, 索引ing runs continuously in the background to keep your knowledge base fresh and 查询able.
AI 搜索 automatically 监控s your data source for 更新s and re索引es your content every 6 hours. During each cycle, 新功能 or modified files are reprocessed to keep your Vectorize 索引 up to date.
You can 监控 the status and history of all 索引ing activity in the 作业s tab, including real-time 日志 for each 作业 to help you troubleshoot and verify 成功ful syncs.
You can control 索引ing behavior through the following 操作s on the 控制面板:
**Sync 索引**: Manually 触发器 AI 搜索 to scan your data source for 新功能, modified, or 删除d files and initiate an 索引ing 作业 to 更新 the associated Vectorize 索引. A 新功能 索引ing 作业 can be initiated every 30 seconds.
**Sync Individual File**: 触发器 a sync for a specific file from the **概述** page. Go to **索引ed Items** and select the sync icon next to the specific file you want to re索引.
**Pause 索引ing**: Temporarily stop all 定时 索引ing checks and reprocessing. Useful for 调试 or freezing your knowledge base.
The total time to 索引 depends on the 数字 and 类型 of files in your data source. Factors that affect 性能 include:
Total 数字 of files and their sizes
File formats (for 示例, images take longer than plain text)
延迟 of Workers AI models used for embedding and image processing
To ensure smooth and reliable 索引ing:
Make sure your files are within the **size limit** and in a 支持的 format to avoid being skipped.
Keep your Service API 令牌 valid to pr事件 索引ing failures.
Regularly clean up outdated or unnecessary content in your knowledge base to avoid hitting Vectorize 索引 限制.

/ai-search/configuration/cache/.title: Similarity cache
headings: What to consider when using similarity cache
paragraphs: Similarity-based 缓存 in AI 搜索 lets you serve 响应s from Cloudflare’s cache for queries that are similar to previous 请求s, rather than creating 新功能, unique 响应s for every 请求. This speeds up 响应 times and cuts costs by reusing answers for questions that are close in meaning.
Unlike with basic 缓存, which 创建s a 新功能 响应 with every 请求, this is what happens when a 请求 is received using similarity-based 缓存:
AI 搜索 checks if a _similar_ prompt (based on your chosen threshold) has been answered before.
If a match is found, it returns the cached 响应 instantly.
If no match is found, it generates a 新功能 响应 and caches it.
To see if a 响应 came from the cache, check the  头部:  for cached and  for 新功能.
Consider these behaviors when using similarity 缓存:
**Volatile Cache**: If two similar 请求s hit at the same time, the first might not cache in time for the second to use it, resulting in a .
**30-Day Cache**: Cached 响应s last 30 days, then expire automatically. No custom durations for now.
**Data Dependency**: Cached 响应s are tied to specific document chunks. If those chunks change or 获取 删除d, the cache clears to keep answers fresh.
AI 搜索’s similarity cache uses MinHash and Locality-Sensitive Hashing (LSH) to find and reuse 响应s for prompts that are worded similarly.
Here’s 工作原理 when a 新功能 prompt comes in:
The prompt is split into small overlapping chunks of words (called shingles), like “what’s the” or “the weather.”
These shingles are turned into a “fingerprint” using MinHash. The more overlap two prompts have, the more similar their fingerprints will be.
Fingerprints are placed into LSH 存储桶s, which help AI 搜索 quickly find similar prompts without comparing every single one.
If a past prompt in the same 存储桶 is similar enough (based on your configured threshold), AI 搜索 reuses its cached 响应.
The similarity threshold decides how close two prompts need to be to reuse a cached 响应. Here are the available thresholds:
测试 these 值s to see which works best with your RAG application.

创建 AI-powered 搜索 for your data

AI 搜索 is Cloudflare’s managed 搜索 service. You can connect your data such as websites or unstructured content, and it automatically 创建s a continuously updating 索引 that you can 查询 with natural language in your applications or AI agents. It natively integrates with Cloudflare’s developer 平台 工具 like Vectorize, AI Gateway, R2, Browser Rendering and Workers AI, while also 支持ing third-party providers and open standards.
It 支持s retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) patterns, enabling you to 构建 enterprise 搜索, natural language 搜索, and AI-powered chat without managing infrastructure.
快速开始
Watch AI 搜索 demo
Automatically and continuously 索引 your data source, keeping your content fresh without manual reprocessing.
创建 multitenancy by scoping 搜索 to each tenant’s data using folder-based metadata 过滤器s.
Call your AI 搜索 instance for 搜索 or AI 搜索 directly from a Cloudflare Worker using the native 绑定 integration.
Cache repeated queries and results to improve 延迟 and reduce compute on repeated 请求s.
Run machine learning models, powered by 无服务器 GPUs, on Cloudflare’s 全球网络.
观察 and control your AI applications with 缓存, 速率限制, 请求 重试, model 回退, and more.
构建 full-stack AI applications with Vectorize, Cloudflare’s vector 数据库.
构建 无服务器 applications and 部署 instantly across the globe for exceptional 性能, reliability, and scale.
Store large amounts of unstructured data without the costly egress 带宽 fees associated with typical cloud storage services.
构建 and 部署 your first Workers AI application.
Connect with the Workers 社区 on Discord to ask questions, share what you
are 构建ing, and discuss the 平台 with other developers.
Follow @CloudflareDev on Twitter to learn about product announcements, and
what is 新功能 in Cloudflare Workers.

/ai-search/how-to/multitenancy/.title: 创建 multitenancy
headings: 2. 搜索 using folder 过滤器s
提示: Use "starts with" 过滤器
paragraphs: AI 搜索 支持s multitenancy by letting you segment content by tenant, so each user, customer, or workspace can only access their own data. This is typically done by organizing documents into per-tenant folders and applying metadata 过滤器s at 查询 time.
When uploading files to R2, structure your content by tenant using unique folder paths.
示例 folder structure:
customer-a
日志/
contracts/
customer-b

When 索引ing, AI 搜索 will automatically store the folder path as metadata under the  attribute. It is recommended to enforce folder separation during upload or 索引ing to pr事件 accidental data access across tenants.
To ensure a tenant only retrieves their own documents, apply a  过滤器 when performing a 搜索.
While an equality 过滤器 tar获取s files at a specific folder, you often want to retrieve all documents belonging to a tenant, including files in subfolders. For 示例, all files in  with a structure like:
profile.md
contracts
property
contract-1.pdf
To achieve this "starts with" behavior, use a range 过滤器:
This range 过滤器 matches all paths starting with  because  comes after  in ASCII order, capturing both  and .

/analytics/graphql-api/.title: GraphQL 分析 API
paragraphs: The GraphQL 分析 API provides data regarding HTTP 请求s passing through Cloudflare's network, as well as data from specific products, such as 防火墙 or Load Balancing. Network 分析 users also have access to packet-level data. Use the GraphQL 分析 API to select specific datasets and 指标 of interest, 过滤器 and aggregate the data along various dimensions, and integrate the results with other applications.

The basis of the API is the GraphQL framework, 创建d and open-sourced by Facebook. There is an 活跃 developer 社区 for GraphQL and powerful clients for 运行中 queries, which makes it easy to 快速开始. GraphQL is especially useful for 构建ing visualizations and powers the 分析 in the Cloudflare 控制面板.

GraphQL models a business 域名 as a graph using a 模式. In the 模式, there are logical definitions for different 类型s of nodes and their connections (edges). These nodes are the datasets you use for your 分析. You write queries in GraphQL much like in SQL: you specify the dataset (表), the 指标 to retrieve (such as 请求s and bytes), and 过滤器 or group by dimensions (for 示例, a time period).
GraphQL differs from a traditional API: it has one single 端点:
You pass the 查询 参数 as a JSON 对象 in the payload of a  请求 to this 端点.
You can use  to make 请求s to the GraphQL 分析 API. Alternatively, you can use a GraphQL client to construct queries and pass 请求s to the GraphQL 分析 API.
We are using GraphiQL for our 示例 GraphQL queries. There are many other popular open-source clients that you can find online, such as Altair and Insomnia.

The purpose of the GraphQL API is to provide aggregated 分析 about various Cloudflare products. These datasets should not be used as a measure for usage that Cloudflare uses for billing purposes. Billable traffic excludes things like DDoS traffic, while GraphQL is a measure of overall consumption/usage, so it will include all measurable traffic.

/analytics/analytics-engine/.title: Workers 分析 Engine
paragraphs: Workers 分析 Engine provides unlimited-cardinality 分析 at scale, via a built-in API to write data points from Workers, and a SQL API to 查询 that data.
You can use Workers 分析 Engine to:
Expose custom 分析 to your own customers
构建 usage-based billing systems
Understand the health of your service on a per-customer or per-user basis
Add instrumentation to frequently called code paths, without impacting 性能 or overwhelming external 分析 systems with 事件

Cloudflare Workers allows developers to 构建 无服务器 applications and 部署 instantly across the globe for exceptional 性能, reliability, and scale.
Detailed 日志 that contain metadata generated by Cloudflare products helpful for 调试, identifying 配置 adjustments, and creating 分析.

/analytics/types-of-analytics/.title: 类型s of 分析
headings: 分析 相关 to specific properties
paragraphs: Cloudflare 分析 is a comprehensive product that encompasses all metadata generated by the Cloudflare network. You can access these insights through the Cloudflare 控制面板. De待处理 on where in the 控制面板 you are, it will show you different aspects from the collected metadata.

Available under 分析 & 日志 in your Cloudflare 控制面板 when you 登录, 账户 分析 (测试版) shows you an 概述 of traffic for all 域名s under your Cloudflare 账户, such as 请求s and 带宽 by country, 信息rmation 相关 to 安全, cache, and 错误, among others. To access 账户 分析, 登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, select the appropriate 账户, and go to 分析 & 日志 > 账户 分析.
Network 分析 provides visibility into network and transport-layer traffic patterns, and DDoS attacks.
The Network 分析 控制面板 is only available for customers with an Enterprise 域名 plan who use Spectrum, Magic Transit, or Bring Your Own IP (BYOIP).

Web Analytics (formerly known as Browser Insights) provides free, privacy-first 分析 for your website. Web Analytics does not collect your visitor's personal data, and allows you to have a detailed view into the 性能 of web pages as experienced by your visitors.
Carbon Impact Report gives you a report on carbon savings from using Cloudflare services versus Internet averages for your usage volume.
Cloudflare is committed to use 100% re新功能able energy sources, but also to remove all greenhouse gases emitted as a result of powering our network since 2010.
Access aggregated traffic, 安全, and 性能 指标 for each 域名 proxied through Cloudflare. To access these 分析, 登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, select your 账户 and 域名, and go to the 分析 & 日志 section.
Data available under the 分析 & 日志 section includes:
**HTTP Traffic** - 请求s, Data transfer, Page views, Visits, and API 请求s.
**安全** - Total Threats, Top Crawlers/Bots, 速率限制, Total Threats 已停止.
**性能** - 源 性能, 带宽 Saved.
**Edge Reachability** - Last mile insights for Enterprise customers.
**Workers** - Detailed 信息rmation 相关 to your Workers per 区域, and Workers KV per 账户.
**日志** - Detailed 日志 of the metadata generated by Cloudflare products for Enterprise customers.
**Instant 日志** - Live stream of traffic for your 域名. Enterprise customers can access this live stream from the Cloudflare 控制面板 or from a command-line interface (CLI).
Beyond the 分析 provided for your properties, you can also access 分析 相关 to specific products:
Bot 分析 - Shows which 请求s are associated with known bots, likely automated traffic, likely human traffic, and more.
Cache 分析 - Insights to that help determine if 资源 are missing from cache, expired, or ineligible for 缓存.
DNS 分析 - Provides insights about DNS queries to your 区域.
Load Balancing 分析 - 特性 指标 to help gain insights into traffic 负载均衡器 steering decisions.
安全 事件 - Highlights attack and mitigation 指标 detected by the Cloudflare WAF and HTTP DDoS protection systems.
安全 分析 - Displays 信息rmation about all incoming HTTP 请求s, including those not affected by 安全 measures (for 示例, from the WAF and DDoS protection systems).

If you would like to have more control over how you visualize the analytic and 登录formation available on the Cloudflare 控制面板, use the GraphQL 分析 API to 构建 customized views. This API replaces and expands on the previous 区域 分析 API.

/analytics/network-analytics/.title: Network 分析
paragraphs: Cloudflare Network 分析 (version 2) provides near real-time visibility into network and transport-layer traffic patterns and DDoS attacks. Network 分析 visualizes packet and bit-level data, the same data available via the Network 分析 dataset of the GraphQL 分析 API.
For a technical deep-dive into Network 分析, refer to our blog post.
The Network 分析 日志 refer to IP traffic of Magic Transit customer prefixes/leased IP addresses or Spectrum applications. These 日志 are not directly associated with the 区域s in your Cloudflare 账户.
The data retention for Network 分析 is 16 weeks. Additionally, data older than eight weeks might have lower resolution when using narrow time frames.

Enable the Sensitive Data Detection 规则et and accompanying 规则

paragraphs: This 指南 will help you 设置 API Shield to identify and address API 安全 最佳实践.
:::注意
Enabling API Shield 特性 will have no impact on your traffic until you choose to move a setting from  to  mode.
模式 validation protects your APIs by ensuring only 请求s matching your  API 模式  are allowed to communicate with your 源.
While not strictly 必需, uploading a pre-existing 模式 will offer the chance to automatically add 端点 to 端点 Management. If you al读取y have a 模式, you can upload it to 模式 validation.
It is recommended to start with 模式 validation 规则 set to  to review logged 请求s in 安全 事件. When you are confident that only the correct 请求s are logged, you should switch the 规则 to .
If you do not have a 模式 to upload, continue 读取ing this 指南 to learn how to generate a 模式 with API Shield.

API Shield works with Cloudflare WAF’s Sensitive Data Detection 规则et to identify  API 端点  that return sensitive data such as social 安全 or credit card 数字s in their HTTP 响应s. 监控 these 端点 can be critical to ensuring sensitive data is returned only when expected.
A subscription is 必需 for Sensitive Data Detection. Contact your 账户 team if you are not entitled for Sensitive Data Detection.
You can identify 端点 returning sensitive data by selecting the icon next to the path in a row. Expand the 端点 to see details on which 规则 were 触发器ed and view more 信息rmation by exploring 事件 in 防火墙 事件.

Cloudflare’s machine learning models have al读取y inspected your existing traffic for the presence of API 端点. By adding 端点 from API Discovery to 端点 Management, you can unlock further 安全, visibility, and management 特性 of the 平台. 端点 Management 监控s the health of your API 端点 by saving, updating, and 监控 性能 指标.
模式 validation, 模式 learning, JWT validation, Sequence 分析, sequence mitigation, and rate limit recommendations only run on 端点 saved to 端点 Management.
You can save your 端点 directly from API Discovery, 模式 validation, or manually by method, path, and host.
This will add the specified 端点 to your 列表 of managed 端点. You can view your 列表 of saved 端点 in the 端点 Management page.
Cloudflare will aggregate 性能 data and 安全 data on your 端点 once it is saved.

Cloudflare will inspect your API traffic and begin to learn its 模式 over the next 24 hours after adding an 端点. De待处理 on how much traffic an individual 端点 sees, our confidence in the resulting 模式 may differ.
Cloudflare will also use the configured 会话 identifiers to suggest rate 限制 per 端点.
For best results, allow at least 24 hours after adding 端点 before proceeding to the following 步骤s.
We recommend proceeding with additional 配置s if this is your first time setting up API Shield and have added your first API 端点 to 端点 Management.
速率限制 规则 allow you to define rate 限制 for 请求s matching an 表达式, and choose the 操作 to perform when those rate 限制 are reached.

You can 观察 Cloudflare suggested rate 限制 in 端点 Management for 端点 using 会话 identifiers. Unlike many 安全 工具, these recommended rate 限制 are per-端点 and per-会话, not site-wide and not based on IP address. When creating a 规则, it will be based on only traffic to that specific 端点 from unique visitors during their 会话. This 特性 allows you to be very specific and tar获取ed with your rate limit enforcement, both lowering abusive traffic and false positives due to broadly scoped 规则.
Cloudflare learns 模式 参数 via traffic inspection for all 端点 stored in 端点 Management. You can export OpenAPI 模式s in OpenAPI v3.0.0 format by 主机名.
By importing the learned 模式, you can protect API 端点 found via API Discovery that were never previously possible to protect due to not knowing about their presence or 模式.

You can import the learned 模式 of an entire 主机名 using the Cloudflare 控制面板. Alternatively, you can apply learned 模式s to individual 端点. Before applying the learned 模式, Cloudflare suggests exporting the 模式 to review what will validate your traffic.

Learned 模式s will always include the 列表ed 主机名 in the servers section, all 端点 by host, method, and path, and detected path 变量s. They can also potentially include detected 查询 参数 and their format. You can 可选ly include API Shield's rate limit threshold recommendations.
You can export your learned 模式s in the Cloudflare 控制面板 or via the API.
Sequence 分析 surfaces a subset of 重要 API 请求 sequences found in your API traffic over time.

You can 观察 the top sequences in your API traffic that contain 端点 stored in 端点 Management. We rank sequences by Correlation Score. High-scoring sequences contain API 请求s which are likely to occur to获取her in order.
Sequence mitigation allows you to enforce 请求 patterns for authenticated clients communicating with your API. Use Sequence 分析 to better understand the 请求 sequences used by your API clients.

You should apply all possible API Shield protections (速率限制 suggestions, 模式 validation, JWT validation, and mTLS) to API 端点 found in high correlation score sequences that make up the critical 请求 flows in your application. You should also check their specific 端点 order with your development team.
For more 信息rmation, refer to Detecting API abuse automatically using sequence analysis blog post.
Use the Cloudflare API to configure JSON Web 令牌s validation, which validates the integrity and validity of JWTs sent by clients to your API or web application.
If your 源 uses GraphQL, you may consider setting 限制 on GraphQL 查询 size and depth.

GraphQL malicious 查询 protection scans your GraphQL traffic for queries that could overload your 源 and result in a denial of service. Customers can 构建 规则 that limit the 查询 depth and size of incoming GraphQL queries in order to block suspiciously large or complex queries.
For more 信息rmation, refer to the blog post.
If you operate an API that requires or would benefit from an extra layer of protection, you may consider using Mutual TLS (mTLS).

Mutual TLS (mTLS) 身份验证 uses client 证书s to ensure traffic between client and server is bidirectionally secure and trusted. mTLS also allows 请求s that do not authenticate via an identity provider, such as Internet-of-things (IoT) devices, to demonstrate they can reach a given resource.

/api-shield/reference/.title: 参考
paragraphs: Refer to the following pages for more 信息rmation about API Shield:

/api-shield/.title: Cloudflare API Shield
headings: Why care about API 安全?
paragraphs: Identify and address your API vulnerabilities.
APIs have become the backbone of popular web services, helping the Internet become more accessible and useful.
As APIs have become more prevalent, however, so have their problems:
Many companies have thousands of APIs, including ones they do not even know about.
To 支持 a large base of users, many APIs are protected by a negative 安全 model that makes them vulnerable to credential-stuffing attacks and automated scanning 工具.
With so many 端点 and users, it’s difficult to recognize brute-force attacks against specific 端点.
Sophisticated attacks are even harder to recognize, often because even development teams are unaware of common and uncommon usage patterns.
Refer to the 快速开始 指南 to 设置 API Shield.
Secure your APIs using API Shield's 安全 特性.
监控 the health of your API 端点.
Cloudflare API 安全 products are available to Enterprise customers only, though anyone can 设置 Mutual TLS with a Cloudflare-managed 证书 authority.
The full API Shield 安全 suite is available as an Enterprise-only paid add-on, but all customers can access 端点 Management and 模式 validation 函数alities.
:::注意
API Shield currently does not work for JDCloud customers.
Cloudflare DDoS protection secures websites, applications, and entire networks while ensuring the 性能 of legitimate traffic is not compromised.
