模式 validation allows you to check if incoming traffic complies with a previously supplied API 模式. When you provide an API 模式 or select from a 列表 of learned 模式, API Shield 创建s 规则 for incoming traffic from the 模式 definitions. These 规则 define which traffic is allowed and which traffic 获取s logged or blocked.

Cloudflare has launched 模式 validation 2.0. For help configuring the previous version of 模式 validation for one or more hosts using the 控制面板, refer to Configure Classic 模式 validation. You can make changes to your Classic 模式 validation 设置 but you cannot add any 新功能 模式s.
You can migrate to 模式 validation 2.0 manually by uploading your 模式s to the 新功能 system.
{/ prettier-ignore /}
端点  must be added to 端点 Management for 模式 validation to protect them. Uploading a 模式 via the Cloudflare 控制面板 will automatically add 端点, or you can manually add them from API Discovery.
If you are uploading a 模式 via the API or Terraform, you must parse the 模式 and add your 端点 manually.
:::注意
To view the contents in your learned 模式, refer to Export a 模式 in 端点 Management.
{/ prettier-ignore-start /}
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Web Assets** page.
Go to the **模式 validation** tab.
Select **Add validation**.
Upload a 模式 file.
Select **Add 模式 and 端点**.
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Select **安全** > **API Shield**.
Go to **模式 validation** and select **Add validation**.
Select your 模式 file for upload.
观察 the 列表ed 端点, their host, method, and path. Any 新功能 端点 will automatically be added to 端点 Management.
Choose an 操作 for the non-compliant 请求s to your 端点.
{/ prettier-ignore-end /}
Changes may take a few minutes to process de待处理 on the 数字 of added 端点.
Select **Apply learned 模式**.
Choose an 操作 and select **Apply 模式**.
Go to **模式 validation** and 过滤器 by the learned 模式 available.
At this time, learned 模式s will not overwrite customer-uploaded 模式s. If an 端点 is covered by a customer-uploaded 模式 and also appears in a learned 模式, the Changes 字段 is set to .
Choose a 主机名 and review the 端点 that will be protected by the learned 模式.
(可选) Change the 操作 if a 请求 does not match the 模式.
Select **Apply 模式**.
If an 端点 is currently protected by a learned 模式, the date of the last applied learned 模式 will be shown in the current 模式 字段.
A fallthrough 规则 acts as a catch-all for 请求s that do not match 端点 in 端点 Management.
By ensuring that all your 端点 in a 模式 are added to 端点 Management, the fallthrough 操作 can protect you against legacy or zombie 端点 that your team may be unaware of.
To 设置 a fallthrough 操作:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **安全 规则** page.
Select **Templates**.
搜索 for the template named  and select **Preview template**.
Give your 规则 a descriptive name.
Choose one or more 主机名s from the dropdown menu and select your 操作.
Select **Save as draft** to 部署 later, or **部署** to 部署 now.
Your current fallthrough 规则 can be viewed in the 安全 规则 列表.
Go to **安全** > **API Shield**.
Under **设置**, go to **Fallthrough 设置**.
Select **Use Template**.
Choose one or more 主机名s from the drop down menu. The fallthrough 规则 will act on all traffic that does not match an existing 端点 in 端点 Management to the selected 主机名s.
Select **Continue to custom 规则**.
Name your 规则 and select your 操作.
Your current fallthrough 规则 can be viewed in the custom 规则 列表.

You can use the  字段 in your own custom 规则 for a more customized logic check. This 字段 evaluates as  when a 请求 does not match an 端点 in 端点 Management. Check against your API 主机名 or root path to ensure that you are not blocking non-API traffic on your 区域.
Check the multi-select box to select all 端点 associated with the 模式.
Select **Change 操作**.
Choose an 操作 from the dropdown menu.
Select **Set 操作**.
Go to **模式 validation** and select the 模式 in the 模式 列表.
Check the multi-select box to select the 端点 shown on the current page.
Choose **Select all 端点**.
模式 validation’s 默认 操作 is visible on the main 模式 validation page. This 操作 applies to any 端点 with its 操作 set to .
操作: 日志 事件 to 防火墙 事件.
操作: blocks 请求s that fail the 模式 for an 端点 and 日志 事件 to 防火墙 事件.
操作: non-compliant 请求s are neither logged nor blocked.
To change the 默认 操作:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **安全 设置** page.
过滤器 by **API abuse**.
Under **模式 validation** > **配置s**, select the edit icon next to **默认 操作**.
Choose a 新功能 操作 from the dropdown menu.
Select **Save**.
Select **模式 validation**.
Under the 默认  操作, select **Change**.
观察 the current 操作 and accept the change by selecting **Change 默认 操作** in the popup window.
Alternatively, you can modify the global 操作 via 安全 > API Shield > 设置.
You can change individual 端点 操作s separately from the 默认 操作 in 模式 validation.

This allows you to be stricter on blocking non-compliant 请求s on certain 端点 when the 默认 操作 is . It can also be used to relax constraints on non-compliant 请求s on certain 端点 when the 默认 操作 is set to . You may want to silence known false positives on an 端点 by setting the 操作 to .
To change the 操作 on an individual 端点:
搜索 for the 端点 to change.
Select the three dots on the 端点's row > **Change 操作**.
Choose a 新功能 操作 from the dropdown menu and select **Set 操作**.
Select **模式 validation** and 过滤器 the selected 端点.
Select the ellipses on the 端点's row.
You can disable 模式 validation entirely for temporary 故障排除. You can override all 操作s at once, pr事件ing 模式 validation from taking any 操作 while you complete your 故障排除.
To disable 模式 validation without changing 操作s:
Select **模式 设置**.
Turn **模式 validation** off.
Go to the **模式 validation** 设置.
Select **Disable**.
Your per-端点 配置s will be saved when modifying the setting, so that you do not lose your 配置. To re-enable your 配置s after 故障排除, navigate back to the 设置 and select Enable.
View your 模式s on **模式 validation** > **活跃 模式s**.
Go to your **模式 validation** 设置.
View your 模式s under **Uploaded 模式s** and **Learned 模式s**.
Select **过滤器** on the 端点 in either 模式.
To export a 模式, refer to Export a 模式.
Deleting the 模式 will remove validation from the currently associated 端点, but it will not 删除 the 端点 from 端点 Management.
To 删除 currently uploaded or learned 模式s:
Select the ellipses to access the menu and download or 删除 the 列表ed 模式.
Cloudflare currently only accepts OpenAPI v3 模式s. The accepted file formats are YAML ( or  file extension) and JSON ( file extension).

OpenAPI 模式s generated by different tooling may not be specific enough to import to 模式 validation. We recommend using a third-party tool such as Swagger Editor to ensure that 模式s are compliant to the OpenAPI specification.
Cloudflare API Shield's 模式 validation (importing) and 模式 learning (exporting) capabilities rely on the OpenAPI Specification (OAS) v3.0.
This 支持 includes all patch versions, such as OAS v3.0.x. We do not currently 支持 OAS v3.1 and do not plan to expand 支持 for OpenAPI 2.0.
Cloudflare recommends using a third-party tool like Swagger Editor to ensure that all 模式s are fully compliant with the OAS v3.0 specification before upload.
Currently, API Shield does not 支持 some 特性 of API 模式s, including the following: all 响应s, external 参考s, non-basic path templating, or unique items.
There is a limit of 10,000 total operations for 已启用 模式s for Enterprise customers 订阅d to API Shield. To raise this limit, contact your 账户 team.

模式 validation inspects 请求 bodies up to a maximum size that depends on your 区域 plan. 请求 bodies that exceed this limit are not validated against your 模式, and the configured 模式 validation 操作 will not apply to those 请求s.
The 默认 正文 size 限制 are:
This limit is separate from the WAF maximum 正文 inspection size, which controls how much of the 请求 payload the WAF scans. Increasing one does not affect the other.
If 模式 validation is blocking or logging 请求s due to the 正文 size limit, you will see 事件 in 安全 > 事件 with the 模式 validation 规则 as the source.
For 限制 on Free, Pro, Business, or Enterprise customers not 订阅d to API Shield, refer to Plans.
Although not strictly 必需 by the OpenAPI specification, 模式 validation strictly requires these 字段s.
[](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#模式-对象)
All 模式s require a 类型 to be set. If the specific 类型 is not 支持的 by 模式 validation, it is usually best to simply set the 类型 to  instead.
模式 validation does not 支持 the content 字段 in 参数. For more details, refer to the 注意s on validated and 支持的 字段s below. Instead, a 模式 is strictly 必需 on all 参数 对象s.
Refer to the 信息rmation below for more details on 模式 validation's current 支持 for various OpenAPI specification (OAS) 对象s and 字段s.
[](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#server-对象)
模式 validation does not 支持 relative URLs.
[](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#server-变量-对象)
Server 变量s are not validated.
[](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#parameter-对象)
Only the 默认 值s are 支持的:  (path or 头部 参数) and  (查询 or Cookie 参数).
Only the 默认 值s are 支持的:  (for form) and  (for simple).
The content 字段 is not 支持的 in 参数. Use the 模式 字段 instead.
Cloudflare currently does not validate 对象 类型 参数.
[](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#参考-对象)
External or relative 参考s are not 支持的.
请求 正文 对象
Media 类型 对象
模式 validation is able to validate  documents. If a given 模式 allows other content 类型s, 模式 validation will accept those 请求s without validation.
Parameter 对象
模式 对象
模式s are currently not 支持的 in parameter 模式s.
Validated formats:
This 字段 is currently not validated by 模式 validation.
API Shield has the ability to identify 正文 specifications contained in uploaded 模式s and validate that the data of incoming API 请求s adheres to them.
模式 validation currently 支持s validating 请求s with content-类型 .
Within the OpenAPI specification, 请求 正文 模式s are associated to media-ranges (such as ,  or ).
When Cloudflare validates incoming 请求s, Cloudflare checks that the 请求's  matches the OpenAPI-specified media-range.
For 示例, when the OpenAPI file specifies  as part of the 请求 正文 content map, Cloudflare will accept 请求s with the content-类型s  and . However, only  bodies will be validated with the supplied 模式.

Cloudflare generally recommends keeping the media-ranges as tight as possible. We suggest setting them to an individual media-类型. If you need to 支持 mul提示le content-类型s on an API 端点, you can utilize 通配符 media-ranges.

Care should also be taken if the 源 is configured to perform MIME sniffing. For 示例, when a 请求 carrying a JSON 正文 is deliberately carrying an  content-类型 and Cloudflare was configured to allow  media-ranges, the 请求 would be passed along to the 源 without validating the JSON 正文 contents. However, an 源 that ignores the content-类型 and either trial deserializes or sniffs the MIME 类型 may deserialize the JSON 正文 with a wrong assumption of having passed 模式 正文 validation.

As such, if you need to 支持  and  on the same 端点, you can use . Cloudflare will validate the provided 模式 for 请求 bodies where the content-类型 is set to . 请求s with content-类型  (and others matching ) will be let through. It is still strongly advised to disable content-类型 sniffing on your 源.
Cloudflare allows specifying the following media-ranges in the OpenAPI 请求 正文 content map:

Media-ranges can also be configured to enforce a  parameter. For this, Cloudflare only accepts the  parameter with a static 值 of  as part of the media-range specification and when configured, we will similarly require the 请求's content-类型 to carry this charset.
This section addresses common issues you may encounter when using 模式 validation.

A  constraint 错误 means an API 请求 已失败 模式 validation because its 正文 did not match exactly one of the options defined in a [](https://swagger.io/docs/specification/v3_0/data-models/oneof-anyof-allof-not/) 列表 within your uploaded 模式.
The 请求 was invalid for one of two reasons:
**Matches Zero**: The payload did not correctly match any of the available sub模式s. This is common when a discriminator 字段 is set, but the payload is missing other 必需 字段s for that 类型.

**Matches Mul提示le**: The payload was ambiguous and matched more than one sub模式. This happens with generic 模式s (for 示例, if a payload includes both an  and a  字段, it might match both an  and a  模式 definition, violating the "exactly one" 规则).

To fix this, check the failing 请求 正文 against the API 模式 definition. It will either be missing 必需 字段s for the intended 类型 or include properties from mul提示le different, conflicting 类型s that make it ambiguous.
模式 validation is available for all customers. Refer to Plans for more 信息rmation based on your plan 类型.
模式 learning is only available for customers 订阅d to API Shield.

/api-shield/security/api-discovery/.title: API Discovery
paragraphs: GlossaryTool提示,
Render,
Tabs,
TabItem,
步骤s,
DashButton,
} from "~/components";
Most development teams struggle to keep track of their APIs. Cloudflare API Discovery helps you map out and understand your attack surface area.
Cloudflare produces a simple, trustworthy map of  API 端点  through a process of path normalization.
For 示例, you might have thousands of APIs, but a lot of the calls look similar, such as:
Both paths serve a similar purpose — allowing users to 登录 to their 账户s — but they are not identical. To simplify your 端点, these 示例 might both map to .
API Discovery runs this process across all your traffic, generating a simple map of 端点 that might look like:
Similarly, if you have mul提示le 子域名s that share the same set of 端点, Cloudflare will consolidate 子域名s:
We will consolidate to .
For more technical details, see our blog post.
API Shield first cata日志 your discovered API 端点 in an email inbox-style view. From API Discovery, you can save 端点 to 端点 Management or ignore 端点 to remove them from view.

You should save all discovered API 端点 to 端点 Management while ignoring any potential false positives in the API Discovery results by selecting Save or Ignore on each line. Alternatively, you can bulk-select 端点 to save or ignore. You can 快速开始 with saving 端点 by saving all 端点 with a 变量. 搜索 for  in the 搜索 box and add all the resulting 端点. You can examine 端点 without path 变量s for accuracy later on.
By adding 端点 to 端点 Management, you will unlock further 安全, visibility, and management 特性 of the 平台. 端点 Management 监控s the health of your API 端点 by saving, updating, and 监控 性能 指标.
To 恢复 any errantly ignored 端点, you can 过滤器 by Ignored and select 恢复.

API Discovery is an ongoing process. Check back regularly for 新功能 API Discovery results. A badge with the 数字 of 端点 needing review will show in the API Shield 控制面板. You may see the quantities in the Needs Review and Ignored 指标 change over time. As your actual API or traffic patterns to your APIs change, API Discovery results that are not saved can disappear.
:::注意
Cloudflare will use your 反馈 on the ignored 端点 to better train the API Discovery Machine Learning model in a future release.
Your API 端点 are discovered with both the 会话 Identifier-based Discovery and the Machine Learning-based Discovery.
To access Machine Learning-based Discovery:
{/ prettier-ignore-start /}
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Web Assets** page.
Go to the **Discovery** tab.
过滤器 the source results by  or  to view results from each Discovery method.
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **API Shield** > **Discovery**.
{/ prettier-ignore-end /}

If all of your 区域’s API traffic contains the  会话 identifier  that you have configured, both sources may deliver the same results due to similarities between their underlying methodology. We expect Machine Learning-based Discovery to excel in discovering API traffic regardless of whether your API uses a 会话 identifier.
You can direct any 反馈 about your API Discovery results to your 账户 team.
To ensure your API 端点 are 成功fully discovered and mapped by Cloudflare, traffic to the 端点 must meet specific operational criteria.

If an 端点 does not appear in the Discovery inbox, it is typically because the system has not 观察d enough valid 请求s over a continuous period. API Discovery only processes 请求s that satisfy all of the following 要求:
The 请求 must return a  响应 code from the Cloudflare edge.
The 请求 must not come directly from Cloudflare Workers.
The 端点 must receive at least 500 请求s within a 10-day period.

端点 discovered using 会话 identifiers will be labeled as such in the Cloudflare 控制面板. If the 端点 are not discovered through 会话 identifiers, they will be discovered using our machine learning-based API Discovery.
API Discovery is only available for Enterprise customers. If you are an Enterprise customer interested in this product, contact your 账户 team.

/api-shield/security/mtls/.title: Mutual TLS (mTLS)
paragraphs: 支持 includes gRPC-based APIs, which use binary formats such as 协议 buffers rather than JSON.
To 设置 mTLS for one or more hosts using the 控制面板, refer to Configure mTLS.
All Cloudflare plans can 设置 mTLS with a Cloudflare-managed 证书 authority (CA). Enterprise customers can upload up to five non-Cloudflare CAs. For higher 限制, contact your 账户 team.
When using Yubi键s, the browser may prompt for unlocking the 键 due to a problem in Yubi键's PKCS#11 library.

/api-shield/security/volumetric-abuse-de.title: Volumetric Abuse Detection
paragraphs: Cloudflare Volumetric Abuse Detection helps you 设置 a system of adaptive 速率限制.
Cloudflare looks for 端点 abuse based on user traffic to individual 端点.
For 示例, your API might see different levels of traffic to a  端点 than a  端点. Additionally, your  端点 might see higher than average traffic after a 成功ful marketing campaign.

These two scenarios speak to the limitations of traditional 速率限制. Not only does traffic vary between 端点, but it also can vary over time for the same 端点. Volumetric Abuse Detection solves these problems with unsupervised learning to develop separate baselines for each API and better adjust to changes in user behavior.

Volumetric Abuse Detection rate 限制 are generated on a per-会话 basis. Unlike traditional rate 限制, which are based on IP addresses, Volumetric Abuse Detection rate 限制 are not as susceptible to false positives when traffic to your API increases.

Volumetric Abuse Detection rate 限制 are a way to pr事件 blatant volumetric abuse while minimizing false positives. If you are trying to pr事件 abusive bot traffic alto获取her, refer to Cloudflare’s Bot solutions.
Volumetric Abuse Detection analyzes your API's individual 会话 traffic statistics to recommend per-端点, per-会话 rate 限制.
To access your 端点:
Old 控制面板: 安全 > API Shield > 端点 Management
新功能 控制面板: 安全 > Web Assets > 端点
Recommendations will continue to 更新 if your traffic pattern changes.

Volumetric Abuse Detection generates rate limit thresholds only after collecting sufficient, statistically safe traffic data for an 端点. If recommendations are missing for a discovered 端点, the traffic likely 已失败 to meet the necessary criteria.
Thresholds are suggested only for 端点 that satisfy all of the following 要求 within the last seven days (or since initial discovery):
The 端点 must receive sufficient valid traffic (traffic that meets the API Discovery criteria). Intermittent or erratic traffic may pr事件 suggestions.
The 端点 must be accessed by at least 50 distinct 会话s in any 24-hour period during the last seven days.
会话 identifiers , such as an 授权 令牌 available as a 请求 头部 or Cookie, must be configured to allow Cloudflare to accurately detect individual 会话s and perform the 必需 per-会话 rate analysis.
After adding a 会话 identifier, allow 24 hours for rate limit recommendations to appear on 端点 in the Cloudflare 控制面板.

Once rate limit recommendations appear in 端点, select the 端点 row to view more detail about the recommendation. You will see the overall recommended rate limit 值, as well as p99, p90, and p50 rate limit 值s.

We calculate the recommended rate limit 值 throughout the day, and the 新功能 calculation may equal the existing recommendation due to similar traffic profiles existing on your API. When we recalculate, we look at 请求s that happened in the last 24 hours.

Cloudflare recommends choosing the overall rate limit recommendation, as our analysis includes the variance of the 请求 rate distribution across your API 会话s. Choosing a single p-值 may cause false positives due to a high 数字 of outliers.
:::注意[p-值s]
p-值s describe what percentile of your traffic fits below the 值. For 示例, if your p90 值 is , then 90% of your 会话s had maximum 请求 rates less than 83 请求s per 10 minutes.

In 端点, you can review our confidence in the recommendation and how many unique 会话s we have seen over the last seven (7) days. In general, 端点 with fewer unique 会话s and high variability of user behavior will have lower confidence scores.

Implementing low confidence rate 限制 can still be helpful to pr事件 API abuse. If you are hesitant due to the recommendation’s confidence, we suggest starting your rate limit 规则 in  mode and observing violations of the 规则 for false positives.
Refer to the 规则 文档 for more 信息rmation on how to 创建 an Advanced 速率限制 规则.
Rate limit recommendations are available via the API if you would like to dynamically 更新 rate 限制 over time.
API Shield will always calculate recommendations when 会话 identifiers are configured. To enable 会话-based rate 限制, 订阅 to Advanced 速率限制.
Volumetric Abuse Detection is only available for Enterprise customers. If you are an Enterprise customer interested in this product, contact your 账户 team.

/api-shield/security/sequence-analytics/.title: Sequence 分析
paragraphs: Sequence 分析 tracks the order of API 端点 请求s over time, allowing you to discover how users interact with your API. Sequence 分析 groups and highlights 重要 user journeys (sequences) across your API. You can enforce preferred sequences using Sequence mitigation.
A sequence is a time-ordered 列表 of HTTP API 请求s made by a specific visitor as they browse a website, use a mobile app, or interact with a B2B partner via API.
For 示例, a portion of a sequence made during a bank funds transfer could look like:

API Shield uses your configured  会话 identifier  and your saved 端点 to 构建 a set of ordered API operations (HTTP host, method, and path) 请求ed per 会话. We may surface sequences in various lengths de待处理 how API Shield scores the sequences.

API Shield scores sequences by a metric called precedence score. Sequence 分析 displays sequences by the highest precedence score. High-scoring sequences contain API 请求s which are likely to occur to获取her in order.

Using the 示例 above, a high score means that the last operation in the sequence  is highly likely to be preceded by the other operations in sequence  followed by . The scores are probabilities, which API Shield estimates using data from the last 24 hours.

To pro活跃ly secure your API, you should inspect your highest-scoring sequences. For each high-scoring sequence, you should confirm with your development team if the final operation in the sequence must legitimately always be preceded by the other operations in the sequence.
Using the above 示例, if  must legitimately always be preceded by  and , you should 创建 an Allow 规则 in sequence mitigation on the final operation of the sequence.
You should also consider applying other API Shield protections to these 端点 (速率限制 suggestions, 模式 validation, JWT validation, and mTLS).
For more 信息rmation, refer to our blog post.
True API usage shows many 成功ively repeated operations. To facilitate exploration, Sequence 分析 collapses 成功ively repeated operations into one.

Sequence 分析 is available for all API Shield customers. Pro, Business, and Enterprise customers who have not purchased API Shield can 快速开始 by enabling the API Shield trial in the Cloudflare 控制面板 or contacting your 账户 manager.
Sequence 分析 currently requires a 会话 identifier and saved 端点 in order to 构建 and track sequences made by an API 消费者. Ensure that you have 设置 your 会话 identifier(s) and saved your 端点.
Sequences are currently limited to nine operations in length.

/argo-smart-routing/.title: Argo Smart 路由
paragraphs: Speed up your global traffic with a single click
了解更多 about the 优势 of Argo Smart 路由.
Argo Smart 路由 includes comprehensive 分析 to compare 性能 improvements with and without Argo 已启用.
Increase cache hit ratios to reduce 请求s to your 源服务器.
Improve 安全 and 性能 within mainland China.
Reduce 延迟 and protect from DDoS attacks using the Cloudflare network.
Compare available Cloudflare plans
Explore 定价 options for Argo in the 控制面板

/argo-smart-routing/get-started/.title: 快速开始
/argo-smart-routing/get-started/.description: Learn how to enable Argo Smart 路由 in the Cloudflare 控制面板.
paragraphs: Argo Smart 路由 is a one-click solution to speed up your global traffic.
To enable Argo Smart 路由 in the 控制面板:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Argo Smart 路由** page.
For **Argo Smart 路由**, switch the toggle to **On**.
Provide your billing 信息rmation.
If you do not have a billing profile, enter your billing 信息rmation.
If you have a billing profile, confirm your billing 信息rmation.
To enable or disable Argo Smart 路由 with the API, send a [](/api/资源/argo/sub资源/smart_路由/方法/edit/) 请求 with the  parameter set to your desired setting ( or ).
You will need to al读取y have a billing profile on your 账户 to enable Argo Smart 路由.
If Cloudflare mitigates attacks on your site - whether through DDoS protection, the WAF, or other mechanisms - that traffic will not be included in any charges for Argo Smart 路由.

Cache works by storing a copy of website content at Cloudflare's 数据中心. Tiered Cache divides these 数据中心 into a hierarchy based on location. This behavior allows Cloudflare to deliver content from 数据中心 closest to your visitor.
Argo Smart 路由 and Tiered Cache work to获取her to provide the most efficient connection for visitors to your site. For more 信息rmation, go to Tiered Cache.

/argo-smart-routing/analytics/.title: 分析
paragraphs: Cloudflare provides 分析 to show the 性能 优势 of Argo Smart 路由.
You can access Argo 分析 for your 域名 in the Cloudflare 控制面板 at 分析 > 性能. For 信息rmation on all 分析 in the 控制面板, refer to 分析.
:::注意
Detailed 性能 data within 源 性能 (Argo) will only display if Argo has 路由d at least 500 源 请求s within the last 48 hours.
The 控制面板 displays two different views for 性能 data:
**源 响应 Time**: A histogram shows 响应 time from your 源 to the Cloudflare network. The blue bars show TTFB without Argo, while the orange bars show TTFB where Argo found a Smart 路由.
**Geography**: A map shows the improvement in 响应 time at each Cloudflare 数据中心.
A negative 值 indicates that 请求s from that location would not have benefited from Argo Smart 路由, so instead would have been 路由d directly.

/automatic-platform-optimization/get-sta.title: 快速开始
paragraphs: Before you configure APO for your WordPress site, complete the following 任务s.

/automatic-platform-optimization/.title: Automatic 平台 优化
paragraphs: Take your WordPress site’s 性能 to the next level with Automatic 平台 优化s (APO). APO allows Cloudflare to serve your entire WordPress site from its 边缘网络 ensuring consistent, fast 性能 for visitors no matter where they are.

Automatic 平台 优化 is the result of using the power of Cloudflare Workers to intelligently cache dynamic content. By 缓存 dynamic content, Cloudflare can serve the entire website from our 边缘网络 to make a site's time to first byte (TTFB) both fast and consistent.
To 阅读更多 about the 优势 of using APO with your site, see The 优势 of Automatic 平台 优化 blog. You must use the Cloudflare for WordPress plugin to begin using APO.

/billing/cancel-subscription/.title: Cancel Cloudflare subscriptions
headings: 步骤 1: Disable the Cloudflare subscription
步骤 2: Cancel the subscription in your billing profile
paragraphs: Cancellations are not processed until the end of the billing period, so you can continue using the add-on or subscription until the 新功能 billing period begins. To avoid unwanted charges, start the cancellation process before the end of your billing period.

If you are making a subscription downgrade including a change from a yearly plan to a monthly plan or a change from a paid plan to a 免费计划, be aware that downgrades and cancellations do not take immediate effect and that you will continue to be billed and have access to your service for the remainder of your contracted service period. You should not contact Cloudflare to ask for exceptions to this policy or refunds for early downgrades and cancellations.
In addition, you may not always see a cancel button present for each subscription and often it is a switch from a paid plan to a 免费计划 that will act as the cancellation of your paid subscription.
:::注意

All billing period dates on invoices and the 控制面板 are in UTC/GMT time区域, not your local time区域. We recommend any downgrades or changes to subscriptions be made at least 24 hours before stated billing date to avoid any time区域 confusion.

Furthermore, please 注意 that changing the DNS on your 域名 name does not cancel your 账户, or your 活跃 subscription plans. The system waits for a manual cancellation of the subscriptions plans, since there are times that the owner of a 域名 name's DNS is 必需 to take 操作 to change it back because they did not intend to remove their 账户.
You will receive a notification regarding the DNS change; however, that does not indicate the cancellation of your subscription plans.
To disable a subscription:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Billing** page.
Select **Subscriptions**.
Select the 特性 you want to disable under **活跃 Subscriptions** (Free or Pro customers) or **Plan 扩展** (Enterprise customers).
Follow the instructions to disable the 特性. Each 特性 has a different process which could include toggling a switch, clicking a button, or choosing *Disable* from a drop-down 列表.
Find the subscription you want to disable and select **Cancel**.
You will be asked to share 反馈 with us. Choose all that apply.
Select **Confirm** to finish.
To alter your plan, select Change under 活跃 Subscriptions and choose a 新功能 plan 类型. If you would like to cancel your paid plan, select Free.

FEES ARE NONREFUNDABLE. YOU WILL BE BILLED IN FULL FOR THE SUBSCRIPTION TERM IN WHICH YOU CANCEL AND NO REFUNDS WILL BE PROVIDED FOR THE UNUSED PORTION OF SUCH SUBSCRIPTION TERM. Following any cancellation, however, you will continue to have access to the Paid Services through the end of your current Subscription Term (except with respect to Services subject to usage-based billing). We may, in our sole discretion, provide a refund, discount, or credit ("Credits") to you in a specific instance, however the provision of Credits in a specific instance does not entitle you to Credits in the future for similar instances or obligate us to provide additional Credits.

/billing/update-billing-info/.title: 更新 billing 信息rmation
headings: Add or change a Tax ID, VAT, or GST 数字
Remove a Tax ID, VAT, or GST 数字
paragraphs: To avoid potential disruptions in your Cloudflare services, make sure your billing 信息rmation is current and accurate.
If Cloudflare is unable to process your payment, review 故障排除 已失败 payments.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Billing** page.
Select **Payment**.
Select **Manage** next to your current payment method. The **Manage payment 方法** dialog opens.
Select **Edit** next to the payment method you would like to edit.
Enter the 必需 信息rmation and select **Save Payment 信息rmation**.
Before removing your payment method from file, you must cancel all Cloudflare paid services.
:::注意
If you currently 订阅 to any add-on services, Cloudflare must always have a payment method on file. If you need to remove a payment method, you must enter a 新功能 one to replace it.
You cannot 删除 a payment method if a payment fails or if there is an outstanding balance. Until Cloudflare processes payment, you can only add or edit your payment method.
From **Payment 方法**, select **Manage**.
Next to your current payment method, select **删除**.
Select **Confirm** to finish.

The billing address is associated with your payment method and is used to calculate your sales tax. If you need to 更新 your billing address, you must also enter your payment method. The process for updating your billing address depends on the payment method.
If paying by credit card:
From **Billing Address**, select **Edit** and enter your 信息rmation.
Review the suggested address in the pop-up window. If the 信息rmation is correct, select **Confirm**.
If paying by PayPal, refer to PayPal's billing address 文档.
Your billing email address is particularly 重要 if you have opted into email invoices.
Go to **Invoices and documents**.
From **Billing email p参考**, select **Change email address**.
Enter and confirm your 新功能 email address, then select **Save**.
If you added a payment method but did not include a Tax ID, VAT or GST 数字, you can add or change the Tax ID, VAT or GST 数字 associated with the payment method afterwards.
:::注意
You cannot apply a VAT or GST 数字 to past invoices. Adding a VAT or GST 数字 will only apply to future invoices issued in the 账户.
Go to **Payment**.
From **Billing Address**, select **Edit**.
In the **VAT/GST** 字段, enter your VAT or GST 数字.
Select **Confirm**.
You cannot remove a VAT or GST 数字 from past invoices. Removing a VAT or GST 数字 will only apply to future invoices issued in the 账户.
In the **VAT/GST** 字段, 删除 the VAT or GST 数字.

/billing/billing-policy/.title: Billing policy
headings: Upgrade or downgrade Cloudflare 付费计划
Billing and payment for 企业计划s
账户 Payment Method Pre授权
paragraphs: The terms subscription and add-on service are used interchangeably in this 支持 指南.
Cloudflare plans and add-on services are billed every 30 days for each 域名 in your 账户.
Cloudflare plans are billed monthly and annually for each 域名 on the 账户. Monthly plans are billed every 30 days for each 域名 in your 账户, and annual plans are billed yearly for each 域名 in your 账户.
Add-on services are only billed monthly and cannot be billed annually.

Cloudflare also collects sales tax as governed by local laws. Sales taxes are computed based on the nine (9) digit postal code of either the shipping or billing address on file for your Cloudflare 账户 where applicable.
:::注意
If you are a US-based customer, you can file for sales tax exemption.
Cloudflare issues a separate invoice for plans and subscriptions (or add-on services) for every 域名 added to a Cloudflare 账户.
Cloudflare issues a monthly or annual invoice based on the plans you purchase. You will receive a $0 invoice even if your 域名 is on a 免费计划.
子域名s do not count as billable 域名s.

For 示例, if 测试1.com and 测试2.com are added to the same Cloudflare 账户 and upgraded to the 专业计划, you will receive an invoice with two charges. 子域名s such as blog.测试1.com or blog.测试2.com will not be included as billable 域名s.

The date you initiate a paid plan or add-on service will be both the start of your billing period and your invoice date. For 示例, if you upgrade your plan on January 10, all future plan charges will be billed on the 10th of every month. Both dates are initialized using the UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) time 区域, and not your local time 区域.
If your 账户 is dunned (downgraded and banned for non payment of dues), the 新功能 start date changes to the day of the upgrade and applies to monthly, yearly, and add-on plans.
When ordering a paid plan, subscription, or add-on service, you must agree to the following:

_By clicking "Enable" you agree that you are purchasing a continuous month-to-month subscription which will automatically re新功能, and that the price of your selected subscription plan level and/or add on(s) will be billed to your designated payment method monthly as a recurring charge, unless you cancel your subscription(s), through your 账户 控制面板,_ _before_ _the beginning of your next monthly billing period._
_You will be billed for the full monthly period in which you cancel and no refunds will be given. By purchasing a subscription, you agree to a minimum one month purchase obligation._
For more 信息rmation on re新功能al terms and cancellation, refer to the Terms of Use.
If your 域名 is on a paid plan (for 示例, Pro) and you upgrade to a higher-priced plan (for 示例, Business),
Your invoice will reflect the prorated cost of the higher-tiered plan, until the end of your billing cycle.
Cloudflare credits the prorated cost of the lower-priced plan, until the end of the billing cycle.
At the beginning of the next billing cycle, your invoice will reflect the full cost of the higher-priced plan.
Your bill cycle start and end dates are calculated using the UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) time 区域, and not your local time 区域.
For 示例, if your billing date is January 1, but you upgrade from Pro to Business, on January 15,
Your invoice will reflect the prorated 商业计划 rate for the period of use January 15 - January 30.
Cloudflare credits the prorated 专业计划 cost from January 1 - January 15.
Your invoice for the billing period of January 1 - January 30 will appear in the Cloudflare 控制面板 on January 31.
账户 credits are automatically added to your 账户 and can only be used on recurring monthly charges for Cloudflare plans or add-on services. Your monthly invoice 列表s any credits.

All credits are added on the backend of your 账户 and are not visible from the invoice. The invoice will, however, reflect any uses of applied 账户 credits. 账户 credits can be used for any future Cloudflare 事务s except Registrar 事务s.
If your 域名 is on a paid plan (for 示例, Business) and you downgrade to a lower-priced plan (for 示例, Pro),
Your plan 类型 and higher-tiered Cloudflare plan 特性 are downgraded at the end of the current billing service period.
You are billed at the lower-tiered plan and 特性 rate for the next billing service period.
For 示例, if your billing date is February 1, but you downgrade to Pro from the 商业计划 on February 15,
You can access 商业计划 特性 and services until March 1.
Your March plan charges will reflect the downgraded cost.
Enterprise customers work with the Cloudflare 账户 team to customize a plan and service contract to best suit their needs. The Cloudflare 账户ing team receives and processes 企业计划 charges.
Enterprise 账户 owners receive invoices directly from the Cloudflare 账户ing team.
Cloudflare only accepts Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, PayPal, Link, and UnionPay. No other payment 方法 (for 示例, Maestro) are possible at this time.
Enterprise customers can use ACH payments for Cloudflare plans and subscriptions.
Please ensure that you're using a valid payment method before changing your plan 类型 or enabling subscriptions.
Gift cards and pre-payment cards may not be accepted for payment as they are not associated with a billing address.

For services subject to usage-based billing, Cloudflare may preauthorize your credit card at any point in a billing period to confirm the payment method on file can cover accrued fees. This is a temporary hold and you will not be charged until the end of your billing period. If your payment method is validated, service will continue normally.

If your payment method fails, we may suspend your access to the usage-based billing services for which we conducted the pre授权. In the case of R2, you will not be able to access your R2 存储桶s and 请求s will return 错误, but your data will remain secure. If you do not 更新 your payment method within 30 days, the data 相关 to any usage-based billing service(s) may be 删除d.

To regain access, you must settle any outstanding balances and pass pre授权 with a valid payment method. To 更新 your primary payment method, navigate to the Manage 账户 > Billing > Payment section of your 账户. Upon validation of your 已更新 payment details, we will promptly reactivate your subscription(s), which will 恢复 access to the relevant data and services.
For assistance, visit our 支持 Portal and submit a Billing 请求 (category: “Payment issue”) to our 支持 team. They will assist you in verifying your 已更新 payment 信息rmation.
The following occurences cannot be refunded:
Billing or re新功能al issues: Often involves charges for re新功能als, unexpected billing, or issues 相关 to subscription payments.
Accidental purchases of services and subscriptions: Includes instances where users bought the wrong service, made a mistake during the purchase process, or unintentionally upgraded their plan.
域名 issues: Incorrect 域名 registration, issues with 域名 transfers, or accidental 域名 purchases.
Service or plan issues: Issues with a service or plan itself, such as attempts to downgrade, cancel unused services, or problems with specific 特性.
支持 issues: Unresolved 支持 issues.
Cloudflare Self-Serve Subscription Agreement
Understanding Cloudflare Invoices
Understanding Cloudflare sales tax

/billing/resolve-cannot-remove-payment-m.title: Resolve "cannot remove payment method
headings: Check for 活跃 paid subscriptions
Check for an upcoming Registrar re新功能al
If none of the above apply
paragraphs: This article will help if you are receiving an 错误 that mentions one of the following 错误 when attempting to remove a payment method:
"You can't remove this payment method while it's linked to 活跃 subscriptions. Go to Billing to manage subscriptions."
"You can't remove a payment method while there are 事务s in progress. Make sure all 事务s are completed and all subscriptions are cancelled."
You still have 活跃 paid subscriptions
You have cancelled your paid subscriptions, but a usage-based charge is still 定时
You have an upcoming Registrar 域名 registration re新功能al within the next 24 hours
You can resolve this by checking your 账户 for each of the potential causes.
You can only remove your payment method once all your paid subscriptions and outstanding charges have been completed. To check your paid subscriptions are cancelled follow this:
登录to the Cloudflare 控制面板 and select your 账户.
Go to **Manage 账户 > Billing**.
Go to **Subscriptions**.
Check **Service status** for any which are marked “活跃”
Click the “Cancel” button
You must complete this for all 活跃 paid subscriptions before you will be able to remove your card.

If you have cancelled all of your paid products & subscriptions, any usage based products cancelled within the last 30 days may still generate charges. We require that your payment method remains on file until those potential usage charges have been processed. As such, if you recently cancelled any of the following products, you will need to wait 30 days before you can remove your payment method:
Images
流
Workers
Argo
Spectrum
Cache Reserve
Load Balancing
速率限制
Log Explorer
Zero Trust
Vectorize
分析
Once your next monthly invoice has been produced you should be able to remove your card.

For Registrar 域名s 定时 for auto-re新功能al, we will attempt to re新功能 approximately 30 days before your re新功能al date. In the 24 hours prior to that, we will automatically process a payment hold using your payment method. During this time you will be unable to remove your payment method.
To check if any of your 域名s are in the process of re新功能al:
Go to **域名 Registration > Manage 域名s**.
Under the **Expires** column, look for any 域名s expiring within the next 31 days which have Auto-re新功能 已启用.

If you have any 域名s with auto-re新功能 已启用 that are expiring in 31 days or less, you will need to wait for them to re新功能 before you can remove your payment method. To understand more about this process, refer to re新功能 域名s.
If you have confirmed all of the above do not apply in your case and you still receive an 错误 消息 when attempting to remove your payment method, please contact 支持.

/billing/.title: Billing
/billing/.description: Manage billing and subscriptions for your 账户.
headings: Cannot 更新 or remove payment 方法
paragraphs: Welcome to the Cloudflare Billing and Payment Help Center! Here you will find answers to some of the most common and impactful issues facing our customers today. Most current issues revolve around:
Payment failures
Outstanding balances
Subscription re新功能als
Cancellation 请求s
Updating payment 方法
Understanding billing cycles
An 概述 of these issues can be found below:
You may receive an 错误 when attempting to purchase a product, pay a balance or change a paid plan. Review the following 指南 for possible solutions:
Resolve a payment failure
Resolve the 区域 cannot be upgraded 错误
Resolve "You cannot modify this subscription"

If the problems above do not apply to you, there are intermittent issues with subscription upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations. This may result in billing charges being applied without the subscription level being 已更新. In these instances, you should contact 支持.
You should be able to add, 更新, and remove payment 方法 in your 账户. If you are seeing difficulties doing this, refer to the following 指南 for possible solutions:
更新 billing 信息rmation
Resolve "Cannot remove payment method"
If the solutions above do not apply to you, we have 观察d 报告 of being unable to 更新 or add payment 方法 within the 控制面板. In these instances, you should contact 支持.
Cancellations are not processed until the end of the billing period. To understand more, refer to Cancel Cloudflare subscriptions.
In accordance with the Cloudflare Billing Policy, be aware that some Non-Refundable occurrences cannot be refunded and you should not contact 支持 to 请求 refunds for these.

/billing/troubleshoot-failed-payments/.title: Resolve a payment failure
headings: Automatic 重试 and product removal
Check your details & contact your bank
Correctly authorise your 事务 with 3D Secure (3DS)
Contact your bank if purchasing / re新功能ing Registrar 域名s
Contact your bank to understand & fix the declined payment
Try a 新功能 payment method
paragraphs: This article will help if you are receiving 错误 about your payment failing when attempting to purchase something, change an existing subscription, pay an invoice or an outstanding balance. You may see one of the following 错误 消息s:
"The payment has 已失败. Please contact your bank or use a different payment method."
"Payment 错误: 授权 已失败 for [“示例.com”]"

Alternatively, you may receive an email from us about a subscription re新功能al payment failure with the subject: “[Cloudflare]: We couldn't process your re新功能al payment” which states “We were unable to process your re新功能al payment”.

If the 已失败 payment relates to a recurring charge for a Cloudflare plan, add-on, or subscription then after a five (5) day grace period, your 账户 is automatically downgraded to a 免费计划. Downgrading to a 免费计划 does not suspend your website, but you will lose any subscriptions or add-on services associated with the Pro, Business, or 企业计划.

To avoid this, follow the Causes & Solution sections below to resolve the failing payment and 重试. If you are unable to resolve the issue within the 5 day grace period, you’ll need to manually re-订阅 to these products. You may need to pay an outstanding balance relating to the five day grace period, if applicable.
Your card details are incorrect
There are not enough funds in your 账户
You did not pass 3D Secure (3DS) correctly
Your bank is 速率限制 payments from us
Your bank is declining the payment
Check your address is valid and matches the one registered with your bank for the payment method you are using
Check that the Card Verification 值 (CVC) you entered is correct for your card
If you are using PayPal, check for a verification email to your PayPal email address and follow the instructions to authorize
Double check your payment method’s balance to ensure that your 账户 has enough funds.

You may be using a bank that mandates the use of 3D Secure (3DS) for online card 事务s. For one off or first time subscription payments, you need to be prepared to pass 3DS 身份验证 when you attempt the payment in the Cloudflare 控制面板. Your bank will contact you to authorise the 事务 in real time. This contact typically comes via an SMS or push notification from your bank’s mobile application.
Please 注意 that for customers of Indian banks, 3DS is mandatory for all 事务s as per the mandate from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).

If you were purchasing or re新功能ing mul提示le 域名s via Cloudflare Registrar each 域名 will be charged as a separate 事务. This may be flagged as fraud by your credit card company. You will need to contact your bank in order to confirm and resolve this.
Cloudflare’s payment system does not know why a payment was declined, so you should contact your bank to confirm specifically why they declined the payment.

Once you have checked the items above, you should try your 事务 again by visiting the Cloudflare 控制面板. If the payment that 已失败 was a re新功能al payment, we will 重试 automatically 5 times in 5 days. However, we recommend you 重试 the payment manually in the 控制面板 to receive instant 反馈. To do this:
登录to the Cloudflare 控制面板 and select your 账户.
Go to **Manage 账户 > Billing**.
Go to **Invoices and documents**.
Click **Pay Now** next to your invoice OR from the Invoice PDF, click the payment link.
Follow the on screen instructions to try again.

If you cannot resolve this using your current payment method, you may wish to try an alternative payment method. We 支持 mul提示le different credit card 类型s and PayPal. To try this, refer add a 新功能 payment method.

/billing/resolve-you-cannot-modify-this-.title: Resolve "you cannot modify this subscription
headings: When will my subscription be cancelled?
Can I receive a refund for my cancelled subscription?
paragraphs: This article will help if you are receiving an 错误 that mentions "You cannot modify this subscription since it is currently 定时 to be cancelled" when attempting to cancel or modify a subscription. For 参考, the full 消息 you receive may be one of the following:
"This subscription is 定时 to be cancelled at the end of the billing period. To make changes or purchase more, please click 'Cancel Downgrade' on the Subscriptions page."
You are attempting to cancel a subscription which you have al读取y 请求ed cancellation for
You are attempting to upgrade a subscription for which a cancellation is al读取y 定时

If your intent is to cancel a subscription, this 请求 has al读取y been 定时 and your subscription will be cancelled at the end of the current billing period. See “When will my subscription be cancelled” below to understand the exact date this will take effect.
Once you have 请求ed cancellation, you will see the date your subscription will be cancelled on the subscriptions page underneath the text “Ending On”.
登录to the Cloudflare 控制面板 and select your 账户.
Go to **Manage 账户 > Billing**.
Go to **Subscriptions**.
Locate the **Product** you have cancelled
Under the **Service status** column, the status should say **ENDING ON** with the date cancellation will take effect
If you previously cancelled the subscription and have changed your decision, you need to cancel the downgrade.
No refund will be issued for a cancelled subscription - instead, your subscription will remain 活跃 until the end of the current billing period.

If you do not wish to pay for the coming billing period, then you should cancel your subscription no later than on the last day of the billing period. You can discover this date by visiting the Subscriptions page and checking the re新功能 date, for 示例 “RE新功能S ON Aug 29, 2025”.
Under the **Service status** column, the status should say **RE新功能S ON** with the date of the next re新功能al
If the cancellation hasn’t taken effect yet, you can click Cancel Downgrade next to the appropriate subscription in the Billing > Subscriptions page.
Under the **操作** column, click **Cancel Downgrade**

/billing/resolve-zone-cannot-be-ugpraded.title: Resolve the 区域 cannot be upgraded 错误
paragraphs: When trying to upgrade a 域名 or purchase a subscription, you may see an 错误 that contains one of the following phrases:
"this 区域 cannot be upgraded"
"there is a problem with your billing profile"
Your 账户 may have an outstanding unpaid balance
Another 账户 previously associated with the 域名 / 区域 your purchase relates to has an outstanding unpaid balance
This 消息 appears when the 账户 or 域名 involved has an outstanding unpaid balance. In the case of a 域名, this may also be 触发器ed by a previous 账户 that owned the 域名. To resolve this you will need to:
Check each Cloudflare 账户 you have access to for an outstanding balance. Refer to Email address and password if you have forgotten these details.
Refer to Pay an outstanding balance to pay this balance
Wait 24 hours after paying this balance
Attempt to upgrade again
As a 参考, the full 错误 消息s you may see are:
"Due to a Billing 相关 issue, the 区域 cannot be upgraded at this time. Please visit the Billing section to ensure there is no outstanding balance."
"Refer to https://cfl.re/3VUQyyL for assistance. For 安全 reasons, there is a problem with your billing profile."

/bots/get-started/.title: 快速开始 with Cloudflare bot solutions
paragraphs: Refer to the following pages to 快速开始 with Cloudflare's bot solutions based on your plan 类型:

/bots/bot-analytics/.title: Bot 分析
paragraphs: Business and Enterprise customers without Bot Management can use Bot 分析 to dynamically examine bot traffic. These 控制面板s offer less 函数ality than Bot Management for Enterprise but still help you understand bot traffic on your 域名.
You can access Bot 分析 by going to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and selecting your 账户 and 域名.
Old 控制面板: 安全 > Bots.
新功能 控制面板: 安全 > 分析 > Bot analysis.
For a full tour of Bot 分析, see our blog post. At a high level, the tool includes:
**请求s by traffic 类型**: View your total 域名 traffic segmented vertically by traffic 类型. Keep an eye on _automated_ and _likely automated_ traffic.
**请求s by detection source**: Identify the most common detection engines used to score your traffic. Hover over a tool提示 to 了解更多 about each engine.
**Top 请求s by attribute**: View more detailed 信息rmation on specific IP addresses and other characteristics.
Bot 分析 shows up to 72 hours of data at a time and can display data up to 30 days old. Bot 分析 displays data in real time in most cases.
Business and Enterprise customers without Bot Management can use Bot 分析 to:
Understand  bot  traffic
Study recent attacks to find trends and detailed 信息rmation
了解更多 about Cloudflare’s detection engines with real data
For more details and granular control over bot traffic, consider upgrading to Bot Management for Enterprise.
Enterprise customers with Bot Management can use Bot 分析 to dynamically examine bot traffic.
Bot Management customers can use Bot 分析 to:
Understand traffic during your onboarding phase.
Tune WAF custom 规则 to be effective but not overly aggressive.
Study recent attacks to find trends and detailed 信息rmation.
了解更多 about Cloudflare’s detection engines with real data.
Data from Bot 分析 is also available via the GraphQL API. You can access  bot scores , bot sources,  bot tags , and bot _decisions_ (_automated_, _likely automated_, etc.), and more.
读取 the GraphQL 分析 API 文档 for more 信息rmation about GraphQL and basic 查询ing.

/bots/reference/.title: 参考
paragraphs: Refer to the following pages for more 信息rmation on Cloudflare's bot solutions:

/bots/.title: Cloudflare bot solutions
headings: Which bot solution do I need?
paragraphs: Identify and mitigate automated traffic to protect your 域名 from bad bots.
While Cloudflare offers several products that relate to bot traffic, this section reviews our bot-specific products, Bot Fight Mode, Super Bot Fight Mode, and Bot Management for Enterprise.

If you have a smaller 域名 and have identified a bot problem, we recommend Bot Fight Mode or Super Bot Fight Mode, which are included with your plan subscription. You can enable either from your 控制面板, but these solutions offer limited 配置 options.

If you have a large 域名 with a lot of traffic, we recommend Bot Management for Enterprise, especially for customers in ecommerce, banking, and 安全. To enable Bot Management for Enterprise and write 规则 to customize your bot protection, contact your 账户 team.
To see the differences in 特性 and 函数ality, visit Plans.
Detect and mitigate bot traffic on your 域名.
Identify traffic matching patterns of known bots, challenge or block bots, protect static 资源, and view 分析 to help you understand bot traffic using Super Bot Fight Mode.
Use Bot 分析 to dynamically examine bot traffic.
Access several 新功能 变量s within the 防火墙 表达式 构建er.
Identify and address API vulnerabilities using API Shield.
Detect and mitigate 分布式拒绝服务 (DDoS) attacks using Cloudflare's Autonomous Edge.
Use Cloudflare's smart CAPTCHA alternative to run less intrusive challenges.
获取 automatic protection from vulnerabilities and the flexibility to 创建 custom 规则.
Compare available Cloudflare plans

/bots/reference/bot-management-variables.title: Bot Management 变量s
paragraphs: Bot Management provides access to several 新功能 变量s within the 表达式 构建er of 规则et Engine-based products such as WAF custom 规则.
**Bot Score** (): An integer between 1-99 that indicates Cloudflare's level of certainty that a 请求 comes from a bot.
**Verified Bot** (): A 布尔值 值 that indicates whether a 请求 源ates from a Cloudflare allowed bot.

Cloudflare maintains a large allow列表 of good, automated bots (such as Google 搜索 Engine and Pingdom) that perform beneficial 任务s. Cloudflare identifies and verifies these bots primarily through reverse DNS validation, ensuring the source IP matches the 请求ing service.

We also use additional validation 方法, including checking ASN blocks and public 列表s. If these 方法 are unavailable, Cloudflare utilizes internal data and machine learning to identify and verify legitimate IP addresses from good bots. Most customers choose to allow this traffic.
**Serves Static Resource** (): An identifier that matches file 扩展 for many 类型s of static 资源. Use this 变量 if you send emails that retrieve static images.
**ja3Hash** () and **ja4** (): A **JA3/JA4 fingerprint** helps you profile specific SSL/TLS clients across different destination IPs, Ports, and X509 证书s.
**Bot Detection IDs** (): 列表 of IDs that correlate to the Bot Management heuristic detections made on a 请求 (you can have mul提示le heuristic detections on the same 请求).
**Verified Bot Categories** (): A 字符串 that allows you to segment your verified bot traffic by its 类型 and purpose.
These 变量s are also available as part of the 请求.cf 对象 via Cloudflare Workers:
The Bot Management Corporate 代理 字段 contains identified cloud-based corporate proxies and secure web 网关s that are Enterprise-only, and provide outbound 安全 services to their clients.
You can access the Corporate 代理 字段 in WAF custom 规则, 速率限制 规则, or Workers to provide different 安全 规则 for traffic from these sources. You can also exempt them from 规则 using Bot Management scores.
Once you enable Bot Management, Cloudflare also surfaces bot 信息rmation in its HTTP 请求s log 字段s:
BotDetectionIDs
BotScore
BotScoreSrc
BotTags

/bots/get-started/bot-fight-mode/.title: Bot Fight Mode
headings: Inter操作 with other app 安全 特性
paragraphs: Tabs,

Bot Fight Mode is a simple, free product that helps detect and mitigate bot traffic on your 域名. When 已启用, the product:
Identifies traffic matching patterns of known bots
Issues computationally expensive challenges in 响应 to these bots
Notifies 带宽 Alliance partners (if applicable) to disable bots
To start using Bot Fight Mode:
If you find that Bot Fight Mode is causing problems with your application traffic, you may want to disable it.
To disable Bot Fight Mode:
{/ prettier-ignore-start /}
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **安全 设置** page.
过滤器 by **Bot traffic**.
Go to **Bot Fight Mode**.
Turn **Bot Fight Mode** off.
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **安全** > **Bots**.
For **Bot Fight Mode**, select **Off**.
{/ prettier-ignore-end /}
Refer to Block AI bots.
:::注意
You can view blocked AI bot traffic via 安全 分析.

You can see bot-相关 操作s by going to 安全 > 事件. Any 请求s challenged by this product will be labeled Bot Fight Mode in the Service 字段. This allows you to 观察, analyze, and follow trends in your bot traffic over time.

You cannot bypass or skip Bot Fight Mode using the _Skip_ 操作 in WAF custom 规则 or using Page 规则. _Skip_, _Bypass_, and _Allow_ 操作s apply to 规则 or 规则ets 运行中 on the 规则et Engine. While Super Bot Fight Mode 规则 are implemented in the 规则et Engine, Bot Fight Mode checks are not. This is why you can skip Super Bot Fight Mode, but not Bot Fight Mode. If you need to skip Bot Fight Mode, consider using Super Bot Fight Mode.
Bot Fight Mode can still 触发器 if you have IP Access 规则, but it cannot 触发器 if an IP Access 规则 matches the 请求. For 示例, the IP Access 规则 matches the connecting IP.

/bots/get-started/super-bot-fight-mode/.title: Super Bot Fight Mode
headings: Inter操作 with other app 安全 特性
Configure exceptions to Super Bot Fight Mode
paragraphs: Super Bot Fight Mode is included in your Pro, Business, or Enterprise subscription. When 已启用, the product:
Identifies traffic matching patterns of known bots
Can challenge or block bots
Offers protection for static 资源
Provides limited 分析 to help you understand bot traffic
账户s with an Enterprise subscription but not the Bot Management add-on will have Super Bot Fight Mode for Business.

Custom 规则 are executed before Super Bot Fight Mode. To configure exceptions to Super Bot Fight Mode, 创建 a custom 规则 with the _Skip_ 操作. The _Skip_ 操作 allows the 请求 to bypass the Super Bot Fight Mode phase without terminating the 请求, enabling it to continue through the rest of the 安全 stack.
To start using Super Bot Fight Mode:
{/ prettier-ignore-start /}
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **安全 设置** page.
过滤器 by **Bot traffic**.
Go to **Super Bot Fight Mode**.
Turn **Super Bot Fight Mode** on.
Choose how your 域名 should respond to various 类型s of traffic by selecting the associated edit icon:
For more details on verified bots, refer to Verified Bots.
For more details on 支持的 file 类型s, refer to Static resource protection.
For more details on invisible code injection, refer to JavaScript detections.
For more details on WordPress 优化, refer to Super Bot Fight Mode for WordPress.
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **安全** > **Bots**.
Select **Configure Super Bot Fight Mode**.
Choose how your 域名 should respond to various 类型s of traffic:
{/ prettier-ignore-end /}
:::注意[警告]
If your organization also uses Cloudflare Tunnel, keep Definitely Automated set to Allow. Otherwise, 隧道s might fail with a  错误.
If you find that Super Bot Fight Mode is causing problems with your application traffic, you may want to disable it.
To disable Super Bot Fight Mode:
For all bot groupings (**Definitely automated traffic**, **Likely automated traffic**, and **Verified bots**), set the 值 to **Allow**.
For all other options (**Static resource protection**, **JavaScript detections**, and **Optimize for WordPress**), select the edit icon and ensure they are off.
Refer to Block AI bots.
:::注意
You can view blocked AI bot traffic via 安全 分析.
Use the Bot Report to 监控 bot traffic for the past 24 hours.

To access the Bot Report, go to 安全 > Bots. If you see a double-digit percentage of automated traffic, you may want to upgrade to Bot Management to save money on 源 costs and protect your 域名 from large-scale attacks.

You can see bot-相关 操作s by going to 安全 > 事件. Any 请求s challenged by this product will be labeled Super Bot Fight Mode in the Service 字段. This allows you to 观察, analyze, and follow trends in your bot traffic over time.
Super Bot Fight Mode runs during the  phase of the 规则et Engine.

/browser-rendering/cdp/.title: Chrome Dev工具 协议 (CDP)
/browser-rendering/cdp/.description: 创建 persistent browser 会话s, manage tabs, and interact with browsers using Chrome Dev工具 协议 (CDP) commands via the /dev工具 端点.
paragraphs: The  端点 provide 会话 management capabilities that follow the Chrome Dev工具 协议 (CDP). These 端点 allow you to 创建 persistent browser 会话s, manage mul提示le tabs, and interact with browsers using CDP commands. This is useful for advanced automation, 调试, and remote browser control.

CDP 端点 can be accessed from any environment that 支持s WebSocket connections, including local development machines, external servers, and CI/CD 管道s. This means you can connect to Browser Rendering from Node.js scripts, Puppeteer, Playwright, or any CDP-compatible client.

The Chrome Dev工具 协议 (CDP) is a remote 调试 协议 that allows you to instrument, inspect, 调试, and profile Chromium-based browsers. It is the same 协议 used by Chrome Dev工具 to control and 监控 the browser. Popular browser automation 库 like Puppeteer and Playwright provide high-level APIs over the Chrome Dev工具 协议, making it easier to automate common 任务s.
The browser 会话s 端点 enable you to:
**创建 and manage persistent browser 会话s** — Launch browser instances that remain 活跃 for extended periods
**Open, close, and 列表 browser tabs (tar获取s)** — Manage mul提示le 调试gable tar获取s (pages, iframes, etc.) within a single browser instance
**Connect via WebSocket to send CDP commands** — Automate browser 操作s programmatically
**View live browser 会话s using Chrome Dev工具 UI** — 调试 and inspect remote browser 会话s visually
**Integrate with existing CDP clients** — Use standard CDP clients like Puppeteer or custom WebSocket implementations
Once you acquire a browser 会话, you can interact with it in two ways:

Connect to the WebSocket 端点  to acquire a 会话 and send CDP commands directly over the connection. This is the standard way to use CDP and works with any CDP client, including Puppeteer, Playwright, and MCP clients.
HTTP 端点 are also available to manage the browser lifecycle without using WebSockets. These follow the standard CDP HTTP 端点:
**创建 会话** —
**列表 tabs** —
**创建 tab** —
**Close tab** —
**Close 会话** —
Check the API 参考 for the full 列表 of 端点.

Playwright is an open-source package developed by Microsoft that can do browser automation 任务s; it is commonly used to write frontend 测试s, 创建 screenshots, or crawl pages.
The Workers team forked a version of Playwright that was modified to be compatible with Cloudflare Workers and Browser Rendering.
Our version is open sourced and can be found in Cloudflare's fork of Playwright. The npm package can be 安装ed from npmjs as @cloudflare/playwright:
:::注意
The current version is  v1.2.0, based on Playwright v1.58.2.
In this 示例, you will run Playwright 测试s in a Cloudflare Worker using the todomvc application.
If you want to skip the 步骤s and 快速开始 quickly, select 部署 to Cloudflare below.
![部署 to Cloudflare](https://部署.workers.cloudflare.com/?url=https://github.com/cloudflare/playwright/tree/main/包/playwright-cloudflare/示例/todomvc)
Make sure you have the browser 绑定 configured in your Wrangler 配置 file:
To use the la测试 version of , your Worker 配置 must include the  兼容性 flag and a  of 2025-09-15 or later. This change is necessary because the library's 函数ality requires the native  API.
安装 the npm package:
Let's look at some 示例 of how to use Playwright:

Using browser automation to take screenshots of web pages is a common use case. This script tells the browser to navigate to https://demo.playwright.dev/todomvc, 创建 some items, take a screenshot of the page, and return the image in the 响应.

A Playwright trace is a detailed log of your 工作流 执行 that captures 信息rmation like user clicks and navigation 操作s, screenshots of the page, and any console 消息s generated and used for 调试. Developers can take a  file and either open it locally or upload it to the Playwright Trace Viewer, a GUI tool that helps you explore the data.
Here's an 示例 of a Worker generating a trace file:

One of the most common 使用场景 for using Playwright is software 测试. Playwright includes 测试 assertion 特性 in its APIs; refer to Assertions in the Playwright 文档 for details. Here's an 示例 of a Worker doing  测试 assertions of the todomvc demo page:
Playwright 支持s storage state to obtain and persist Cookies and other storage data. In this 示例, you will use storage state to persist Cookies and other storage data in Workers KV.
First, ensure you have a KV 命名空间. You can 创建 a 新功能 one with:
Then, add the KV 命名空间 to your Wrangler 配置 file:
Now, you can use the storage state to persist Cookies and other storage data in KV:

If users omit the  statement, the browser instance will stay open, 读取y to be connected to again and re-used but it will, by 默认, close automatically after 1 minute of inactivity. Users can 可选ly extend this idle time up to 10 minutes, by using the  option, set in milliseconds:
Using the above, the browser will stay open for up to 10 minutes, even if 不活跃.

This is an inactivity 超时, not a maximum 会话 duration. 会话s can remain open longer than 10 minutes as long as they stay 活跃. To keep a 会话 open beyond the inactivity 超时, send a command at least once within your configured window (for 示例, every 10 minutes). Refer to 会话 duration 限制 for more 信息rmation.

The best way to improve the 性能 of your browser rendering Worker is to reuse 会话s by keeping the browser open after you've finished with it, and connecting to that 会话 each time you have a 新功能 请求. Playwright handles [](https://playwright.dev/docs/api/class-browser#browser-close) differently from Puppeteer. In Playwright, if the browser was obtained using a  会话, the 会话 will disconnect. If the browser was obtained using a  会话, the 会话 will close.

To specify a custom user agent in Playwright, set it in the options when creating a 新功能 browser context with . All pages subsequently 创建d from this context will use the 新功能 user agent. This is useful if the tar获取 website serves different content based on the user agent.
The  parameter does not bypass bot protection. 请求s from Browser Rendering will always be identified as a bot.
When developing locally with  or , Chrome runs in headless mode by 默认. To launch Chrome in visible (headful) mode, set the  环境变量:
Or with the Cloudflare Vite plugin:
This opens a browser window so you can watch your Playwright automation in real time, making it easier to 调试 navigation, element selection, and page inter操作s.
When using  in headful mode, two Chrome windows may appear. This is expected behavior due to how Playwright handles browser contexts via CDP.
In order to facilitate browser 会话 management, we have extended the Playwright API with 新功能 方法:

Notice that the 会话  has an 活跃 Worker connection (), while 会话  is free. While a connection is 活跃, no other workers may connect to that 会话.

会话  was closed explicitly with  by the client, while 会话  was closed due to reaching the maximum idle time (check 限制).
You should also be able to access this 信息rmation in the 控制面板, albeit with a slight delay.

The full Playwright API can be found at the Playwright API 文档.
The following capabilities are not yet fully 支持的, but we’re 活跃ly working on them:
Playwright 测试 except Assertions

This is not an exhaustive 列表 — expect rapid changes as we work toward broader parity with the 源al 特性 set. You can also check la测试 测试 results for a granular up to date 列表 of the 特性 that are fully 支持的.

/browser-rendering/limits/.title: 限制
/browser-rendering/limits/.description: Learn about the 限制 associated with Browser Rendering.
headings: Can I increase the browser 超时?
Is there a maximum 会话 duration?
I upgraded from the Workers 免费计划, but I'm still hitting the 10-minute per day limit. What should I do?
Why is my browser usage higher than expected?
paragraphs: Browser Rendering 限制 are based on your Cloudflare Workers plan.
For 定价 信息rmation, refer to Browser Rendering 定价.
:::注意[Need higher 限制?]
If you are on a Workers 免费计划 and you want to increase your 限制, upgrade to a Workers Paid plan in the Workers plans page of the Cloudflare 控制面板:
The  端点 has additional 限制 for Workers 免费计划 users:
If you are on a Workers Paid plan and you want to increase your 限制 beyond those 列表ed here, Cloudflare will grant 请求s for higher 限制 on a case-by-case basis.
[^1]: Browsers close upon 任务 completion or sixty seconds of inactivity (if you do not extend your browser 超时). Therefore, in practice, many 工作流s do not require a high 数字 of concurrent browsers.
[^2]: By 默认, a browser will time out after 60 seconds of inactivity. You can extend this to up to 10 minutes using the  option. Call  to release the browser instance immediately.

[^3]: Enforced with a fixed per-second fill rate, not as a burst allowance. This means you cannot send all your 请求s at once. The API expects them to be sp读取 evenly over the minute. If you exceed the limit, refer to 故障排除 the  错误.
By 默认, a browser instance will time out after 60 seconds of inactivity. If you want to keep the browser open longer, you can use the  option, which allows you to extend the 超时 to up to 10 minutes.

There is no fixed maximum lifetime for a browser 会话 as long as it remains 活跃. By 默认, Browser Rendering closes 会话s after one minute of inactivity to pr事件 unintended usage. You can increase this inactivity 超时 to up to 10 minutes.

If you need 会话s to remain open longer, keep them 活跃 by sending a command at least once within your configured inactivity window (for 示例, every 10 minutes). 会话s also close when Browser Rendering rolls out a 新功能 release.
If you recently upgraded to the Workers Paid plan but still encounter the 10-minute per day limit, re部署 your Worker to ensure your usage is correctly associated with the 新功能 plan.

If you are hitting the daily limit or seeing higher usage than expected, the most common cause is browser 会话s that are not being closed properly. When a browser 会话 is not explicitly closed with , it remains open and continues to consume browser time until it times out (60 seconds by 默认, or up to 10 minutes if you use the  option).
To minimize usage:
Always call  when you are finished with a browser 会话.
Wrap your browser code in a  block to ensure  is called even if an 错误 occurs.

Use [](/browser-rendering/puppeteer/#列表-recent-会话s) or [](/browser-rendering/playwright/#列表-recent-会话s) to review recent 会话s and identify any that closed due to  instead of . 会话s that close due to idle 超时 indicate the browser was not closed explicitly.
You can 监控 your usage and view 会话 close reasons in the Cloudflare 控制面板 on the Browser Rendering page:
Refer to Browser close reasons for more 信息rmation.
When you make too many 请求s in a short period of time, Browser Rendering will respond with HTTP status code . You can view your 账户's rate 限制 in the Workers Free and Workers Paid sections above.
The 示例 below demonstrates how to handle 速率限制 gracefully by 读取ing the  值 and 重试ing the 请求 after that delay.

This  错误 indicates you have hit the daily browser limit on the Workers 免费计划. Workers 免费计划 账户s are limited to 10 minutes of Browser Rendering usage per day. If you exceed that limit, you will receive a  错误 until the next UTC day.
You can increase your 限制 by upgrading to a Workers Paid plan on the Workers plans page of the Cloudflare 控制面板:
If you recently upgraded but still encounter the 10-minute per day limit, re部署 your Worker to ensure your usage is correctly associated with the 新功能 plan.

[](https://github.com/cloudflare/playwright-mcp) is a Playwright MCP server fork that provides browser automation capabilities using Playwright and Browser Rendering.
This server enables LLMs to interact with web pages through structured accessibility 快照s, bypassing the need for screenshots or visually-tuned models. Its 键 特性 are:
Fast and lightweight. Uses Playwright's accessibility tree, not pixel-based input.
LLM-friendly. No vision models needed, operates purely on structured data.
Deterministic tool application. Avoids ambiguity common with screenshot-based approaches.
:::注意
The current version of Cloudflare Playwright MCP v1.1.1 is in sync with 上游 Playwright MCP v0.0.30.
If you are al读取y familiar with Cloudflare Workers and you want to 快速开始 with Playwright MCP right away, select this button:
![部署 to Cloudflare](https://部署.workers.cloudflare.com/?url=https://github.com/cloudflare/playwright-mcp/tree/main/cloudflare/示例)
This 创建s a repository in your GitHub 账户 and 部署s the application to Cloudflare Workers. Use this option if you are familiar with Cloudflare Workers, and wish to skip the 步骤-by-步骤 guidance.
Check our GitHub page for more 信息rmation on how to 构建 and 部署 Playwright MCP.
Follow these 步骤s to 部署 :
安装 the Playwright MCP npm package.
Make sure you have the browser rendering and durable 对象 绑定 and 迁移s in your Wrangler 配置 file.
Your Worker 配置 must include the  兼容性 flag and a  of 2025-09-15 or later.
Edit the code.
部署 the server.
The server is now available at  and you can use it with any MCP client.
Cloudflare AI Playground is a great way to 测试 MCP servers using LLM models available in Workers AI.
Navigate to https://playground.ai.cloudflare.com/
Ensure that the model is set to
In **MCP Servers**, set **URL** to
Click **Connect**
Status should 更新 to **Connected** and it should 列表 23 available 工具
You can now start to interact with the model, and it will run necessary the 工具 to accomplish what was 请求ed.
For best results, give simple instructions consisting of one single 操作, e.g. "创建 a 新功能 todo entry", "Go to cloudflare site", "Take a screenshot"
Try this sequence of instructions to see Playwright MCP in 操作:
"Go to demo.playwright.dev/todomvc"
"创建 some todo entry"
"Nice. Now 创建 a todo in parrot style"
"And 创建 another todo in Yoda style"
"Take a screenshot"
You can also use other MCP clients like Claude Desktop.
Check our GitHub page for more 示例 and MCP client 配置 options and our developer 文档 on how to 构建 Agents on Cloudflare.

/browser-rendering/features/reuse-sessio.title: Reuse 会话s
headings: 3. Configure the Wrangler 配置 file
paragraphs: By 默认, each Browser 会话s 请求 launches a 新功能 browser instance. Reusing 会话s eliminates cold-start time and improves 性能 by reconnecting to an existing browser instead of launching a 新功能 one.
This 特性 applies to Browser 会话s (Puppeteer, Playwright, and CDP). Quick 操作s handle 会话 lifecycle automatically.
There are two approaches to reusing 会话s:

**Disconnect and reconnect** (covered in this page): Use  instead of  to keep the browser alive, then reconnect to it on the next 请求. Best for stateless workloads where any available browser 会话 will do.
**Durable Objects**: Persist a long-运行中 browser inside a Durable 对象 for stateful 会话 management. Best when you need to maintain state across 请求s or 路由 specific users to specific browser instances.

/browser-rendering/pricing/.title: 定价
headings: 示例 of Workers Paid 定价
示例: Quick 操作s 定价
示例: Browser 会话s 定价
How do I estimate my Browser Rendering costs?
Do 已失败 API calls, such as those that time out, add to billable browser hours?
How is the 数字 of concurrent browsers calculated?
paragraphs: Billing depends on how you use Browser Rendering:
**Quick 操作s**: Charged for browser hours only.
**Browser 会话s** (Puppeteer, Playwright, CDP): Direct browser control, charged for both browser hours and concurrent browsers.
Browser hours are shared across all 方法.
If a Workers Paid user uses Quick 操作s for 50 hours during the month, the estimated cost for the month is as follows.
For browser hours:
50 hours - 10 hours (included in plan) = 40 hours
40 hours × $0.09 per hour = $3.60

If a Workers Paid plan user uses Browser 会话s (Puppeteer, Playwright, or CDP) for 50 hours during the month, and uses 10 concurrent browsers for the first 15 days and 20 concurrent browsers the last 15 days, the estimated cost for the month is as follows.
For concurrent browsers:
((10 browsers × 15 days) + (20 browsers × 15 days)) = 450 total browsers used in month
450 browsers used in month ÷ 30 days in month = 15 browsers (averaged monthly)
15 browsers (averaged monthly) − 10 (included in plan) = 5 browsers
5 browsers × $2.00 per browser = $10.00
For browser hours and concurrent browsers:
You can 监控 Browser Rendering usage in two ways:
To 监控 your Browser Rendering usage in the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Browser Rendering** page.
The  头部, which is returned in every Quick 操作s 响应, 报告 browser time used for the 请求 (in milliseconds). You can also access this 头部 using the Typescript SDK with the .as响应() method:
You can then use the 表s above to estimate your costs based on your usage.
No. If a Quick 操作s 请求 fails with a  错误, the browser 会话 is not charged.

Cloudflare calculates concurrent browsers as the monthly average of your daily peak usage. In other words, we 记录 the peak 数字 of concurrent browsers each day and then average those 值s over the month. This approach reflects your typical traffic and ensures you are not disproportionately charged for brief spikes in browser 并发.

At the end of each day, Cloudflare totals all of your browser usage for that day in seconds. At the end of each billing cycle, we add up all of the daily totals to find the monthly total of browser hours, rounded to the nearest whole hour. In other words, 1,800 seconds (30 minutes) or more is rounded up to the nearest hour, and 1,799 seconds or less is rounded down to the nearest whole hour.

For 示例, if you only use one minute of browser time in a day, that day counts as one minute. If you do that every day for a 30-day month, your total would be 30 minutes. For billing, we round that up to one browser hour.
