/cloudflare-one/team-and-resources/devic.title: Cloudflare One Client
headings: How the Cloudflare One Client works
键 优势 of using the Cloudflare One Client
paragraphs: The Cloudflare One Client (formerly WARP) securely and privately sends traffic from your devices to Cloudflare's 全球网络, where Cloudflare Gateway can apply advanced web 过滤. The client also 报告 device health 信息rmation — such as OS version, disk 加密 status, and the presence of specific applications — so that you can enforce device posture checks in your Access and Gateway policies.
The Cloudflare One Client 创建s encrypted connections between your device and Cloudflare's network. It does this in two ways:
**代理 tunnel** — Encrypts and 路由 your device's internet and private network traffic through Cloudflare, using the WireGuard or MASQUE 协议.
**DNS 代理** — Sends your device's DNS queries to Cloudflare over an encrypted channel (DNS-over-HTTPS), where Gateway DNS policies can 过滤器 them.
The client runs on all major operating systems and can be 部署ed through common 端点 management 工具 (such as Intune, JAMF, or JumpCloud).
The Cloudflare One Client consists of:
**Graphical User Interface (GUI):** A control panel that allows end users to view the client's status and perform 操作s such as connecting or disconnecting.
**WARP daemon (or service):** The core background process responsible for establishing the encrypted connections described above and handling all client 函数ality on your device.
For more 信息rmation on how the Cloudflare One Client 路由 traffic, refer to the client architecture page and watch the video below.
部署ing the Cloudflare One Client significantly enhances your organization's 安全 and visibility within Cloudflare Zero Trust:
**Unified 安全 policies everywhere**: With the Cloudflare One Client 部署ed in the Traffic and DNS mode, Gateway policies are not location-dependent — they can be enforced anywhere.
**Advanced web 过滤 and threat protection**: Activate Gateway 特性 for your device traffic, including:
Anti-Virus scanning
HTTP 过滤
Browser Isolation
Identity-based policies

**Application and device-specific insights**: View which SaaS applications your users are accessing and review their approval status on the Shadow IT Discovery page. 监控 device and network 性能 with Digital Experience 监控 (DEX) to detect connectivity or 性能 issues before users report them.

**Device posture checks**: The Cloudflare One Client provides advanced Zero Trust protection by making it possible to check for device posture. By setting up device posture checks, you can 构建 Access or Gateway policies that check for a device's location, disk 加密 status, OS version, and more.

**Secure private and infrastructure access**: Connect devices to internal networks and applications through Cloudflare Tunnel without exposing them to the public internet. The client is also 必需 for Access for Infrastructure, which provides SSH access using short-lived 证书s and detailed audit logging.
The Cloudflare One Client offers flexible operating modes to suit your specific needs:

**Traffic and DNS mode** (默认) — 路由 device traffic (by 默认, all ports and 协议s) and DNS queries through Cloudflare for 过滤, inspection, and policy enforcement. Traffic exclusions can be configured with Split 隧道s.
**DNS-only mode** — 路由 only DNS queries through Cloudflare. Use this mode if you only need DNS-level 过滤 without inspecting web or application traffic.
Other modes (Traffic only, Local 代理, Posture only) are also available. For details, refer to the operating modes page.
Review the first-time 设置 指南 to 安装 and 部署 the Cloudflare One Client on your corporate devices.
Review possible client modes and 设置 to best suit your organization's needs.
Explore Cloudflare Gateway to enforce advanced DNS, network, HTTP, and egress policies with the Cloudflare One Client.

/cloudflare-one/traffic-policies/.title: Traffic policies
headings: 设置 Cloudflare Gateway traffic policies
How to choose a Cloudflare Gateway policy 类型
paragraphs: Every organization needs a way to control what users can reach on the Internet — blocking malware sites, restricting risky applications, and deciding how traffic exits the corporate network. Think of traffic policies as a set of 安全 checkpoints, each inspecting a different layer of your traffic before it is allowed through.

Cloudflare Gateway, a Secure Web Gateway (a cloud service that sits between your users and the Internet to enforce 安全 规则), allows you to 设置 policies that inspect and 过滤器 your organization's Internet traffic. Traffic policies control which websites, applications, and services your users can access — and how that traffic leaves your network.
Gateway 支持s several policy 类型s because network traffic can be inspected at different layers — from raw packets up to full HTTP 请求s. Each policy 类型 gives you control at a specific layer:
Packet 过滤 inspects raw network packets and blocks traffic based on properties like source IP address or 协议. It does not need to know who the user is or what 会话 they belong to.
Use packet 过滤 to drop unwanted traffic before it reaches any other policy.

DNS policies check every DNS 查询 your users make. When a 查询 matches a policy 规则, Gateway can block the 域名 from resolving — the site never loads because the 域名 name is never translated to an IP address.

DNS policies act at the earliest stage of a connection, before any content is fetched. This makes them the fas测试 policy 类型 to 部署 and the broadest in scope. For more 信息rmation on DNS 过滤, refer to the Cloudflare Learning Center.

Use DNS policies to block malicious 域名s, restrict content categories, or pr事件 entire sites from loading. For full threat protection, 对 DNS policies with HTTP policies — DNS blocks known bad 域名s, while HTTP catches threats hidden in allowed traffic.

Network policies inspect individual TCP, UDP, and Generic 路由 Encapsulation (GRE) packets. They can match on IP addresses, ports, 协议s, and the server name sent at the start of an encrypted connection (Server Name Indication, or SNI).
Use network policies to block access to specific ports or non-HTTP services such as SSH and RDP.

HTTP policies inspect the full content of web 请求s — including URLs, 头部, and uploaded or downloaded files. Gateway decrypts HTTPS traffic so it can examine what DNS and network policies cannot see. This requires 安装ing a Cloudflare root 证书 on user devices.

Use HTTP policies to block specific URLs, scan file uploads for sensitive data, block malware in downloads, and control which 账户s users can 登录 to — for 示例, allow your company Google Workspace 账户 but block personal Gmail.
Egress policies control how traffic leaves your network by assigning fixed IP addresses that belong to your organization. Third-party services can recognize these IPs as yours.
Use egress policies to connect to partners or services that only allow traffic from a known 列表 of IP addresses.
解析器 policies send DNS queries to specific DNS servers instead of the 默认 Cloudflare 解析器.
Use 解析器 policies to resolve private 主机名s on your internal network, 路由 queries to your own DNS servers for compliance, or reach internal 资源 while connected through Cloudflare One.
:::注意
When creating or editing policies, it may take up to 60 seconds for that policy to be 已更新 across all of Cloudflare's 数据中心.

Before you 创建 Cloudflare Gateway traffic policies, you need connect the devices or networks you want to protect and confirm that Cloudflare Gateway can inspect their traffic. For each traffic policy 类型, follow this 工作流:
Connect the devices or networks you want to protect.
Verify that Gateway is receiving traffic from your devices.
设置 recommended 安全 policies — for 示例, block all 安全 threat categories with a DNS policy.
Add policies specific to your organization's needs.

For 示例, if your goal is to pr事件 employees from accessing known malware 域名s, you would start by enrolling devices with the Cloudflare One Client (步骤 1), confirm DNS queries appear in your Gateway 日志 (步骤 2), then 创建 a DNS policy that blocks all 安全-risk categories (步骤 3).
For 步骤-by-步骤 设置 指南, refer to DNS, Network, and HTTP policies.
The following 表 maps common traffic-过滤 goals to the best Cloudflare Gateway policy 类型:
After you choose a Cloudflare Gateway policy 类型, continue with the matching 设置 指南 to 创建 the policy that fits your traffic-过滤 goal.
For help resolving common issues with Cloudflare Gateway policies, refer to 故障排除.

headings: Ensure Docker is 运行中 locally
Each Container is backed by its own Durable 对象

paragraphs: In this 指南, you will 部署 a Worker that can make 请求s to one or more Containers in 响应 to end-user 请求s.
In this 示例, each container runs a small webserver written in Go.
This 示例 Worker should give you a sense for simple Container use, and provide a starting point for more complex 使用场景.
In this 指南, we will 构建 and push a container image alongside your Worker code. By 默认, this process uses
Docker to do so.
{/ FUTURE CHANGE: Add some image you can use if you don't have Docker 运行中. /}
{/ FUTURE CHANGE: Link to docs on alternative 构建/push options /}
Run the following command to 创建 and 部署 a 新功能 Worker with a container, from the starter template:
When you want to 部署 a code change to either the Worker or Container code, you can run the following command using Wrangler CLI:
When you run , the following things happen:
Wrangler 构建s your container image using Docker.
Wrangler pushes your image to a Container Image Registry that is automatically
integrated with your Cloudflare 账户.
Wrangler 部署s your Worker, and configures Cloudflare's network to be 读取y to spawn instances of your container
The 构建 and push usually take the longest on the first 部署. Subsequent 部署s
are faster, because they reuse cached image layers.
:::注意
After you 部署 your Worker for the first time, you will need to wait several minutes until
it is 读取y to receive 请求s. Unlike Workers, Containers take a few minutes to be provisioned.
During this time, 请求s are sent to the Worker, but calls to the Container will 错误.
After 部署ing, run the following command to show a 列表 of containers containers in your Cloudflare 账户, and their 部署 status:
And see images 部署ed to the Cloudflare Registry with the following command:
Now, open the URL for your Worker. It should look something like .
If you make 请求s to the paths  or , your Worker 路由 请求s to specific containers.
Each different path after "/container/" 路由 to a unique container.
If you make 请求s to , you will load balanace 请求s to one of 3 containers chosen at random.
You can confirm this behavior by 读取ing the output of each 请求.
Now that you've 部署ed your first container, let's explain what is happening in your Worker's code, in your 配置 file, in your container's code, and how 请求s are 路由d.
Incoming 请求s are initially handled by the Worker, then passed to a container-已启用 Durable 对象.
To simplify and reduce boilerplate code, Cloudflare provides a  class as part of the  NPM package.
You don't have to be familiar with Durable Objects to use Containers, but it may be helpful
to understand the basics.
Each Durable 对象 runs alongside an individual container instance, manages starting and stopping it, and
can interact with the container through its ports. Containers will likely run near the Worker instance
请求ing them, but not necessarily. Refer to "How Locations are Selected"
for details.
In a simple app, the Durable 对象 may just boot the container and 代理 请求s to it.
In a more complex app, having container-已启用 Durable Objects allows you to 路由 请求s to individual stateful container
instances, manage the container lifecycle, pass in custom starting commands and 环境变量 to containers, run hooks
on container status changes, and more.
See the 文档 for Durable 对象 container 方法 and the
class repository for more details.
Your Wrangler 配置 file defines the 配置 for both your Worker and your container:
重要 points about this config:
points to a Dockerfile, to a directory containing a Dockerfile, or to a fully qualified image 参考 such as .
must be a Durable 对象 class name.
declares the maximum 数字 of simultaneously 运行中 container instances
that will run.
The Durable 对象 must use [](/durable-对象s/best-practices/access-durable-对象s-storage/#创建-sqlite-backed-durable-对象-class) not .
Your container image must be able to run on the  architecture, but aside from that, has few limitations.
In the 示例 you just 部署ed, it is a simple Golang server that responds to 请求s on port 8080 using
the  环境变量 that will be set in the Worker and an [auto-generated
环境变量](/containers/平台-details/#environment-变量s)
After 部署ing the 示例 code, to 部署 a different image, you can replace the provided image with one of your own.
First 注意  which extends the [](https://github.com/cloudflare/containers) class:
This defines basic 配置 for the container:
sets the port that the  and  方法 will use to communicate with the container. It also blocks
请求s until the container is 列表ening on this port.
sets the 超时 for the container to sleep after it has been idle for a certain amount of time.
sets 环境变量 that will be passed to the container when it starts.
, , and  are hooks that run when the container starts, stops, or 错误, respectively.
See the Container class 文档 for more details and 配置 options.
When a 请求 enters Cloudflare, your Worker's  处理程序 is invoked. This is the code that handles the incoming 请求. The fetch 处理程序 in the 示例 code, launches containers in two ways, on different 路由:
Making 请求s to  passes 请求s to a 新功能 container for
each path. This is done by spinning up a 新功能 Container instance. You may 注意
that the first 请求 to a 新功能 path takes longer than subsequent 请求s, this is
because a 新功能 container is booting.
Making 请求s to  will load balance 请求s across several containers.
This uses a simple  helper method, which picks an ID at random
from a set 数字 (in this case 3), then 路由 to that Container instance. You can replace this with any 路由 or load balancing logic you choose to implement:
This allows for mul提示le ways of using Containers:
If you simply want to send 请求s to many stateless and interchangeable containers,
you should load balance.
If you have stateful services or need individually addressable
containers, you should 请求 specific Container instances.
If you are 运行中 short-lived 作业s, want fine-grained control over the container
lifecycle, want to parameterize container entrypoint or env vars, or
want to chain to获取her mul提示le container calls, you should 请求 specific
Container instances.
Currently, 路由 请求s to one of many interchangeable Container instances is accomplished
with the  helper.
This is temporary — we plan to add native 支持 for 延迟-aware autoscaling and load balancing in the coming months.

信息rmation about your Containers, including:
Status and Health

A link to associated Workers and Durable Objects

/containers/beta-info/.title: 测试版 信息 & Roadmap
headings: Atomic code 更新s across Workers and Containers
paragraphs: Currently, Containers are in 测试版. There are several changes we plan to make prior to GA:
Container 限制 will be raised in the future. We plan to increase
both maximum instance size and maximum 数字 of instances in an 账户.
See the 限制 文档 for more 信息rmation.
Currently, Containers are not autoscaled or load balanced. Containers can be scaled manually
by calling  on their 绑定 with a unique ID.
We plan to add official 支持 for utilization-based autoscaling and 延迟-aware load balancing
in the future.
See the Autoscaling 文档 for more 信息rmation.
Currently, the  class uses Durable 对象 alarms to help manage Container shutdown. This
results in unnecessary log noise in the Worker 日志. You can 过滤器 these 日志 out in the 控制面板
by adding a 查询, but this is not ideal.
We plan to automatically reduce log noise in the future.
The 控制面板 will be 已更新 to show:
links from Workers to their associated Containers
Currently, Durable Objects are not co-located with their associated Container. When 请求ing a container,
the Durable 对象 will find one close to it, but not on the same machine.
We plan to co-locate Durable Objects with their Container in the future.
We currently prewarm servers across our 全球网络 with container images to ensure 快速开始 times.
There are times in which you may 请求 a 新功能 container and it will be started in a location that
farther from the end user than is desired. We are optimizing this process to ensure that this happens
as little as possible, but it may still occur.
When 部署ing a Container with , the Worker code will be immediately
已更新 while the Container code will slowly be 已更新 using a rolling 部署.
This means that you must ensure Worker code is backwards compatible with the old Container code.
In the future, Worker code in the Durable 对象 will only 更新 when associated Container code 更新s.
There are several areas where we wish to gather 反馈 from users:
Do you want to integrate Containers with any other Cloudflare services? If so, which ones and how?
Do you want more ways to interact with a Container via Workers? If so, how?
Do you need different mechanisms for 路由 请求s to containers?
Do you need different mechanisms for scaling containers? (see scaling 文档 for 信息rmation on autoscaling plans)
At any point during the 测试版, feel free to give 反馈 using this form.

/d1/platform/pricing/.title: 定价
paragraphs: D1 bills based on:
**Usage**: Queries you run against D1 will count as rows 读取, rows written, or both (for 事务s or batches).
**Scale-to-zero**: You are not billed for hours or capacity units. If you are not 运行中 queries against your 数据库, you are not billed for compute.
**Storage**: You are only billed for storage above the included 限制 of your plan.
Frequently asked questions 相关 to D1 定价:

/d1/.title: Cloudflare D1
paragraphs: 创建 新功能 无服务器 SQL 数据库s to 查询 from your Workers and Pages projects.
D1 is Cloudflare's managed, 无服务器 数据库 with SQLite's SQL semantics, built-in disaster recovery, and Worker and HTTP API access.

D1 is designed for horizontal scale out across mul提示le, smaller (10 GB) 数据库s, such as per-user, per-tenant or per-entity 数据库s. D1 allows you to 构建 applications with thousands of 数据库s at no extra cost for isolating with mul提示le 数据库s. D1 定价 is based only on 查询 and storage costs.
创建 your first D1 数据库 by following the 快速开始 指南, learn how to import data into a 数据库, and how to interact with your 数据库 directly from Workers or Pages.
创建 your first D1 数据库, establish a 模式, import data and 查询 D1 directly from an application built with Workers.
Execute SQL with SQLite's SQL 兼容性 and D1 Client API.
Time Travel is D1’s approach to 备份s and point-in-time-recovery, and allows you to 恢复 a 数据库 to any minute within the last 30 days.
构建 无服务器 applications and 部署 instantly across the globe for exceptional 性能, reliability, and scale.
部署 dynamic front-end applications in 记录 time.
Learn about D1's 定价 and how to estimate your usage.
Learn about what 限制 D1 has and how to work within them.
Browse what developers are 构建ing with D1.

Follow @CloudflareDev on Twitter to learn about product announcements, and what is 新功能 in Cloudflare Developer 平台.

/d1/platform/limits/.title: 限制
paragraphs: :::注意[Batch 限制]
限制 for individual queries (列表ed above) apply to each individual statement contained within a batch statement. For 示例, the maximum SQL statement length of 100 KB applies to each statement inside a .

[^1]: The maximum 数字 of 数据库s per 账户 can be increased by 请求 on Workers Paid and 企业计划s, with 支持 for millions to tens-of-millions of 数据库s (or more) per 账户. Refer to the guidance on limit increases on this page to 请求 an increase.
[^2]: The maximum storage per 账户 can be increased by 请求 on Workers Paid and 企业计划s. Refer to the guidance on limit increases on this page to 请求 an increase.

[^3]: A single Worker script can have up to 1 MB of script metadata. A 绑定 is defined as a 绑定 to a resource, such as a D1 数据库, KV 命名空间, environmental 变量, or 密钥. Each resource 绑定 is approximately 150-bytes, however environmental 变量s and 密钥 are controlled by the size of the 值 you provide. Excluding environmental 变量s, you can bind up to \~5,000 D1 数据库s to a single Worker script.
[^4]: 请求s to Cloudflare API must resolve in 30 seconds. Therefore, this duration limit also applies to the entire batch call.
[^5]: The imported file is uploaded to R2. Refer to R2 upload limit.

Cloudflare also offers other storage solutions such as Workers KV, Durable Objects, and R2. Each product has different advantages and 限制. Refer to Choose a data or storage product to review which storage option is right for your use case.

D1 also allows you to export a 数据库. This can be useful for local development or 测试.
To import an existing SQLite 数据库 into D1, you must have:
The Cloudflare Wrangler CLI 安装ed.
A 数据库 to use as the tar获取.
An existing SQLite (version 3.0+) 数据库 file to import.
:::注意
You cannot import a raw SQLite 数据库 ( files) directly. Refer to how to convert an existing SQLite file first.

From here, you can now 查询 our 新功能 表 from our Worker using the D1 Workers 绑定 API.
:::注意[已知限制]
For imports,  is limited to 5GiB files, the same as the R2 upload limit. For imports larger than 5GiB, we recommend splitting the data into mul提示le files.
In order to convert a raw SQLite3 数据库 dump (a  file) you will need the sqlite command-line tool 安装ed on your system.

If you have an existing SQLite 数据库 from another system, you can import its 表s into a D1 数据库. Using the  command-line tool, you can convert an  file into a series of SQL statements that can be imported (executed) against a D1 数据库.

Export is not 支持的 for virtual 表s, including 数据库s with virtual 表s. D1 支持s virtual 表s for full-text 搜索 using SQLite's FTS5 module. As a workaround, 删除 any virtual 表s, export, and then re创建 virtual 表s.
A 运行中 export will block other 数据库 请求s.
Any numeric 值 in a column is affected by JavaScript's 52-bit precision for 数字s. If you store a very large 数字 (in ), then retrieve the same 值, the returned 值 may be less precise than your 源al 数字.
If you receive an 错误 when trying to import an existing 模式 and/or dataset into D1:
Ensure you are importing data in SQL format (typically with a  file extension). Refer to how to convert SQLite files if you have a  数据库 dump.
Make sure the 模式 is SQLite3 compatible. You cannot import data from a MySQL or PostgreSQL 数据库 into D1, as the 类型s and SQL syntax are not directly compatible.
If you have foreign 键 relationships between 表s, ensure you are importing the 表s in the right order. You cannot refer to a 表 that does not yet exist.
If you receive a  错误, make sure you have removed  and  from your dumped SQL statements.
If you encounter a  错误 when trying to import a large SQL file into D1, it means that one of the SQL statements in your file exceeds the maximum allowed length.

/d1/reference/community-projects/.title: 社区 projects
paragraphs: Members of the Cloudflare developer 社区 and broader developer ecosystem have built and/or contributed tooling — including ORMs (对象 Relational Mapper) 库, 查询 构建ers, and CLI 工具 — that 构建 on top of D1.
:::注意
社区 projects are not maintained by the Cloudflare D1 team. They are managed and 已更新 by the project authors.
Sutando is an ORM designed for Node.js. With Sutando, each 表 in a 数据库 has a corresponding model that handles CRUD (创建, 读取, 更新, 删除) operations.
GitHub
D1 with Sutando ORM 示例
knex-cloudflare-d1 is the Cloudflare D1 dialect for Knex.js. 注意 that this is not an official dialect provided by Knex.js.

Prisma ORM is a next-generation JavaScript and TypeScript ORM that unlocks a 新功能 level of developer experience when working with 数据库s thanks to its intuitive data model, automated 迁移s, 类型-safety and auto-completion.
Tutorial
Docs
Kysely is a 类型-safe and autocompletion-friendly typescript SQL 查询 构建er. With this adapter you can interact with D1 with the familiar Kysely interface.
Kysely GitHub
D1 adapter

The  数据库 adapter follows the FeathersJS 查询 Syntax standard and works with any framework. It is built on the D1 adapter for Kysely and 支持s passing queries directly from client applications. Since the FeathersJS 查询 syntax is a subset of MongoDB's syntax, this is a great tool for MongoDB users to use Cloudflare D1 without previous SQL experience.
feathers-kysely on npm
feathers-kysely on GitHub

Drizzle is a headless TypeScript ORM with a head which runs on Node, Bun and Deno. Drizzle ORM lives on the Edge and it is a JavaScript ORM too. It comes with a drizzle-kit CLI companion for automatic SQL 迁移s generation. Drizzle automatically generates your D1 模式 based on 类型s you define in TypeScript, and exposes an API that allows you to 查询 your 数据库 directly.
D1 示例

is a zero-dependency 查询 构建er that provides a simple standardized interface while keeping the 优势 and speed of using raw queries over a traditional ORM. While not intended to provide ORM-like 函数ality,  makes it easier to interact with your 数据库 from code for direct SQL access.
文档

Instead of 运行中 the  command in your terminal every time you want to interact with your 数据库, you can interact with D1 from within the . 创建d by a Discord 社区 Champion, this gives the benefit of executing multi-line queries, obtaining command history, and viewing a cleanly formatted 表 output.

is a package that brings some Cloudflare Worker ecosystem 绑定 into PHP and Laravel via the Cloudflare API. It provides inter操作 with D1 via PDO, KV and Queues, with more services to add in the future, making PHP integration with Cloudflare a real breeze.
Packagist
Staff Directory is a demo project using D1, HonoX, and Cloudflare Pages. It uses D1 to store employee data, and is an 示例 of a full-stack application built on top of D1.
D1 函数ality

is a Nuxt module that brings Cloudflare Worker 绑定 into your Nuxt application with no 配置. It leverages the Wrangler 平台 代理 in development and direct 绑定 in production to interact with D1, KV and R2 with server composables (,  and ).
also provides a way to use your remote D1 数据库 in development using the  command.

/d1/reference/time-travel/.title: Time Travel and 备份s
headings: Export D1 into R2 using 工作流s
paragraphs: Time Travel is D1's approach to 备份s and point-in-time-recovery, and allows you to 恢复 a 数据库 to any minute within the last 30 days.
You do not need to enable Time Travel. It is always on.
数据库 history and restoring a 数据库 incur no additional costs.
Time Travel automatically 创建s bookmarks on your behalf. You do not need to manually 触发器 or remember to initiate a 备份.

By not having to rely on 定时 备份s and/or manually initiated 备份s, you can go back in time and 恢复 a 数据库 prior to a 已失败 迁移 or 模式 change, a  or  statement without a specific  clause, and in the future, fork/copy a production 数据库 directly.
:::注意[支持 for Time Travel]
数据库s using D1's 新功能 storage subsystem can use Time Travel. Time Travel replaces the 快照-based 备份s used for legacy 内测版 数据库s.
To understand which storage subsystem your 数据库 uses, run  and inspect the  字段 in the output. 数据库s with  支持 the 新功能 Time Travel API. 数据库s with  only 支持 the older, 快照-based 备份 API.
Time Travel leverages D1's concept of a   bookmark   to 恢复 to a point in time.
Bookmarks older than 30 days are invalid and cannot be used as a 恢复 point.

Restoring a 数据库 to a specific bookmark does not remove or 删除 older bookmarks. For 示例, if you 恢复 to a bookmark representing the state of your 数据库 10 minutes ago, and determine that you needed to 恢复 to an earlier point in time, you can still do so.
Bookmarks are lexicographically sor表. 排序 orders a 列表 of bookmarks from oldest-to-新功能est.
Bookmarks can be derived from a Unix timestamp (seconds since Jan 1st, 1970), and conversion between a specific timestamp and a bookmark is deterministic (s表).
Bookmarks are also leveraged by 会话s API to ensure sequential 一致性 within a 会话.
Time Travel 支持s two timestamp formats:
Unix timestamps, which correspond to seconds since January 1st, 1970 at midnight. This is always in UTC.

The JavaScript date-time 字符串 format, which is a simplified version of the ISO-8601 timestamp format. An valid date-time 字符串 for the July 27, 2023 at 11:18AM in Americas/新功能_York (EST) would look like .
[](/workers/wrangler/安装-and-更新/)  or later 安装ed to use Time Travel commands.
A 数据库 on D1's production backend. You can check whether a 数据库 is using this backend via  - the output show .
You can retrieve a bookmark for the current timestamp by calling the  command, which 默认s to returning the current bookmark:
To retrieve the bookmark for a timestamp in the past, pass the  flag with a valid Unix or RFC3339 timestamp:
To 恢复 a 数据库 to a specific point-in-time:
:::注意
Restoring a 数据库 to a specific point-in-time is a _destructive_ operation, and overwrites the 数据库 in place. In the future, D1 will 支持 branching & cloning 数据库s using Time Travel.
注意 that:
Timestamps are converted to a deterministic, s表 bookmark. The same timestamp will always represent the same bookmark.
Queries in flight will be cancelled, and an 错误 returned to the client.
The 恢复 operation will return a bookmark that allows you to undo and revert the 数据库.
You can undo a 恢复 by:
Taking 注意 of the previous bookmark returned as part of a  operation
Restoring directly to a bookmark in the past, prior to your last 恢复.
To fetch a bookmark from an earlier state:
You can automatically export your D1 数据库 into R2 storage via REST API and Cloudflare 工作流s. This may be useful if you wish to store a state of your D1 数据库 for longer than 30 days.
Refer to the 指南 Export and save D1 数据库.
You can quickly 获取 the Unix timestamp from the command-line in macOS and Windows via .

Time Travel does not yet allow you to clone or fork an existing 数据库 to a 新功能 copy. In the future, Time Travel will allow you to fork (clone) an existing 数据库 into a 新功能 数据库, or overwrite an existing 数据库.
You can 恢复 a 数据库 back to a point in time up to 30 days in the past (Workers Paid plan) or 7 days (Workers 免费计划). Refer to 限制 for details on Time Travel's 限制.

/d1/sql-api/sql-statements/.title: SQL statements
paragraphs: D1 is compatible with most SQLite's SQL convention since it leverages SQLite's 查询 engine. D1 支持s a 数字 of 数据库-level statements that allow you to 列表 表s, 索引es, and inspect the 模式 for a given 表 or 索引.
You can execute any of these statements via the D1 console in the Cloudflare 控制面板, [](/workers/wrangler/commands/d1/#d1-execute), or with the D1 Worker 绑定 API.
D1 支持s some SQLite PRAGMA statements. The PRAGMA statement is an SQL extension for SQLite. PRAGMA commands can be used to:
Modify the behavior of certain SQLite operations.
查询 the SQLite library for internal data about 模式s or 表s (but 注意 that PRAGMA statements cannot 查询 the contents of a 表).
Control environmental 变量s.
You can also 查询 the  表 to show all 表s, 索引es, and the 源al SQL used to generate them:
You can perform a 搜索 using SQL's  operator:
Learn how to 创建 索引es in D1.
Use D1's JSON 函数s to 查询 JSON data.
Use [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#dev) to run your Worker and D1 locally and 调试 issues before 部署ing.

/d1/worker-api/.title: Workers 绑定 API

Run the prepared statement.

/data-localization/geo-key-manager/.title: Geo 键 Manager
headings: Cloudflare 数据中心 flow 示例
paragraphs: Geo 键 Manager offers enhanced control over the storage location of private SSL/TLS 键s, ensuring compliance with regional data regulations and 安全 要求.

By 默认, private 键s will be encrypted and securely distributed to each 数据中心, where they can be utilized for local SSL/TLS termination. Geo 键 Manager allows you to choose where you want to store your private 键s.

Geo 键 Manager was restricted to the US, EU, and high-安全 数据中心, but with the 新功能 version of Geo 键 Manager, available in Closed 测试版, you can now 创建  and  of countries in which your private 键s will be stored. That means that you will be able define specific geographic locations where to store 键s, for instance you can store your private 键s exclusively in Australia or limit private 键s storage to the EU and the UK.

The following diagram is a high-level 示例 of the flow of the Cloudflare 数据中心 without private TLS 键. In this process, 数据中心 have to 请求 and 创建 temporary 会话 键s to perform TLS termination by reaching out to Cloudflare 数据中心 which hold the private TLS 键s:
For detailed 信息rmation on 设置 and 支持的 options, refer to Geo 键 Manager 文档.

/data-localization/.title: Data Localization Suite
paragraphs: The Data Localization Suite (DLS) is a collection of 工具 that enable customers to choose the location where Cloudflare inspects and stores data, while maintaining the 安全 and 性能 优势 of our 全球网络.
Keep your 键s within a specified region, ensuring compliance and data sovereignty.
Ensure that any traffic metadata that can identify your end user stays in the region you selected.
Comply with regional restrictions by choosing which subset of 数据中心 decrypts and services your HTTPS traffic.
Cloudflare SSL/TLS encrypts your web traffic to pr事件 data theft and other tampering.
Cloudflare's global DNS 平台 provides speed and resilience. DNS customers also benefit from free DNSSEC, and protection against 路由 leaks and hijacking.
Refer to our la测试 资源 to 了解更多 about privacy.
读取 articles about the la测试 更新s to the Data Localization Suite.

/data-localization/metadata-boundary/.title: Customer Metadata Boundary
paragraphs: As part of the Data Localization Suite, the Customer Metadata Boundary (CMB) ensures that Customer 日志 — any traffic metadata that can identify a customer’s end user (that is, contains the customer's 账户 ID) — will stay in the  (European Union) or in the  (United States), de待处理 on the region the customer selects. For 示例, if a customer selects the  Customer Metadata Boundary, metadata will only be sent to our core 数据中心 located in the European Union.

An exception is made if "Allow out-of-region access" is 已启用. When 已启用, Customer 日志 will still be stored in the configured regions but will be accessible to authorized end users, regardless of physical location. Refer to Out of region access for more details.

The following diagram is a high-level 示例 of the flow of how metadata about a customer's traffic is generated on a Cloudflare 数据中心. 日志 are exclusively sent to the EU core 数据中心 for Cloudflare customers and their authorized users to access and view.
Additionally, customers have the option to configure Logpush to push their Customer 日志 to various storage services, SIEMs, and log management providers.
For detailed 信息rmation about product-specific behavior regarding Metadata Boundary, refer to the Cloudflare product 兼容性 page.

/data-localization/regional-services/.title: Regional Services
paragraphs: Regional Services gives you the ability to accommodate regional restrictions by choosing which subset of 数据中心 decrypt and service HTTPS traffic.

Regional Services proceeds and processes traffic within certain regions for customers who have to meet regional compliance or have p参考s for maintaining regional control over their data. 示例 of 使用场景 could be a customer that needs to accommodate regional restrictions like GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation), or customers that are bound by agreement with their own customers that include geographic restrictions on data flows or data processing.

With Regional Services, TLS is only terminated inside the configured region. For 示例, if a 主机名 is configured to regionalize to the European Union (EU), any HTTPS 请求 from the United States (US) will 路由 to the EU.
Regional Services globally ingests traffic, implementing L3/L4 DDoS mitigations. Meanwhile, 安全, 性能, and reliability 函数s are serviced at only in-region Cloudflare locations.
Regional Services ensures that all edge application services operate within the selected region. This includes (the following 列表 is not exhaustive):
Storing and retrieving content from Cache.
Blocking malicious HTTP payloads with the Web 应用防火墙 (WAF).
Detecting and blocking suspicious activity with Bot Management.
运行中 Cloudflare Workers scripts.
Load Balancing traffic to the best 源服务器s (or other  端点 ).
The following diagram is a high-level 示例 of the flow of a 请求 coming from an end user located within the US connecting to a website using Cloudflare Regional Services set to EU.

For more details about the products that are compatible with Regional Services, refer to the Cloudflare product 兼容性 page. If you have purchased these products as part of your Enterprise subscription plan, Cloudflare will only terminate TLS connections for these products in the geographic region you have configured for Regional Services.

/ddos-protection/advanced-ddos-systems/o.title: Advanced TCP Protection
paragraphs: Advanced TCP Protection can simultaneously protect against different kinds of attacks:
Pinpointed attacks tar获取ing a specific destination IP/port combination.
Broad attacks tar获取ing mul提示le IP addresses of an IP prefix at the same time.
Advanced TCP Protection can track TCP connections even when they move between Cloudflare 数据中心.
The 特性 offers two 类型s of protection:
SYN Flood Protection: Protects against attacks such as fully randomized SYN and SYN-ACK floods.
Out-of-state TCP Protection: Protects against out-of-state TCP DDoS attacks such as fully randomized ACK floods and RST floods.
Each protection 类型 is configured independently using 规则 and (可选ly) 过滤器s. You should configure at least one 规则 for each 类型 of protection before enabling Advanced TCP Protection.
This system protects against attacks such as fully randomized SYN and SYN-ACK floods. You should configure at least one SYN flood 规则 before enabling Advanced TCP Protection.

In mitigation mode, SYN flood 规则 will challenge 新功能 connection initiation 请求s (SYN, SYN-ACK) if they exceed the configured packet-per-second thresholds. The threshold should be higher than the normal rate of legitimate SYN and SYN-ACK packets that your network receives. Packets below the threshold will not be challenged. Using the rate sensitivity and burst sensitivity 设置 you can increase or decrease the tolerance of SYN and SYN-ACK packets.
For more 信息rmation on the 配置 设置 of SYN flood 规则, refer to 规则 设置.
This system protects against out-of-state TCP DDoS attacks such as fully randomized ACK floods and RST floods. You should configure one out-of-state TCP 规则 before enabling Advanced TCP Protection.

In mitigation mode, out-of-state TCP 规则 will drop out-of-state packets that do not belong to existing (and tracked) TCP connections if their rates exceed the configured thresholds. The threshold should be higher than the normal rate of non SYN or SYN-ACK TCP packets that your network receives. Packets below the threshold will not be evaluated. Using the rate sensitivity and burst sensitivity 设置 you can increase or decrease the tolerance of out-of-state TCP packets.
For more 信息rmation on the 配置 设置 of out-of-state TCP 规则, refer to 规则 设置.

/ddos-protection/.title: Cloudflare DDoS Protection
paragraphs: Detect and mitigate distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks automatically.
Cloudflare automatically detects and mitigates  distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks  via our autonomous DDoS systems.

These systems include mul提示le dynamic mitigation 规则 exposed as DDoS attack protection managed 规则ets. You can customize the mitigation 规则 included in these 规则ets to optimize and tailor the protection to your needs.
Protect against a variety of DDoS attacks across layers 3/4 (network layer) and layer 7 (application layer) of the OSI model.
获取 increased protection against sophisticated DDoS attacks on layer 7 and layers 3/4.
Detect and mitigate sophisticated out-of-state TCP attacks such as randomized and spoofed ACK floods, or SYN and SYN-ACK floods.
Protect against DNS-based DDoS attacks, specifically sophisticated and fully randomized DNS attacks such as random prefix attacks.
部署 custom eBPF packet logic across Cloudflare's network to inspect and mitigate DDoS attacks against UDP-based Layer 7 协议s.
Provides 安全 and acceleration for any TCP or UDP based application.
A network 安全 and 性能 solution that offers DDoS protection, traffic acceleration, and more for on-premise, cloud-hosted, and hybrid networks.
获取 automatic protection from vulnerabilities and the flexibility to 创建 custom 规则.

/ddos-protection/advanced-ddos-systems/o.title: Programmable Flow Protection (测试版)
headings: Safe program and 规则 部署 最佳实践
支持的 BPF helper 函数s and structures
paragraphs: Programmable Flow Protection is a DDoS protection system that protects against DDoS attacks over custom or standardized Layer 7 UDP-based 协议s, such as gaming 协议s, financial services 协议s, VoIP, telecom, and 流ing. In terms of topology, it 支持s both asymmetric and symmetric 配置s, but it will only inspect ingress traffic.

Programmable Flow Protection is currently in closed 测试版 and available as an add-on for the Magic Transit (BYOIP or Cloudflare-leased IPs) service only. If you would like to enable the system, contact your 账户 team or fill out this form.

The Programmable Flow Protection system allows you to write and run your own packet-layer stateful program in C across Cloudflare's global anycast network as extended Berkeley Packet 过滤器 (eBPF) programs 运行中 in the user space. An eBPF program is a packet 过滤器 system that allows a developer to write performant custom networking logic.

Programmable Flow Protection inspects and parses your UDP-based application's 协议s (deep packet inspection) and determines the outcome of the packets based on your program. Using your custom program's logic, you can permit authorized users while 活跃ly blocking attacks.

The system is built on top of the  平台, Cloudflare's stateful mitigation 平台. The Programmable Flow Protection system relies on the DDoS Advanced Protection system's general 设置 to operate. It respects the prefixes that you have selected to 路由 through the Advanced Protection systems, as well as the allow列表. The Advanced DDoS Protection system should be 已启用 for the Programmable Flow Protection system to operate.
While in 测试版, Cloudflare will assist and provide guidance to users to write their own code. Out-of-the-box code snippets (templates) for popular gaming 协议s and VoIP 协议s may be provided later on.
After Programmable Flow Protection has been 已启用 to your 账户, go to Networking > L3/4 DDoS Protection > Advanced Protection in the Cloudflare 控制面板. Within the Programmable Flow Protection tab:
Upload your eBPF program written in C.

The program is validated by the system and stored in your 账户. The API compiles the program, then runs a verifier against the compiled program to enforce memory checks and verify program termination. If the program fails compilation or verification, the Cloudflare 控制面板 will return a detailed 错误 消息.
创建 a 规则
To 观察 the program's behavior, 查询 the  group in GraphQL.
:::注意
The Network 分析 控制面板 does not yet 支持 过滤 by the Programmable Flow Protection 特性. This 特性 will be added soon.
You can 创建 additional 规则 with different 规则 设置 scoped to various regions and Cloudflare locations to change the mode (Mitigation or 监控) to accommodate for your traffic patterns and business 使用场景.
The Programmable Flow Protection system 支持s the Data Localization suite.
:::注意[测试版 函数ality limitations]
For more 信息rmation on 测试版 services, refer to section 2.6 in the Enterprise Terms of Service.

Programmable Flow Protection will only give UDP packets to a BPF program.
In the 示例 snippet below, the program will drop any packet where the IPv6 头部 exists or where the UDP destination port is 66.
It will then check the application 头部 值 in the UDP payload and verify its last byte is a fixed 值 .

The currently 支持的 return 值s are:
The verifier, which runs when you upload a program to the API, will enforce that the program returns only known 值 类型s.
For 参考, the 示例 below is the basic program in its entirety:

The 示例 program below implements a UDP-based challenge-响应 mechanism using helper 函数s to maintain state between packets from the same source IP. This is useful for mitigating DDoS attacks by requiring clients to prove they can receive and respond to challenges before allowing their traffic through.
The challenge mechanism works as follows:
When a packet arrives from an unknown source IP, the program generates a challenge packet containing a random nonce and marks the source IP as "challenged" in the state 表. The 源al packet is dropped.

If a packet arrives from a source IP that has al读取y been challenged, the program checks if the packet contains the correct challenge 响应 (the nonce XORed with a 密钥 值). If the 响应 is correct, the source IP is marked as "verified". If incorrect, the source IP is immediately block列表ed.
Packets from verified source IPs are passed through without further checks.
Include the Cloudflare eBPF 头部 files and define the helper version.
Define constants for the challenge-响应 协议.
The challenge 响应 is computed by XORing the nonce with a 密钥 值. The expiry time determines how long a challenged or verified status remains valid.
Define a structure for challenge packets.
The challenge packet contains the nonce that the client must respond to, and space for the client's 响应.
Define the entry 函数 and parse the packet.
Check the source IP status using .
The status indicates whether this source IP is 新功能, challenged, verified, or block列表ed. The expiry timestamp indicates when the status expires.
Handle verified source IPs.
The Programmable Flow Protection 平台 will drop packets from block列表ed IPs before the program is invoked. There is no need to explicitly handle the block列表ed case.
If the source IP has been verified (passed a previous challenge), allow the packet through.
Check if this is a challenge 响应 from a challenged source IP.

If the source IP was previously challenged, check if the current packet contains a valid challenge 响应. If the 响应 is correct, mark the source IP as verified. If the 响应 is incorrect, block列表 the source IP immediately.
Issue a 新功能 challenge for 新功能 source IPs.
Generate a random nonce, store it in the state 表, 创建 a challenge packet, and send it using .
For 参考, the 示例 below is the complex program in its entirety:
This program demonstrates several 键 concepts:
**State management**: Using , , , and  to track the challenge state for each source IP.
**Challenge emission**: Using  to send a challenge packet back to the client.
**Cryptographic verification**: Using a shared 密钥 to verify that the client correctly responded to the challenge.
**Expiry handling**: Using timestamps to expire stale state entries.
A helper 函数 is a 函数 provided by the Cloudflare 运行时 that a customer program calls.

Helper 函数s are crucial because the BPF Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) only 支持s certain system calls. For safety purposes, Cloudflare will only compile a BPF 对象 file with a predetermined 列表 of known 库 that a program developer cannot modify.
Helper 函数s may be removed or changed. 新功能 helper 函数s may be introduced in the future as well.
The 表 below provides a 列表 of currently 支持的 helper 函数s:
With the exception of , , , , , , , and , all helper 函数s return a  on 成功 and non-zero 值 on failure.
To upload a program, navigate to Networking > L3/4 DDoS protection > Advanced Protection in the Cloudflare 控制面板. Then select the tab titled Programmable Flow Protection.
Under Programs, click the button "Upload 新功能 program." This will prompt you to select a file to upload with your  source code.
The Cloudflare API will receive the source code in the  file, compile it into BPF bytecode, and run the verifier against it.
If compilation or verification fails, the API will return a detailed 错误 消息.
If compilation and verification succeeds, Cloudflare will store the source code and 对象 file to the 账户 and return the program ID.
During the development process, you may find it useful to 更新 the same program (identified by the same program ID) instead of repeatedly creating 新功能 programs as 新功能 资源.
To 更新 the program, select the three dots next to your program. Then, select Overwrite. This will prompt you to choose a file to upload as your  source code.

It is possible to 更新 and overwrite a program that is currently in use by one or more 规则. When doing so, you will be warned that the program is currently 活跃 and will be overwritten. However, if an 活跃 program is being 已更新 with a program that either does not compile or can not be verified, the 更新 will fail and the old program will continue to be in use.
To view all uploaded programs and their 成功 statuses, view the 表 under the section entitled Programs.
To 删除 a program, select the three dots next to the program that you wish to 删除. Then, select 删除.
注意 that you will not be able to 删除 a program that is 参考d in an 活跃 规则.
注意 that programs that have a "已失败" status (meaning they 已失败 to compile or pass verification) will be automatically and permanently 删除d after 30 days of inactivity.
To 创建 a 规则, go to Networking > L3/4 DDoS protection > Advanced Protection in the Cloudflare 控制面板. Then, select Programmable Flow Protection.
Under 规则, select 创建 规则. Fill out the corresponding 字段s of your 新功能 规则.
To view 规则 and their associated 规则 IDs, use the following  端点.
To 更新 an existing 规则, use the following  端点.
You can modify the mode, scope, and 表达式 of an existing 规则. The 示例 below modifies an existing 规则 to make it run in  mode.
To 删除 an existing 规则, use the following  端点.
This does not 删除 the 参考d program, but 删除s the directive to execute the program.
To 删除 all 规则 for an 账户, use the following  端点.
This does not 删除 the 参考d programs, but 删除s the directive to execute all 参考d programs.
This API 端点 调试s a program by intaking:
A local path to the input PCAP file provided as 请求ed data in binary format. The input PCAP file has a maximum size limit of 5 MB and will be rejected if it is too large.
An IP offset 值 provided as a 查询 parameter. This is the 数字 of bytes that the IP 头部 is offset by in each packet of the input PCAP file.
For 示例, if the PCAP file captures Ethernet packets, the IP offset 值 would be 14\. This 端点 assumes that all packets in a PCAP have the same IP offset 值 and will otherwise parse packets incorrectly.
The program ID provided in the 请求 path.

This 端点 runs the 参考d BPF program against the input PCAP and outputs a 新功能 annotated PCAP file. The output PCAP file will contain the exact same packets as the input PCAP file, and will also include the program verdict annotated in the Packet Comment section of each packet.
The Packet Comment annotation may contain:
Program return 值: .
correlates to  and  correlates to .
Ignored, if it is not UDP.

You will want to safely 部署 and 测试 programs without impacting existing production traffic. An initial 部署 approach could be to set a global scoped 规则 to  and set a colo or region level scoped 规则 to  with a 过滤器 表达式 only acting on some subset of IP traffic.

Each Cloudflare region or colo will apply the most granular 规则. So, in the scenario described above, the colos or regions specified in the  规则 will execute the developer program in  mode, while every other Cloudflare location will not execute the program at all. The  规则 would only execute on traffic that matches the 过滤器 表达式.
Then, after verifying the correct behavior with Network 分析, you can 更新 and expand the  规则's scope and 过滤器 表达式. 事件ually, you can 删除 the  and  规则 and apply a global  规则.
Using the  字段 to limit programs to a subset of IPs or prefixes and the  字段 to dictate whether a program actually drops packets ensures a program's safety and granularity upon rollout.
Traffic flowing through Programmable Flow Protection can be found in the Network 分析 控制面板.
You can use the Cloudflare GraphQL API to granularly 查询 traffic data in the  group.
For 示例, the curl command below executes a 查询 that shows the total sum of bits and packets that went through Programmable Flow Protection in a time frame.
and  must be changed to correlate to the user's 账户.
Cloudflare recommends using a client like GraphQL to explore all the dimensions and 字段s available for 查询ing in .

/ddos-protection/advanced-ddos-systems/o.title: Advanced DNS Protection
headings: No data about Advanced DNS Protection in Network 分析
paragraphs: Cloudflare's Advanced DNS Protection works by first learning your traffic patterns and forming a baseline of the 类型 of DNS queries you normally receive. Later, the system will be able to distinguish between legitimate and malicious queries, protecting your DNS infrastructure without impacting legitimate traffic.
Currently, the protection system only analyzes DNS over UDP (it does not include DNS over TCP).
The Network 分析 控制面板 will display system-specific 分析 for Advanced DNS Protection in the DNS protection tab, including the queried 域名s and 记录 类型s.
Cloudflare collects DNS-相关 data such as 查询 类型 (for 示例,  记录) and the queried 域名s. For details, refer to Data collection.
:::注意

Currently, to disable this data collection you must remove your prefixes either in the Cloudflare 控制面板 or through the 删除 a prefix API operation. However, this procedure will remove the prefixes from both Advanced DNS Protection and Advanced TCP Protection.
If you cannot find any data 相关 to Advanced DNS Protection in the DNS Protection tab of Network 分析, it could be because one of these reasons:
You did not add your prefixes to Advanced L3/4 DDoS Protection.
账户s that existed before January 2025 were not automatically provisioned. If you onboarded before January 2025, Advanced DNS Protection may not have been 已启用 for your 账户.
You do not have any DNS over UDP traffic.
Advanced DNS Protection can protect you against volumetric DNS DDoS attacks. To perform DNS 缓存, 代理ing, and 配置, use the Cloudflare DNS 防火墙.
Currently, Advanced DNS Protection is not available for DNS 防火墙.

/ddos-protection/reference/.title: 参考

/ddos-protection/get-started/.title: 快速开始
headings: Free, Pro, and 商业计划s
Adjust the provided DDoS 规则
1. Configure 规则et 操作s to Log
3. Customize managed 规则et 规则
4. Switch 规则et 操作s back to the 默认
paragraphs: The DDoS Attack Protection managed 规则ets provided by Cloudflare are 已启用 by 默认 on 区域s onboarded to Cloudflare, IP applications onboarded to Spectrum, and IP Prefixes onboarded to Magic Transit.
In some situations, the 默认 protection offered by DDoS 规则 may need to be fine-tuned to your specific situation. You may also want to configure additional protection using other Cloudflare products.

If one or more DDoS 规则 provided by Cloudflare affects legitimate traffic, you can adjust them so that they do not perform any mitigation 操作 against this kind of traffic. Follow the 步骤s in handling a false positive to reduce the sensitivity level of one or more DDoS 规则 and allow incoming legitimate traffic.
To configure additional protection against DDoS attacks, refer to the 相关 Cloudflare products 列表ed in Network-layer DDoS Attack Protection and HTTP DDoS Attack Protection.

Cloudflare's DDoS protection systems automatically detect and mitigate DDoS attacks. Additionally, the systems may flag suspiciously-looking incoming traffic from legacy applications, Internet services, or faulty client applications as malicious and apply mitigation 操作s. If the traffic is in fact legitimate, the mitigation 操作s can cause service disruptions and outages in your Internet properties.
To pr事件 this situation, Cloudflare recommends that you perform these 步骤s to 快速开始:
Set the 规则et 操作s for all the DDoS Attack Protection managed 规则ets to _Log_.
Analyze the flagged traffic.
Adjust the sensitivity or 操作 of individual managed 规则et 规则, if 必需.
Switch 规则et 操作s from _Log_ back to the 默认.
You must have one of the following:
A 区域 onboarded to Cloudflare but without 已更新 DNS 记录s.
An IP application onboarded to Spectrum.
An IP Prefix onboarded to Magic Transit.
:::注意
The _Log_ 操作 is only available to Enterprise customers.
Configure all the 规则 in the HTTP DDoS Attack Protection managed 规则et, setting their 操作 to _Log_.
Configure all the 规则 in the Network-layer DDoS Attack Protection managed 规则et, setting the 操作 to _Log_.
Alternatively, if you are using the API, define an override at the 规则et level to set the 操作 of all managed 规则et 规则 to  by following these instructions:
Configure an override for the HTTP DDoS Attack Protection managed 规则et
Configure an override for the Network-layer DDoS Attack Protection managed 规则et
Go to your 分析 控制面板 (the exact 控制面板 depends on your Cloudflare services).
Apply one or more 过滤器s, if 必需, and identify any 规则 that would have blocked legitimate traffic if _Log_ mode were 已禁用. Take 注意 of the 规则 IDs.
Customize the specific managed 规则et 规则 you identified, changing their sensitivity or their 操作, using the Cloudflare 控制面板 or using the API.
If you are using the Cloudflare 控制面板, refer to:
Configure HTTP DDoS Attack Protection in the 控制面板
Configure Network-layer DDoS Attack Protection in the 控制面板
If you are using the API, refer to:
Configure HTTP DDoS Attack Protection via API
Configure Network-layer DDoS Attack Protection via API
When using the API, ensure that you add any 必需 规则 overrides without removing the 规则et override you configured in 步骤 1.
Revert the change you did in 步骤 1, changing the 操作 of each managed 规则et 规则 back to _默认_ in 规则et 操作.

Alternatively, if you are using the API, remove the override you previously configured at the 规则et level for each managed 规则et. Ensure that you only remove the 规则et override and not any of the 规则 overrides you may have configured in 步骤 3.

/ddos-protection/managed-rulesets/adapti.title: Adaptive DDoS Protection
/ddos-protection/managed-rulesets/adapti.description: Explore Cloudflare's Adaptive DDoS Protection, which learns traffic patterns to defend against sophisticated DDoS attacks on layers 3/4 and 7.
paragraphs: Adaptive DDoS Protection learns your unique traffic patterns and adapts to them to provide better protection against sophisticated DDoS attacks on layer 7 and layers 3/4, de待处理 on your 订阅d Cloudflare services.
Adaptive DDoS Protection provides the following 类型s of protection:
**Adaptive DDoS Protection for 源s**: Detects and mitigates traffic that deviates from your site's 源 错误 profile.

**Adaptive DDoS Protection for User-Agents**: Detects and mitigates traffic that deviates from the top User Agents seen by Cloudflare on the network. The User Agent profile is built from the entire Cloudflare network and not only from the customer's 区域.

**Adaptive DDoS Protection for Locations**: Detects and mitigates traffic that deviates from your site's geo-distribution profile. The profile is calculated from the rate for every client country and region, using the rates from the past seven days.
**Adaptive DDoS Protection for 协议s**: Detects and mitigates traffic that deviates from your traffic's IP 协议 profile. The profile is calculated as a global rate for each of your prefixes.
Cloudflare Adaptive DDoS Protection is available to Enterprise customers according to the following 表:
1  _WAF/CDN customers on the 企业计划 with the Advanced DDoS
Protection subscription._
2  _Magic Transit and Spectrum BYOIP customers on an 企业计划._

Adaptive DDoS Protection 创建s a traffic profile by looking at the maximum rates of traffic every day, for the past seven days. These profiles are recalculated every day, keeping the seven-day time window. Adaptive DDoS Protection stores the maximal traffic rates seen for every predefined dimension 值 (the profiling dimension varies for each 规则). Every profile uses one dimension, such as the source country of the 请求, the user agent, and the IP 协议. Incoming traffic that deviates from your profile may be malicious.

To eliminate outliers, rate calculations only consider the 95th percentile rates (discarding the top 5% of the highest rates). Cloudflare requires a minimum amount of 请求s per second (rps) to 构建 traffic profiles. HTTP Adaptive DDoS Protection 规则 also take into 账户 Cloudflare's Machine Learning (ML) models to identify traffic that is likely automated.
Cloudflare may change the logic of these protection 规则 from time to time to improve them.
:::注意

HTTP Adaptive DDoS Protection 规则 calculate the traffic profile at the 区域-level. Therefore, the HTTP Adaptive 规则 may be ineffective for an SSL for SaaS 区域 shared by many of your customers' custom 主机名s. The traffic profile would be 创建d based on the varied and aggregated traffic of all of the various custom 主机名s. It will not be accurate for an individual customer's 主机名.
To view traffic flagged by HTTP Adaptive DDoS Protection 规则:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **安全 分析** page.
Go to **事件**.
过滤器 by  and by 规则 ID.
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **安全** > **事件**.
To view traffic flagged by L3/4 Adaptive DDoS Protection 规则:
过滤器 by 规则 ID.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Network 分析** page.
You may also obtain 信息rmation about flagged traffic through Logpush or the GraphQL API.
To determine if an adaptive 规则 fits your traffic in a way that will only mitigate attack traffic and will not cause false positives, review the traffic that is _Logged_ by the adaptive 规则.

You may not see any traffic matching the adaptive 规则. This can be because there was no deviation from your traffic profile, so you may want to increase the time range and look for any _Logged_ traffic. Another reason why you may not see _Logged_ traffic by the adaptive 规则 is that there was not sufficient traffic volume to generate a traffic profile for your 区域.

If you do see traffic that was _Logged_ by the adaptive 规则, use the 控制面板 to determine if the traffic matches the characteristics of legitimate users or that of attack traffic. As each Internet property is unique, understanding if the traffic is legitimate requires your understanding of how your legitimate traffic looks. For 示例, the user agent, source country, 头部, 查询 字符串 for HTTP 请求s, and 协议s and ports for L3/4 traffic.
In cases where you are certain that the 规则 is only flagging attack traffic, you should consider creating an override and enabling that 规则 with a Managed Challenge or  操作.

In cases where you see legitimate traffic being flagged, you should lower the sensitivity level of the 规则 and 观察 the flagged traffic. You can continue reducing the sensitivity level until you reach a point where legitimate traffic is not flagged. Then, you should 创建 an override to enable the 规则 with a mitigation 操作.
If the 规则 is still flagging legitimate traffic you can consider using the 表达式 过滤器s to 条件 the 规则 to exclude certain 类型s of traffic.

The 默认 规则 操作 for  with a sensitivity set to  will only show packets or 请求s with suspected attack traffic over internal  thresholds in your 日志. For instance, if you set the threshold to  or , then only packets over those thresholds will be logged.
You can adjust the 操作 and sensitivity of the Adaptive DDoS Protection 规则. The 默认 操作 is _Log_. Use this 操作 to first 观察 what traffic is flagged before deciding on a mitigation 操作.
To configure a 规则, refer to the instructions in the following pages:
Configure HTTP DDoS Attack Protection in the 控制面板 (for L7 规则)
Configure Network-layer DDoS Attack Protection in the 控制面板 (for L3/4 规则)
For more 信息rmation on the available 配置 参数, refer to the following pages:
For the (L7) DDoS protection 规则 for 源s, User-Agents, and Locations:
HTTP DDoS Attack Protection 参数
For the (L3/4) DDoS protection 规则 for 协议s:
Network-layer DDoS Attack Protection 参数

/ddos-protection/managed-rulesets/.title: Managed 规则ets
headings: Pro活跃 false positive detection for 新功能 规则
paragraphs: The DDoS Attack Protection managed 规则ets provide comprehensive protection against a variety of DDoS attacks across L3/4 (network layer) and L7 (application layer) of the OSI model.
The available managed 规则ets are:
**HTTP DDoS Attack Protection**
This 规则et includes 规则 to detect and mitigate DDoS attacks over HTTP and HTTPS.
**Network-layer DDoS Attack Protection**
This 规则et includes 规则 to detect and mitigate DDoS attacks on L3/4 of the OSI model such as UDP floods, SYN-ACK reflection attacks, SYN Floods, and DNS floods.
:::注意
Only available on Business and 企业计划s.
When Cloudflare 创建s a 新功能 managed 规则, we check the 规则 impact against the traffic of Business and Enterprise 区域s while the 规则 is not blocking traffic yet.

If a false positive is detected, we pro活跃ly reach out to the affected customers and help them make 配置 changes (for 示例, to lower the sensitivity level of the 新功能 规则) before the 规则 starts mitigating traffic. This pr事件 the 新功能 规则 from causing service disruptions and outages to your Internet properties.

/dmarc-management/.title: Cloudflare DMARC Management
paragraphs: Stop brand impersonation.
When someone receives an email that claims to be from your 域名, email servers check whether that 消息 is authentic. Three DNS-based mechanisms handle this verification:
**SPF (Sender Policy Framework)** confirms the email was sent from an IP address or 域名 your 域名 authorizes.
**DKIM (域名键s Identified Mail)** authenticates the sender's 域名 and verifies the email content was not altered in transit, using a cryptographic signature.
**DMARC (域名-based 消息 身份验证 Reporting and Conformance)** ties SPF and DKIM to获取her and tells receiving servers what to do when a check fails (for 示例, reject the email, quarantine it, or take no 操作).

Cloudflare DMARC Management helps you track every source that is sending emails from your 域名 and review DMARC 报告 for each source. These 报告 show whether 消息s sent from your 域名 are passing SPF, DKIM, and DMARC checks — so you can identify unauthorized senders and protect your 域名 from being used in phishing or spoofing attacks.
:::注意
DMARC Management is available to all Cloudflare customers with Cloudflare DNS.
Protect your email inbox with Email 安全.
Fast, resilient and easy-to-manage DNS service.

/dns/cname-flattening/.title: CNAME flattening
paragraphs: CNAME flattening speeds up CNAME resolution and allows you to use a CNAME 记录 at your  区域 apex  ().
:::注意
This 函数ality is also what allows you to use a root 自定义域名 with a Cloudflare Pages site.

With CNAME flattening, Cloudflare finds the IP address that a CNAME points to. This process could involve a single lookup or mul提示le (if your CNAME points to another CNAME). Cloudflare then returns the final IP address instead of a CNAME 记录, helping DNS queries resolve faster.
For more details on the 步骤s involved in CNAME flattening, review the CNAME flattening diagram and refer to the Cloudflare blog post.
For 信息rmation about CNAME flattening in Internal DNS, refer to internal DNS 记录s.
CNAME flattening happens by 默认 in some cases. Refer to 设置 for details.
CNAME to a different Cloudflare 账户 is prohibited and will result in 错误 1014: CNAME Cross-User Banned

/dns/dnssec/.title: DNSSEC
headings: 2. Add DS 记录 to your registrar
paragraphs: When you enable DNSSEC, Cloudflare signs your 区域, publishes your public signing 键s, and generates your DS 记录.
If you are using Cloudflare as your Secondary DNS provider and want to configure DNSSEC on your secondary 区域(s), you have three options de待处理 on your 设置.

Contact your registrar to ask for DNSSEC with modern 加密.

Simplify private network management with Cloudflare DNS for your internal
资源.

Manage DNS 记录s that should only be accessible within your private network. Internal DNS 区域s and views 对 up with Gateway 解析器 policies so that you can control how a DNS 查询 should be responded to according to 查询 context, such as 查询 source IP.
You can use different connectivity options to on-ramp your traffic to Cloudflare. Then, Cloudflare Gateway 解析器 acts as an interface between the DNS client and internal DNS 区域s.
Internal DNS 区域s do not 获取 assigned Cloudflare 名称服务器s and can only be queried via Cloudflare Gateway 解析器.

Internal DNS 区域s are grouped into DNS views, which are selected by the 解析器 policy you define. Views are usually logical groupings relevant to your organization, such as different geographical locations.

Internal DNS 区域s contain the  DNS 记录s  that should be used to resolve an internal DNS 查询. Also, if no internal 记录 is found within a matching internal 区域, Cloudflare will check if the matching internal 区域 is referencing another internal 区域.

In this 示例, a 查询 for  路由d to view ID 111 would go to 区域 600, which presents the longest matching 区域 name (). 区域 600 does not contain a 记录 for  but, if it is referencing 区域 601, Cloudflare will then look for the queried 记录 within the 参考 区域.
设置 policies to inspect DNS, Network, HTTP, and Egress traffic.
Improve 安全 and 性能 for your entire corporate networking,
reducing cost and operation complexity.

/dns/get-started/.title: 快速开始
paragraphs: You can use Cloudflare DNS with a variety of 设置s. For an 概述 of what these 设置s are and an 简介 to specific DNS terminology, refer to Concepts.
In the most common 设置 (full), you add your 域名, import your DNS 记录s, and 更新 your 名称服务器s to make Cloudflare your primary authoritative DNS provider.
:::注意
Make sure to review your DNS 记录s before updating your 名称服务器s. If you activate your 域名 on Cloudflare _without_ setting up the correct DNS 记录s, your 域名 may not be reachable.
Once the 设置 is completed:
You manage DNS 记录s through the Cloudflare 控制面板 or API. This is how you control which 资源 are available on the apex 域名 () or specific 子域名s () of your website, as well as control other 配置s.
Cloudflare responds to all DNS queries for your 主机名s and your DNS 记录s are propagated across the Cloudflare 全球网络, speeding up your 域名.
The following links introduce 重要 concepts and will 指南 you through 操作s you may need to take while having your website or application on Cloudflare.
DNS 记录s: DNS 记录s contain 信息rmation about your 域名 and are used to make your website or application available to visitors and other web services.

名称服务器s: In the context of Cloudflare DNS, 名称服务器s refer to authoritative 名称服务器s. When a 名称服务器 is authoritative for , it means that DNS 解析器s will consider 响应s from this 名称服务器 when a user tries to access .

代理 status: 代理 status affects how Cloudflare treats incoming HTTP/S 请求s to A, AAAA, and CNAME 记录s. When a 记录 is proxied, Cloudflare responds with anycast IPs, which speeds up and protects HTTP/S traffic with our cache/CDN, DDoS protection, WAF, and more.
How Cloudflare works: An 概述 of how Cloudflare works as a DNS provider and as a 反向代理.
DNS 分析: An 概述 of the different data sources and insights you can 获取 when using Cloudflare DNS.
故障排除: A full 资源 列表 for when something is not working.

/dns/.title: Cloudflare DNS
paragraphs: Leverage Cloudflare's 全球网络 to deliver excellent 性能 and reliability to your 域名.

Cloudflare DNS is a fast, resilient, and easy-to-manage authoritative DNS service. It delivers excellent 性能 and reliability to your 域名 while also protecting your business from DDoS attacks and 路由 leaks and hijacking. To know where to begin, refer to 快速开始.
Enterprise customers can also use Cloudflare DNS for their private network with Internal DNS (测试版).
DNS 记录s make 资源 available on your 域名, and allow you to configure services such as email.
DNS 安全 扩展 (DNSSEC) adds cryptographic signatures to your DNS 记录s, pr事件ing anyone else from 重定向ing traffic intended for your 域名.
Cloudflare also 支持s Multi-signer DNSSEC.
CNAME flattening delivers better 性能 and allows you to add a CNAME 记录 at your apex 域名 (). Paid 账户s can choose to flatten all CNAME 记录s on their 域名.
Refer to DNS 特性 and 可用性 for a complete 列表 of 特性 and their 可用性 according to different Cloudflare plans.
Before you can start using Cloudflare DNS you must first have a 域名. Buy and re新功能 your 域名 at cost with Cloudflare Registrar.
Cloudflare DNS focuses on businesses and their 域名 administration. If you are a 消费者 and want a more private way to browse the Internet, check out 1.1.1.1, Cloudflare's public DNS 解析器.

/dns/manage-dns-records/how-to/create-dn.title: Manage DNS 记录s
headings: 更新 an 源 IP address
paragraphs: Consider the sections below for 步骤-by-步骤 instructions on managing DNS 记录s at Cloudflare.
To better understand what DNS 记录s are, refer to 概述. For context around common 记录s you want to review when 快速开始 at Cloudflare, refer to review DNS 记录s.
:::注意
If your 域名 is added to Cloudflare by a hosting partner, manage your DNS 记录s via the hosting partner.
To 创建 a DNS 记录 in the 控制面板:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **DNS 记录s** page.
Select **Add 记录**.
Choose a 记录 **类型**.
Complete the 必需 字段s, which vary per 记录. Particularly 重要 字段s (for some 记录s) include:
**代理 status**: For , , and  记录s, decide whether 主机名 traffic is  proxied through Cloudflare .
**TTL**: Short for _Time to Live_, this 字段 controls how long each 记录 is valid and — as a result — how long it takes for 记录 更新s to reach your end users.
**Comment** and **Tag**: 记录 attributes meant for your 参考.
Select **Save**.
To 创建 记录s with the API, use a POST 请求. For 字段 definitions, select a 记录 类型 under the 请求 正文 specification.
For specific API 示例, refer to DNS 记录 类型s.
To edit DNS 记录s in the 控制面板:
On a specific 记录, select **Edit**.
Make any necessary changes.
To 更新 part of a 记录 with the API, use a PATCH 请求. If you want to overwrite the entire existing 记录, use a PUT 请求.
To 删除 DNS 记录s in the 控制面板:
Select **删除**.
Select **删除** again to confirm.
To 删除 记录s with the API, use a 删除 请求.
If your hosting provider changes or your 源 IP address changes, 更新 the Content 值 of the relevant DNS 记录s (usually  or  记录s).
If you are not sure which IP address to use, refer to your hosting provider's 文档.
If you need a placeholder address for an 源less 设置 (also referred to as parked 域名 or 重定向-only), you can use the reserved IPv6 address  or the reserved IPv4 address  in a  proxied  DNS 记录.
This allows you to 路由 请求s using products such as 重定向 规则, Page 规则, or Workers.

/dns/reference/all-features/.title: 特性 and plans
/dns/reference/all-features/.description: Review 信息rmation on all Cloudflare DNS 特性 and their 可用性.
paragraphs: Cloudflare provides the following 特性 for different plans.

/durable-objects/api/alarms/.title: Alarms
headings: Scheduling mul提示le 事件 with a single alarm
paragraphs: Durable Objects alarms allow you to schedule the Durable 对象 to be woken up at a time in the future. When the alarm's 定时 time comes, the  处理程序 method will be called. Alarms are modified using the  Storage API , and alarm operations follow the same 规则 as other storage operations.
Notably:
Each Durable 对象 is able to schedule a single alarm at a time by calling .
Alarms have guaranteed at-least-once 执行 and are retried automatically when the  处理程序 throws.
重试 are performed using exponential backoff starting at a 2 second delay from the first failure with up to 6 重试 allowed.
:::注意[How are alarms different from Cron 触发器s?]
Alarms are more fine grained than Cron 触发器s. A Worker can have up to three Cron 触发器s configured at once, but it can have an unlimited amount of Durable Objects, each of which can have an alarm set.

Alarms are directly 定时 from within your Durable 对象. Cron 触发器s, on the other hand, are not programmatic. Cron 触发器s execute based on their schedules, which have to be configured through the Cloudflare 控制面板 or API.

Alarms can be used to 构建 distributed primitives, like queues or batching of work atop Durable Objects. Alarms also provide a mechanism to guarantee that operations within a Durable 对象 will complete without relying on incoming 请求s to keep the Durable 对象 alive. For a complete 示例, refer to Use the Alarms API.

Although each Durable 对象 can only have one alarm set at a time, you can manage many 定时 and recurring 事件 by storing your 事件 schedule in storage and having the  处理程序 process due 事件, then reschedule itself for the next one.
获取Alarm() :
If there is an alarm set, then return the currently set alarm time as the 数字 of milliseconds elapsed since the UNIX epoch. Otherwise, return .
If  is called while an [](/durable-对象s/api/alarms/#alarm) is al读取y 运行中, it returns  unless  has also been called since the alarm 处理程序 started 运行中.
setAlarm(定时TimeMs  )  :
Set the time for the alarm to run. Specify the time as the 数字 of milliseconds elapsed since the UNIX epoch.
If you call  when there is al读取y one 定时, it will override the existing alarm.
:::注意[Calling  inside the constructor]
If you wish to call  inside the constructor of a Durable 对象, ensure that you are first checking whether an alarm has al读取y been set.

This is due to the fact that, if the Durable 对象 wakes up after being 不活跃, the constructor is invoked before the  处理程序. Therefore, if the constructor calls , it could interfere with the next alarm which has al读取y been set.
Unset the alarm if there is a currently set alarm.
Calling  inside the  处理程序 may pr事件 重试 on a best-effort basis, but is not guaranteed.
alarm(alarm信息  ) :
Called by the system when a 定时 alarm time is reached.
The 可选 parameter  对象 has two properties:
: The 数字 of times this alarm 事件 has been retried.
: A 布尔值 值 to indicate if the alarm has been retried. This 值 is  if this alarm 事件 is a 重试.
Only one instance of  will ever run at a given time per Durable 对象 instance.

The  处理程序 has guaranteed at-least-once 执行 and will be retried upon failure using exponential backoff, starting at 2 second delays for up to 6 重试. This only applies to the most recent  call. 重试 will be performed if the method fails with an uncaught exception.
This method can be .
:::注意[Catching exceptions in alarm 处理程序s]

Because alarms are only retried up to 6 times on 错误, it's recommended to catch any exceptions inside your  处理程序 and schedule a 新功能 alarm before returning if you want to make sure your alarm 处理程序 will be retried indefinitely. Otherwise, a sufficiently long outage in a 下游 service that you depend on or a bug in your code that goes unfixed for hours can exhaust the limited 数字 of 重试, causing the alarm to not be re-run in the future until the next time you call .
This 示例 shows how to both set alarms with the  method and handle alarms with the  处理程序 within your Durable 对象.
The  处理程序 will be called once every time an alarm fires.
If an unexpected 错误 terminates the Durable 对象, the  处理程序 may be re-instantiated on another machine.
Following a short delay, the  处理程序 will run from the beginning on the other machine.

paragraphs: Durable Objects  are a
powerful compute API that provides a compute with storage 构建ing block. Each
Durable 对象 has its own private, 事务al, and strongly consistent
storage. Durable Objects
Storage API  provides
access to a Durable 对象's attached storage.

A Durable 对象's in-memory state is preserved as long as the Durable 对象 is not evicted from memory. 不活跃 Durable Objects with no incoming 请求 traffic can be evicted. There are normal operations like code 部署s that 触发器 Durable Objects to restart and lose their in-memory state. For these reasons, you should use Storage API to persist state durably on disk that needs to survive eviction or restart of Durable Objects.
Storage API 方法 are available on  parameter passed to the Durable 对象 constructor. Storage API has several 方法, including SQL, point-in-time recovery (PITR), 键-值 (KV), and alarm APIs.
Only Durable 对象 classes with a SQLite storage backend can access SQL API.
Use  on the 迁移 in your Worker's Wrangler file:
SQL API is available on  parameter passed to the Durable 对象 constructor.
SQLite-backed Durable Objects also offer point-in-time recovery API, which uses  bookmarks  to allow you to 恢复 a Durable 对象's embedded SQLite 数据库 to any point in time in the past 30 days.

A common pattern is to initialize a Durable 对象 from persistent storage and set instance 变量s the first time it is accessed. Since future accesses are 路由d to the same Durable 对象, it is then possible to return any initialized 值s without making further calls to persistent storage.

A Durable 对象 fully ceases to exist if, when it shuts down, its storage is empty. If you never write to a Durable 对象's storage at all (including setting  alarms ), then storage remains empty, and so the Durable 对象 will no longer exist once it shuts down.

However if you ever write using Storage API, including setting alarms, then you must explicitly call [](/durable-对象s/api/sqlite-storage-api/#删除all) to empty storage and [](/durable-对象s/api/sqlite-storage-api/#删除alarm) if you've configured an alarm. It is not sufficient to simply 删除 the specific data that you wrote, such as deleting a 键 or dropping a 表, as some metadata may remain. The only way to remove all storage is to call . Calling  ensures that a Durable 对象 will not be billed for storage.
You can use TypeScript 类型 参数 to provide a 类型 for your results, allowing you to benefit from 类型 hints and checks when iterating over the results of a 查询.
:::注意
Providing a 类型 parameter does _not_ validate that the 查询 result matches your 类型 definition. In TypeScript, properties (字段s) that do not exist in your result 类型 will be silently dropped.
Your 类型 must conform to the shape of a TypeScript 记录 类型 representing the name () of the column and the 类型 of the column. The column 类型 must be a valid : one of .
