Creating 索引es for your most queried 表s and 过滤器ed columns reduces how much data is scanned and improves 查询 性能 at the same time. If you have a 读取-heavy workload (most common), this can be particularly advantageous. /durable-objects/best-practices/websocke.title: Use WebSockets
headings: Batch 消息s to reduce overhead
paragraphs: Tabs,
TabItem,

Durable Objects can act as WebSocket servers that connect thousands of clients per instance. You can also use WebSockets as a client to connect to other servers or Durable Objects.
Two WebSocket APIs are available:
**Hibernation WebSocket API** - Allows the Durable 对象 to hibernate without disconnecting clients when idle. **(recommended)**
**Web Standard WebSocket API** - Uses the familiar  事件 pattern.
WebSockets are long-lived TCP connections that enable bi-directional, real-time communication between client and server.
键 characteristics:
Both Workers and Durable Objects can act as WebSocket 端点 (client or server)
WebSocket 会话s are long-lived, making Durable Objects ideal for accepting connections
A single Durable 对象 instance can coordinate between mul提示le clients (for 示例, chat rooms or mul提示layer games)
Refer to Cloudflare Edge Chat Demo for an 示例 of using Durable Objects with WebSockets.
The Hibernation WebSocket API reduces costs by allowing Durable Objects to sleep when idle:
Clients remain connected while the Durable 对象 is not in memory
Billable Duration (GB-s) charges do not accrue during hibernation
When a 消息 arrives, the Durable 对象 wakes up automatically
The Hibernation WebSocket API extends the Web Standard WebSocket API to reduce costs during periods of inactivity.
When a Durable 对象 receives no 事件 (such as alarms or 消息s) for a short period, it is evicted from memory. During hibernation:
WebSocket clients remain connected to the Cloudflare network
In-memory state is reset
When an 事件 arrives, the Durable 对象 is re-initialized and its  runs
To 恢复 state after hibernation, use [](#websocketserializeattachment) and [](#websocketdeserializeattachment) to persist data with each WebSocket connection.
Refer to Lifecycle of a Durable 对象 for more 信息rmation.
To use WebSockets with Durable Objects:
代理 the 请求 from the Worker to the Durable 对象
Call [](/durable-对象s/api/state/#acceptwebsocket) to accept the server side connection
Define 处理程序 方法 on the Durable 对象 class for relevant 事件
If an 事件 occurs for a hibernated Durable 对象, the 运行时 re-initializes it by calling the constructor. Minimize work in the constructor when using hibernation.
A full 示例 is available in 构建 a WebSocket server with WebSocket Hibernation.
:::注意[Local development 支持]

Prior to  and Miniflare , WebSockets did not hibernate in local development. Hiberna表 WebSocket 事件 like [](/durable-对象s/api/base/#websocket消息) are still delivered. However, the Durable 对象 is never evicted from memory.
The Cloudflare 运行时 automatically handles WebSocket 协议 ping frames:
Incoming ping frames receive automatic pong 响应s
Ping/pong handling does not interrupt hibernation
The  处理程序 is not called for control frames
This behavior keeps connections alive without waking the Durable 对象.

Each WebSocket 消息 incurs processing overhead from context switches between the JavaScript 运行时 and the underlying system. Sending many small 消息s can overwhelm a single Durable 对象. This happens even if the total data volume is small.
To maximize 吞吐量:
**Batch mul提示le logical 消息s** into a single WebSocket frame
**Use a simple envelope format** to pack and unpack batched 消息s
**Tar获取 fewer, larger 消息s** rather than many small ones

WebSocket 读取s require context switches between the kernel and JavaScript 运行时. Each individual 消息 触发器s this overhead. Batching 10-100 logical 消息s into a single WebSocket frame reduces context switches proportionally.
For high-frequency data like sensor 读取ings or game state 更新s, use time-based or count-based batching. Batch every 50-100ms or every 50-100 消息s, whichever comes first.
:::注意
Hibernation is only 支持的 when a Durable 对象 acts as a WebSocket server. Outgoing WebSockets do not hibernate.
事件 such as alarms, incoming 请求s, and 定时 callbacks pr事件 hibernation. This includes  and  usage. 阅读更多 about when a Durable 对象 incurs duration charges.
The following 方法 are available on the Hibernation WebSocket API. Use them to persist and 恢复 state before and after hibernation.
serializeAttachment(值  )
Keeps a copy of  associated with the WebSocket connection.
键 behaviors:
Serialized attachments persist through hibernation as long as the WebSocket remains healthy
If either side closes the connection, attachments are lost
Modifications to  after calling this method are not retained unless you call it again
The  can be any 类型 支持的 by the structured clone algorithm
Maximum serialized size is 2,048 bytes
For larger 值s or data that must persist beyond WebSocket lifetime, use the Storage API and store the corresponding 键 as an attachment.
Retrieves the most recent 值 passed to , or  if none exists.
Use  and  to persist per-connection state across hibernation:

JavaScript is a single-th读取ed and 事件-driven programming language. This means that JavaScript 运行时s, by 默认, allow 请求s to interleave with each other which can lead to 并发 bugs. The Durable Objects 运行时 uses a combination of  input gates  and  output gates  to avoid this 类型 of 并发 bug when performing storage operations. 了解更多 in our blog post.
The  interface encapsulates 方法 that modify the SQLite 数据库 embedded within a Durable 对象. The  interface is accessible via the  property of  class.

The column names of the 查询 in the order they appear in each row 数组 returned by the  iterator.

Any numeric 值 in a column is affected by JavaScript's 52-bit precision for 数字s. If you store a very large 数字 (in ), then retrieve the same 值, the returned 值 may be less precise than your 源al 数字.
:::注意[SQL 事务s]

注意 that  cannot execute 事务-相关 statements like  or . Instead, use the [](/durable-对象s/api/sqlite-storage-api/#事务) or [](/durable-对象s/api/sqlite-storage-api/#事务sync) APIs to start a 事务, and then execute SQL queries in your callback.
The current SQLite 数据库 size in bytes.

For SQLite-backed Durable Objects, the following point-in-time-recovery (PITR) API 方法 are available to 恢复 a Durable 对象's embedded SQLite 数据库 to any point in time in the past 30 days. These 方法 apply to the entire SQLite 数据库 contents, including both the 对象's stored SQL data and stored 键-值 data using the 键-值  API. The PITR API is not 支持的 in local development because a durable log of data changes is not stored locally.

The PITR API represents points in time using 'bookmarks'. A bookmark is a mostly 内测版numeric 字符串 like . Bookmarks are designed to be lexically comparable: a bookmark representing an earlier point in time compares less than one representing a later point, using regular 字符串 comparison.
ctx.storage.获取CurrentBookmark() :  " />
Returns a bookmark representing the current point in time in the 对象's history.
ctx.storage.获取BookmarkForTime(timestamp:  ) :  " />
Returns a bookmark representing approximately the given point in time, which must be within the last 30 days. If the timestamp is represented as a 数字, it is converted to a date as if using .
ctx.storage.onNext会话恢复Bookmark(bookmark:  ) :  " />

Configures the Durable 对象 so that the next time it restarts, it should 恢复 its storage to exactly match what the storage contained at the given bookmark. After calling this, the application should typically invoke  to restart the Durable 对象, thus completing the point-in-time recovery.

This method returns a special bookmark representing the point in time immediately before the recovery takes place (even though that point in time is still technically in the future). Thus, after the recovery completes, it can be undone by performing a second recovery to this bookmark.

/durable-objects/concepts/what-are-durab.title: What are Durable Objects?
paragraphs: Durable Objects have properties that make them a great fit for distributed stateful scalable applications.
无服务器 compute, zero infrastructure management
Durable Objects are built on-top of the Workers 运行时, so they 支持 exactly the same code (JavaScript and WASM), and similar memory and CPU 限制.

Each Durable 对象 is implicitly 创建d on first access. User applications are not concerned with their lifecycle, creating them or destroying them. Durable Objects migrate among healthy servers, and therefore applications never have to worry about managing them.

Each Durable 对象 stays alive as long as 请求s are being processed, and remains alive for several seconds after being idle before hibernating, allowing applications to exploit in-memory 缓存 while handling many consecutive 请求s and boosting their 性能.
Storage colocated with compute
Each Durable 对象 has its own durable, 事务al, and strongly consistent storage (up to 10 GB[^1]), persisted across 请求s, and accessible only within that 对象.
Single-th读取ed 并发
Each Durable 对象 instance has an identifier, either randomly-generated or user-generated, which allows you to globally address which Durable 对象 should handle a specific 操作 or 请求.

Durable Objects are single-th读取ed and cooperatively multi-任务ed, just like code 运行中 in a web browser. For more details on how safety and correctness are achieved, refer to the blog post "Durable Objects: Easy, Fast, Correct — Choose three".
Elastic horizontal scaling across Cloudflare's 全球网络

Durable Objects can be sp读取 around the world, and you can 可选ly influence where each instance should be located. Durable Objects are not yet available in every Cloudflare 数据中心; refer to the where.durable对象s.live project for live locations.
Each Durable 对象 类型 (or "命名空间 绑定" in Cloudflare terms) corresponds to a JavaScript class implementing the actual logic. There is no hard limit on how many Durable Objects can be 创建d for each 命名空间.
Durable Objects scale elastically as your application 创建s millions of 对象s. There is no need for applications to manage infrastructure or plan ahead for capacity.

Each Durable 对象 has its own in-memory state. Applications can use this in-memory state to optimize the 性能 of their applications by keeping 重要 信息rmation in-memory, thereby avoiding the need to access the durable storage at all.
Useful cases for in-memory state include batching and aggregating 信息rmation before persisting it to storage, or for immediately rejecting/handling incoming 请求s meeting certain criteria, and more.

In-memory state is reset when the Durable 对象 hibernates after being idle for some time. Therefore, it is 重要 to persist any in-memory data to the durable storage if that data will be needed at a later time when the Durable 对象 receives another 请求.

The Durable 对象存储 API allows Durable Objects to access fast, 事务al, and strongly consistent storage. A Durable 对象's attached storage is private to its unique instance and cannot be accessed by other 对象s.
There are two flavors of the storage API, a 键-值 (KV) API and an SQL API.
When using the 新功能 SQLite in Durable Objects storage backend, you have access to both the APIs. However, if you use the previous storage backend you only have access to the 键-值 API.

Durable Objects provide an Alarms API which allows you to schedule the Durable 对象 to be woken up at a time in the future. This is useful when you want to do certain work periodically, or at some specific point in time, without having to manually manage infrastructure such as 作业 scheduling runners on your own.
You can combine Alarms with in-memory state and the durable storage API to 构建 batch and aggregation applications such as queues, 工作流s, or advanced data 管道s.

WebSockets are long-lived TCP connections that enable bi-directional, real-time communication between client and server. Because WebSocket 会话s are long-lived, applications commonly use Durable Objects to accept either the client or server connection.

Because Durable Objects provide a single-point-of-coordination between Cloudflare Workers, a single Durable 对象 instance can be used in parallel with WebSockets to coordinate between mul提示le clients, such as participants in a chat room or a mul提示layer game.

Durable Objects 支持 the WebSocket Standard API, as well as the WebSockets Hibernation API which extends the Web Standard WebSocket API to reduce costs by not incurring billing charges during periods of inactivity.
Durable Objects 支持 Workers Remote-Procedure-Call (RPC) which allows applications to use JavaScript-native 方法 and 对象s to communicate between Workers and Durable Objects.
Using RPC for communication makes application development easier and simpler to reason about, and more efficient.

Another way to describe and think about Durable Objects is through the lens of the Actor programming model. There are several popular 示例 of the Actor model 支持的 at the programming language level through 运行时s or library 框架, like Erlang, Elixir, Akka, or Microsoft Orleans for .NET.

The Actor model simplifies a lot of problems in distributed systems by abstracting away the communication between actors using RPC calls (or 消息 sending) that could be implemented on-top of any transport 协议, and it avoids most of the 并发 pitfalls you 获取 when doing 并发 through shared memory such as race 条件s when mul提示le processes/th读取s access the same data in-memory.

Each Durable 对象 instance can be seen as an Actor instance, receiving 消息s (incoming HTTP/RPC 请求s), executing some logic in its own single-th读取ed context using its attached durable storage or in-memory state, and finally sending 消息s to the outside world (outgoing HTTP/RPC 请求s or 响应s), even to another Durable 对象 instance.
Each Durable 对象 has certain capabilities in terms of how much work it can do, which should influence the application's architecture to fully take advantage of the 平台.
Durable Objects are natively integrated into Cloudflare's infrastructure, giving you the ultimate 无服务器 平台 to 构建 distributed stateful applications exploiting the entirety of Cloudflare's network.
Many of Cloudflare's products use Durable Objects. Some of our technical blog posts showcase real-world applications and use-cases where Durable Objects make 构建ing applications easier and simpler.
These blog posts may also serve as inspiration on how to architect scalable applications using Durable Objects, and how to integrate them with the rest of Cloudflare Developer 平台.
Durable Objects aren't just durable, they're fast: a 10x speedup for Cloudflare Queues
Behind the scenes with Stream Live, Cloudflare's live 流ing service
DO it again: how we used Durable Objects to add WebSockets 支持 and 身份验证 to AI Gateway
Workers 构建s: integrated CI/CD built on the Workers 平台
构建 durable applications on Cloudflare Workers: you write the 工作流s, we take care of the rest
构建ing D1: a Global 数据库
Billions and billions (of 日志): scaling AI Gateway with the Cloudflare Developer 平台
索引ing millions of HTTP 请求s using Durable Objects
Finally, the following blog posts may help you learn some of the technical implementation aspects of Durable Objects, and how they work.
Durable Objects: Easy, Fast, Correct — Choose three
Zero-延迟 SQLite storage in every Durable 对象
Workers Durable Objects 测试版: A 新功能 Approach to Stateful 无服务器
快速开始 now by following the "快速开始" 指南 to 创建 your first application using Durable Objects.
[^1]: Storage per Durable 对象 with SQLite is currently 1 GB. This will be raised to 10 GB for general 可用性.

This allows the Durable 对象 to consume up to 30 seconds of compute after each incoming network 请求, with each 新功能 network 请求 resetting the timer. If you consume more than 30 seconds of compute between incoming network 请求s, there is a heightened chance that the individual Durable 对象 is evicted and reset. CPU time per 请求 调用 can be increased.

This allows the Durable 对象 to consume up to 30 seconds of compute after each incoming network 请求, with each 新功能 network 请求 resetting the timer. If you consume more than 30 seconds of compute between incoming network 请求s, there is a heightened chance that the individual Durable 对象 is evicted and reset. CPU time per 请求 调用 can be increased.

/durable-objects/platform/pricing/.title: 定价
paragraphs: Durable Objects can incur two 类型s of billing: compute and storage.
On Workers 免费计划:
If you exceed any one of the free tier 限制, further operations of that 类型 will fail with an 错误.
Daily free 限制 reset at 00:00 UTC.
These 示例 exclude the costs for the Workers calling the Durable Objects. When modelling the costs of a Durable 对象, 注意 that:
不活跃 对象s receiving no 请求s do not incur any duration charges.

The WebSocket Hibernation API can dramatically reduce duration-相关 charges for Durable Objects communicating with clients over the WebSocket 协议, especially if 消息s are only transmitted occasionally at sparse intervals.
This 示例 represents a simple Durable 对象 used as a co-ordination service invoked via HTTP.
A single Durable 对象 was called by a Worker 1.5 million times
It is 活跃 for 1,000,000 seconds in the month
In this scenario, the estimated monthly cost would be calculated as:
请求s:
(1.5 million 请求s - included 1 million 请求s) x $0.15 / 1,000,000 = $0.075
Compute Duration:
Estimated total: \~$0.075 (请求s) + $0.00 (compute duration) + minimum $5/mo usage = $5.08 per month
This 示例 represents a moderately trafficked Durable Objects based application using WebSockets to broadcast game, chat or real-time user state across connected clients:
100 Durable Objects have 50 WebSocket connections established to each of them.
Clients send approximately one 消息 a minute for eight 活跃 hours a day, every day of the month.
50 WebSocket connections \* 100 Durable Objects to establish the WebSockets = 5,000 connections 创建d each day \* 30 days = 150,000 WebSocket connection 请求s.
50 消息s per minute \* 100 Durable Objects \* 60 minutes \* 8 hours \* 30 days = 72,000,000 WebSocket 消息 请求s.
150,000 + (72 million 请求s / 20 for WebSocket 消息 billing ratio) = 3.75 million billing 请求.
(3.75 million 请求s - included 1 million 请求s) x $0.15 / 1,000,000 = $0.41.
100 Durable Objects \* 60 seconds \* 60 minutes \* 8 hours \* 30 days = 86,400,000 seconds.
Estimated total: $0.41 (请求s) + $133.24 (compute duration) + minimum $5/mo usage = $138.65 per month.
This 示例 represents a horizontally scaled Durable Objects based application using WebSockets to communicate user-specific state to a single client connected to each Durable 对象.
100 Durable Objects each have a single WebSocket connection established to each of them.
Clients sent one 消息 every second of the month so that the Durable Objects were 活跃 for the entire month.
100 WebSocket connection 请求s.
1 消息 per second \* 100 connections \* 60 seconds \* 60 minutes \* 24 hours \* 30 days = 259,200,000 WebSocket 消息 请求s.
100 + (259.2 million 请求s / 20 for WebSocket billing ratio) = 12,960,100 请求s.
(12.9 million 请求s - included 1 million 请求s) x $0.15 / 1,000,000 = $1.79.
100 Durable Objects \* 60 seconds \* 60 minutes \* 24 hours \* 30 days = 259,200,000 seconds
Estimated total: $1.79 (请求s) + $409.72 (compute duration) + minimum $5/mo usage = $416.51 per month
This 示例 represents a moderately trafficked Durable Objects based application using WebSocket Hibernation to broadcast game, chat or real-time user state across connected clients:
100 Durable Objects each have 100 Hiberna表 WebSocket connections established to each of them.

Clients send one 消息 per minute, and it takes 10ms to process a single 消息 in the  处理程序. Since each Durable 对象 handles 100 WebSockets, cumulatively each Durable 对象 will be 活跃ly executing JS for 1 second each minute (100 WebSockets \* 10ms).
100 WebSocket connections \* 100 Durable Objects to establish the WebSockets = 10,000 initial WebSocket connection 请求s.
100 消息s per minute 1  \* 100 Durable Objects \* 60 minutes \* 24 hours \* 30 days = 432,000,000 请求s.
10,000 + (432 million 请求s / 20 for WebSocket billing ratio) = 21,610,000 million 请求s.
(21.6 million 请求s - included 1 million 请求s) x $0.15 / 1,000,000 = $3.09.
100 Durable Objects \* 1 second 2  \* 60 minutes \* 24 hours \* 30 days = 4,320,000 seconds
Estimated total: $3.09 (请求s) + $1.91 (compute duration) + minimum $5/mo usage = $10.00 per month
1  100 消息s per minute comes from the fact that 100 clients
connect to each DO, and each sends 1 消息 per minute.
2  The 示例 uses 1 second because each Durable 对象 is 活跃 for
1 second per minute. This can also be thought of as 432 million 请求s that
each take 10 ms to execute (4,320,000 seconds).

/durable-objects/reference/in-memory-sta.title: In-memory state in a Durable 对象
paragraphs: In-memory state means that each  Durable 对象  has one 活跃 instance at any particular time. All 请求s sent to that Durable 对象 are handled by that same instance. You can store some state in memory.
变量s in a Durable 对象 will maintain state as long as your Durable 对象 is not evicted from memory.

A common pattern is to initialize a Durable 对象 from persistent storage and set instance 变量s the first time it is accessed. Since future accesses are 路由d to the same Durable 对象, it is then possible to return any initialized 值s without making further calls to persistent storage.
A given instance of a Durable 对象 may share global memory with other instances defined in the same Worker code.

In the 示例 above, using a global 变量  instead of the instance 变量  would be incorrect. Two different instances of  will each have their own separate memory for , but might share memory for the global 变量 , leading to unexpected results. Because of this, it is best to avoid global 变量s.
:::注意[Built-in 缓存]

The Durable 对象's storage has a built-in in-memory cache of its own. If you use  to retrieve a 值 that was 读取 or written recently, the result will be instantly returned from cache. Instead of writing initialization code like above, you could use  whenever you need it, and rely on the built-in cache to make this fast. Refer to the 构建 a counter 示例 to 了解更多 about this approach.

However, in applications with more complex state, explicitly storing state in your 对象 may be easier than making Storage API calls on every access. De待处理 on the 配置 of your project, write your code in the way that is easiest for you.

A 迁移 is a mapping process from a class name to a 运行时 state. This process communicates the changes to the Workers 运行时 and provides the 运行时 with instructions on how to deal with those changes.
To apply a 迁移, you need to:
Edit your Wrangler 配置 file, as explained below.
Re-部署 your Worker using .
You must initiate a 迁移 process when you:
创建 a 新功能  Durable 对象 class .
Rename a Durable 对象 class.
删除 a Durable 对象 class.
Transfer an existing Durable Objects class.
:::注意

Updating the code for an existing Durable 对象 class does not require a 迁移. To 更新 the code for an existing Durable 对象 class, run [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#部署). This is true even for changes to how the code interacts with persistent storage. Because of global uniqueness, you do not have to be concerned about old and 新功能 code interacting with the same storage simultaneously. However, it is your responsibility to ensure that the 新功能 code is backwards compatible with existing stored data.
The most common 迁移 performed is a 新功能 class 迁移, which 信息rms the 运行时 that a 新功能 Durable 对象 class is being uploaded. This is also the 迁移 you need when creating your first Durable 对象 class.

If you want to transfer stored Durable Objects between two Durable 对象 classes in the same Worker code file, use Rename 迁移s instead.
Do not run a 创建 迁移 for the destination class before 运行中 a Transfer 迁移. The Transfer 迁移 will 创建 the destination class for you.

Edit your Wrangler 配置 file in the following way:

You can transfer stored Durable Objects from  to  from a Worker script named . The 配置 of the Wrangler 配置 file for your 新功能 Worker code (destination Worker code) would look like this:
迁移s are performed through the  配置s 键 in your  file or  键 in your  file.
迁移s require a 迁移 tag, which is defined by the  property in each 迁移 entry.
迁移 tags are treated like unique names and are used to determine which 迁移s have al读取y been applied. Once a given Worker code has a 迁移 tag set on it, all future Worker code 部署s must include a 迁移 tag.
The 迁移 列表 is an ordered 数组 of 表s, specified as a 键 in your Wrangler 配置 file.
You can define the 迁移 for each environment, as well as at the top level.
Top-level 迁移 is specified at the top-level  键 in the Wrangler 配置 file.

示例 Wrangler file:
If a 迁移 is only specified at the top-level, but not at the environment-level, the environment will inherit the top-level 迁移.
迁移s at at the environment-level override 迁移s at the top level.
All 迁移s are applied at 部署. Each 迁移 can only be applied once per environment.
Each 迁移 in the 列表 can have mul提示le directives, and mul提示le 迁移s can be specified as your project grows in complexity.
:::注意[重要]
The destination class (the class that stored Durable Objects are being transferred to) for a Rename or Transfer 迁移 must be exported by the 部署ed Worker.
You should not 创建 the destination Durable 对象 class before 运行中 a Rename or Transfer 迁移. The 迁移 will 创建 the destination class for you.
After a Rename or Transfer 迁移, 请求s to the destination Durable 对象 class will have access to the source Durable 对象's stored data.

After a 迁移, any existing 绑定 to the 源al Durable 对象 class (for 示例, from other Workers) will automatically forward to the 已更新 destination class. However, any Workers bound to the 已更新 Durable 对象 class must 更新 their Durable 对象 绑定 配置 in the  配置 file for their next 部署.
注意 that  files do not allow line breaks in inline 表s (the  syntax), but line breaks in the surrounding inline 数组 are accep表.

You cannot enable a SQLite storage backend on an existing, 部署ed Durable 对象 class, so setting  on later 迁移s will fail with an 错误. Automatic 迁移 of 部署ed classes from their 键-值 storage backend to SQLite storage backend will be available in the future.

Durable 对象 迁移s are atomic operations and cannot be gradually 部署ed. To provide early 反馈 to developers, 新功能 Worker versions with 新功能 迁移s cannot be uploaded. Refer to Gradual 部署s for Durable Objects for more 信息rmation.

/dynamic-workers/usage/bindings/.title: 绑定
/dynamic-workers/usage/bindings/.description: Give Dynamic Workers access to external APIs.
headings: Custom 绑定 with Dynamic Workers
提示: Tell your agent TypeScript 类型s
paragraphs: 绑定 are a way to grant Dynamic Workers access to specific APIs and 资源. They are similar to regular Workers 绑定. However, Dynamic Worker 绑定 don't typically point at regular Workers 平台 资源 like KV 命名空间s or R2 存储桶s. Instead, they point to anything you want.
When using Dynamic Workers, you can invent your own 绑定 to give to the Worker, by defining arbitrary Workers RPC interfaces.

Workers RPC — also known as Cap'n Web — is an RPC system designed to make it easy to present rich TypeScript interfaces across a 安全 boundary. Cap'n Web implements a capability-based 安全 model. That means, it 支持s passing 对象s "by 参考" across RPC boundaries. When you receive an 对象 参考 (also known as a "stub") over RPC, you are implicitly granted the ability to call that 对象's 方法; doing so makes further RPC calls back to the 源al 对象. 对象s do not have any URL or global identifier, so the only way to address one is to have received a stub pointing to it — if you haven't received a stub, you can't call the 对象.

Capability-based 安全 is essential to the design of most, if not all, 成功ful sandboxes, though it is often an implementation detail that users and even developers don't see. Android has Binder, Chrome has Mojo, and Cloudflare Workers has Cap'n Proto and Cap'n Web.
Dynamic Workers directly expose this power to you, the developer, so that you can 构建 your own strong sandbox.

Imagine you are using Dynamic Workers to implement an agent that can post 消息s to a chat room. Different agents will be able to post to different chat rooms, but any particular agent is only allowed to post to one specific chat room. The agent writes code, which you run in a Dynamic Worker.

You want to make sure the code can only access the _specific_ chat room that the given agent is authorized for. One way to do this would be to pass the chat room name into the Dynamic Worker (or to the agent), and then verify that all 请求s coming out of the Worker are addressed to the correct room, blocking them if not. However, Dynamic Workers offers a better approach: give the Worker a 绑定 that represents the _specific_ chat room.

To define a custom 绑定, your parent Worker needs to implement a  class and export it. In this case, we will be defining a class called . Of course, we don't want to export a 新功能 class for every possible chat room. Instead, we can specialize the interface for a specific room using [](/workers/运行时-apis/context#props).
Now we can load a Dynamic Worker and give it a Chat Room. To 创建 the chat room RPC stub, we use [](/workers/运行时-apis/context#exports), then we simply pass it into the Dynamic Worker Loader in the  对象:
We have achieved an elegant sandbox:
The agent can only post to the desired room.
The posts are made using an API 键, but the API 键 is never visible to the agent.
We 重写 the 消息s to include the agent's identity (this is just an 示例; we could perform any 重写).
All this happens without any cooperation from the agent itself. It doesn't even know any of this is happening!

In order for an AI agent to write code against your 绑定, you have to tell it what interface they implement. The best way to do this is to give your agent TypeScript 类型s describing the API, complete with comments documenting each declaration. Modern LLMs understand TypeScript well, having trained on a huge quantity of it, making it by far the most concise way to describe a JavaScript API. 注意 that even if the agent is actually writing plain JavaScript, you can still explain the interface to them using TypeScript.
Of course, you should declare your  class to extend the TypeScript 类型, ensuring that it actually matches.

Sometimes, you may simply want to pass a standard Workers 绑定, like a KV 命名空间, R2 存储桶, etc., into a Dynamic Worker. At this time, this is not directly 支持的. However, you can of course 创建 a wrapper RPC interface, using the approach outlined above, which emulates a regular Workers 绑定, forwarding to a real 绑定 in its implementation. Such a wrapper may even be preferable as it offers the opportunity to narrow the scope of the 绑定, 过滤器 or 重写 请求s, etc. That said, in the future, we plan to 支持 passing the 绑定 directly.

/dynamic-workers/examples/.title: 示例
/dynamic-workers/examples/.description: See common Dynamic Workers patterns for code 执行 and sandboxing.
paragraphs: These 示例 show how teams use Dynamic Workers to run code safely, bundle 依赖, and return results to an application or agent.

/dynamic-workers/usage/egress-control/.title: Egress control
/dynamic-workers/usage/egress-control/.description: Restrict, intercept, and audit outbound network access for dynamic Workers.
paragraphs: When you run untrusted or AI-generated code in a dynamic Worker, you need to control what it can access on the network. You might want to:
block all outbound access so the dynamic Worker can only use the 绑定 you give it
restrict outbound 请求s to a specific set of allowed destinations
inject credentials into outbound 请求s without exposing 密钥 to the dynamic Worker
log or audit every outbound 请求 for 可观测性
The  option in the  对象 returned by  or passed to  controls all of this. It intercepts every  and  call the dynamic Worker makes.
Set  to  to fully isolate the dynamic Worker from the network:
This causes any  or  请求 from the dynamic Worker to throw an exception.

In this mode, you can still give the Dynamic Worker direct access to specific 资源 and services using 绑定. This is the cleanest and most secure way to design your sandbox: block the Internet, then constructively offer specific capabilities via 绑定.
That said, if you need to offer 兼容性 with existing HTTP client 库 运行中 directly inside your Dynamic Worker sandbox, then blocking  may be infeasible, and you may prefer to intercept 请求s instead.

To intercept outbound 请求s, define a  class in the loader Worker that acts as a gateway. Every  and  call the dynamic Worker makes goes through this gateway instead of hitting the network directly. Pass the gateway to the dynamic Worker with  and :
From here, you can add any logic to the gateway, such as restricting destinations, injecting credentials, or logging 请求s.

A common pattern is attaching credentials to outbound 请求s so the dynamic Worker never sees the 密钥. Similar to custom 绑定, you can use [](/workers/运行时-apis/context/#props) to pass per-tenant or per-请求 context to the gateway.
The dynamic Worker calls  normally.  intercepts the 请求, attaches the 令牌 from the loader Worker's environment, and forwards it. The dynamic Worker never has access to .

/dynamic-workers/usage/observability/.title: 可观测性
/dynamic-workers/usage/observability/.description: Capture, retrieve, and forward 日志 from dynamic Workers.
headings: Capture 日志 with Tail Workers
Enable Workers 日志 on the loader Worker
Attach the Tail Worker to the Dynamic Worker
Return 日志 in real time
paragraphs: Dynamic Workers 支持 日志 with  calls, exceptions, and 请求 metadata captured during 执行. To access those 日志, you attach a Tail Worker, a callback that runs after the Dynamic Worker finishes that passes along all the 日志, exceptions, and metadata it collected.
This 指南 will show you how to:
Store Dynamic Worker 日志 so you can 搜索, 过滤器, and 查询 them
Collect 日志 during 执行 and return them in real time, for development and 调试
To save 日志 emitted by a Dynamic Worker, you need to capture them and write them somewhere they can be stored. Setting this up requires three 步骤s:
Enabling Workers 日志 on the loader Worker so that log output is saved.
Defining a Tail Worker that receives 日志 from the Dynamic Worker and writes them to Workers 日志.
Attaching the Tail Worker to the Dynamic Worker when you 创建 it.
:::注意
Tail Workers run asynchronously after the Dynamic Worker has al读取y sent its 响应, so they do not add 延迟 to the 请求.

Enable Workers 日志 by adding the  setting to the loader Worker's Wrangler 配置. However, Workers 日志 only captures log output from the loader Worker itself. Dynamic Workers are separate, so their  calls are not included automatically. To 获取 Dynamic Worker 日志 into Workers 日志, you need to define a Tail Worker that receives 日志 from the Dynamic Worker and writes them into the loader Worker's Workers 日志.
When a Dynamic Worker runs, the 运行时 collects all of its  calls, exceptions, and 请求 metadata. By 默认, those 日志 are discarded after the Dynamic Worker finishes.

To keep them, you define a Tail Worker on the loader Worker. A Tail Worker is a class with a  method. This is where you write the code that decides what happens with the 日志. The 运行时 will call this method after the Dynamic Worker finishes, passing in everything it collected during 执行.

Inside , you write each log entry to Workers 日志 by calling  with a JSON 对象. Include a  字段 in each entry so you can tell which Dynamic Worker produced each log and use it to 过滤器 and 搜索 the 日志 by Dynamic Worker later on.
The Tail Worker 读取s  from . You set this 值 when you attach the Tail Worker to the Dynamic Worker in the next 步骤.
Since the Tail Worker is defined within the loader Worker, its  output is saved to Workers 日志 along with the loader Worker's own 日志.

When you 创建 the Dynamic Worker, pass the Tail Worker in the [](/dynamic-workers/api-参考/#tails) 数组. This tells the 运行时: after this Dynamic Worker finishes, send its collected 日志 to the Tail Worker you defined.

To 参考 the  class you defined in the previous 步骤, use [](/workers/运行时-apis/context/#exports).  is the third parameter in the loader Worker's  处理程序.  gives you access to classes that are exported from the loader Worker. Because the Dynamic Worker runs in a separate context and cannot access the class directly, you use  to 创建 a 参考 that the 运行时 can wire up to the Dynamic Worker.

You also need to tell the Tail Worker which Dynamic Worker it is logging for. Since the Tail Worker runs separately from the loader Worker's  处理程序, it does not have access to your local 变量s. To pass it 信息rmation, use the [](/workers/运行时-apis/context/#props) option when you 创建 the instance.  is a plain 对象 of 键-值 对s that you set when attaching the Tail Worker and that the Tail Worker can 读取 at  when it runs. In this case, you pass the  so the Tail Worker knows which Dynamic Worker produced the 日志.

The 设置 above stores 日志 for later, but sometimes you need 日志 right away for real-time development. The challenge is that the Tail Worker and the loader Worker's  处理程序 run separately. The Tail Worker has the 日志, but the  处理程序 is the one 构建ing the 响应. You need a shared place where the Tail Worker can write the 日志 and the  处理程序 can 读取 them.

A Durable 对象 works well for this. Both the Tail Worker and the  处理程序 can look up the same Durable 对象 instance by name. The Tail Worker writes 日志 into it after the Dynamic Worker finishes, and the  处理程序 读取s them out and includes them in the 响应.
The pattern works like this:
The  处理程序 创建s a log 会话 in a Durable 对象 before 运行中 the Dynamic Worker.
The Dynamic Worker runs and produces 日志.
After the Dynamic Worker finishes, the Tail Worker writes the collected 日志 to the same Durable 对象.
The  处理程序 读取s the 日志 from the Durable 对象 and returns them in the 响应.
For a full working implementation, refer to the Dynamic Workers Playground 示例.

/dynamic-workers/usage/durable-object-fa.title: Durable 对象 Facets
/dynamic-workers/usage/durable-object-fa.description: Run dynamically-loaded code with isolated persistent storage.
headings: Load and run a dynamic class
The 对象 returned by the  callback.
paragraphs: 类型Script示例,
WranglerConfig,
类型,
Meta信息,
} from "~/components";

Durable 对象 Facets let you load a Durable 对象 class from a Dynamic Worker and run it as a child of your own Durable 对象. The child (the facet) 获取s its own isolated SQLite 数据库, while your class acts as a supervisor that controls access.

This is useful when you want dynamically-generated code — for 示例, code written by an AI agent — to have persistent storage, without giving it direct access to a Durable 对象 命名空间. Your supervisor loads the code, 创建s the facet, and forwards 请求s into it. You stay in control of what the dynamic code can do.
A facet-based 设置 has three layers:
**Supervisor class** — A normal Durable 对象 class that you write and 部署. It is configured with a SQLite storage backend like any other Durable 对象.
**Dynamic code** — Code loaded at 运行时 through the Worker Loader API. This code exports a class that extends .
**Facet** — An instance of the dynamic class, 创建d by calling  inside your supervisor. Each facet has its own SQLite 数据库, separate from the supervisor's.
The supervisor's 数据库 and the facet's 数据库 are stored to获取her as part of the same overall Durable 对象. The dynamic code cannot 读取 the supervisor's 数据库 — it only has access to its own.
Your Worker needs two things: a Durable 对象 class with a SQLite storage backend, and a Worker Loader 绑定.
The following 示例 shows a supervisor Durable 对象 () that loads dynamic code, 创建s a facet from it, and forwards HTTP 请求s to the facet.

The dynamic code is a simple counter app that tracks how many 请求s it has received, using its own SQLite-backed storage. In a real application, this code would come from an AI agent or user upload rather than a static 字符串.
In this 示例:
is your supervisor Durable 对象. You 部署 it normally and it owns a Durable 对象 命名空间.
The dynamic code exports a class () that extends . This class uses  to 读取 and write data, just like any Durable 对象.
创建s the facet. The  字符串 names the facet — each name 获取s its own SQLite 数据库 within the parent Durable 对象.
The facet's 数据库 is fully isolated from the supervisor's 数据库.  and  each have their own storage that the other cannot access.

The  对象 is available inside any Durable 对象 class. It provides 方法 to 创建, shut down, and 删除 facets. A single Durable 对象 can have any 数字 of facets with different names, each with its own independent SQLite 数据库.
this.ctx.facets.获取(name  , callback   FacetStartupOptions" />)
创建s or resumes a facet with the given name and returns a stub you can use to send it 请求s.

If the facet has not started yet, or has hibernated, the 运行时 calls  to determine what code to load. Otherwise, the existing facet is reused and the callback is not invoked.  can 可选ly be  (i.e. returning ).
The returned stub behaves like a Durable 对象 stub. You can call  on it to send HTTP 请求s, or call RPC 方法 directly.
this.ctx.facets.abort(name  , reason  )
Shuts down a 运行中 facet and invalidates all existing stubs. Any subsequent call on an invalidated stub throws . The facet's storage is preserved.

After aborting, you can call  again to restart the facet — including with a different class. This makes  useful for code 更新s: abort the facet 运行中 the old version, then call  with a callback that returns the 新功能 class.
this.ctx.facets.删除(name  )
Aborts the facet (if 运行中) and permanently 删除s its SQLite 数据库. If you call  with the same name afterward, the facet starts with an empty 数据库.
Use  to clean up storage for facets that are no longer needed.
The 对象 returned by the  callback.
The Durable 对象 class to instantiate for the facet. Obtain this by calling  on a Dynamic Worker stub.
The ID the facet sees as its own . If omitted, the facet inherits the parent Durable 对象's ID.
The supervisor and each facet have separate SQLite 数据库s. The dynamic code uses the standard Durable 对象存储 APIs with all operations tar获取ing the facet's own 数据库.

This isolation means you do not need to trust the dynamic code with your supervisor's data. You can store metadata, billing counters, or access-control state in the supervisor's 数据库, and the facet cannot 读取 or modify any of it.
In production, you would typically store the dynamic code itself in the supervisor's 数据库 and load it in the  method. This keeps the code 对ed with the Durable 对象 instance that manages it.

/dynamic-workers/.title: Dynamic Workers
/dynamic-workers/.description: Spin up isolated Workers on demand to execute code.
paragraphs: Spin up Workers at 运行时 to execute code on-demand in a secure, sandboxed environment.

Dynamic Workers let you spin up an unlimited 数字 of Workers to execute arbitrary code specified at 运行时. Dynamic Workers can be used as a lightweight alternative to containers for securely sandboxing code you don't trust.

Dynamic Workers are the lowest-level primitive for spinning up a Worker, giving you full control over defining how the Worker is composed, which 绑定 it receives, whether it can reach the network, and more.
部署 the Dynamic Workers Playground to 创建 and run Workers dynamically from code you write or import from GitHub, with real-time 日志 and 可观测性.
![部署 to Cloudflare](https://部署.workers.cloudflare.com/?url=https://github.com/dinasaur404/dynamic-workers-playground)
Use this pattern when code needs to run quickly in a secure, isolated environment.

**AI Agent "Code Mode"**: LLMs are trained to write code. Instead of supplying an agent with tool calls to perform 任务s, give it an API and let it write and execute code. Save up to 80% in inference 令牌s and cost by allowing the agent to programmatically process data instead of sending it all through the LLM.
**AI-generated applications / "Vibe Code"**: Run generated code for proto类型s, projects, and automations in a secure, isolated sandboxed environment.
**Fast development and previews**: Load proto类型s, previews, and playgrounds in milliseconds.
**Custom automations**: 创建 custom 工具 on the fly that execute a 任务, call an integration, or automate a 工作流.
**平台s**: Run applications uploaded by your users.
Because you compose the Worker that runs the code at 运行时, you control how that Worker is configured and what it can access.
**绑定**: Decide which 绑定 and structured data the dynamic Worker receives.
**可观测性**: Attach Tail Workers and capture 日志 for each run.
**Network access**: Intercept or block Internet access for outbound 请求s.
**Durable 对象 Facets**: Run dynamically-loaded code as a Durable 对象 with its own isolated SQLite storage.

/email-routing/get-started/enable-email-.title: Enable Email Routing
paragraphs: :::注意[重要]

Enabling Email Routing adds the appropriate  记录s to the DNS 设置 of your 区域 in order for the service to work. You can change these  记录s at any time. However, de待处理 on how you configure them, Email Routing might stop working.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Email Routing** page.
Review the 记录s that will be added to your 区域.
Select **Add 记录s and enable**.
Go to **路由 规则**.
For **Custom addresses**, select **创建 address**.
Enter the custom email address you want to use (for 示例, ).
In **Destination addresses**, enter the full email address you want your emails to be forwarded to — for 示例, .
:::注意[注意s]

If you have several destination addresses linked to the same custom email address (规则), Email Routing will only process the most recent 规则. To avoid this, do not link several destination addresses to the same custom address.

The current implementation of email forwarding only 支持s a single destination address per custom address. To forward a custom address to mul提示le destinations you must 创建 a Workers script to 重定向 the email to each destination. All the destinations used in the Workers script must be al读取y validated.
Select **Save**.
Cloudflare will send a verification email to the address provided in the **Destination address** 字段. You must verify your email address before being able to proceed.
In the verification email Cloudflare sent you, select **Verify email address** > **Go to Email Routing** to activate Email Routing.
Your Destination address should now show **Verified**, under **Status**. Select **Continue**.

Cloudflare needs to add the relevant  and  记录s to DNS 记录s for Email Routing to work. This 步骤 is automatic and is only needed the first time you configure Email Routing. It is meant to ensure you have the proper 记录s configured in your 区域. Select **Add 记录s and finish**.
Email Routing is now 已启用. You can add other custom addresses to your 账户.
:::注意

When Email Routing is configured and 运行中, no other email services can be 活跃 in the 域名 you are configuring. If there are other  记录s al读取y configured in DNS, Cloudflare will ask you if you wish to 删除 them. If you do not 删除 existing  记录s, Email Routing will not be 已启用.

/email-routing/email-workers/.title: Email Workers
paragraphs: With Email Workers you can leverage the power of Cloudflare Workers to implement any logic you need to process your emails and 创建 complex 规则. These 规则 determine what happens when you receive an email.

Creating your own 规则 with Email Workers is as easy or complex as you want. You can begin using one of the starter templates that are pre-populated with code for popular use-cases. These templates allow you to 创建 a block列表, allow列表, or send 通知 to Slack.

If you prefer, you can skip the templates and use custom code. You can, for 示例, 创建 logic that only accepts 消息s from a specific address, and then forwards them to one or more of your verified email addresses, while also alerting you on Slack.
The following is an 示例 of an allow列表 Email Worker:
Refer to the Workers Languages for more 信息rmation regarding the languages you can use with Workers.
To use Email Routing with Email Workers there are three 步骤s involved:
Creating the Email Worker.
Adding the logic to your Email Worker (like email addresses allowed or blocked from sending you emails).
绑定 the Email Worker to a 路由. This is the email address that forwards emails to the Worker.

The 路由, or email address, bound to the Worker forwards emails to your Email Worker. The logic in the Worker will then decide if the email is forwarded to its final destination or dropped, and what further 操作s (if any) will be applied.

For 示例, say that you 创建 an allow列表 Email Worker and bind it to a  路由. This 路由 will be the email address you share with the world, to make sure that only email addresses on your allow列表 are forwarded to your destination address. All other emails will be dropped.

/email-routing/get-started/.title: 快速开始
paragraphs: To enable Email Routing, start by creating a custom email address linked to a destination address or Email Worker. This forms an email 规则. You can enable or disable 规则 from the Cloudflare 控制面板. Refer to Enable Email Routing for more details.

Custom addresses you 创建 with Email Routing work as forward addresses only. Emails sent to custom addresses are forwarded by Email Routing to your destination inbox. Cloudflare does not process outbound email, and does not have an SMTP server.
The first time you access Email Routing, you will see a wizard guiding you through the process of creating email 规则. You can skip the wizard and add 规则 manually.
If you need to pause Email Routing or offboard to another service, refer to Disable Email Routing.

/email-routing/get-started/email-routing.title: 分析
paragraphs: The 概述 page shows you a summary of your 账户. You can check details such as how many custom and destination addresses you have configured, as well as the status of your 路由 service.

In Email Routing summary you can check 指标 相关 the 数字 of emails received, forwarded, dropped, and rejected. To 过滤器 this 信息rmation by time interval, select the drop-down menu. You can choose preset periods between the previous 30 minutes and 30 days, as well as a custom date range.

This section allows you to sort through emails received, and check Email Routing 操作s - for 示例, , , or . Select a specific email to expand its details and check 信息rmation regarding the SPF, DKIM, and DMARC statuses. De待处理 on the 信息rmation shown, you can opt to mark an email as spam or block the sender.

/email-security/account-setup/sso/.title: SSO integration
paragraphs: For added 安全 and convenience, Email 安全 (formerly Area 1) offers 支持 for  安全 Assertion Markup Language based (SAML-based)  single sign-on (SSO) logins. Organizations are able to choose between having users access Email 安全 (formerly Area 1) with a username and password plus a  two-factor 身份验证 (2FA)  code, or using an SSO provider, such as OneLogin or Okta.

**Identity Provider initiated (IDP-initiated) SAML**: IDP-initiated 配置s (like Okta or OneLogin) require the IDP to be accessible to the Email 安全 infrastructure in order to 成功fully authenticate users. At the most basic level, the user selects an application from their IDP. Then, the IDP communicates with Email 安全 using a SAML assertion to provide identity 信息rmation for the user 请求ing to login to the Email 安全 控制面板.

**Service Provider Initiated (SP-initiated) SAML**: SP-initiated 配置s are the most common SAML 身份验证 mechanisms. The main difference compared to IDP is that the service provider (like Email 安全) does not require any direct connection to the IDP in order to authenticate a user. The user's browser provides the ability for the SAML exchange to occur but the service provider and the IDP do not directly communicate with each other.
Email 安全 (formerly Area 1) only 支持s IDP-initiated SAML 设置 at this point.
For more details on 设置, refer to the following 资源:

/email-security/email-configuration/enha.title: Business email compromise (BEC)
paragraphs: Attackers often try to impersonate executives within an organization when sending malicious emails (with 请求s about banking 信息rmation, trade 密钥, etc.).

The Business email compromise (BEC) 特性 protects against these attacks by adding an attribute to any spoofed email 消息s matching these sensitive email addresses. 信息rmation about 键 users you enter in the 控制面板 is used by Email 安全 to run enhanced scan techniques and find these spoofed emails.
You have several options for adding email addresses to BEC protection.
Using the 控制面板, you can add email addresses individually or upload a CSV file:
登录 to the Email 安全 控制面板.
Go to **设置** (the gear icon).
On **Email 配置**, go to **Enhanced Detections**.
Select **新功能 Display Name**.
Enter an email address manually or upload a CSV file.
You can also upload a CSV file of mul提示le email addresses. The CSV file must be smaller than 150 KB, start with a 头部 row of all 必需 值s, and contain no additional 字段s.
An 示例 file would look like this:

If you want your BEC contacts automatically synced, Email 安全 also 支持s directory integration for Microsoft and Gmail. Refer to Office 365 directory 指南 and Google Workspaces directory integration for more 信息rmation.
Email 安全's 控制面板 has at-a-glance insights regarding BEC attacks, such as top email addresses tar获取ed. Refer to Statistics 概述 and 类型s of malicious detections for more 信息rmation.

/firewall/cf-dashboard/create-edit-delet.title: 创建, edit, and 删除 规则
headings: 创建 a 防火墙 规则
测试 防火墙 规则 with 规则 Preview
paragraphs: A 防火墙 规则 has two main attributes: an 表达式 and an 操作.
When an incoming HTTP 请求 matches a 防火墙 规则 表达式, Cloudflare performs the specified 操作. For more 信息rmation, refer to 表达式s and 操作s.
:::注意
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and website.
Go to **安全** > **WAF** > **防火墙 规则**.
Select **创建 a 防火墙 规则**.
In the **创建 防火墙 规则** page that displays, use the **规则 name** input to supply a descriptive name.

Under **When incoming 请求s match**, use the **字段** drop-down 列表 to choose an HTTP property (refer to the 字段s 参考 for details). For each 请求, the 值 of the property you choose for **字段** is compared to the 值 you specify for **值**.
Alternatively, use the 表达式 Editor to define the 规则 表达式.
Use the **Operator** drop-down 列表 to choose a comparison operator. For an 表达式 to match, the 值 of the 请求 **字段** and the 值 specified in the **值** input must satisfy the comparison operator.
Next, specify the 值 to match. If the 值 is an enumeration, then the **值** control will be a drop-down 列表. Otherwise, it will be a text input.
To add a 新功能 sub-表达式 to the 规则 表达式, select **And** or **Or** next to **值**.
Select an 操作 for your 规则 in the **操作** drop-down 列表.
To save and 部署 your 规则, select **部署**. If you are not 读取y to 部署 your 规则, select **Save as draft**.
After you choose an option, you return to the 规则 列表, which displays your 新功能 规则.
Use the available options in the 规则 列表 to manage 防火墙 规则.
Select Edit (wrench icon) located on the right of your 规则 in the 规则 列表 to open the Edit 防火墙 规则 panel and make the changes you want.
Use the toggle switch associated with a 防火墙 规则 to enable or disable it.
Next to the 规则 you want to 删除, select **删除** (**X** icon).
In the confirmation dialog, select **删除** to complete the operation.

By 默认, Cloudflare evaluates 防火墙 规则 in 列表 order, where 规则 are evaluated in the order they appear in the 规则 列表. When 列表 ordering is 已启用, the 规则 列表 allows you to drag and drop 防火墙 规则 into position, as shown below.

Once there are more than 200 total 规则 (including 不活跃 规则), you must manage evaluation using priority ordering, in which Cloudflare evaluates 防火墙 规则 in order of their priority 数字, starting with the lowest. When you cross this threshold, the 防火墙 规则 interface automatically switches to priority ordering. For more on working with priority ordering, refer to Order and priority.
规则 Preview allows customers on an 企业计划 to understand the potential impact of a 新功能 防火墙 规则, by 测试 it against a sample of 请求s drawn from the last 72 hours of traffic.
规则 Preview is built into the 创建 防火墙 规则 and Edit 防火墙 规则 panels so that you can 测试 a 规则 as you edit it. For more 信息rmation, refer to Preview 规则.

/firewall/api/.title: Manage 规则 via the APIs
paragraphs: Cloudflare offers APIs that work to获取her to achieve the same effect as the UI-based 防火墙 规则 特性 under 安全 > WAF.
These APIs are the following:
**防火墙 规则 API**: Manage 防火墙 规则 and their 操作s, based on criteria separately defined through 过滤器s.
**过滤器s API**: Manage the 过滤器s that enable 规则 matching.
**列表s API**: Manage named 列表s of items (such as IP addresses) that you can use in the 规则 of different Cloudflare products.

/firewall/cf-firewall-rules/actions/.title: 防火墙 规则 操作s
headings: 注意s about challenge 操作s
paragraphs: The 操作 of a 防火墙 规则 tells Cloudflare how to handle HTTP 请求s that have matched the 规则 表达式.
The 表 below 列表s the 操作s available in 防火墙 规则. These 操作s are 列表ed in order of precedence. If the same 请求 matches two different 规则 which have the same priority, precedence determines the 操作 to take.

For 示例, the _Allow_ 操作 takes precedence over the _Block_ 操作. In a case where a 请求 matches a 规则 with the _Allow_ 操作 and another with the _Block_ 操作, precedence resolves the tie, and Cloudflare allows the 请求.

There are two exceptions to this behavior: the _Log_ and _Bypass_ 操作s. Unlike other 操作s, _Log_ and _Bypass_ do not terminate further evaluation within 防火墙 规则. This means that if a 请求 matches two different 规则 and one of those 规则 specifies the _Log_ or _Bypass_ 操作, the second 操作 will be 触发器ed instead, even though _Log_/_Bypass_ has precedence.
:::注意
For 参考 信息rmation on 规则 操作s available for Cloudflare products powered by the 规则et Engine, refer to 规则 language: 操作s.
{/ prettier-ignore /}

based on the order of 执行.

and challenge 操作s 触发器ed by other 防火墙 规则.

This option is not recommended. Instead, choose  Managed Challenge , which issues inter活跃 challenges to visitors only when necessary.

For additional 信息rmation, refer to  注意s about challenge 操作s .

challenges across the Internet.

following 操作s based on specific criteria:

click a button or to perform a 任务).

Useful for ensuring that bots and spam cannot access the 请求ed

When you configure a 防火墙 规则 with one of the challenge 操作s — _Non-Inter活跃 Challenge_, _Managed Challenge_, or _Inter活跃 Challenge_ — and a 请求 matches the 规则, one of two things can happen:
The 请求 is blocked if the visitor fails the challenge
The 请求 is allowed if the visitor passes the challenge
In this last case, no further 防火墙 规则 will be processed. This means that the 操作 of any later 规则 with a challenge or _Block_ 操作 also matching the 请求 will not be applied, and the 请求 will be allowed.

/firewall/.title: Cloudflare 防火墙 规则
paragraphs: Cloudflare 防火墙 规则 allows you to 创建 规则 that inspect incoming traffic and block, challenge, log, or allow specific 请求s.
**规则-based protection**: Use pre-defined 规则ets provided by Cloudflare, or define your own 防火墙 规则. 创建 规则 in the Cloudflare 控制面板 or via API.
**Complex custom 规则**: Each 规则's 表达式 can 参考 mul提示le 字段s from all the available HTTP 请求 参数 and 字段s, allowing you to 创建 complex 规则.
This 表 outlines the 防火墙 规则 特性 and entitlements available with each customer plan:
Unless you are al读取y an advanced user, refer to 表达式s and 操作s to 了解更多 about the basic elements of 防火墙 规则.

/fundamentals/get-started/.title: 快速开始
paragraphs: Before you can begin using Cloudflare products, first 创建 an 账户. If mul提示le people manage your Cloudflare 账户, 设置 member 权限 to control which 资源 each person can access.

构建 is where developers 创建 and 部署 simple sites and full-stack applications on the Workers compute 平台, and connect them to storage and 数据库 primitives like KV (键-值 data store), D1 (SQL 数据库), R2 (对象存储), Queues (asynchronous messaging), Durable Objects (stateful coordination), and more.

Our compute, orchestration, AI, storage, media, and 安全 services are integrated, so you can quickly extend existing apps or launch 新功能 ones while Cloudflare's 全球网络 takes care of infrastructure, scaling, and 性能 for you.
Protect & Connect is where you establish fast and reliable connections between your websites, apps, and users, while protecting them from attackers and unwanted traffic.

Application 安全 and 性能 focuses on 资源 available on the public Internet. Manage delivery 性能 by controlling and speeding up primarily Layer 7 (Application) traffic, in addition to managing media such as video and images. 快速开始 by onboarding a 域名.
Zero Trust protects private, internal users/devices and the 资源 they access. 快速开始 with Zero Trust.

Network services extend protection and acceleration to cloud, on-premise, or hybrid networks, and you can also manage network connections and optimize Layer 3 (Network) and 4 (Transport) traffic. 快速开始 with Magic Transit or Cloudflare WAN (formerly Magic WAN).

/google-tag-gateway/.title: Google tag gateway for advertisers
headings: Configure in the Cloudflare 控制面板
paragraphs: Google tag gateway for advertisers allows website owners using Cloudflare as a CDN to 获取 the most out of ad measurement 工具 with just a few clicks. It allows you to 部署 Google scripts using your own 域名, enhancing data privacy and improving signal measurement recovery. Unlike standard 设置s where tags are 请求ed from a Google 域名, Google tag gateway for advertisers loads the tag from your 域名 and sends measurement 事件 to your 域名, where they are forwarded to Google.
了解更多 about why we built it and 工作原理 in our blog post.
Google tag gateway for advertisers is free to use. 请求s 路由d through the gateway do not count toward usage or billing for other Cloudflare products such as CDN, WAF, or Bot Management.
Site owners can enable this 特性 in one of two ways: through the Google tag console, or through the Cloudflare 控制面板.
The fas测试 way to 设置 Google tag gateway for advertisers is in Google Tag Manager. Follow the 步骤s in Google's Help Center.
:::注意
Your Cloudflare 控制面板 user must have one of the following 账户 角色: Super Administrator, Administrator or Zaraz Admin. If you are using 域名 Scoped 角色, your 域名 Role must be 域名 Administrator.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Google Tag Gateway** page.
Select your 域名.
Enable the toggle for **Turn on and configure Google tag gateway**.
Add your Google tag ID and the path on your website reserved for the Google tag.
The Google tag ID can be found in the Google Tag Experience 控制面板. The measurement path is an unused path on your site that will load Google Tag Manager and all subsequent measurement 请求s.
Once you click **Save**, Google tag gateway for advertisers will be 已启用 on your 区域. If you al读取y have a GTM script on your website, this First Party Tag will override the existing script.
Now that you have authenticated into your Cloudflare 账户 and configured GTM in first-party mode, your Google Tags will be loaded using and subsequent measurement 请求s will be served by Cloudflare.
Google Developer Docs: 设置 Google tag gateway for advertisers
Google Help Center: 设置 Google tag gateway for advertisers in the Google tag with Cloudflare
Google Help Center: 设置 Google tag gateway for advertisers in Google Tag Manager with Cloudflare

/health-checks/.title: 健康检查
paragraphs: Standalone 健康检查 监控s an IP address or 主机名 for 源服务器s or applications and notifies you in near real-time if there happens to be a problem.
:::注意
Standalone 健康检查 are different from health 监控s associated with 负载均衡器s. For more details about health 监控s, refer to the Load Balancing 文档.
You can use 健康检查 分析 to evaluate 源 正常运行时间, 延迟, failure reason, and specific 事件 日志 to 调试 possible 源 issues.
Cloudflare Load Balancing distributes traffic across your  端点 , which reduces 端点 strain and 延迟 and improves the experience for end users.

/health-checks/get-started/.title: 快速开始
headings: 创建 a 健康检查
paragraphs: This 指南 will 获取 you started with creating and managing configured 健康检查.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **健康检查** page.
Select **创建** and fill out the form, paying special attention to:
The 值s for **Interval** and **Check regions**, because decreasing the **Interval** and increasing **Check regions** may increase the load on your 源服务器.
**重试**, which specify the 数字 of 重试 to attempt in case of a 超时 before marking the 源 as unhealthy.
**响应 正文**, which specifies a sub字符串 that must be present in the first 10 KB of the 响应 正文 for the check to succeed.
Select **Save and 部署**.
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板 and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **Traffic** > **健康检查**.
Navigate to your 健康检查 and select **Edit**.
Edit your 健康检查.
Select **Save**.
:::注意
You can also enable, disable, or 删除 configured 健康检查.
Authenticated 源 pull is not 支持的 by Standalone 健康检查.

/health-checks/health-checks-analytics/.title: 健康检查 分析
paragraphs: Once you have 设置 a standalone 健康检查 including notification emails, use 健康检查 分析 to 调试 possible 源 issues.
To access 健康检查 分析:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **健康检查 分析** page.
You can configure 告警 to notify you of any changes in your 健康检查 status.

/hyperdrive/concepts/query-caching/.title: 查询 缓存
paragraphs: Hyperdrive automatically caches all cacheable queries executed against your 数据库 when 查询 缓存 is turned on, reducing the need to go back to your 数据库 (incurring 延迟 and 数据库 load) for every 查询 which can be especially useful for popular queries. 查询 缓存 is 已启用 by 默认.
Because Hyperdrive uses 数据库 协议s, it can differentiate between a mutating 查询 (a 查询 that writes to the 数据库) and a non-mutating 查询 (a 读取-only 查询), allowing Hyperdrive to safely cache 读取-only queries.
Besides determining the difference between a  and an , Hyperdrive also parses the 数据库 wire-协议 and uses it to differentiate between a mutating or non-mutating 查询.
For 示例, a 读取 查询 that populates the front page of a 新功能s site would be cached:

Mutating queries (including , , or ) and queries that use 函数s designated as [](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/xfunc-volatility.html) or [](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/xfunc-volatility.html) by PostgreSQL are not cached:
Common PostgreSQL 函数s that are not cacheable include:

Only 函数s designated as  by PostgreSQL (函数s whose return 值 never changes for the same inputs) are compatible with Hyperdrive 缓存. If your 查询 uses a  or  函数, move the 函数 call to your application code and pass the resulting 值 as a 查询 parameter instead.
:::注意[函数 detection is text-based]
Hyperdrive uses text-based pattern matching to detect uncacheable 函数s in your queries. This means that even 参考s to 函数 names inside SQL comments will cause the 查询 to be marked as uncacheable.
For 示例, the following 查询 would not be cached because  appears in the comment:
Avoid referencing uncacheable 函数 names anywhere in your 查询 text, including comments.
The 默认 缓存 behaviour for Hyperdrive is defined as below:
= 60 seconds (1 minute)
= 15 seconds
The  setting determines the maximum lifetime a 查询 响应 will be served from cache. Cached 响应s may be evicted from the cache prior to this time if they are rarely used.
The  setting allows Hyperdrive to continue serving stale cache results for an additional period of time while it is revalidating the cache. In most cases, revalidation should happen rapidly.
You can set a maximum  of 1 hour.
Disable 缓存 on a per-Hyperdrive basis by using the Wrangler CLI to set the  option to .
For 示例:

You can also configure mul提示le Hyperdrive connections from a single application: one connection that enables 缓存 for popular queries, and a second connection where you do not want to cache queries, but still benefit from Hyperdrive's 延迟 优势 and connection pooling.
For 示例, using 数据库 drivers:
The Wrangler 配置 remains the same both for PostgreSQL and MySQL.
For more 信息rmation, refer to How Hyperdrive works.
To connect to PostgreSQL, refer to Connect to PostgreSQL.
For 故障排除 guidance, refer to Troubleshoot and 调试.

/hyperdrive/get-started/.title: 快速开始
headings: 3. Connect Hyperdrive to a 数据库
5. Run a 查询 against your 数据库
安装 a 数据库 driver
paragraphs: Hyperdrive accelerates access to your existing 数据库s from Cloudflare Workers, making even single-region 数据库s feel globally distributed.

By maintaining a connection pool to your 数据库 within Cloudflare's network, Hyperdrive reduces seven round-trips to your 数据库 before you can even send a 查询: the TCP 握手 (1x), TLS negotiation (3x), and 数据库 身份验证 (3x).
Hyperdrive understands the difference between 读取 and write queries to your 数据库, and caches the most common 读取 queries, improving 性能 and reducing load on your 源 数据库.
This 指南 will instruct you through:
Creating your first Hyperdrive 配置.
Creating a Cloudflare Worker and 绑定 it to your Hyperdrive 配置.
Establishing a 数据库 connection from your Worker to a public 数据库.
:::注意
Hyperdrive currently works with PostgreSQL, MySQL and many compatible 数据库s. This includes CockroachDB and Materialize (which are PostgreSQL-compatible), and PlanetScale.
了解更多 about the 数据库s that Hyperdrive 支持s.
开始之前, ensure you have completed the following:
注册 for a Cloudflare 账户 if you have not al读取y.

安装 [](https://docs.npmjs.com/downloading-and-安装ing-node-js-and-npm). Use a Node version manager like nvm or Volta to avoid permission issues and change Node.js versions. Wrangler requires a Node version of  or later.

Have a publicly accessible PostgreSQL or MySQL (or compatible) 数据库. _If your 数据库 is in a private network (like a VPC)_, refer to Connect to a private 数据库 for instructions on using Cloudflare Tunnel with Hyperdrive.
Before creating your Hyperdrive 绑定, 登录 with your Cloudflare 账户 by 运行中:

You will be directed to a web page asking you to 登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板. After you have logged in, you will be asked if Wrangler can make changes to your Cloudflare 账户. Scroll down and select Allow to continue.
:::注意[新功能 to Workers?]
Refer to How Workers works to learn about the Workers 无服务器 执行 model works. Go to the Workers 快速开始 指南 to 设置 your first Worker.
创建 a 新功能 project named  by 运行中:
This will 创建 a 新功能  directory. Your 新功能  directory will include:
A  Worker at .
A [](/workers/wrangler/配置/) 配置 file.  is how your  Worker will connect to Hyperdrive.
Node.js 兼容性 is 必需 for 数据库 drivers, and needs to be configured for your Workers project.
Hyperdrive works by connecting to your 数据库, pooling 数据库 connections globally, and speeding up your 数据库 access through Cloudflare's network.
It will provide a secure connection 字符串 that is only accessible from your Worker which you can use to connect to your 数据库 through Hyperdrive.
This means that you can use the Hyperdrive connection 字符串 with your existing drivers or ORM 库 without needing significant changes to your code.
To 创建 your first Hyperdrive 数据库 配置, change into the directory you just 创建d for your Workers project:
To 创建 your first Hyperdrive, you will need:
The IP address (or 主机名) and port of your 数据库.
The 数据库 username (for 示例, ).
The password associated with that username.
The name of the 数据库 you want Hyperdrive to connect to. For 示例,  or .
Hyperdrive accepts the combination of these 参数 in the common connection 字符串 format used by 数据库 drivers:
Most 数据库 providers will provide a connection 字符串 you can copy-and-paste directly into Hyperdrive.
To 创建 a Hyperdrive connection, run the  command, replacing the placeholder 值s passed to the  flag with the 值s of your existing 数据库:
:::注意[Manage 缓存]
By 默认, Hyperdrive will cache 查询 results. If you wish to disable 缓存, pass the flag .
Alternatively, you can use the  flag to specify the maximum duration (in seconds) for which items should persist in the cache, before they are evicted. 默认 值 is 60 seconds.
Refer to Hyperdrive Wrangler commands for more 信息rmation.
If 成功ful, the command will output your 新功能 Hyperdrive 配置:
Copy the  字段: you will use this in the next 步骤 to make Hyperdrive accessible from your Worker script.

Hyperdrive will attempt to connect to your 数据库 with the provided credentials to verify they are correct before creating a 配置. If you encounter an 错误 when attempting to connect, refer to Hyperdrive's 故障排除 文档 to 调试 possible causes.
Once you have 创建d a Hyperdrive 配置 and bound it to your Worker, you can run a 查询 against your 数据库.

To connect to your 数据库, you will need a 数据库 driver which allows you to authenticate and 查询 your 数据库. For this tutorial, you will use node-postgres (pg), one of the most widely used PostgreSQL drivers.
To 安装 , ensure you are in the  directory. Open your terminal and run the following command:
If you are using TypeScript, you should also 安装 the 类型 definitions for :
With the driver 安装ed, you can now 创建 a Worker script that queries your 数据库.
To connect to your 数据库, you will need a 数据库 driver which allows you to authenticate and 查询 your 数据库. For this tutorial, you will use mysql2, one of the most widely used MySQL drivers.
After you have 设置 your 数据库, you will run a SQL 查询 from within your Worker.
Go to your  Worker and open the  file.
The  file is where you configure your Worker's inter操作s with Hyperdrive.
Populate your  file with the following code:

Returns the 响应 as JSON to the client. Hyperdrive automatically cleans up the client connection when the 请求 ends, and keeps the underlying 数据库 connection open in its pool for reuse.
You can 测试 your Worker locally before 部署ing by 运行中 . This runs your Worker code on your machine while connecting to your 数据库.
The  字段 works with both local and remote 数据库s and allows you to connect directly to your 数据库 from your Worker project 运行中 locally. You must specify the SSL/TLS mode if 必需 ( for Postgres,  for MySQL).

You can now 部署 your Worker to make your project accessible on the Internet. To 部署 your Worker, run:

By finishing this tutorial, you have 创建d a Hyperdrive 配置, a Worker to access that 数据库 and 部署ed your project globally.

paragraphs: Hyperdrive 支持s MySQL and MySQL-compatible 数据库s, popular drivers, and 对象 Relational Mapper (ORM) 库 that use those drivers.
:::注意
新功能 to Hyperdrive? Refer to the 快速开始 指南 to learn how to 设置 your first Hyperdrive.
To 创建 a Hyperdrive that connects to an existing MySQL 数据库, use the Wrangler CLI or the Cloudflare 控制面板.
When using Wrangler, replace the placeholder 值 provided to  with the connection 字符串 for your 数据库:
The command above will output the ID of your Hyperdrive, which you will need to set in the Wrangler 配置 file for your Workers project:
This will allow Hyperdrive to generate a dynamic connection 字符串 within your Worker that you can pass to your existing 数据库 driver. Refer to Driver 示例 to learn how to 设置 a 数据库 driver with Hyperdrive.
Refer to the 示例 文档 for 步骤-by-步骤 指南 on how to 设置 Hyperdrive with several popular 数据库 providers.

/hyperdrive/.title: Hyperdrive
paragraphs: CardGrid,
Description,
特性,
LinkTitleCard,
Plan,
相关Product,
Tabs,
TabItem,
LinkButton,
} from "~/components";
Turn your existing regional 数据库 into a globally distributed 数据库.
Hyperdrive is a service that accelerates queries you make to existing 数据库s, making it faster to access your data from across the globe from Cloudflare Workers, irrespective of your users' location.
Hyperdrive 支持s any Postgres or MySQL 数据库, including those hosted on AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, Neon and PlanetScale. Hyperdrive also 支持s Postgres-compatible 数据库s like CockroachDB and Timescale.
You do not need to write 新功能 code or replace your favorite 工具: Hyperdrive works with your existing code and 工具 you use.
Use Hyperdrive's connection 字符串 from your Cloudflare Workers application with your existing Postgres drivers and 对象-relational mapping (ORM) 库:
快速开始
Connect Hyperdrive to your existing 数据库 and 部署 a Worker that queries it.
Hyperdrive allows you to connect to any PostgreSQL or PostgreSQL-compatible 数据库.
Hyperdrive allows you to connect to any MySQL 数据库.
默认-on 缓存 for your most popular queries executed against your 数据库.
构建 无服务器 applications and 部署 instantly across the globe for exceptional 性能, reliability, and scale.
部署 dynamic front-end applications in 记录 time.
Learn about Hyperdrive's 定价.
Learn about Hyperdrive 限制.

Workers.

are 构建ing, and discuss the 平台 with other developers.
Follow @CloudflareDev on Twitter to learn about product announcements, and
what is 新功能 in Cloudflare Developer 平台.

/hyperdrive/platform/limits/.title: 限制
paragraphs: The following 限制 apply to Hyperdrive 配置s, connections, and queries made to your configured 源 数据库s.
These 限制 apply when creating or updating Hyperdrive 配置s.
[^1]: This is a limit enforced by PostgreSQL. Some 数据库 providers may enforce smaller 限制.
These 限制 apply to connections between Hyperdrive and your 源 数据库.
Hyperdrive does not limit the 数字 of concurrent client connections from your Workers. However, Hyperdrive 限制 connections to your 源 数据库 because most hosted 数据库s have connection 限制.

[^2]: Hyperdrive is a distributed system, so a client may be unable to reach an existing pool. In this scenario, a 新功能 pool is established with its own connection allocation. This prioritizes 可用性 over strict limit enforcement, which means connection counts may occasionally exceed the 列表ed 限制.
When Hyperdrive cannot acquire a connection to your 源 数据库, you may see one of the following 错误:
For a complete 列表 of 错误 codes, refer to Troubleshoot and 调试.
These 限制 apply to queries sent through Hyperdrive.
Queries exceeding the maximum duration are terminated. 查询 响应s larger than 50 MB are not cached but are still returned to your Worker.
You can 请求 adjustments to 限制 that conflict with your project goals by contacting Cloudflare. Not all 限制 can be increased.
To 请求 an increase, submit a Limit Increase 请求 form. You can also ask questions in the Hyperdrive channel on Cloudflare's Discord 社区.

/hyperdrive/platform/pricing/.title: 定价
headings: Does connection pooling or 查询 缓存 incur additional charges?
Are cached queries counted the same as uncached queries?
Does Hyperdrive charge for data transfer / egress?
paragraphs: Hyperdrive 限制 are automatically adjusted when 订阅d to a Workers Paid plan. Hyperdrive's connection pooling and 查询 缓存 are included in Workers Paid plan, so do not incur any additional charges.
No. Hyperdrive's built-in cache and connection pooling are included within the stated plans above. There are no hidden 限制 other than those published.
Yes, any 查询 made through Hyperdrive, whether cached or uncached, whether 查询 or mutation, is counted according to the 限制 above.
No.
:::注意
For questions about 定价, refer to the 定价 常见问题s.

headings: Make your first API 请求
