You can always re-enable telemetry collection.

/pages/functions/.title: 函数s
paragraphs: Pages 函数s allows you to 构建 full-stack applications by executing code on the Cloudflare network with Cloudflare Workers. With 函数s, you can introduce application aspects such as authenticating, handling form submissions, or working with 中间件. Workers 运行时 特性 are configurable on Pages 函数s, including 兼容性 with a subset of Node.js APIs and the ability to set a 兼容性 date or 兼容性 flag. Use 函数s to 部署 server-side code to enable dynamic 函数ality without 运行中 a dedicated server.
To provide 反馈 or ask questions on 函数s, join the Cloudflare Developers Discord and connect with the Cloudflare team in the #函数s channel.

/pages/get-started/direct-upload/.title: Direct Upload
/pages/get-started/direct-upload/.description: Upload your prebuilt assets to Pages and 部署 them via the
headings: 部署 your project with drag and drop
创建 a 新功能 部署
paragraphs: Direct Upload enables you to upload your prebuilt assets to Pages and 部署 them to the Cloudflare 全球网络. You should choose Direct Upload over Git integration if you want to integrate your own 构建 平台 or upload from your local computer.
This 指南 will instruct you how to upload your assets using Wrangler or the drag and drop method.
:::注意[You cannot switch to Git integration later]
If you choose Direct Upload, you cannot switch to Git integration later. You will have to 创建 a 新功能 project with Git integration to use automatic 部署s.
Before you 部署 your project with Direct Upload, run the appropriate 构建 command to 构建 your project.
After you have your prebuilt assets 读取y, there are two ways to begin uploading:
Wrangler.
Drag and drop.
:::注意

Within a Direct Upload project, you can switch between creating 部署s with either Wrangler or drag and drop. For existing Git-integrated projects, you can manually 创建 部署s using [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#部署). However, you cannot use drag and drop on the 控制面板 with existing Git-integrated projects.
Below is the 支持的 file 类型s for each Direct Upload options:
Wrangler: A single folder of assets. (Zip files are not 支持的.)
Drag and drop: A zip file or single folder of assets.
To begin, 安装 [](https://docs.npmjs.com/获取ting-started). Then 安装 Wrangler, the Developer 平台 CLI.
登录 to Wrangler with the  command. Then run the  command:

You will then be prompted to specify the project name. Your project will be served at  (or your project name plus a few random characters if your project name is al读取y taken). You will also be prompted to specify your production branch.
Subsequent 部署s will reuse both of these 值s (saved in your  folder).

From here, you have 创建d an empty project and can now 部署 your assets for your first 部署 and for all subsequent 部署s in your production environment. To do this, run the [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#部署) command:
Find the appropriate 构建 output directory for your project in 构建 directory under Framework presets.

To 部署 with drag and drop:

After you have your project 创建d, select 创建 a 新功能 部署 to begin a 新功能 version of your site. Next, choose whether your 新功能 部署 will be made to your production or preview environment. If choosing preview, you can 创建 a 新功能 部署 branch or enter an existing one.

/pages/get-started/git-integration/.title: Git integration
/pages/get-started/git-integration/.description: Connect your Git provider to Pages.
headings: Connect your Git provider to Pages
paragraphs: In this 指南, you will 快速开始 with Cloudflare Pages and 部署 your first website to the Pages 平台 through Git integration. The Git integration enables automatic 构建s and 部署s every time you push a change to your connected GitHub or GitLab repository.
:::注意[You cannot switch to Direct Upload later]

If you 部署 using the Git integration, you cannot switch to Direct Upload later. However, if you al读取y use a Git-integrated project and do not want to 触发器 部署s every time you push a commit, you can disable automatic 部署s on all branches. Then, you can use Wrangler to 部署 directly to your Pages projects and make changes to your Git repository without automatically 触发器ing a 构建.

/pages/configuration/rollbacks/.title: Rollbacks
paragraphs: Rollbacks allow you to instantly revert your project to a previous production 部署.

Any production 部署 that has been 成功fully built is a valid rollback tar获取. When your project has rolled back to a previous 部署, you may still rollback to 部署s that are 新功能er than your current version. 注意 that preview 部署s are not valid rollback tar获取s.

In order to perform a rollback, go to 部署s in your Pages project. Browse the All 部署s 列表 and select the three dotted 操作s menu for the desired tar获取. Select Rollback to this 部署 for a confirmation window to appear. When confirmed, your project's production 部署 will change instantly.

/pages/platform/limits/.title: 限制
paragraphs: Below are 限制 观察d by the Cloudflare 免费计划. For more details on removing these 限制, refer to the Cloudflare plans page.
Each time you push 新功能 code to your Git repository, Pages will 构建 and 部署 your site. You can 构建 up to 500 times per month on the 免费计划. Refer to the Pro and 商业计划s in 定价 if you need more 构建s.
构建s will 超时 after 20 minutes. Concurrent 构建s are counted per 账户.
Based on your Cloudflare plan 类型, a Pages project is limited to a specific 数字 of 自定义域名. This limit is on a per-project basis.
[^1]: If you need more 自定义域名, contact your 账户 team.

Pages uploads each file on your site to Cloudflare's globally distributed network to deliver a low 延迟 experience to every user that visits your site. Cloudflare Pages sites can contain up to 20,000 files on the 免费计划.
付费计划 (such as Pro, Business, and 企业计划s) can have up to 100,000 files per site. To enable this increased limit, set the 环境变量  in your Pages project 设置.
The maximum file size for a single Cloudflare Pages site asset is 25 MiB.
:::注意[Larger Files]
To serve larger files, consider uploading them to R2 and utilizing the public 存储桶 特性. You can also use 自定义域名, such as , for serving these files.
请求s to Pages 函数s count towards your quota for Workers plans, including 请求s from your 函数 to KV or Durable 对象 绑定.
Pages 支持s the Standard usage model.
A  file can have a maximum of 100 头部 规则.
An individual 头部 in a  file can have a maximum of 2,000 characters. For managing larger 头部, it is recommended to implement Pages 函数s.
You can have an unlimited 数字 of preview 部署s 活跃 on your project at a time.
A  file can have a maximum of 2,000 static 重定向s and 100 dynamic 重定向s, for a combined total of 2,100 重定向s. It is recommended to use Bulk 重定向s when you have a need for more than the  file 支持s.

Your Pages site can be managed by an unlimited 数字 of users via the Cloudflare 控制面板. 注意 that this does not correlate with your Git project – you can manage both public and private repositories, open issues, and accept pull 请求s via without impacting your Pages site.

Cloudflare Pages has a soft limit of 100 projects within your 账户 in order to pr事件 abuse. If you need this limit raised, contact your Cloudflare 账户 team or use the Limit Increase 请求 Form at the top of this page.

In order to protect against abuse of the service, Cloudflare may temporarily disable your ability to 创建 新功能 Pages projects, if you are 部署ing a large 数字 of applications in a short amount of time. Contact 支持 if you need this limit increased.

/pipelines/platform/limits/.title: 限制
paragraphs: While in open 测试版, the following 限制 are currently in effect:

/pipelines/pipelines/.title: 管道s
paragraphs: 管道s connect 流s and sinks via SQL 转换ations, which can modify 事件 before writing them to storage. This enables you to shift left, pushing validation, 模式tization, and processing to your ingestion layer to make your queries easy, fast, and correct.
管道s enable you to 过滤器, 转换, enrich, and restructure 事件 in real-time as data flows from 流s to sinks.

/pipelines/platform/pricing/.title: 定价
paragraphs: Cloudflare 管道s is in open 测试版 and available to any developer with a Workers Paid plan.
We are not currently billing for 管道s during open 测试版. However, you will be billed for standard R2 storage and operations for data written by sinks to R2 存储桶s.
We plan to bill based on the volume of data processed by 管道s, 转换ed by 管道s, and delivered to sinks. We'll provide at least 30 days notice before we make any changes or start charging for 管道s usage.

/pipelines/sinks/.title: Sinks
paragraphs: Sinks define destinations for your data in Cloudflare 管道s. They 支持 writing to R2 Data Catalog as Apache Iceberg 表s or to R2 as raw JSON or Parquet files.

Sinks provide exactly-once delivery guarantees, ensuring 事件 are never duplicated or dropped. They can be configured to write files frequently for low-延迟 ingestion or to write larger, less frequent files for better 查询 性能.

/pipelines/reference/.title: 参考
/pipelines/reference/.description: 参考 文档 for Cloudflare 管道s.
paragraphs: 管道s 参考 文档:

/pipelines/streams/.title: 流s
paragraphs: 流s are durable, buffered queues that receive and store 事件 for processing in Cloudflare 管道s. They provide reliable data ingestion via HTTP 端点 and Worker 绑定, ensuring no data loss even during 下游 processing delays or failures.

A single stream can be 读取 by mul提示le 管道s, allowing you to 路由 the same data to different destinations or apply different 转换ations. For 示例, you might send user 事件 to both a real-time 分析 管道 and a data warehouse 管道.
流s currently accept 事件 in JSON format and 支持 both structured 事件 with defined 模式s and unstructured JSON. When a 模式 is provided, 流s will validate and enforce it for incoming 事件.

/privacy-gateway/get-started/.title: 快速开始
headings: 步骤 4 - Relay 请求s through Cloudflare
paragraphs: Privacy Gateway implementation consists of three main parts:
Application Gateway Server/backend 配置 (operated by you).
Client 配置 (operated by you).
Connection to a Privacy Gateway Relay Server (operated by Cloudflare).
Privacy Gateway is currently in closed 测试版. If you are interested, 联系我们.

As a customer of the Privacy Gateway, you also need to add server 支持 for OHTTP by implementing an application gateway server. The application gateway is responsible for decrypting incoming 请求s, forwarding the inner 请求s to their destination, and encrypting the corresponding 响应 back to the client.

The server implementation will handle incoming 请求s and produce 响应s, and it will also advertise its public 键 配置 for clients to access. The public 键 配置 is generated securely and made available via an API. Refer to the 读取ME for details about 配置.
Applications can also implement this 函数ality themselves. Details about public 键 配置, HTTP 消息 加密 and 解密, and server-specific details can be found in the OHTTP specification.
Use the following 资源 for help with server 配置:
Sample gateway server
Gateway library
**Rust**: Gateway library
**JavaScript / TypeScript**: Gateway library

As a customer of the Privacy Gateway, you need to 设置 client-side 支持 for the gateway. Clients are responsible for encrypting 请求s, sending them to the Cloudflare Privacy Gateway, and then decrypting the corresponding 响应s.

Additionally, app developers need to configure the client to fetch or otherwise discover the gateway’s public 键 配置. How this is done depends on how the gateway makes its public 键 配置 available. If you need help with this 配置, 联系我们.
Use the following 资源 for help with client 配置:
**对象ive C**: Sample application
**Rust**: Client library
**JavaScript / TypeScript**: Client library

After you have configured your client and server, review your application to make sure you are only sending intended data to Cloudflare and the application backend. In particular, application data should not contain anything unique to an end-user, as this would invalidate the 优势 that OHTTP provides.
Applications should scrub identifying user data from 请求s forwarded through the Privacy Gateway. This includes, for 示例, names, email addresses, phone 数字s, etc.
Applications should encourage users to disable crash reporting when using Privacy Gateway. Crash 报告 can contain sensitive user 信息rmation and data, including email addresses.

Where possible, application data should be encrypted on the client device with a 键 known only to the client. For 示例, iOS generally has good 支持 for client-side 加密 (and 键 synchronization via the 键Chain). Android likely has similar 特性 available.
Before sending any 请求s, you need to first 设置 your 账户 with Cloudflare. That requires contacting us and providing the URL of your application gateway server.
Then, make sure you are forwarding 请求s to a mutually agreed URL with the following conventions.

/privacy-gateway/.title: Cloudflare Privacy Gateway
paragraphs: Implements the Oblivious HTTP IETF standard to improve client privacy.

Privacy Gateway is a managed service 部署ed on Cloudflare’s 全球网络 that implements part of the Oblivious HTTP (OHTTP) IETF standard. The goal of Privacy Gateway and Oblivious HTTP is to hide the client's IP address when interacting with an application backend.

OHTTP introduces a trusted third party between client and server, called a relay, whose purpose is to forward encrypted 请求s and 响应s between client and server. These 消息s are encrypted between client and server such that the relay learns nothing of the application data, beyond the length of the encrypted 消息 and the server the client is interacting with.
Privacy Gateway is currently in closed 测试版 – available to select privacy-oriented companies and partners. If you are interested, 联系我们.
Learn how to 设置 Privacy Gateway for your application.
Learn about the different parties and data shared in Privacy Gateway.
Learn about how to 查询 Privacy Gateway 指标.

/privacy-gateway/reference/metrics/.title: Privacy Gateway 指标
paragraphs: Privacy Gateway now 支持s enhanced 监控 through our GraphQL API, providing detailed insights into your gateway traffic and 性能. To access these 指标, ensure you have:
A relay gateway 代理 implementation where Cloudflare acts as the oblivious relay party.
An API 令牌 with 分析 读取 权限.
We offer two GraphQL nodes to retrieve 指标:  and . The first node provides comprehensive 请求 data, while the second facilitates grouped 分析.
The  node is designed for detailed insights into individual OHTTP 请求s with adaptive sampling. This node can help in understanding the 性能 and load on your server and client 设置.
必需
Apply 过滤器s to narrow down your data set.  is a 必需 过滤器.
可选
Specify the maximum 数字 of 记录s to return.
Choose how to sort your data, with options for various dimensions and 指标.
int 可选

Total bytes received from the client.
字符串 可选
Airport code of the Cloudflare 数据中心 that served the 请求.
Time 可选
The date and time when the 事件 was 记录ed.
Status code returned by the gateway.
Status code returned by the relay.
This node is useful for a granular view of traffic, helping you identify patterns, 性能 issues, or anomalies in your data flow.

The  node allows for aggregated analysis of OHTTP 请求 指标 with adaptive sampling. This node is particularly useful for identifying trends and patterns across different dimensions of your traffic and operations.
The 数字 of 记录s that meet the criteria.
Specifies the grouping dimensions for your data.
Aggregated totals for various 指标, per dimension.
Dimensions
You can group your 指标 by various dimensions to 获取 a more segmented view of your data:
The airport code of the Cloudflare 数据中心.
Date 可选
The date of OHTTP 请求 指标.
Timestamp truncated to fifteen minutes.
Timestamp truncated to five minutes.
Timestamp truncated to the hour.
Timestamp truncated to the minute.
The appId that generated traffic.
Sum 字段s
Sum 字段s offer a cumulative view of various 指标 over your selected time period:
Total bytes sent from the gateway to the client.
Total bytes from the client to the gateway.
Total 数字 of client 请求 错误.
Total 数字 of gateway 响应 错误.
Utilize the ohttp指标AdaptiveGroups node to gain comprehensive, aggregated insights into your traffic patterns, helping you optimize 性能 and user experience.

/privacy-gateway/reference/legal/.title: Legal
paragraphs: Privacy Gateway is a managed gateway service 部署ed on Cloudflare’s 全球网络 that implements the Oblivious HTTP IETF standard to improve client privacy when connecting to an application backend.

OHTTP introduces a trusted third party (Cloudflare in this case), called a relay, between client and server. The relay’s purpose is to forward 请求s from client to server, and likewise to forward 响应s from server to client. These 消息s are encrypted between client and server such that the relay learns nothing of the application data, beyond the server the client is interacting with.
The Privacy Gateway service follows Cloudflare’s privacy policy.

While Cloudflare will never see the contents of the encrypted application HTTP 请求 proxied through the Privacy Gateway service – because the client will first connect to the OHTTP relay server operated in Cloudflare’s 全球网络– Cloudflare will see the following 信息rmation: the connecting device’s IP address, the application service they are using, including its DNS name and IP address, and metadata associated with the 请求, including the 类型 of browser, device operating system, hardware 配置, and timestamp of the 请求 ("Privacy Gateway 日志").
Cloudflare retains the Privacy Gateway 日志 信息rmation for the most recent quarter plus one month (approximately 124 days).
The application content of 请求s.
The IP address and associated metadata of the Cloudflare Privacy Gateway server the 请求 came from.

/privacy-proxy/concepts/authentication/.title: 身份验证
headings: 身份验证 in double-hop 部署s
代理 A to 代理 B
paragraphs: Privacy 代理 requires clients to authenticate before 代理ing traffic. This page explains the 支持的 身份验证 方法 and when to use them.
Privacy 代理 支持s three 身份验证 方法:
Pre-shared 键s provide a simple way to authenticate during development and proof-of-concept 测试. Cloudflare provides a 密钥 键 that clients include in each 请求.
Include the PSK in the  头部:
The 代理 validates the 键 and allows the connection if it matches.
PSK 身份验证 has limitations that make it unsui表 for production.
**Shared 密钥**: All clients use the same 键, so you cannot revoke access for individual users.
**No 速率限制 per user**: You cannot enforce per-user quotas or 限制.
**Linkability**: The 代理 can link all 请求s using the same PSK, which reduces user privacy.
Use PSK only for 测试. For production 部署s, use Privacy Pass 令牌s.
Privacy Pass is a 协议 that allows clients to authenticate without revealing their identity. 令牌s are cryptographically unlinkable, meaning the 代理 cannot correlate different 请求s from the same user.
Privacy Pass uses a three-party architecture:
**At测试er**: Verifies that the Client is a legitimate user (for 示例, has a valid 账户) and forwards 令牌 请求s to the Issuer.
**Issuer**: Signs blinded 令牌s without learning which Client 请求ed them.
**源 (Privacy 代理)**: Accepts 令牌s as proof of 授权.
The Client sends the 令牌 请求 through the At测试er to maintain unlinkability. This ensures the Issuer cannot correlate 令牌 请求s with specific at测试ation 事件 or Client identities.
The Privacy 代理 validates the 令牌 using the Issuer's public 键. The 代理 learns only that the 令牌 is valid, not who it was issued to.
The 令牌 issuance process:
The client proves their identity to the at测试er (for 示例, by signing in with an 账户).
The at测试er confirms the client is valid.
The client generates blinded 令牌s and sends them to the issuer.
The issuer signs the blinded 令牌s and returns them.
The client unblinds the 令牌s and stores them.
When making a 请求:
The client includes a 令牌 in the  头部.
The 代理 verifies the 令牌 signature with the issuer's public 键.
The 代理 allows the connection if the 令牌 is valid.
Because 令牌s are blinded during issuance, the issuer cannot link 令牌s to specific issuance 请求s. The 代理 sees only that a 令牌 is valid, not who it was issued to.
Privacy Pass 令牌s are included in the  头部 using the  scheme:
For production 部署s using Privacy Pass:
Choose an issuer. Cloudflare can operate a 令牌 issuer, or you can integrate with a third-party issuer.
Configure at测试ation to define how clients prove their identity before receiving 令牌s.
Distribute issuer 配置. Clients need the issuer's public 键 and 端点 to 请求 令牌s.
联系我们 to configure Privacy Pass for your 部署.
Mutual TLS (mTLS) 身份验证 ensures that traffic is both secure and trusted in both directions. The client presents a 证书 to the 代理, and the 代理 validates it before allowing the connection.
The client includes a TLS client 证书 during the TLS 握手. The 代理 validates the 证书 against a configured 证书 authority (CA) and allows the connection if the 证书 is trusted.

You must provision and manage 证书s for each client or service. mTLS is designed for server-to-server communication, not for authenticating individual users. The 代理 can identify the client by its 证书, which reduces privacy compared to Privacy Pass.
Use mTLS for server-to-server 集成 where both parties are trusted services.
In double-hop 部署s, 身份验证 occurs at two levels:
代理 A (which you operate) authenticates users. Common 方法 include:
账户 credentials (username/password, SSO)
Privacy Pass 令牌s issued by your infrastructure
Client 证书s (mTLS)
代理 B authenticates itself to 代理 A using TLS. De待处理 on your 配置, this can use:
Standard TLS 证书s
Raw Public 键 (RPK) TLS extension for reduced 证书 overhead
Privacy Pass Working Group - IETF working group developing the Privacy Pass 协议.
支持ing the la测试 version of the Privacy Pass 协议 - Cloudflare blog post on Privacy Pass implementation.

/privacy-proxy/concepts/geolocation/.title: Geolocation
paragraphs: Privacy 代理 preserves user geolocation without exposing real IP addresses. This ensures location-based services work correctly while maintaining privacy.
Many online services use IP addresses to determine user location:
搜索 engines return locally relevant results.
Content providers enforce regional licensing restrictions.
E-commerce sites show local 定价 and shipping options.
新功能s sites display region-specific content.

Traditional VPNs and proxies often break these services because traffic exits from 数据中心 far from the user's actual location. Privacy 代理 solves this by selecting egress IP addresses that match the user's geographic region.
Privacy 代理 uses geohashes to preserve location without revealing precise coordinates.
A geohash is a compact representation of latitude and longitude. Geohashes use a hierarchical encoding where longer 字符串s represent more precise locations:
Privacy 代理 uses reduced-precision geohashes (typically four to five characters) to locate users to a city or region without pinpointing their exact location.
When a client connects to Privacy 代理:
The client (or first-hop 代理 in double-hop 部署s) determines the user's approximate location.
The client sends a geohash in the  头部.
Privacy 代理 validates the geohash and selects an egress IP address from a pool registered to that geographic area.
Destination servers see the egress IP and geolocate the user to the correct region.
The  头部 includes the geohash and country code:
The format is . The country code helps resolve edge cases where geohashes span country borders.
Cloudflare maintains egress IP pools in hundreds of cities worldwide. When you register egress IPs with geolocation 数据库s, they map to specific locations.
Privacy 代理 achieves:
**City-level accuracy** by 默认, so users 获取 locally relevant 搜索 results.
**Country-level accuracy** as a 回退 if city-level is not available.
Users can opt for coarser geolocation (country and time区域 only) if they prefer less precise location sharing.

A simple way to verify geolocation accuracy is to 搜索 for "pizza near me" through the 代理. If results show pizza places in the user's actual city rather than a distant 数据中心, geolocation is working correctly.

Privacy 代理 achieves better geolocation precision over IPv6. If your 源服务器s 支持 IPv6 (AAAA DNS 记录s), the 代理 prefers IPv6 egress addresses, which are registered with greater geographic precision than IPv4 equivalents.
To maximize geolocation accuracy for your users, ensure your services are reachable over IPv6.
In double-hop 部署s, 代理 A (which you operate) is responsible for determining and forwarding the geohash:
代理 A geolocates the client's IP address.
代理 A converts the location to a geohash with appropriate precision.
代理 A includes the geohash in the forwarded CONNECT 请求 to 代理 B.
代理 B (Cloudflare) selects an egress IP based on the geohash.
The geohash is cryptographically protected to pr事件 clients from spoofing their location.
Geo-egress: Improving WARP user experience on a larger network - How Cloudflare implements geolocation-aware egress.

paragraphs: Description,

A MASQUE-based forward 代理 that protects user privacy while preserving geolocation accuracy.
Privacy 代理 is a managed 代理 service that runs on Cloudflare's 全球网络. It uses the MASQUE 协议 suite to 代理 TCP and UDP traffic via HTTP CONNECT and CONNECT-UDP 方法 over HTTP/2 and HTTP/3.

Privacy 代理 separates user identity from user activity. Users authenticate to the 代理 without revealing which destinations they visit, and destination servers see 请求s from Cloudflare IP addresses without learning who made them.
Privacy 代理 powers services like Microsoft Edge Secure Network and serves as a second-hop relay for iCloud Private Relay.
部署 Privacy 代理 as a standalone 代理 where Cloudflare handles 身份验证, 代理ing, and egress.
Operate your own first-hop 代理 to authenticate users, then relay traffic through Cloudflare for additional privacy separation.
Maintain accurate geolocation for users without exposing their real IP addresses, ensuring location-relevant content and services work correctly.
Authenticate users with Privacy Pass 令牌s for production 部署s, ensuring privacy-preserving access control.
Implements the Oblivious HTTP (OHTTP) standard for 请求-level privacy, hiding client IP addresses from application backends.
Cloudflare's 消费者 VPN application that uses similar privacy-preserving 代理 technology.
Privacy 代理 is available as a managed service for Enterprise customers. 联系我们 to discuss your use case and 快速开始.

headings: Use curl for 测试 locally
paragraphs: This 指南 walks you through connecting to Privacy 代理 and verifying that traffic is proxied correctly.
Privacy 代理 is a managed service. Before you can connect, Cloudflare will provision an 端点 and provide you with:
**代理 端点 URL**: The 主机名 for your Privacy 代理 instance (for 示例, ).
**Pre-shared 键 (PSK)**: A 密钥 键 for proof-of-concept 身份验证.
**Egress IP ranges**: The IP addresses that destination servers will see for proxied traffic.
联系我们 to 请求 access and receive your 配置 details.

Privacy 代理 accepts connections over HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 using the HTTP CONNECT method. Because Privacy 代理 requires 身份验证 头部, you cannot configure browsers to connect directly. Instead, use one of the following approaches:
For quick 测试s, use curl with the  and  flags to pass 身份验证 directly:
Chaussette is a local SOCKS5 代理 that handles 身份验证 and forwards 请求s to Privacy 代理.
Start Chaussette with your PSK and 代理 端点:
Configure your browser to use the local SOCKS5 代理:
To confirm that traffic is 路由 through Privacy 代理, check your apparent IP address:
The 响应 includes connection metadata. Look for the  字段, which should show a Cloudflare egress IP address rather than your real IP.
The  值 confirms the egress IP address used by the 代理.
Privacy 代理 preserves user geolocation by selecting egress IP addresses based on the client's location. You can specify a geohash to 测试 this behavior:
The  头部 provides a geohash that the 代理 uses to select an appropriate egress IP. The format is .
The 响应 should show a  值 corresponding to the geohash region.

/privacy-proxy/concepts/deployment-model.title: 部署 models
headings: 示例: Microsoft Edge Secure Network
paragraphs: Privacy 代理 支持s two 部署 architectures: single-hop and double-hop. The right choice depends on your privacy 要求 and operational p参考s.
In a single-hop 部署, Cloudflare operates the entire 代理 infrastructure. Clients connect directly to Cloudflare's Privacy 代理, which handles 身份验证, 代理ing, and egress.
The client establishes an HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 connection to the Cloudflare 代理 端点.
The client authenticates using Privacy Pass 令牌s or a pre-shared 键.
The client sends CONNECT 请求s to establish 隧道s to destination servers.
Cloudflare proxies traffic and selects egress IP addresses based on client geolocation.
Single-hop 部署 works well when:
You want Cloudflare to manage the complete 代理 infrastructure.
Your privacy model requires hiding client IP addresses from destinations, but not from the 代理 operator.
You need a straightforward integration with minimal client-side changes.

Microsoft Edge Secure Network uses single-hop 部署. The Edge browser connects directly to Cloudflare's Privacy 代理, which handles 身份验证 via Privacy Pass and proxies traffic to destination servers. Users 获取 protection from network 观察rs and destination servers without needing to configure additional infrastructure.

In a double-hop 部署, you operate the first 代理 (代理 A), and Cloudflare operates the second 代理 (代理 B). This 创建s stronger privacy separation because no single party sees both user identity and destination.
The client connects to 代理 A, which you operate.
代理 A authenticates the user and verifies they can use the service.
代理 A establishes a tunnel to Cloudflare's 代理 B, forwarding the client's CONNECT 请求.
代理 B connects to the destination and proxies traffic.
代理 B selects egress IPs based on geolocation provided by 代理 A.
The double-hop architecture ensures:
代理 A knows who the user is but cannot see where they are going (the destination is encrypted). 代理 B knows the destination but not who is making the 请求. Neither party has the complete picture.
Double-hop 部署 works well when:
You need stronger privacy guarantees where no single operator sees both identity and destination.
You want to maintain control over user 身份验证 and 账户 management.
Regulatory or compliance 要求 mandate separation of user data.

iCloud Private Relay uses double-hop 部署. Apple operates the first-hop 代理, which authenticates users with their Apple ID and encrypts the destination. Cloudflare operates the second-hop 代理, which decrypts the destination and connects to the server. Apple knows who the user is but not where they browse. Cloudflare knows the destinations but not who is browsing.
Consider these questions when selecting a 部署 model:
Who should manage user 身份验证?
If you want Cloudflare to handle 身份验证, use single-hop. If you need control over user 账户s, use double-hop.
What are your privacy 要求?
If your threat model requires that no single party sees both user identity and browsing activity, use double-hop.
What operational capacity do you have?
Double-hop requires you to operate and maintain a 代理. If you prefer a fully managed solution, use single-hop.
联系我们 to discuss which 部署 model fits your use case.

/pulumi/.title: Pulumi
paragraphs: 创建, 部署, and manage Cloudflare 资源 in various programming languages.

Provision and manage Cloudflare using infrastructure as code through Pulumi. With the Pulumi Cloudflare package, you can 构建, 部署, and manage Cloudflare 资源 using standard programming languages (TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, .NET, Java, Go, and YAML). You can define the desired state for your infrastructure in code and leverage language 特性 like loops, 函数s, classes, and package management.
Pulumi is open source and uses the Apache 2.0 license.
Use TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, .Net, Java, or YAML to write Pulumi programs. Each language is as capable as the other and 支持s the entire Pulumi Registry.
Pulumi Cloud fully manages infrastructure state and 密钥, provides rich 搜索 capabilities, and more.
Pulumi AI is an 实验性 特性 that lets you use natural-language prompts to generate Pulumi infrastructure-as-code programs in any language.
Pulumi ESC provides centralized management of environments, 密钥, and 配置s.
To 了解更多 about Pulumi.
Report Pulumi 配置 issues via GitHub.

/queues/configuration/configure-queues/.title: Configure Queues
paragraphs: Cloudflare Queues can be configured using Wrangler, the command-line interface for Cloudflare's Developer 平台, which includes Workers, R2, and other developer products.
Each 生产者 and 消费者 Worker has a Wrangler 配置 file that specifies 环境变量, 触发器s, and 资源, such as a 队列. To enable Worker-to-resource communication, you must 设置 a 绑定 in your Worker project's Wrangler file.
Use the options below to configure your 队列.
:::注意
Below are options for queues, refer to the Wrangler 文档 for a full 参考 of the Wrangler 配置 file.
The following 队列 level 设置 can be configured using Wrangler:
How long a published 消息 is delayed for, before it is delivered to 消费者s.
Must be between 0 and 86400 (24 hours).
默认s to 0.
How long 消息s are retained on the 队列.
默认s to 345600 (4 days).
Must be between 60 and 1209600 (14 days)

A 生产者 is a Cloudflare Worker that writes to one or more queues. A 生产者 can accept 消息s over HTTP, asynchronously write 消息s when handling 请求s, and/or write to a 队列 from within a Durable 对象. Any Worker can write to a 队列.
To produce to a 队列, 设置 a 绑定 in your Wrangler file. These options should be used when a Worker wants to send 消息s to a 队列.
队列
The name of the 队列.
绑定
The name of the 绑定, which is a JavaScript 变量.
To consume 消息s from one or more queues, 设置 a 绑定 in your Wrangler file. These options should be used when a Worker wants to receive 消息s from a 队列.
Refer to 限制 to review the maximum 值s for each of these options.
max\_batch\_size
The maximum 数字 of 消息s allowed in each batch.
默认s to  消息s.
max\_batch\_超时
The maximum 数字 of seconds to wait until a batch is full.
默认s to  seconds.
max\_重试
The maximum 数字 of 重试 for a 消息, if it fails or [](/queues/配置/javascript-apis/#消息batch) is invoked.
默认s to  重试.
dead\_letter\_队列
The name of another 队列 to send a 消息 if it fails processing at least  times.
If a  is not defined, 消息s that repeatedly fail processing will 事件ually be discarded.
If there is no 队列 with the specified name, it will be 创建d automatically.
max\_并发
The maximum 数字 of concurrent 消费者s allowed to run at once. Leaving this unset will mean that the 数字 of 调用s will scale to the currently 支持的 maximum.
Refer to 消费者 并发 for more 信息rmation on how 消费者s autoscale, particularly when 消息s are retried.
A 队列 can have a HTTP-based 消费者 that pulls from the 队列. This 消费者 can be any HTTP-speaking service that can communicate over the Internet. Review Pull 消费者s to learn how to configure a pull-based 消费者.

/queues/configuration/batching-retries/.title: Batching, 重试 and Delays
paragraphs: When configuring a 消费者 Worker for a 队列, you can also define how 消息s are batched as they are delivered.
Batching can:
Reduce the total 数字 of times your 消费者 Worker needs to be invoked (which can reduce costs).
Allow you to batch 消息s when writing to an external API or service (reducing writes).
Disperse load over time, especially if your 生产者 Workers are associated with user-facing activity.
There are two ways to configure how 消息s are batched. You configure batching when connecting your 消费者 Worker to a 队列.
- The maximum size of a batch delivered to a 消费者 (默认s to 10 消息s).
- the _maximum_ amount of time the 队列 will wait before delivering a batch to a 消费者 (默认s to 5 seconds)
:::注意[Batch size 配置]
Both  and  work to获取her. Whichever limit is reached first will 触发器 the delivery of a batch.

For 示例, a  and a  means that if 30 消息s are written to the 队列, the 消费者 will receive a batch of 30 消息s. However, if it takes longer than 10 seconds for those 30 消息s to be written to the 队列, then the 消费者 will 获取 a batch of 消息s that contains however many 消息s were on the 队列 at the time (somewhere between 1 and 29, in this case).
:::注意[Empty queues]

When a 队列 is empty, a push-based (Worker) 消费者's  处理程序 will not be invoked until there are 消息s to deliver. A 队列 does not attempt to push empty batches to a 消费者 and thus does not invoke unnecessary 读取s.
Pull-based 消费者s that attempt to pull from a 队列, even when empty, will incur a 读取 operation.

When determining what size and 超时 设置 to configure, you will want to consider 延迟 (how long can you wait to receive 消息s?), overall batch size (when writing to external systems), and cost (fewer-but-larger batches).
The following batch-level 设置 can be configured to adjust how Queues delivers batches to your configured 消费者.

You can acknowledge individual 消息s within a batch by explicitly acknowledging each 消息 as it is processed. 消息s that are explicitly acknowledged will not be re-delivered, even if your 队列 消费者 fails on a subsequent 消息 and/or fails to return 成功fully when processing a batch.
Each 消息 can be acknowledged as you process it within a batch, and avoids the entire batch from being re-delivered if your 消费者 throws an 错误 during batch processing.
Acknowledging individual 消息s is useful when you are calling external APIs, writing 消息s to a 数据库, or otherwise performing non-idempotent (state changing) 操作s on individual 消息s.
To explicitly acknowledge a 消息 as delivered, call the  method on the 消息.

You can also call  to explicitly force a 消息 to be redelivered in a subsequent batch. This is referred to as "negative acknowledgement". This can be particularly useful when you want to process the rest of the 消息s in that batch without throwing an 错误 that would force the entire batch to be redelivered.

You can also acknowledge or negatively acknowledge 消息s at a batch level with  and . Calling  on the batch of 消息s () delivered to your 消费者 Worker has the same behaviour as a 消费者 Worker that 成功fully returns (does not throw an 错误).
注意 that calls to ,  and their  /  equivalents follow the below precedence 规则:
If you call  on a 消息, subsequent calls to  or  are silently ignored.
If you call  on a 消息 and then call : the  is ignored. The first method call wins in all cases.

If you call either  or  on a single 消息, and then either/any of  or  on the batch, the call on the single 消息 takes precedence. That is, the batch-level call does not apply to that 消息 (or 消息s, if mul提示le calls were made).

When a 消息 is 已失败 to be delivered, the 默认 behaviour is to 重试 delivery three times before marking the delivery as 已失败. You can set  (默认s to 3) when configuring your 消费者, but in most cases we recommend leaving this as the 默认.
消息s that reach the configured maximum 重试 will be 删除d from the 队列, or if a dead-letter 队列 (DLQ) is configured, written to the DLQ instead.
:::注意
Each 重试 counts as an additional 读取 operation per Queues 定价.

When a single 消息 within a batch fails to be delivered, the entire batch is retried, unless you have explicitly acknowledged a 消息 (or 消息s) within that batch. For 示例, if a batch of 10 消息s is delivered, but the 8th 消息 fails to be delivered, all 10 消息s will be retried and thus redelivered to your 消费者 in full.
:::注意[Retried 消息s and 消费者 并发]
重试ing 消息s with  or calling  on a batch will not cause the 消费者 to autoscale down if 消费者 并发 is 已启用. Refer to 消费者 并发 to 了解更多.
When publishing 消息s to a 队列, or when marking a 消息 or batch for 重试, you can choose to delay 消息s from being processed for a period of time.

Delaying 消息s allows you to defer 任务s until later, and/or respond to backpressure when consuming from a 队列. For 示例, if an 上游 API you are calling to returns a , you can delay 消息s to slow down how quickly you are consuming them before they are re-processed.
消息s can be delayed by up to 24 hours.
Configuring delivery and 重试 delays via the  CLI or when developing locally requires  version  or greater. Use  to always use the la测试 version of .
To delay a 消息 or batch of 消息s when sending to a 队列, you can provide a  parameter when sending a 消息.
You can also configure a 默认, global delay on a per-队列 basis by passing  when creating a 队列 via the  CLI:
When consuming 消息s from a 队列, you can choose to explicitly mark 消息s to be retried. 消息s can be retried and delayed individually, or as an entire batch.
To delay an individual 消息 within a batch:
To delay a batch of 消息s:

You can also choose to set a 默认 重试 delay to any 消息s that are retried due to either implicit failure or when calling  explicitly. This is set at the 消费者 level, and is 支持的 in both push-based (Worker) and pull-based (HTTP) 消费者s.
Delays can be configured via the  CLI:
Delays can also be configured in the Wrangler 配置 file with the  setting for 生产者s (when sending) and/or the  (when 重试ing) per-消费者:
If you use both the  CLI and the Wrangler 配置 file to change the 设置 associated with a 队列 or a 队列 消费者, the most recent 配置 change will take effect.
Refer to the Queues REST API 文档 to learn how to configure 消息 delays and 重试 delays programmatically.
消息s can be delayed by 默认 at the 队列 level, or per-消息 (or batch).
Per-消息/batch delay 设置 take precedence over 队列-level 设置.
Setting  on a 消息 when sending or 重试ing will ignore any 队列-level delays and cause the 消息 to be delivered in the next batch.
A 消息 sent or retried with  to a 队列 with a shorter 默认 delay will still respect the 消息-level setting.
You can apply a backoff algorithm to increasingly delay 消息s based on the current 数字 of attempts to deliver the 消息.
Each 消息 delivered to a 消费者 includes an  property that tracks the 数字 of delivery attempts made.
For 示例, to generate an exponential backoff for a 消息, you can 创建 a helper 函数 that calculates this for you:
In your 消费者, you then pass the 值 of  and your desired delay factor as the argument to  when calling  on an individual 消息:

/queues/configuration/pull-consumers/.title: Pull 消费者s
headings: How to choose between push or pull 消费者

A pull-based 消费者 allows you to pull from a 队列 over HTTP from any environment and/or programming language outside of Cloudflare Workers. A pull-based 消费者 can be useful when your 消息 consumption rate is limited by 上游 infrastructure or long-运行中 任务s.
Deciding whether to configure a push-based 消费者 or a pull-based 消费者 will depend on how you are using your queues, as well as the 配置 of infrastructure 上游 from your 队列 消费者.

**Starting with a push-based 消费者 is the easiest way to 快速开始 and consume from a 队列**. A push-based 消费者 runs on Workers, and by 默认, will automatically scale up and consume 消息s as they are written to the 队列.

Use a pull-based 消费者 if you need to consume 消息s from existing infrastructure outside of Cloudflare Workers, and/or where you need to carefully control how fast 消息s are consumed. A pull-based 消费者 must explicitly make a call to pull (and then acknowledge) 消息s from the 队列, only when it is 读取y to do so.
You can remove and attach a 新功能 消费者 on a 队列 at any time, allowing you to change from a pull-based to a push-based 消费者 if your 要求 change.
:::注意[Retrieve an API bearer 令牌]
To configure a pull-based 消费者, 创建 an API 令牌 with both the  and  权限. A 消费者 must be able to write to a 队列 to acknowledge 消息s.
To configure a pull-based 消费者 and receive 消息s from a 队列, you need to:
Enable HTTP pull for the 队列.
创建 a valid 身份验证 令牌 for the HTTP client.
Pull 消息 batches from the 队列.
Acknowledge and/or 重试 消息s within a batch.
You can enable HTTP pull or change a 队列 from push-based to pull-based via the the  CLI or via the Cloudflare 控制面板. Enabling HTTP pull from a Wrangler 配置 file is no longer 支持的.
:::注意
If you have specified  in your Wrangler 配置 file, remove and re部署. Your Worker will retain access to the HTTP pull 端点, and HTTP pull will remain 已启用 on your 队列.
You can enable a pull-based 消费者 on any existing 队列 by using the  sub-commands and providing a 队列 name.
If you have an existing push-based 消费者, you will need to remove that first.  will return an 错误 if you attempt to call  on a 队列 with an existing 消费者 配置:
If you remove the Worker 消费者 with  but do not 删除 the  配置 from your Wrangler 配置 file, subsequent 部署s of your Worker will fail when they attempt to add a conflicting 消费者 配置.
Ensure you remove the 消费者 配置 first.
HTTP Pull 消费者s require an API 令牌 with the  and  权限.
Both 读取 _and_ write are 必需 as a pull-based 消费者 needs to write to the 队列 state to acknowledge the 消息s it receives. Consuming 消息s mutates the 队列.
API 令牌s are presented as Bearer 令牌s in the  头部 of a HTTP 请求 in the format . The following 示例 shows how to pass an API 令牌 using the  HTTP client:
You may authenticate and run mul提示le concurrent pull-based 消费者s against a single 队列.
To 创建 an API 令牌:
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板.
Go to **My Profile** > API 令牌s.
Select **创建 令牌**.
Scroll to the bottom of the page and select **创建 Custom 令牌**.
Give the 令牌 a name. For 示例, .
Under the **权限** section, choose **账户** and then **Queues**. Ensure you have selected **Edit** (读取+write).
(可选) Select **All 账户s** (默认) or a specific 账户 to scope the 令牌 to.
Select **Continue to summary** and then **创建 令牌**.
You will need to 注意 the 令牌 down: it will only be displayed once.
To pull a 消息, make a HTTP POST 请求 to the Queues REST API with a JSON-encoded 正文 that 可选ly specifies a  and a , or an empty JSON 对象 ():
This will return an 数组 of 消息s (up to the specified ) in the below format:

Pull 消费者s follow a "short polling" approach: if there are 消息s available to be delivered, Queues will return a 响应 immediately with 消息s up to the configured . If there are no 消息s to deliver, Queues will return an empty 响应. Queues does not hold an open connection (often referred to as "long polling") if there are no 消息s to deliver.
The [](/api/资源/queues/sub资源/消息s/方法/pull/) and [](/api/资源/queues/sub资源/消息s/方法/ack/) 端点 use the 新功能  API format, as defined in the Queues API 文档.
The undocumented  端点 are not 支持的 and will be 已弃用 as of June 30th, 2024.
Each 消息 对象 has five 字段s:
- this may be base64 encoded based on the content-类型 the 消息 was published as.
- a unique, 读取-only ephemeral identifier for the 消息.
- when the 消息 was published to the 队列 in milliseconds since the Unix epoch. This allows you to determine how old a 消息 is by subtracting it from the current timestamp.
- how many times the 消息 has been attempted to be delivered in full. When this reaches the 值 of , the 消息 will not be re-delivered and will be 删除d from the 队列 permanently.
- the encoded lease ID of the 消息. The  is used to explicitly acknowledge or 重试 the 消息.

The  allows your pull 消费者 to explicitly acknowledge some, none or all 消息s in the batch or mark them for 重试. If 消息s are not acknowledged or marked for 重试 by the 消费者, then they will be marked for re-delivery once the  is reached. A  is no longer valid once this 超时 has been reached.
You can configure both  and  when pulling from a 队列:
(默认s to 5; max 100) - how many 消息s are returned to the 消费者 in each pull.

(默认s to 30 second; max 12 hours) - defines how long the 消费者 has to explicitly acknowledge 消息s delivered in the batch based on their . Once this 超时 expires, 消息s are assumed unacknowledged and 队列d for re-delivery again.

You may have mul提示le HTTP clients pulling from the same 队列 concurrently: each client will receive a unique batch of 消息s and retain the "lease" on those 消息s up until the  expires, or until those 消息s are marked for 重试.

消息s marked for 重试 will be put back into the 队列 and can be delivered to any 消费者. 消息s are _not_ tied to a specific 消费者, as 消费者s do not have an identity and to avoid a slow or stuck 消费者 from holding up processing of 消息s in a 队列.
Mul提示le 消费者s can be useful in cases where you have mul提示le 上游 资源 (for 示例, GPU infrastructure), where you want to autoscale based on the backlog of a 队列, and/or cost.
消息s pulled by a 消费者 need to be either acknowledged or marked for 重试.
To acknowledge and/or mark 消息s to be retried, make a HTTP  请求 to  端点 of your 队列 per the Queues REST API by providing an 数组 of  对象s to acknowledge and/or 重试:
You may 可选ly specify the 数字 of seconds to delay a 消息 for when marking it for 重试 by providing a  对象 in the  数组:
Additionally:
You should provide every  in the 请求 to the  端点 if you are processing those 消息s in your 消费者. If you do not acknowledge a 消息, it will be marked for re-delivery (put back in the 队列).

You can 可选ly mark 消息s to be retried: for 示例, if there is an 错误 processing the 消息 or you have 上游 resource pressure. Explicitly marking a 消息 for 重试 will place it back into the 队列 immediately, instead of waiting for a (potentially long)  to be reached.
You can make mul提示le calls to the  端点 as you make progress through a batch of 消息s, but we recommend grouping acknowledgements to reduce the 数字 of API calls 必需.

Queues aims to be permissive when it comes to lease IDs: if a 消费者 acknowledges a 消息 by its lease ID _after_ the visibility 超时 is reached, Queues will still accept that acknowledgment. If the 消息 was delivered to another 消费者 during the intervening period, it will also be able to acknowledge the 消息 without an 错误.
:::注意
When attaching a pull-based 消费者 to a 队列, you should ensure that 消息s are sent with only a ,  or  content 类型.
The 默认 content 类型 is .
Pull-based 消费者s cannot decode the  content 类型 as it is specific to the Workers 运行时.
When publishing to a 队列 that has an external 消费者, you should be aware that certain content 类型s may be encoded in a way that allows them to be safely serialized within a JSON 对象.
For both the  and  content 类型s, this means that they will be base64-encoded (RFC 4648). The  类型 will be sent as a plain UTF-8 encoded 字符串.
Your 消费者 will need to decode the  and  类型s before operating on the data.

/queues/configuration/dead-letter-queues.title: Dead Letter Queues
paragraphs: A Dead Letter 队列 (DLQ) is a common concept in a messaging system, and represents where 消息s are sent when a delivery failure occurs with a 消费者 after  is reached. A Dead Letter 队列 is like any other 队列, and can be produced to and consumed from independently.

With Cloudflare Queues, a Dead Letter 队列 is defined within your 消费者 配置. 消息s are delivered to the DLQ when they reach the configured 重试 limit for the 消费者. Without a DLQ configured, 消息s that reach the 重试 limit are 删除d permanently.
For 示例, the following 消费者 配置 would send 消息s to our DLQ named  after 重试ing delivery (by 默认, 3 times):
You can also configure a DLQ when creating a 消费者 from the command-line using :
To process 消息s placed on your DLQ, you need to configure a 消费者 for that 队列 as you would with any other 队列.
消息s delivered to a DLQ without an 活跃 消费者 will persist for four (4) days before being 删除d from the 队列.

/queues/event-subscriptions/.title: 事件 subscriptions
/queues/event-subscriptions/.description: 订阅 to 事件 from Cloudflare services to 构建 custom 工作流s, 集成, and logic with Workers.
paragraphs: 事件 subscriptions allow you to receive 消息s when 事件 occur across your Cloudflare 账户. Cloudflare products (e.g., KV, Workers AI, Workers) can publish structured 事件 to a 队列, which you can then consume with Workers or HTTP pull 消费者s to 构建 custom 工作流s, 集成, or logic.

An 事件 is a structured 记录 of something happening in your Cloudflare 账户 – like a Workers AI batch 请求 being 队列d, a Worker 构建 completing, or an R2 存储桶 being 创建d. When you 订阅 to these 事件, your 队列 will automatically start receiving 消息s when the 事件 occur.

paragraphs: Cloudflare Queues is integrated with Cloudflare Workers. To send and receive 消息s, you must use a Worker.
A Worker that can send 消息s to a 队列 is a 生产者 Worker, while a Worker that can receive 消息s from a 队列 is a 消费者 Worker. It is possible for the same Worker to be a 生产者 and 消费者, if desired.

In the future, we expect to 支持 other APIs, such as HTTP 端点 to send or receive 消息s. To report bugs or 请求 特性, go to the Cloudflare 社区 Forums. To give 反馈, go to the [](https://discord.cloudflare.com) Discord channel.

You have built a 队列 and a 生产者 Worker to publish 消息s to the 队列. You will now 创建 a 消费者 Worker to consume the 消息s published to your 队列. Without a 消费者 Worker, the 消息s will stay on the 队列 until they expire, which 默认s to four (4) days.
A 消费者 Worker receives 消息s from your 队列. When the 消费者 Worker receives your 队列's 消息s, it can write them to another source, such as a logging console or storage 对象s.

In this 指南, you will 创建 a 消费者 Worker and use it to log and inspect the 消息s with [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#tail). You will 创建 your 消费者 Worker in the same Worker project that you 创建d your 生产者 Worker.

Queues also 支持s pull-based 消费者s, which allows any HTTP-based client to consume 消息s from a 队列. This 指南 创建s a push-based 消费者 using Cloudflare Workers.

In this 示例, your 消费者 Worker 转换s the 队列's JSON formatted 消息 into a 字符串 and 日志 that output. In a real world application, your 消费者 Worker can be configured to write 消息s to 对象存储 (such as R2), write to a 数据库 (like D1), further process 消息s before calling an external API (such as an email API) or a data warehouse with your legacy cloud provider.

(默认s to 10) helps to reduce the amount of times your 消费者 Worker needs to be called. Instead of being called for every 消息, it will only be called after 10 消息s have entered the 队列.

You should receive a  消息 in your browser window.
If you receive a  消息, refresh the URL a few times to generate 消息s and push them onto the 队列.
With  运行中, your 消费者 Worker will start logging the 请求s generated by refreshing.
If you refresh less than 10 times, it may take a few seconds for the 消息s to appear because batch 超时 is configured for 10 seconds. After 10 seconds, 消息s should arrive in your terminal.

If you 获取 错误 when you refresh, check that the 队列 name you 创建d in 步骤 2 and the 队列 you 参考d in your Wrangler file is the same. You should ensure that your 生产者 Worker is returning  and is not returning an 错误.
By completing this 指南, you have now 创建d a 队列, a 生产者 Worker that publishes 消息s to that 队列, and a 消费者 Worker that consumes those 消息s from it.

/queues/reference/how-queues-works/.title: How Queues Works
headings: 创建 a 消费者 Worker
paragraphs: Cloudflare Queues is a flexible messaging 队列 that allows you to 队列 消息s for asynchronous processing. 消息 queues are great at decoupling components of applications, like the checkout and order fulfillment services for an e-commerce site. Decoupled services are easier to reason about, 部署, and implement, allowing you to ship 特性 that delight your customers without worrying about synchronizing complex 部署s. Queues also allow you to batch and buffer calls to 下游 services and APIs.
There are four major concepts to understand with Queues:
Queues
生产者s
消费者s
消息s
A 队列 is a buffer or 列表 that automatically scales as 消息s are written to it, and allows a 消费者 Worker to pull 消息s from that same 队列.
Queues are designed to be reliable, and 消息s written to a 队列 should never be lost once the write succeeds. Similarly, 消息s are not 删除d from a 队列 until the 消费者 has 成功fully consumed the 消息.
Queues does not guarantee that 消息s will be delivered to a 消费者 in the same order in which they are published.
Developers can 创建 mul提示le queues. Creating mul提示le queues can be useful to:
Separate different use-cases and processing 要求: for 示例, a logging 队列 vs. a password reset 队列.
Horizontally scale your overall 吞吐量 (消息s per second) by using mul提示le queues to scale out.
Configure different batching strategies for each 消费者 connected to a 队列.
For most applications, a single 生产者 Worker per 队列, with a single 消费者 Worker consuming 消息s from that 队列 allows you to logically separate the processing for each of your queues.
A 生产者 is the term for a client that is publishing or producing 消息s on to a 队列. A 生产者 is configured by 绑定 a 队列 to a Worker and writing 消息s to the 队列 by calling that 绑定.
For 示例, if we bound a 队列 named  to a 绑定 of , 消息s can be written to the 队列 by calling  on the 绑定:
:::注意
You can also use [](/workers/运行时-apis/context/#waituntil) to send the 消息 without blocking the 响应.
注意 that because  is non-blocking, any 错误 raised from the  or  方法 on a 队列 will be implicitly ignored.

A 队列 can have mul提示le 生产者 Workers. For 示例, you may have mul提示le 生产者 Workers writing 事件 or 日志 to a shared 队列 based on incoming HTTP 请求s from users. There is no limit to the total 数字 of 生产者 Workers that can write to a single 队列.
Additionally, mul提示le queues can be bound to a single Worker. That single Worker can decide which 队列 to write to (or write to mul提示le) based on any logic you define in your code.

消息s published to a 队列 can be published in different formats, de待处理 on what interoperability is needed with your 消费者. The 默认 content 类型 is , which means that any 对象 that can be passed to  will be accepted.
To explicitly set the content 类型 or specify an alternative content 类型, pass the  option to the  method of your 队列:
To only accept simple 字符串s when writing to a 队列, set  instead:
The [](/queues/配置/javascript-apis/#队列scontent类型) API 文档 describes how each format is serialized to a 队列.
Queues 支持s two 类型s of 消费者:
A 消费者 Worker, which is push-based: the Worker is invoked when the 队列 has 消息s to deliver.
A HTTP pull 消费者, which is pull-based: the 消费者 calls the 队列 端点 over HTTP to receive and then acknowledge 消息s.
A 队列 can only have one 类型 of 消费者 configured.
A 消费者 is the term for a client that is subscribing to or consuming 消息s from a 队列. In its most basic form, a 消费者 is defined by creating a  处理程序 in a Worker:
You then connect that 消费者 to a 队列 with  or by defining a  配置 in your Wrangler 配置 file manually:
重要ly, each 队列 can only have one 活跃 消费者. This allows Cloudflare Queues to achieve at least once delivery and minimize the risk of duplicate 消息s beyond that.
:::注意[Best practice]
Configure a single 消费者 per 队列. This both logically separates your queues, and ensures that 错误 (failures) in processing 消息s from one 队列 do not impact your other queues.
Notably, you can use the same 消费者 with mul提示le queues. The 队列 处理程序 that defines your 消费者 Worker will be invoked by the queues it is connected to.
The  that is passed to your  处理程序 includes a  property with the name of the 队列 the batch was 读取 from.
This can reduce the amount of code you need to write, and allow you to process 消息s based on the name of your queues.
For 示例, a 消费者 configured to consume 消息s from mul提示le queues would resemble the following:
To remove a 队列 from your project, run  and then remove the desired 队列 below the  in Wrangler file.
A 队列 can have a HTTP-based 消费者 that pulls from the 队列, instead of 消息s being pushed to a Worker.
This 消费者 can be any HTTP-speaking service that can communicate over the Internet. Review the pull 消费者 指南 to learn how to configure a pull-based 消费者 for a 队列.
A 消息 is the 对象 you are producing to and consuming from a 队列.
Any JSON serializable 对象 can be published to a 队列. For most developers, this means either simple 字符串s or JSON 对象s. You can explicitly set the content 类型 when sending a 消息.

消息s themselves can be batched when delivered to a 消费者. By 默认, 消息s within a batch are treated as all or nothing when determining 重试. If the last 消息 in a batch fails to be processed, the entire batch will be retried. You can also choose to explicitly acknowledge 消息s as they are 成功fully processed, and/or mark individual 消息s to be retried.

/queues/reference/delivery-guarantees/.title: Delivery guarantees
paragraphs: Delivery guarantees define how strongly a messaging system enforces the delivery of 消息s it processes.

As you make stronger guarantees about 消息 delivery, the system needs to perform more checks and acknowledgments to ensure that 消息s are delivered, or maintain state to ensure a 消息 is only delivered the specified 数字 of times. This increases the 延迟 of the system and reduces the overall 吞吐量 of the system. Each 消息 may require an additional internal acknowledgements, and an equivalent 数字 of additional roundtrips, before it can be considered delivered.
**Queues provides *at least once* delivery by 默认** in order to optimize for reliability.
This means that 消息s are guaranteed to be delivered at least once, and in rare occasions, may be delivered more than once.
For the majority of applications, this is the right balance between not losing any 消息s and minimizing end-to-end 延迟, as exactly once delivery incurs additional overheads in any messaging system.

In cases where processing the same 消息 more than once would introduce unintended behaviour, generating a unique ID when writing the 消息 to the 队列 and using that as the primary 键 on 数据库 inserts and/or as an idempotency 键 to de-duplicate the 消息 after processing. For 示例, using this idempotency 键 as the ID in an 上游 email API or payment API will allow those services to reject the duplicate on your behalf, without you having to carry additional state in your application.

paragraphs: Cross-源 Resource Sharing (CORS) is a standardized method that pr事件 域名 X from accessing the 资源 of 域名 Y. It does so by using special 头部 in HTTP 响应s from 域名 Y, that allow your browser to verify that 域名 Y permits 域名 X to access these 资源.
While CORS can help protect your data from malicious websites, CORS is also used to interact with 对象s in your 存储桶 and configure policies on your 存储桶.
CORS is used when you interact with a 存储桶 from a web browser, and you have two options:

Set a 存储桶 to public: This option makes your 存储桶 accessible on the Internet as 读取-only, which means anyone can 请求 and load 对象s from your 存储桶 in their browser or anywhere else. This option is ideal if your 存储桶 contains images used in a public blog.
Presigned URLs: Allows anyone with access to the unique URL to perform specific 操作s on your 存储桶.
Before you configure CORS, you must have:
An R2 存储桶 with at least one 对象. If you need to 创建 a 存储桶, refer to 创建 a public 存储桶.
A 域名 you can use to access the 对象. This can also be a .
(可选) Access 键s. An access 键 is only 必需 when creating a presigned URL.
To use CORS with a public 存储桶, ensure your 存储桶 is set to allow public access.
Next, add a CORS policy to your 存储桶 to allow the file to be shared.

Presigned URLs allow temporary access to perform specific 操作s on your 存储桶 without exposing your credentials. While presigned URLs handle 身份验证, you still need to configure CORS when making 请求s from a browser.

When a browser makes a 请求 to a presigned URL on a different 源, the browser enforces CORS. Without a CORS policy, browser-based uploads and downloads using presigned URLs will fail, even though the presigned URL itself is valid.
To enable browser-based access with presigned URLs:
Add a CORS policy to your 存储桶 that allows 请求s from your application's 源.
Set  to match the operations your presigned URLs perform, use , , , and/or .
Set  to include any 头部 the client will send when using the presigned URL, such as 头部 for content 类型, checksums, 缓存, or custom metadata.
(可选) Set  to allow your JavaScript to 读取 响应 头部 like , which contains the 对象's hash and is useful for verifying uploads.
(可选) Set  to cache the preflight 响应 and reduce the 数字 of preflight 请求s the browser makes.
The following 示例 allows browser-based uploads from  with a  头部:
自定义域名 connected to an R2 存储桶 with a CORS policy automatically return CORS 响应 头部 for cross-源 请求s.
Cross-源 请求s must include a valid  请求 头部, for 示例, . If you are 测试 directly or using a command-line tool such as , you will not see CORS  响应 头部 unless the  请求 头部 is included in the 请求.
:::注意[缓存 and CORS 头部]

If you set a CORS policy on a 存储桶 that is al读取y serving traffic using a 自定义域名, any existing cached assets will not reflect the CORS 响应 头部 until they are refreshed in cache. Use Cache Purge to purge the cache for that 主机名 after making any CORS policy 相关 changes.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **R2 对象存储** page.
Locate and select your 存储桶 from the 列表.
Select **设置**.
Under **CORS Policy**, select **Add CORS policy**.
From the **JSON** tab, manually enter or copy and paste your policy into the text box.
When you are done, select **Save**.
Your policy displays on the 设置 page for your 存储桶.
You can configure CORS 规则 using the Wrangler CLI.
创建 a JSON file with your CORS 配置:
Apply the CORS policy to your 存储桶:
Verify the CORS policy was applied:
The following 字段s in an R2 CORS policy map to HTTP 响应 头部. These 响应 头部 are only returned when the incoming HTTP 请求 is a valid CORS 请求.
This 示例 shows a CORS policy added for a 存储桶 that contains the  对象, which is a font file.
The  specify the web server being used, and  is the 主机名 where the web server is 运行中. The  specify that only  请求s are allowed and can 读取 对象s in your 存储桶.
In general, a good strategy for making sure you have set the correct CORS 规则 is to look at the network 请求 that is being blocked by your browser.
Make sure the 规则's  includes the 源 where the 请求 is being made from. (like  or )
Make sure the 规则's  includes the blocked 请求's method.
Make sure the 规则's  includes the blocked 请求's 头部.
Also 注意 that CORS 规则 propagation can, in rare cases, take up to 30 seconds.
Only a cross-源 请求 will include CORS 响应 头部.
A cross-源 请求 is identified by the presence of an  HTTP 请求 头部, with the 值 of the  representing a valid, allowed 源 as defined by the  字段 of your CORS policy.
A 请求 without an  HTTP 请求 头部 will _not_ return any CORS 响应 头部. 源 值s must match exactly.
The 值(s) for  in your CORS policy must be a valid HTTP 源 头部 值. A valid  头部 does _not_ include a path component and must only be comprised of a  (where port is 可选).
Valid  值:  - includes the scheme and host. A port is 可选 and implied by the scheme.
Invalid  值:  or  - incorrectly includes the path component.
If you need to access specific 头部 值s via JavaScript on the 源 page, such as when using a video player, ensure you set  correctly and include the 头部 your JavaScript needs access to, such as .

/r2/buckets/public-buckets/.title: Public 存储桶s
headings: Add your 域名 to Cloudflare
Connect a 存储桶 to a 自定义域名
paragraphs: Public 存储桶 is a 特性 that allows users to expose the contents of their R2 存储桶s directly to the Internet. By 默认, 存储桶s are never publicly accessible and will always require explicit user permission to enable.
Public 存储桶s can be 设置 in either one of two ways:
Expose your 存储桶 as a 自定义域名 under your control.
Expose your 存储桶 using a Cloudflare-managed  子域名 for non-production 使用场景.
These options can be used independently. Enabling 自定义域名 does not require enabling  access.
To use 特性 like WAF custom 规则, 缓存, access controls, or bot management, you must configure your 存储桶 behind a 自定义域名. These capabilities are not available when using the  development url.
:::注意
Currently, public 存储桶s do not let you 列表 the 存储桶 contents at the root of your (sub) 域名.
域名 access through a 自定义域名 allows you to use Cloudflare Cache to accelerate access to your R2 存储桶.
Configure your cache to use Smart Tiered Cache to have a single upper tier 数据中心 next to your R2 存储桶.
By 默认, only certain file 类型s are cached. To cache all files in your 存储桶, you must set a Cache Everything page 规则.
For more 信息rmation on 默认 Cache behavior and how to customize it, refer to 默认 Cache Behavior
To restrict access to your 自定义域名's 存储桶, use Cloudflare's existing 安全 products.
Cloudflare Zero Trust Access: Protects 存储桶s that should only be accessible by your teammates. Refer to Protect an R2 存储桶 with Cloudflare Access tutorial for more 信息rmation.
Cloudflare WAF 令牌 身份验证: Restricts access to documents, files, and media to selected users by providing them with an access 令牌.
:::注意
Disable public access to your  子域名 when using products like WAF or Cloudflare Access. If you do not disable public access, your 存储桶 will remain publicly available through your  子域名.

To customise the minimum TLS version or cipher suites of a custom 主机名 of an R2 存储桶, you can issue an API call to edit R2 自定义域名 设置. You will need to add the 可选  and  参数 to the 请求 正文. For a 列表 of the cipher suites you can specify, refer to 支持的 cipher suites.
The 域名 being used must have been added as a 区域 in the same 账户 as the R2 存储桶.
If your 域名 is al读取y managed by Cloudflare, you can proceed to Connect a 存储桶 to a 自定义域名.
If your 域名 is not managed by Cloudflare, you need to set it up using a partial (CNAME) 设置 to add it to your 账户.
Once the 域名 exists in your Cloudflare 账户 (regardless of 设置 类型), you can link it to your 存储桶.
To view the added DNS 记录, select ... next to the connected 域名 and select Manage DNS.
If the 区域 is on an 企业计划, make sure that you release the 区域 hold before adding the 自定义域名.
A 区域 hold would pr事件 the custom 子域名 from activating.
Disabling a 域名 will turn off public access to your 存储桶 through that 域名. Access through other 域名s or the managed  子域名 are unaffected.
The specified 域名 will also remain connected to R2 until you remove it or 删除 the 存储桶.
To disable a 域名:
In **R2**, select the 存储桶 you want to modify.
On the 存储桶 page, Select **设置**, go to **自定义域名**.
Next to the 域名 you want to disable, select **...** and **Disable 域名**.
The badge under **Access to 存储桶** will 更新 to **Not allowed**.

Removing a 自定义域名 will disconnect it from your 存储桶 and 删除 its 配置 from the 控制面板. Your 存储桶 will remain publicly accessible through any other 已启用 access method, but the 域名 will no longer appear in the connected 域名s 列表.
To remove a 域名:
Next to the 域名 you want to disable, select **...** and **Remove 域名**.
Select **Remove 域名** in the confirmation window. This 步骤 also removes the CNAME 记录 pointing to the 域名. You can always add the 域名 again.
Expose the contents of this R2 存储桶 to the internet through a Cloudflare-managed r2.dev 子域名. This 端点 is intended for non-production traffic.
Public access through  子域名s are rate limited and should only be used for development purposes.
To enable access management, Cache and bot management 特性, you must 设置 a 自定义域名 when enabling public access to your 存储桶.
Avoid creating a CNAME 记录 pointing to the  子域名. This is an 不支持的 access path, and we cannot guarantee consistent reliability or 性能. For production use, add your 域名 to Cloudflare instead.
When you enable public development URL access for your 存储桶, its contents become available on the internet through a Cloudflare-managed  子域名.
To enable access through  for your 存储桶s:
On the 存储桶 page, select **设置**.
Under **Public Development URL**, select **Enable**.
In **Allow Public Access?**, confirm your choice by typing  to confirm and select **Allow**.
You can now access the 存储桶 and its 对象s using the Public 存储桶 URL.
To verify that your 存储桶 is publicly accessible, check that Public URL Access shows Allowed in you 存储桶 设置.
Disabling public development URL access removes your 存储桶's exposure through the  子域名. The 存储桶 and its 对象s will no longer be accessible via the Public 存储桶 URL.
If you have connected other 域名s, the 存储桶 will remain accessible for those 域名s.
To disable public access for your 存储桶:
Under **Public Development URL**, select **Disable**.
In **Disallow Public Access?**, 类型  to confirm and select **Disallow**.

/r2/pricing/.title: 定价
headings: Will I be charged for unauthorized 请求s to my R2 存储桶?
paragraphs: R2 charges based on the total volume of data stored, along with two classes of operations on that data:
Class A operations which are more expensive and tend to mutate state.
Class B operations which tend to 读取 existing state.
For the Infrequent Access storage class, data retrieval fees apply. There are no charges for egress 带宽 for any storage class.
All included usage is on a monthly basis.
:::注意
To learn about potential cost savings from using R2, refer to the R2 定价 calculator.
:::注意[Billable unit rounding]
Cloudflare rounds up your usage to the next billing unit.
For 示例:
If you have performed one million and one operations, you will be billed for two million operations.
If you have used 1.1 GB-month, you will be billed for 2 GB-month.
If you have retrieved data (for infrequent access storage) for 1.1 GB, you will be billed for 2 GB.
You can use the following amount of storage and operations each month for free.
:::注意
The free tier only applies to Standard storage, and does not apply to Infrequent Access storage.
Storage is billed using gigabyte-month (GB-month) as the billing metric. A GB-month is calculated by averaging the _peak_ storage per day over a billing period (30 days).
Storing 1 GB constantly for 30 days will be charged as 1 GB-month.
Storing 3 GB constantly for 30 days will be charged as 3 GB-month.
Storing 1 GB for 5 days, then 3 GB for the remaining 25 days will be charged as
For 对象s stored in Infrequent Access storage, you will be charged for the 对象 for the minimum storage duration even if the 对象 was 删除d or moved before the duration specified.
Class A Operations include , , , , , , , , , , , , ,  and .
Class B Operations include , , , , , ,  and .
Free operations include ,  and .
Data retrieval fees apply when you access or retrieve data from the Infrequent Access storage class. This includes any time 对象s are 读取 or copied.
For 对象s stored in Infrequent Access storage, you will be charged for the 对象 for the minimum storage duration even if the 对象 was 删除d, moved, or replaced before the specified duration.

R2 Data Catalog is in public 测试版, and any developer with an R2 subscription can start using it. Currently, outside of standard R2 storage and operations, you will not be billed for your use of R2 Data Catalog. We will provide at least 30 days' notice before we make any changes or start charging for usage.
To 了解更多 about our thinking on future 定价, refer to the R2 Data Catalog announcement blog.

Super Slurper is free to use. You are only charged for the Class A operations that Super Slurper makes to your R2 存储桶. 对象s with sizes < 100MiB are uploaded to R2 in a single Class A operation. Larger 对象s use mul提示art uploads to increase transfer 成功 rates and will perform mul提示le Class A operations. 注意 that your source 存储桶 might incur additional charges as Super Slurper copies 对象s over to R2.
Once 迁移 completes, you are charged for storage & Class A/B operations as described in previous sections.

Sippy is free to use. You are only charged for the operations Sippy makes to your R2 存储桶. If a 请求ed 对象 is not present in R2, Sippy will copy it over from your source 存储桶. 对象s with sizes < 200MiB are uploaded to R2 in a single Class A operation. Larger 对象s use mul提示art uploads to increase transfer 成功 rates, and will perform mul提示le Class A operations. 注意 that your source 存储桶 might incur additional charges as Sippy copies 对象s over to R2.
As 对象s are migrated to R2, they are served from R2, and you are charged for storage & Class A/B operations as described in previous sections.
If a user writes 1,000 对象s in R2 Standard storage for 1 month with an average size of 1 GB and 读取s each 对象 1,000 times during the month, the estimated cost for the month would be:

If a user writes 1,000 对象s in R2 Infrequent Access storage with an average size of 1 GB, stores them for 5 days, and then 删除s them (删除 operations are free), and during those 5 days each 对象 is 读取 1,000 times, the estimated cost for the month would be:
注意 that the minimal storage duration for infrequent access storage is 30 days, which means the billable quantity is 1,000 GB-months, rather than 167 GB-months.
If a user writes 100,000 files with an average size of 100 KB 对象 and 读取s 10,000,000 对象s per day, the estimated cost in a month would be:
To 了解更多 about how usage is billed, refer to Cloudflare Billing Policy.
No. You are not charged for operations when the caller does not have permission to make the 请求 (HTTP 401  响应 status code).

[^1]: Egressing directly from R2, including via the Workers API, S3 API, and  域名s does not incur data transfer (egress) charges and is free. If you connect other metered services to an R2 存储桶, you may be charged by those services.

/r2/reference/data-location/.title: Data location
headings: Set hints via the Cloudflare 控制面板
Set hints via the S3 API
Set jurisdiction via the Cloudflare 控制面板
Using jurisdictions with the S3 API
paragraphs: Learn how the location of data stored in R2 is determined and about the different available inputs that control the physical location where 对象s in your 存储桶s are stored.
When you 创建 a 新功能 存储桶, the data location is set to Automatic by 默认. Currently, this option chooses a 存储桶 location in the closest available region to the 创建 存储桶 请求 based on the location of the caller.
Location Hints are 可选 参数 you can provide during 存储桶 creation to indicate the primary geographical location you expect data will be accessed from.

Using Location Hints can be a good choice when you expect the majority of access to data in a 存储桶 to come from a different location than where the 创建 存储桶 请求 源ates. Keep in mind Location Hints are a best effort and not a guarantee, and they should only be used as a way to optimize 性能 by placing regularly 已更新 content closer to users.
You can choose to automatically 创建 your 存储桶 in the closest available region based on your location or choose a specific location from the 列表.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **R2 对象存储** page.
Select **创建 存储桶**.
Enter a name for the 存储桶.
Under **Location**, leave _None_ selected for automatic selection or choose a region from the 列表.
Select **创建 存储桶** to complete the 存储桶 creation process.
You can set the Location Hint via the  parameter using the S3 API:
Refer to 示例 for additional 示例 from other S3 SDKs.
The following hint locations are 支持的:
Location Hints are only honored the first time a 存储桶 with a given name is 创建d. If you 删除 and re创建 a 存储桶 with the same name, the 源al 存储桶’s location will be used.
Jurisdictional Restrictions guarantee 对象s in a 存储桶 are stored within a specific jurisdiction.
Use Jurisdictional Restrictions when you need to ensure data is stored and processed within a jurisdiction to meet data residency 要求, including local regulations such as the GDPR or FedRAMP.
Under **Location**, select **Specify jurisdiction** and choose a jurisdiction from the 列表.
To access R2 存储桶s that belong to a jurisdiction from Workers, you will need to specify the jurisdiction as well as the 存储桶 name as part of your 绑定 in your Wrangler 配置 file:
For more 信息rmation on 快速开始, refer to Use R2 from Workers.
When interacting with R2 资源 that belong to a defined jurisdiction with the S3 API or existing S3-compatible SDKs, you must specify the jurisdiction in your S3 端点:
You can use your jurisdiction-specific 端点 for any 支持的 S3 API operations. When using a jurisdiction 端点, you will not be able to access R2 资源 outside of that jurisdiction.
The 示例 below shows how to 创建 an R2 存储桶 in the  jurisdiction using the [](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@aws-sdk/client-s3) package for JavaScript.
The following jurisdictions are 支持的:
:::注意
Cloudflare Enterprise customers may contact their 账户 team or Cloudflare 支持 to 获取 access to the FedRAMP jurisdiction.
The following services do not interact with R2 资源 with assigned jurisdictions:
Super Slurper (_即将推出_)
Logpush. As a workaround to this limitation, you can 设置 a Logpush 作业 using an S3-compatible 端点 to store 日志 in an R2 存储桶 in the jurisdiction of your choice.
Once an R2 存储桶 is 创建d, the jurisdiction cannot be changed.
