The Cloudflare Web 应用防火墙 (Cloudflare WAF) checks incoming web and API 请求s and 过滤器s undesired traffic based on sets of 规则 called 规则ets.
This page will 指南 you through the recommended initial 步骤s for configuring the WAF to 获取 immediate protection against the most common attacks.
Refer to Concepts for more 信息rmation on WAF concepts, main components, and 角色.
:::注意
This 指南 focuses on configuring WAF for individual 域名s, known as  区域s . The WAF 配置 is also available at the 账户 level for Enterprise customers with a paid add-on.
Make sure that you have 设置 a Cloudflare 账户 and added your 域名 to Cloudflare.

Users on the 免费计划 have access to the Cloudflare Free Managed 规则et, a subset of the Cloudflare Managed 规则et. The Free Managed 规则et is 部署ed by 默认 on 免费计划s and is not specifically covered in this 指南. If you are on a 免费计划, you may skip to 5. Review traffic in 安全 控制面板s.

The Cloudflare Managed 规则et protects against Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) and known attack vectors. This 规则et is designed to identify common attacks using signatures, while generating low false positives. 规则 changes are published on a weekly basis in the WAF 更新日志. Cloudflare may also add 规则 at any time during emergency releases for high profile zero-day protection.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the 安全 **设置** page.
(可选) 过滤器 by **Web application exploits**.
Turn on **Cloudflare managed 规则et**.
登录 to the Cloudflare 控制面板, and select your 账户 and 域名.
Go to **安全** > **WAF** and select the **Managed 规则** tab.
Under **Managed 规则ets**, select **部署** next to the Cloudflare Managed 规则et.

By 默认, the Cloudflare Managed 规则et enables only a subset of 规则 and it is designed to strike a balance between protection and false positives. You can review and enable additional 规则 based on your application technology stack.

In particular situations, enabling the managed 规则et can cause some false positives. False positives are legitimate 请求s inadvertently mitigated by the WAF. For 信息rmation on addressing false positives, refer to Troubleshoot managed 规则.
If you are 测试 the WAF against pen测试 工具, it is recommended that you enable all 规则 by using the following 规则et 配置:
**规则et 操作**: _Block_
**规则et status**: _已启用_ (enables all 规则 in the 规则et)
For more 信息rmation on configuring the Cloudflare Managed 规则et in the 控制面板, refer to Cloudflare Managed 规则et.
WAF attack score is only available to Business customers (limited access to a single 字段) and Enterprise customers (full access).

WAF attack score is a machine-learning layer that complements Cloudflare's managed 规则ets, providing additional protection against SQL injection (SQLi), cross-site scripting (XSS), and many remote code 执行 (RCE) attacks. It helps identify 规则 bypasses and potentially 新功能, undiscovered attacks.
If you are an Enterprise customer, do the following:
Reach out to your 账户 team to 获取 access to WAF attack score.
创建 a custom 规则 using the  Attack Score  字段:
**When incoming 请求s match**:
**Choose 操作**: Block
If you are on a 商业计划, 创建 a custom 规则 as mentioned above but use the WAF Attack Score Class 字段 instead. For 示例, you could use the following 规则 表达式: .

Bot score is only available to Enterprise customers with Bot Management. Customers on Pro and 商业计划s should turn on Super Bot Fight Mode instead, which provides built-in bot protection without creating custom 规则.
Enterprise customers with Bot Management should first configure bot protection using 安全 设置, which provide baseline protection without creating custom 规则:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **安全 设置** page and 过滤器 by **Bot traffic**.
Configure the **Definitely automated**, **Likely automated**, and **Verified bots** 设置 according to your needs.
Turn on **Block AI bots** if you want to block AI crawlers.
These built-in 设置 auto-更新 with 新功能 bot signatures and do not count toward your custom 规则 限制. For more details, refer to Bot Management.
可选ly, if you need more granular control — for 示例, a different score threshold or 规则 that combine bot score with other 字段s — 创建 a custom 规则 using the  Bot Score  and  Verified Bot  字段s:
**Choose 操作**: Managed Challenge
This 规则 uses a threshold of 20 (instead of the 默认 threshold of 30 used by the 设置), providing stricter protection for traffic in the 20-29 score range.
For a more comprehensive 示例 of baseline protection against malicious bots, refer to Challenge bad bots.
For more 信息rmation about the bot-相关 字段s you can use in 表达式s, refer to Bot Management 变量s.
Once you have 部署ed the Cloudflare Managed 规则et and 规则 based on attack score and bot score, you will have achieved substantial protection, limiting the chance of false positives.

After configuring the Cloudflare Managed 规则et and attack score, you can also 部署 the Cloudflare OWASP Core 规则et. This managed 规则et is Cloudflare's implementation of the OWASP Mod安全 Core 规则 Set. Its attack coverage significantly overlaps with Cloudflare Managed 规则et by detecting common attack vectors such as SQLi and XSS.
Turn on **OWASP Core**.
This will 部署 the Cloudflare OWASP Core 规则et with the 默认 配置: paranoia level = _PL1_ and score threshold = _Medium - 40 and higher_.
Go to your 域名 > **安全** > **WAF** and select the **Managed 规则** tab.
Under **Managed 规则ets**, select **部署** next to the Cloudflare OWASP Core 规则et.
This will 部署 the 规则et with the 默认 配置: paranoia level = _PL1_ and score threshold = _Medium - 40 and higher_.

Unlike the signature-based Cloudflare Managed 规则et, the Cloudflare OWASP Core 规则et is score-based. You select a certain paranoia level (levels vary from _PL1_ to _PL4_, where _PL1_ is the lowest level), which enables an increasing larger group of 规则. You also select a score threshold, which decides when to perform the configured 操作. Low paranoia with a high score threshold usually leads to fewer false positives. For an 示例 of how the OWASP Core 规则et is evaluated, refer to OWASP evaluation 示例.
Follow one of these strategies to configure the 规则et according to your needs:

Start from a strict 配置 (paranoia level = _PL4_, score threshold = _Low - 60 and higher_). Reduce the score threshold and paranoia level until you achieve a good false positives/true positives rate for your incoming traffic.

Alternatively, start from a more permissive 配置 (paranoia level = _PL1_, score threshold = _High - 25 and higher_) and increase both 参数 to adjust your protection, trying to keep a low 数字 of false positives.
For more 信息rmation on configuring the Cloudflare OWASP Core 规则et in the 控制面板, refer to Configure in the 控制面板.
After setting up your WAF 配置, review how incoming traffic is being affected by your current 设置 using the following 控制面板s:
Use 安全 分析 to explore all traffic, including traffic not affected by WAF mitigation measures. All data provided by traffic detections is available in this 控制面板.
Use 安全 事件 to 获取 more 信息rmation about 请求s that are being mitigated by Cloudflare 安全 products.
Enterprise customers can also obtain data about HTTP 请求s and 安全 事件 using Cloudflare 日志.
After configuring the WAF based on the 信息rmation in the previous sections, you should have a strong base protection against possible threats to your applications.
You can explore the following recommendations to 获取 additional protection for specific 使用场景.
创建 a custom 规则 to allow traffic from IP addresses in allow列表 only.
创建 a custom 规则 to block traffic from specific countries.
创建 a 速率限制 规则 to apply 速率限制 on a login 端点.
Use leaked credentials detection to pr事件  credential stuffing  attacks on your applications.
Available to Enterprise customers with a paid add-on.
Use WAF content scanning to scan content being uploaded to your application, 搜索ing for malicious content.
Available to Enterprise customers.

Cloudflare protects your APIs from 新功能 and known application attacks and exploits such as SQL injection attacks. API-specific 安全 products extend those protections to the unique risks in APIs such as API discovery and 身份验证 management.
For more 信息rmation on Cloudflare's API 安全 特性, refer to Cloudflare API Shield.

/waf/detections/malicious-uploads/.title: Malicious uploads detection
headings: What is a content 对象?
paragraphs: The malicious uploads detection is a traffic detection that scans files and other content uploaded to your application for malware.

When you turn on this detection, the WAF inspects incoming uploads and checks them for malicious signatures. The scan results are available as 字段s you can use in custom 规则 and 速率限制 规则 to act on 请求s containing malicious content.
:::注意
This 特性 is available to customers on an 企业计划 with a paid add-on.
Once you turn on this detection, Cloudflare inspects all incoming traffic and identifies  content 对象s  automatically.
When Cloudflare detects one or more content 对象s in a 请求, it sends them to an antivirus (AV) scanner for analysis. The AV scanner is the same one used in Cloudflare Zero Trust.

Based on the scan results, the detection populates 字段s you can 参考 in 规则 表达式s. For 示例, you can 创建 a 规则 to block 请求s with malicious files, or a more specific 规则 that also matches on file size, file 类型, or URI path.
:::注意[注意s]
Content scanning does not block or challenge 请求s on its own. It provides detection signals only. To act on these signals, 创建 custom 规则 or 速率限制 规则.
For more 信息rmation on detection versus mitigation, refer to Concepts.

A content 对象 is a file or binary payload in a 请求 that Cloudflare identifies as scannable content. The malicious uploads detection uses heuristics to find content 对象s automatically, without relying on the 请求's  头部 (since this 头部 can be manipulated).
The following content 类型s are excluded from scanning: , , , , and . All other detected content is treated as a content 对象. Common 示例 include:
Execu表 files (for 示例, , , , and )
Documents (for 示例, , , , , and )
Compressed files (for 示例, , , and )
Image files (for 示例, , , , , and )
Video and audio files
If Cloudflare detects a malicious 对象 but cannot determine its exact content 类型, it 报告 the 对象 as .
Content scanning can check the following content 对象s for malicious content:
Uploaded files in a 请求
Portions of the 请求 正文 for mul提示art 请求s encoded as  or
Specific JSON properties in the 请求 正文 (containing, for 示例, files encoded in Base64) according to the custom scan 表达式s you provide
All content 对象s in an incoming 请求 will be checked, namely for 请求s with mul提示le uploaded files (for 示例, a submitted HTML form with several file inputs).
The content scanner will fully check content 对象s with a size up to 50 MB. For larger content 对象s, the scanner will analyze the first 50 MB and provide scan results based on that portion of the 对象.
The AV scanner will not scan some particular 类型s of files, namely the following:
Password-protected archives
Archives with more than three recursion levels
Archives with more than 300 files
PGP-encrypted files
In rare cases, the AV scanner may time out and fail to analyze a content 对象. When this happens, the  字段 will be set to true.
Sometimes, you may want to specify where to find the content 对象s, such as when the content is a Base64-encoded 字符串 within a JSON payload. For 示例:
In these situations, configure a custom scan 表达式 to tell the content scanner where to find the content 对象s. For more 信息rmation, refer to Configure a custom scan 表达式.
For more 信息rmation and additional 示例 of looking up 字段s in nested JSON payloads, refer to the [](/规则et-engine/规则-language/函数s/#lookup_json_字符串) 函数 文档.
The content scanner will automatically decode Base64 字符串s.
When content scanning is 已启用, you can use the following 字段s in WAF 规则:
[1]: /规则et-engine/规则-language/字段s/参考/cf.waf.content_scan.has_obj/
[2]: /规则et-engine/规则-language/字段s/参考/cf.waf.content_scan.has_malicious_obj/
[3]: /规则et-engine/规则-language/字段s/参考/cf.waf.content_scan.num_malicious_obj/
[4]: /规则et-engine/规则-language/字段s/参考/cf.waf.content_scan.has_已失败/
[5]: /规则et-engine/规则-language/字段s/参考/cf.waf.content_scan.num_obj/
[6]: /规则et-engine/规则-language/字段s/参考/cf.waf.content_scan.obj_sizes/
[7]: /规则et-engine/规则-language/字段s/参考/cf.waf.content_scan.obj_类型s/
[8]: /规则et-engine/规则-language/字段s/参考/cf.waf.content_scan.obj_results/
For 示例 of 规则 表达式s using these 字段s, refer to 示例 规则.

/waf/custom-rules/.title: Custom 规则
headings: Inter操作 with other app 安全 特性
paragraphs: Custom 规则 are evaluated in order, and some 操作s like _Block_ will stop the evaluation of other 规则. This means that if an earlier 规则 blocks a 请求, later 规则 will not run for that 请求. For more details on 操作s and their behavior, refer to 操作s.
To define sets of custom 规则 that apply to more than one 区域, use custom 规则ets. At the 区域 level, all customers can 创建 and 部署 custom 规则ets. Custom 规则ets at the 账户 level require an 企业计划.
:::注意
Currently, the Cloudflare 控制面板 does not 支持 working with custom 规则ets at the 区域 level. You will need to use the Cloudflare API to configure or 部署 these 规则ets.
The maximum 数字 of custom 规则 applies to all 规则 in the  phase, which is where custom 规则 run. Each scope (区域 or 账户) has a separate maximum 数字 of 规则, counted in the following way:
区域: All custom 规则 plus all the 规则 across custom 规则ets defined at the 区域 level.
账户: All the 规则 across custom 规则ets defined at the 账户 level.
Refer to the following pages for instructions on creating custom 规则:
创建 a custom 规则 in the 控制面板
创建 a custom 规则 via API
WAF custom 规则 配置 using Terraform
For 示例 of using custom 规则 to address common 使用场景, refer to Common 使用场景.

/waf/change-log/.title: WAF 更新日志 概述
headings: 更新日志 for managed 规则ets
paragraphs: The WAF 更新日志 provides 信息rmation about changes to managed 规则ets and general 更新s to WAF protection.
View 更新日志
View 定时 changes
Cloudflare regularly releases 更新s and adds 新功能 规则 to WAF managed 规则ets. 更新s improve 规则 accuracy, reduce false positives, or increase protection in 响应 to changes in the threat landscape.
新功能 and 已更新 规则 follow a seven-day release cycle, typically on Monday or Tuesday (adjusted for public holidays).

Week 1 — Logging only: Cloudflare 部署s 新功能 or 已更新 规则 in logging-only mode with the _Log_ 操作. 规则 in this mode 记录 matching 请求s but do not block traffic. Most 新功能ly 创建d 规则 carry both the  and  tags. Use this period to review your 安全 事件 for unexpected matches that could be false positives.
Week 2 — 默认 操作: On the following release day, the 规则 change from the _Log_ 操作 to their intended 默认 操作 (shown in the 新功能 操作 column of the 更新日志 表). The  and  tags are removed.
For 更新s to existing 规则, Cloudflare first 部署s the 已更新 version as a separate  规则 (注意d in the 规则 description) with a  tag, before updating the 源al 规则 on the next release cycle.

Cloudflare may also add 规则 in 已禁用 mode on the same release cycle. These 规则 make remediation logic available without affecting traffic, and allow Cloudflare to perform impact 测试 and 性能 checks. You can activate a 已禁用 规则 at any time if you need its protection. 已禁用 规则 do not carry the  or  tags.
For 新功能 vulnerabilities, Cloudflare may release 规则 outside the regular seven-day cycle. These are emergency releases.
:::注意
规则et overrides and tag overrides apply to existing and future 规则 in a managed 规则et. This means overrides you configure today will automatically apply to 规则 added in regular and emergency releases.

If you notice a 新功能 or 已更新 规则 generating an increased volume of 安全 事件, you can disable it or change its 操作 from the 默认. Once you change a 规则 to use an 操作 other than the 默认 one, Cloudflare will not be able to override the 规则 操作.
The 更新日志 also includes general 更新s to WAF protection that are not specific to managed 规则ets.

/waf/managed-rules/.title: Managed 规则
headings: Inter操作 with other app 安全 特性
paragraphs: **Cloudflare Managed 规则et**: 创建d by the Cloudflare 安全 team, this 规则et provides fast and effective protection for all of your applications. It covers known attack techniques and zero-day vulnerabilities (新功能ly discovered flaws with no available patch). The 规则et is 已更新 frequently to address 新功能 threats and reduce false positives (legitimate 请求s incorrectly flagged). 规则et ID:

**Cloudflare OWASP Core 规则et**: Cloudflare's implementation of the Open Web Application 安全 Project (OWASP) Mod安全 Core 规则 Set. This 规则et uses a scoring model — each matching 规则 adds its score to a cumulative threat score, and the WAF executes the configured 操作 when the score exceeds the threshold. 规则et ID:

**Cloudflare Exposed Credentials Check**: 部署s an automated credentials check on your end-user 身份验证 端点. For any credential 对, the Cloudflare WAF performs a lookup against a public 数据库 of stolen credentials to determine if they were previously compromised. Cloudflare recommends that you use leaked credentials detection instead of this 规则et. 规则et ID:

**Cloudflare Free Managed 规则et**: Available on all Cloudflare plans. Provides protection against high-impact and widely exploited vulnerabilities. The 规则 are safe to 部署 on most applications. If you have al读取y 部署ed the Cloudflare Managed 规则et, you do not need this 规则et — the Cloudflare Managed 规则et includes broader coverage. 规则et ID:
The following managed 规则ets run in a 响应 phase:

**Cloudflare Sensitive Data Detection**: 创建d by Cloudflare to address common data loss threats. These 规则 监控 the download of specific sensitive data — for 示例, financial and personally identifiable 信息rmation. 规则et ID:
The managed 规则ets you can 部署 depend on your Cloudflare plan.
Managed 规则 inspect the 正文 of each incoming 请求 up to a maximum size. This limit varies by plan:
For Enterprise customers, the maximum 正文 size is 128 KB.
For other 付费计划, the limit is lower by 默认 — contact your 账户 team or Cloudflare 支持 to increase the limit.
For users in the 免费计划, the limit is 1 MB.

请求 content beyond this limit may not be fully analyzed, which can affect how managed 规则 behave. For 示例, the OWASP Core 规则et calculates a cumulative threat score based on the scores of individual 规则 that match a 请求. Larger payloads give more content for 规则 to match against, which increases the score and makes it more likely to exceed the score threshold — resulting in a false positive.

If included in your plan, you can use 请求 正文 字段s in custom 规则 to apply appropriate 操作s to 请求s that have not been fully analyzed. The  字段 indicates whether the 请求 正文 was truncated, while  indicates whether the 请求 contained too many 头部 for all of them to be included.

At the 区域 level, you can 部署 each managed 规则et once. At the 账户 level, you can 部署 each managed 规则et mul提示le times, which allows you to apply different 配置s of the same 规则et to different subsets of incoming traffic.

/waf/rate-limiting-rules/.title: 速率限制 规则
headings: Inter操作 with other app 安全 特性
paragraphs: 速率限制 规则 allow you to define rate 限制 for 请求s matching an 表达式, and the 操作 to perform when those rate 限制 are reached. Use 速率限制 规则 to pr事件 abuse of your websites and APIs — for 示例, to protect a login 端点 from brute-force attacks or to cap how many API calls a single client can make in a given time window.
In the 新功能 安全 控制面板, 速率限制 规则 are one of the available 类型s of 安全 规则. 安全 规则 perform 安全-相关 操作s on incoming 请求s that match specified 过滤器s.
Some Enterprise customers can 创建 速率限制 规则ets at the 账户 level that they can 部署 to mul提示le Enterprise 区域s.
Like other 规则 evaluated by Cloudflare's 规则et Engine, 速率限制 规则 have the following basic 参数:
Refer to 速率限制 参数 for more 信息rmation on mandatory and 可选 参数.
Refer to How Cloudflare determines the 请求 rate to learn how Cloudflare uses the 参数 above when determining the rate of incoming 请求s.
速率限制 规则 are evaluated in order, and some 操作s like _Block_ will stop the evaluation of other 规则. For more details on 操作s and their behavior, refer to 操作s.

速率限制 规则 are not designed to allow a precise 数字 of 请求s to reach your 源服务器. There may be a delay of up to a few seconds between detecting a 请求 and updating rate counters. Due to this delay, excess 请求s could still reach the 源 before Cloudflare enforces a mitigation 操作 such as blocking or challenging. For more 信息rmation on how counters work, including their per-data-center scope, refer to 请求 rate calculation.
Applying 速率限制 规则 to verified bots might affect 搜索 Engine 优化 (SEO). For more 信息rmation, refer to Improve SEO.
Refer to the following 资源:
创建 a 速率限制 规则 in the 控制面板 for a 区域
创建 a 速率限制 规则 via API for a 区域
For Terraform 示例, refer to 速率限制 规则 配置 using Terraform.
Learning Center: What is 速率限制?
Cloudflare 速率限制 (previous version, no longer available): 文档 for the previous version of 速率限制 规则 (billed based on usage).

/waiting-room/additional-options/create-.title: 创建 定时 事件
headings: 创建 an 事件 from the 控制面板
创建 an 事件 via API
Preview an 事件 配置
paragraphs: When you want to customize the behavior of a waiting room for a specific period of time — such as changing the 队列ing method or increasing the total 活跃 users — 设置 a 定时 事件. You can do this from the 控制面板 or via the API.
Any properties set on the 事件 will override the 默认 property on the waiting room for the duration of the 事件.
:::注意
Only some customers can 支持 定时 事件 with their waiting rooms. For more details, refer to our Plans page.
Within your application, go to **Traffic** > **Waiting Room**.
Expand a waiting room  and select **Schedule 事件**.
Customize the details for your 事件: name the 事件, add a description (可选), and select a Start Date Time and an End Date Time.

You can also enable the pre-队列ing — in this case you need to define a pre-队列ing time. And you can also select **Shuffle at 事件 start** and all users in the pre-队列 will be randomly admitted at 事件 start.

Enabling pre-queuing will send all 新功能 users to your pre-队列 during the pre-队列ing time period. If you would like to also pre-队列 users al读取y 活跃, make the pre-队列ing time period longer than the 会话 duration and disable 会话 re新功能al in the 设置 section. Once 活跃 users 会话s expire, they will be placed into the pre-队列 before your 事件 starts.
Select **Next**.

In the **设置** section, you can define 新功能 值s for your Total 活跃 users, 新功能 users per minute, 会话 duration, 会话 Re新功能al, and 队列ing Method. For each of these 设置 you also have the option to always inherit the 值s defined in your waiting room. With this option, if you change the 设置 of your base waiting room, the corresponding 事件 setting will 更新 as well.
If you choose to override the 值s of Total 活跃 users, you must also override the 数字 of 新功能 users per minute, and vice versa.

In the customization section, you can select Always inherit your waiting room’s template (默认) or you can override it with a Custom 事件 Template. In this case, you need to import your own template. Make sure to preview the result before continuing.
Select **Next** and review your 事件 details and 设置.
Select **Save**.
The waiting room must be set to 已启用 for the 事件 to activate. If your 事件 is set to 已启用 but your waiting room is not, the 事件 will not activate.

In your waiting room page, in the Next 事件 column you can visualize the date of the next 事件 定时. This columns will 读取  in case there is no 事件 定时 for that waiting room. You can always suspend, edit or 删除 your 事件.
You have a limit of five 事件 per waiting room. To 创建 a 新功能 事件 after you have reached this limit, you can 删除 a previous 事件.
To 创建 an 事件, make a POST 请求 including 必需 and 可选 参数. Any properties set on the 事件 will override the 默认 property on the waiting room for the duration of the 事件.
If you are using a custom template, you may want to add relevant 变量s to your template (列表ed under the  parameter).
If you need to 创建 overlapping 事件, use different waiting rooms.
Though most 参数 are identical to those in a regular waiting room, there are a few unique to creating an 事件. For a complete 列表 of 事件 设置, please refer to 创建 an 事件.
(必需): Unique name with 内测版numeric characters, hyphens, and underscores.
(必需): ISO 8601 timestamp that marks the start of the 事件. At this time, 队列d users will be processed with the 事件's 配置. Must occur at least 1 minute before .
(必需): ISO 8601 timestamp that marks the end of the 事件.
: If **true** and  is not 空值, users in the pre队列 will be shuffled randomly at the . Commonly used to ensure fairness if your 事件 is using a **FIFO** 队列ing method.
: ISO 8601 timestamp that marks when to begin 队列ing all users before the 事件 starts. Must occur at least **5 minutes before** .
: A text description providing more detail about the 事件.
: If **true**, the 事件 is ignored and traffic is handled based on the waiting room's typical 配置.
When setting up 事件, you may want to also adjust the 默认 队列ing 方法 for your waiting room.
Set the waiting room's 队列ing method to Passthrough when you want to allow traffic normally, but then restrict traffic during a 定时 事件.
Set the waiting room's 队列ing method to Reject when you want to block all traffic normally, but then allow traffic during special 事件 like signups or ticket sales.
设置 a "lottery" system to reward all users who enter into the 队列 prior to your 事件 start time.

Users who reach your application during the pre队列 period are randomly assigned a place in line when the 事件 starts. If the 事件 uses FIFO ordering, users who reach your application after the pre队列 period are assigned places after users from the pre队列.
To 设置 a "lottery", include the following 参数 in your API 请求:
Since some properties set on an 事件 will override the 默认 property of a waiting room for the duration of an 事件, you should use the API to preview an 事件 配置 before it begins.
This command shows you the 事件's 配置 as if it were 活跃, meaning that inherited 字段s from the waiting room will display their current 值s.
To edit an 事件, use a PATCH 请求.
You can disable an 事件 by setting its  parameter to .
Additionally, 事件 will not become 活跃 if a waiting room itself is 已禁用.
Follow these 步骤s if you would like to 部署 a 定时 maintenance page, with no 队列ing before or after the maintenance window.
创建 a waiting room with Passthrough 队列ing method 已启用.
创建 a waiting room 事件 for this room with Reject 队列ing method 已启用.
After the 定时 事件 has ended, users will have access to your site.  You can end the maintenance window before the 定时 事件 is over by setting the 事件 to 已禁用.

/waiting-room/how-to/customize-waiting-r.title: Customize a waiting room
headings: Customize a waiting room from the 控制面板
Customize a waiting room via API
Preview the HTML code for a customized waiting room
Preview the 默认 or current waiting room web page
paragraphs: You can customize your waiting room from the 控制面板 or via API.
To design and preview the appearance of a waiting room, select the Customization tab in the 创建 waiting room page.
Cloudflare offers options to customize the appearance of your waiting room:
默认 waiting room: An unbranded waiting room that displays an estimated waiting time to visitors.

Select a language for your 默认 waiting room page. You can choose from the following languages: English, Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Dutch, Polish, Portuguese (Brazilian), Turkish and Chinese (Simplified and Traditional).
Custom waiting room: Edit template text or 创建 your own HTML code:
Customize both HTML or CSS content, including fonts, colors, static images, additional languages and more.
Edit content directly in the 控制面板 or import relevant files.
Return a JSON-friendly waiting room 响应: Toggle to also enable a JSON 响应 with a user's status in the waiting room.
To choose the 默认, unbranded waiting room:
Select a waiting room.
Go to the **Customization** 步骤.
Select **默认 waiting room**.
Select the language for your waiting room 默认 page.
:::注意
Only certain customers can customize their waiting rooms. For more details, refer to our Plans page.
To customize a waiting room:
Select **Custom waiting room**.
You can edit the HTML code directly in the text box:
Select **Download 默认 template** to download a HTML file containing the 默认 template content to your computer.
Select **Download** to download a HTML file containing the text box content to your computer.
Select **Copy** to copy the text from the text box to your clipboard, then paste it into an editor of your choice.
The template text contains code to display the wait time. If you want to display the estimated wait time to visitors, do not 删除 this content.
Select **Import** to upload a HTML file from your computer.
Select the file in the dialog and select **Open**. The HTML file size limit is 1,048,576 bytes (1 MB).
Make further edits in the text box. Include the code to display the wait time to display the estimated 队列 time on the waiting room page or 创建 your own custom page using available 变量s.
The following content in the  section of the template HTML code displays the wait time:
The following script within the  section after  fetches the wait time:

If you are using Turnstile for your customized waiting room, you will need to ensure the  变量 is added. The 默认 queuing page template and any 新功能ly 创建d custom templates al读取y include this 变量. If you have an existing custom HTML template and wish to enable the Turnstile integration, you will need to add  somewhere in the template to let Waiting Room know where the wid获取 should be placed. Waiting Room uses Mustache templates, so including raw HTML within your template without escaping requires three curly braces instead of two.
When using Infinite 队列 (especially with managed challenges which may be inter活跃), you may want to let users know that they will not be in the 队列 until they complete the challenge.
When you 创建 a waiting room with custom HTML, you can have access to several 变量s to customize your 响应. For a full 列表 of 变量s, refer to the  parameter in the Cloudflare API docs.

Customizable waiting rooms can display text in any language 支持的 by the UTF-8 character set. To display estimated wait time, you can use numeric 变量s like  and  within your waiting room template, regardless of user language. However, at the time, the following 变量s are only available in English: , , and .

If you would like to display different languages within your custom waiting room de待处理 on path or 子域名, you can add JavaScript code to your custom HTML to do so. Below you can find a couple of starter templates that you can use as an 示例 to start from:

To display a different language based on path, download this template. The template displays the content in English if the path contains  or as a 默认, Japanese if the path contains , French if the path contains , and Spanish if the path contains .

To display a different language based on 子域名, download this template. The template displays the content in English as a 默认 or if the 子域名 contains , Japanese if the 子域名 contains , French if the 子域名 contains , and Spanish if the 子域名 contains .

Download either of these templates and customize them however you would like. 更新 the path or 子域名 to reflect your site’s language selection structure. You may edit these templates to include other languages by adding translations to the  对象 for each of the locales.

If you are using images or other 资源 for your customized waiting room, do not host those assets on the 主机名 covered by your waiting room. Otherwise, any 请求s for these assets will not be able to pass through the waiting room.
To preview the appearance of a waiting room:
In your application, go to **Traffic** > **Waiting Room**.
Either 创建 a waiting room or edit an existing one.
Go to the **Review** 步骤.
Select **Preview waiting room**:
Choose **队列ing** to display the waiting room appearance when it is 已启用 on the 控制面板 and **队列-all** is not 已启用.
Choose **队列-All** to display the waiting room appearance when it is 已启用 on the 控制面板 and **队列-all** is 已启用. When **队列-all** is 已启用 for a waiting room, the estimated wait time is not displayed.

If you notice something unexpected when previewing your waiting room, review your custom code for proper syntax. Often, you might for获取 to close each tag with its appropriate closing tag (the tag name with a ).
You can use the Waiting Room API to customize the web page served to visitors when they are placed in a virtual waiting room.
In the following  请求, the  字段 contains the HTML code for the customized waiting room:

示例 请求:

You do not have to have a Cloudflare 账户 to access the preview link, so you can validate the waiting room webpage on mul提示le devices.
After generating a preview URL, use the following 端点 to generate a link to preview the currently configured web page for a waiting room, or the 默认 page if no custom page is configured.
The link in the 响应 displays the content of the  字段, rendered with mustache.
Use the 可选  查询 parameter to preview the waiting room web page when all traffic is force-队列d.

/waiting-room/.title: Cloudflare Waiting Room
paragraphs: A virtual waiting room to manage peak traffic.
Cloudflare Waiting Room allows you to 路由 excess users of your website to a customized waiting room, helping preserve customer experience and protect 源服务器s from being overwhelmed with 请求s.
Waiting Room protects your 源服务器 by pr事件ing surges in legitimate traffic that may overload your 源.
Waiting Room also 优势 your visitors by:
Keeping your application online and pr事件ing them from reaching 错误 pages.
Showing estimated wait times that are continuously 已更新.
Opening up 新功能 spots more quickly by tracking dynamic inflow and outflow.
Remembering each visitor's status to pr事件 someone from losing their place in line or having to re-队列 if they leave your site.
Appearing in your own branding and style, which enhances trust and lets you provide additional 信息rmation as needed.
Customize the behavior of a waiting room for a specific period of time.
创建 规则 to indicate specific traffic or areas of your site or application that you do not want a waiting room to apply to.
获取 insights into the traffic going through your waiting room.
Apply a single waiting room to mul提示le 主机名s and paths within the same 区域.
Cloudflare for SaaS allows you to extend the 安全 and 性能 优势 of Cloudflare’s network to your customers via their own custom or vanity 域名s.
Cloudflare 规则 allows you to make adjustments to 请求s and 响应s, configure Cloudflare 设置, and 触发器 specific 操作s for matching 请求s.
Cloudflare SSL/TLS encrypts your web traffic to pr事件 data theft and other tampering.
The following customers have access to Cloudflare Waiting Room:
Those qualified under Project Fair Shot
Customers on a Business or 企业计划
Access to certain 特性 depends on a customer's plan 类型.
Cloudflare’s CDN is 必需 to use the Waiting Room 特性.
Configure a proxied DNS 记录 or a proxied 负载均衡器 for the waiting room’s 主机名. A DNS 记录 is not auto-configured after a waiting room is 创建d.
Visitors must enable Cookies. Refer to Waiting Room Cookies for 信息rmation on how Cookies are used in Cloudflare Waiting Room.
Explore 定价 options for Waiting Room.

The 类型 of Turnstile wid获取 to use - refer to the Turnstile 文档 for details. Valid 值s are  off ,  invisible ,  visible_non_inter活跃 , and  visible_managed . Setting this to  off  will completely disable the Turnstile integration.
Setting this to  invisible  makes sense for most rooms, unless you would like end users to be aware the challenge is 运行中.
Turnstile Fail 操作
turnstile_操作
Yes, 默认s to  log .
The 操作 to take when an end user fails a Turnstile challenge. Valid 值s are  log  and  infinite_队列 .
Setting this to  log  makes sense for most rooms, unless you wish to aggressively block bots using an infinite 队列.
When you configure , this 值 is not the 数字 of users added per minute.
Instead, it is the threshold of users allowed per minute (less than or equal to the 数字 of ). You should set this 值 at 100% of your expected peak traffic to ensure users are only 队列d when necessary.
会话 duration improves user experience in two ways.

First, it pr事件 visitors from having to pass through a waiting room twice for the same 事务. For 示例, a visitor might want to make a purchase at . There's a lot of traffic at , so they 队列 in the waiting room before entering the online store. After a period of time, they leave the waiting room and enter the online store. They make a purchase and leave the online store.

However, they forgot to add a 注意 to the order or 请求 a receipt. As long as their 会话 Cookie is still valid (for the length of time specified by the ), they can re-enter your application without having to re-队列 in the waiting room.

Second, 会话 duration lets your waiting room 创建 a dynamic outflow from your application (in addition to dynamic inflow). A user's 会话 Cookie expires after a period of inactivity, meaning that 新功能 spots can open up as soon as space becomes available and estimated wait times are lower and more accurate.

/waiting-room/get-started/.title: 快速开始
headings: 步骤 1 — Plan out your waiting room
步骤 2 — 创建 your waiting room
Prepare your waiting room for mobile application traffic
步骤 3 — Activate your waiting room
paragraphs: Before you start this tutorial, make sure you have:
Reviewed the About Waiting Room page.).
Reviewed your 速率限制 规则 to make sure they allow at least one 请求 every 20 seconds (必需 for automatic page refreshes).
Before you 创建 your waiting room, think about how you want it to appear and operate.
Which page will you cover with a waiting room? You can only have one waiting room per page, so you need to identify the high-traffic areas of your website.
Specify the URL for your page by setting the  and  in your 配置 设置.
Advanced Waiting Room customers can also specify mul提示le 主机名 and path combinations for the same 区域.
You can direct visitors to your high-traffic page:
Directly (via URL)
Indirectly (via a 重定向)
When you activate your waiting room, choose whether:
**All visitors** to be 队列d, in preparation for a product release or other time-based 事件.
Only **some visitors** to be 队列d, as traffic reaches the thresholds defined in  and .
创建 your waiting room by:
Using the 控制面板.
Using the API.
Some customers can customize the design of their waiting room by editing the page's HTML and CSS.
If you have this ability, think about how you want the page to appear.

If you need to manage traffic in a non-browser environment such as a mobile app or web app, use a JSON-friendly waiting room that can be consumed via your API 端点. 注意 that if you have a mobile app or web app that depends on 资源 that would be protected by a waiting room, you will need to 更新 those clients to handle Waiting Room appropriately.
De待处理 on your 队列 activation, you may 部署 your waiting room differently.
After you have 创建d and 部署ed your first waiting room, you might also want to:
测试 your waiting room before it goes live.
监控 your traffic in real time.
Troubleshoot potential issues.

/waiting-room/how-to/place-waiting-room/.title: Place a waiting room
headings: Apply to mul提示le 主机名s and paths
创建 exceptions to waiting room coverage
paragraphs: When configuring a waiting room, you need to indicate which pages the waiting room will cover.

Your waiting room requires at least one 主机名 and path in its 配置 设置. There is an implied 通配符 after the path, meaning the waiting room will also apply to any subpaths. When there is an 活跃 队列, all 新功能 users will enter the 队列 at any URLs covered by this 主机名 and path combination. If you have mul提示le waiting rooms, the waiting room with the most specific subpath takes precedence.

Advanced Waiting Room customers can apply a single waiting room to mul提示le 主机名s and paths. To do so via the UI, after adding the first 主机名 and path, select Add 主机名 and Path and then input the next 主机名 and path combination you would like the waiting room to cover. If adding more than one 主机名 and path for a single waiting room, you must also 创建 a unique waiting room Cookie by filling out the Custom Cookie 字段. To add mul提示le 主机名s and paths via the API, utilize the  字段 and customize the Cookie suffix with the  字段.
You cannot add any 主机名 and path combinations al读取y configured for another waiting room. 主机名s must belong to the 区域 that the waiting room is configured on.
A single waiting room can be applied to mul提示le custom 主机名s as long as the following is true:
The apex 域名 is the same between the custom 主机名s
Each custom 主机名 is configured explicitly in SSL for SaaS 设置.
To 部署 a waiting room to a custom 主机名, the non-通配符 custom 主机名 must be configured and 活跃 in SSL for SaaS. Then, 创建 a Waiting Room and 可选ly apply it to mul提示le 主机名s with the same apex 域名.

This means that – if you want a waiting room for  –  must be an 活跃 custom 主机名. You will not be able to 创建 a waiting room at  based on only  being 设置 as a custom 主机名, even if you have 已启用 通配符s for this custom 主机名 in SSL for SaaS 设置.

If there are subpaths or 查询 字符串s of the path you have configured for your waiting room that you would not like the waiting room to apply to, you can 创建 a Waiting Room bypass 规则 to ensure that traffic is not 队列d at these parts of your site.

/waiting-room/waiting-room-analytics/.title: Waiting Room 分析
headings: 活跃 users vs. 队列d users
Why is there no data for my waiting room?
paragraphs: Waiting Room 分析 gives you historical insights into the traffic going through your waiting room compared to your waiting room 设置. Data is stored for the past 30 days.
Using Waiting Room 分析, you can:
Evaluate peak traffic flow through your waiting room and onto your site.
Determine how long users spent in the waiting room.
Use 分析 to help calibrate your waiting room 设置.
To access your waiting room’s 分析 in the 控制面板:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Waiting Room** page.

Expand the waiting room you would like to review 指标 for, to display a preview of your waiting room 分析. The preview gives you insights into peak traffic through your waiting room over the last 24 hours including: Maximum 活跃 users, Maximum 队列d users and Typical time in 队列 for 队列d users.
Select **View More** under the Waiting Room 分析 section to 获取 more historical 分析 for your waiting room.

The time range for all of the 指标 displayed 默认s to the last 24 hours. To change the time range, select from the drop down. You can select any time range from the last 30 days that is a minimum of 30 minutes.

If your waiting room has a completed 定时 事件, you can quickly access the 事件’s 分析 by expanding the row for the waiting room you are interested in and selecting the 事件 time. The link opens the 分析 view for that waiting room, including 信息rmation from the pre-队列ing period to the end of the 事件.

To save this 事件 信息rmation, you can either select Download data or Print report. If you 删除 the 事件, the time period link will no longer appear in your 控制面板. If you edit the timing of the 事件, the time period link will 更新 as well.
If you do not 获取 a link to your 事件’s 分析, one of the following may have happened:
Your 事件 has not happened yet.
Your 事件 started more than 30 days ago.
These are 指标 available in the 分析 控制面板 and how they are calculated.

Time in 队列 summary 值s give you an insight into the user experience by indicating how long 队列d users spent waiting to enter your application. It displays the time waited for the typical user, as well as for those who waited the longest, for the time period you have selected. These 值s are an indicator of the impact your waiting room 设置 combined with the traffic to your waiting room had on wait times.
If wait times are higher than you would like, and you feel comfor表 doing so, you could consider taking any or all of the following 操作s:
Increase  configured 值.
Decrease .
Disable 会话 re新功能al.
:::注意
注意 that wait times are only calculated for users who went from the waiting room to your 源.

Time on 源 summary 值s estimate how long users spent on the pages covered by your waiting room before leaving. For the time period selected, you will have access to the estimated time spent on 源 for the typical user, as well as the time on 源 for those who spend the most time on your site. Keep in mind that if your 会话 re新功能al is 已禁用 and there is no 活跃 队列ing, users are issued a 新功能 waiting room 会话 every  minutes.  Therefore, these users may be staying for mul提示le 会话s.  The time on 源 for these users restarts each time a 会话 expires.
The following are some takeaways you could have de待处理 on the time on 源 值s.
You may want to increase 会话 duration, giving users more time to make sub请求s, and/or enable 会话 re新功能al if:
You have 会话 re新功能al 已禁用.
You have frequent, 活跃 队列ing with long wait times.
The typical time on 源 is around 70% of your configured 会话 duration.
These may be indicators that users need more time to complete their desired 任务s on your site.
You may want to decrease 会话 duration and/or disable 会话 re新功能al if:
Your top 5% time on 源 is less than 70% of your configured 会话 duration.
You are seeing high 队列 times and do not want to increase traffic 限制.
These may be indicators that users do not need as much time on your site and are taking up spots on your 源.

The 活跃 users chart is a time series chart that displays the maximum 活跃 users on any URLs covered by your waiting room as well as maximum 队列d users. These 值s are shown compared to your configured 活跃 user tar获取 threshold.

A 新功能 user is a novel 请求 made to any URLs covered by the waiting room. Waiting Room counts the 请求 as 新功能 if no waiting room Cookie is tied to the 请求. Once the 请求 is made, a waiting room Cookie is issued. If there is an 活跃 队列, the user will be considered a 队列d user. Once that user makes it through the 队列 and onto the site, they are now an 活跃 user and remain 活跃 as long as they keep making HTTP 请求s to waiting room URLs at least once every  minutes.

To identify and hone in on peak traffic, select a longer time period, such as 30 days. Then, drag your cursor to the left and right of any time period you would like to check with more granularity to zoom in. You can zoom in until each bar represents a one minute interval. All other 指标 on the page will 更新 automatically to reflect the data behind the time period selected.
To check for more details about a particular moment in time, hover over a bar on the graph. This displays a tool提示 which will indicate the following for the time period that bar represents:
Maximum 活跃 users reached
Maximum 队列d users reached
Configured 活跃 user tar获取 值s
队列ing may occur below your configured 限制, and 活跃 users may sometimes exceed your configured 限制. Refer to the Queuing activation section for more 信息rmation.

The 新功能 users per minute chart shows how many 新功能 users per minute passed through the waiting room to your 源 compared to your configured 新功能 users per minute tar获取 threshold. Like the 活跃 users chart, you can zoom in by highlighting to the left and right of the time period you are interested in, which will 更新 the other chart as well as summary 值s. As you zoom out, each data point is averaged. Therefore, as you zoom in, 值s may fluctuate.
The Turnstile wid获取 traffic chart shows the 数字 of challenges issued per minute and the distribution of traffic seen with these challenges. Traffic is categorized into three main categories:
Likely Human - This represents the 数字 of challenges that were 成功fully solved.
Likely Bots - This represents the 数字 of unsolved challenges.
Bots - This represents the 数字 of 已失败 challenges.
If your waiting room has the infinite 队列 option 已启用, you will see a line on the graph representing the 数字 of refresh 请求s from bots in the infinite 队列.

You can 查询 your Waiting Room 分析 data via GraphQL API. Waiting Room 分析 provides near real-time visibility into your Waiting Room, allowing you to visualize the traffic to your application and how it is managed respecting the configured 限制.
Here are some 查询 示例 to 快速开始:

This is a simple 查询 to fetch 指标 值s. You can 过滤器 the data with the 区域 tag and 查询 the   dataset. In this 示例, we have applied this 查询 only on two 指标, but you can explore the 模式 and fetch the raw 值s from the GraphQL dataset without applying any aggregation 方法.

/waiting-room/reference/waiting-room-coo.title: Cookies
headings: Estimated wait time (FIFO 队列ing method)
paragraphs: A waiting room only uses the  Cookie when a visitor 请求s access to a host and path combination with an 已启用 and associated waiting room. When the waiting room is suspended, traffic goes to the 源 and the  Cookie is not 创建d. The  Cookie is encrypted to pr事件 modification by users. You may append a custom suffix to your waiting room Cookie to customize the name of your waiting room Cookie.
:::注意[重要:]
Cloudflare Waiting Room requires the  Cookie. When a waiting room is 活跃ly 队列ing, users cannot visit that host and path combination without enabling Cookies.
The  Cookie is used to:
Track a user's position in the waiting room 队列 and serve them in the correct order.
监控 each visitor's duration in the application to provide an accurate entry time to visitors 队列ing in the waiting room.
To allow re-entry for a period of time (specified by 会话\_duration) without going back in the waiting room.

While a visitor stays in a waiting room,  Cookie expiration is always set to five minutes, but re新功能s every 20 seconds automatically as long as the visitor does not close the tab or leaves your application.
When the visitor accesses the application, the  Cookie expires after an interval (specified by 会话\_duration).
You can customize the name of your waiting room Cookie by adding a custom suffix to the end of .

To do this via the UI, complete the Custom Cookie 字段 in the 创建 or Edit 工作流. To do this via the API, enter a 值 for  when creating or editing a waiting room. The Cookie suffix is a 必需 字段 when using additional 主机名s and paths for a single waiting room. Ensure your Cookie is compliant with any applicable policies.

When a visitor first enters the host and path combination for your waiting room, they receive the  Cookie. That Cookie contains a unique group ID, which corresponds to the minute your visitor entered the waiting room. Using this 值, we can tell how many visitors are in front of a specific group.

Each Cookie also contains a 值 for , which corresponds to the minute your visitor entered your application. This 值 lets us know how many visitors per minute are leaving the waiting room to enter your application.
We combine these pieces of 信息rmation to calculate estimated wait time for each group of visitors.
For more details about the technical implementation of Cloudflare Waiting Room, refer to the blog post.

The Cloudflare WARP client allows individuals to have a faster, more secure, and more private experience online. The WARP client sits between your device and the Internet, and has several connection modes to better suit different needs.
The WARP client has several modes to better suit your connection needs.
The WARP client runs on several operating systems, including iOS and Android.
1.1.1.1 is Cloudflare’s public DNS 解析器. It offers a fast and private way
to browse the Internet.
The enterprise version of WARP allows organizations to apply 安全 policies
to corporate devices.

/warp-client/warp-modes/.title: WARP modes
paragraphs: The WARP client has several modes to better suit different connection needs.

1.1.1.1 is Cloudflare’s public DNS 解析器. It offers a fast and private way to browse the Internet. It also offers a DNS 加密 service through DNS over HTTPS (DoH) or DNS over TLS (DoT) for increased 安全 and privacy.
Refer to 1.1.1.1 解析器 to 了解更多 about DNS 加密.

The WARP application uses MASQUE to encrypt and send traffic from your device directly to Cloudflare's 全球网络. This ensures Internet traffic between your device and the Internet is secure and private, while also pr事件ing third parties from accessing your traffic. All traffic[^1] 隧道ed over the MASQUE connection is encrypted using post-quantum cryptography to protect against harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks.
[^1]: Post-quantum cryptography requires the following minimum WARP versions:   Android: 2.4.3  iOS: 1.11.1   Windows, macOS, and Linux: 2025.6.1335.0

If the site you are visiting is al读取y a Cloudflare customer, the content is immediately sent to your device. If not, Cloudflare uses its 全球网络 of 数据中心 to devise the shor测试 path to the site. For more 信息rmation, refer to our blog post Introducing WARP: Fixing Mobile Internet 性能 and 安全.
:::注意

WARP does not provide anonymity, and it is not designed to pr事件 servers you communicate with from identifying you. WARP also does not allow you to pretend to be accessing the Internet from a different country.

Currently, this mode is available on desktop clients only. When WARP is configured as a local 代理, only the applications that you configure to use the 代理 (HTTPS or SOCKS5) will have their traffic sent through WARP. This allows you to pick and choose which traffic is encrypted — for 示例, your web browser or a specific application. Everything else will not be encrypted and will be sent over a regular Internet connection.

Because this 特性 restricts WARP to just applications configured to use the local 代理, leaving all other traffic over the Internet unencrypted by 默认, we have hidden it in the Advanced menu. To turn it on:
Navigate to **P参考s** > **Advanced** and select **Configure 代理**.
On the window that opens, check the box and configure the port you want to 列表en on.
This will enable the WARP via Local 代理 option in the WARP 设置 menu.
If you enable FIPS compliance for TLS 解密, you must disable QUIC in your users' browsers. Otherwise, HTTP/3 traffic will bypass inspection by the WARP client.

While WARP can take advantage of the many Cloudflare 数据中心 around the world to give you a more private and robust connection, WARP+ Unlimited 订阅者s 获取 access to a larger network. More cities to connect to means you are likely to be closer to a Cloudflare 数据中心, which can reduce 延迟 and improve your browsing speed.
WARP+ Unlimited is a paid, monthly subscription that can be purchased via the Apple App Store or Google Play Store.
To 订阅 to WARP+ Unlimited:
On an iOS or Android device, launch the **1.1.1.1: Faster Internet** app.
Select **设置** > **Upgrade to WARP+**. A dialog will appear with the subscription price.

To confirm your subscription, select **订阅 to WARP+ Unlimited**. All payments are handled by the Apple/Google app store, and the payment 信息rmation associated with your Apple/Google 账户 will be charged for these subscriptions.
WARP+ Unlimited is now 活跃 on this device. You can use your license 键 on up to five devices.

/web-analytics/data-metrics/dimensions/.title: Dimensions
paragraphs: Dimensions are the labels used to describe different 类型s of 指标 or data. For 示例, Referer is the data collected from external links referring visits to a page, while Browser shows which browsers accessed your website.
Below you can find a 列表 of the different dimensions you can use to 过滤器 Web Analytics:
**Country**: The visitor's country.
**Host**: The 域名 of the site's URL.
**Path**: The links within your site referring visits to a page.
**Referer**: The external links referring visits to a page. You can access  data on the 控制面板. Additionally, you can access data for the  from the GraphQL API.
**Device 类型**: The device visitors use to access a page (for 示例, desktop, mobile, or 表t).
**Browser**: The web browser (for 示例, Chrome, Safari) visitors use to access your website.
**Operating system**: The operating system visitors use to access a page.
**Site**: The website's 域名 name. Used for high-level segmentation of data. For 示例, you can use it for a particular 区域 or gray-clouded website.
**Exclude Bots**: Exclude bot traffic from the dataset. With this dimension set to , the resulting dataset will be a closer representation of real user traffic.

/web3/ethereum-gateway/concepts/ethereum.title: Ethereum network
headings: Connect your website to the gateway
paragraphs: The Ethereum network is a distributed consensus 平台 that allows users to
write and compute smart contracts in a distributed manner. Smart contracts are
essentially Turing complete programs that are available at a unique address of
the network. When the smart contract is run as part of a 事务, the result
and the current state of the contract are stored in a verifiable consensus that
is agreed upon by the entire network of nodes.
When a user wants to run a smart contract on some desired inputs, they provide
currency known as ETH with their command. This currency is allocated to a
specific set of nodes that contribute to adding the 事务 to the
distributed consensus.
If the smart contract involves moving monetary amounts to different individuals
in the network then this is also 记录ed in the consensus. As such, this
consensus represents the current state of the network along with exactly how
much Ethereum currency is owned by each individual.
All 事务s on the network are stored in 'blocks' that make up the entire
consensus. In brief, the consensus is a single sequence of blocks with
increasing unique identifiers that represent all the 事务s in the entire
history of the Ethereum 平台.
Each block also has a unique hash identifier (a long hexadecimal 字符串 like
) that can
be used to retrieve and inspect the block. Each 事务 within a block also
has a unique hash identifier that can be used to retrieve individual
事务s. When any 新功能 事务 is uploaded to the consensus as part of a
block, this 更新 is sent around the entire network and anyone can 读取 the
nature of the 事务 that took place. This makes the entire state of the
network 账户able.
To 读取 content, a user needs to interact with a working Ethereum node. Such
nodes can be run locally on a user's machine as daemons (such as:
https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/). When the user accesses such a node,
they can use the official JSONRPC API to send queries to the network, and learn
about specific aspects of the consensus.
Writing content is just as simple, and can be done using a [single JSON
command](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_sendraw事务).
However, writing 事务s also requires supplying a certain amount of
currency along with a 密钥 键 for an [Ethereum
wallet](https://www.ethereum.org/use/#_3-what-is-a-wallet-and-which-one-should-i-use)
that holds that currency. If this can be provided, then the write 事务 is
sent to the wider network and added to the consensus.
If you want to be able to access the Ethereum network accessible from a custom
域名 name, you can do that by creating a 新功能 Ethereum Gateway.
If you’re interested in learning more, you can 读取 the official [RPC
文档](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC), along with the
official 文档 provided by Ethereum.

/web3/ethereum-gateway/.title: Ethereum Gateway
paragraphs: Cloudflare's Ethereum Gateway gives you 读取 and write access to the Ethereum network without 安装ing any software on your computer.

In particular, users can 读取 all 信息rmation that has been agreed upon by the consensus of existing nodes in the network. In addition, they can write their own 事务s and smart contracts to be stored by these nodes in a distributed manner. Anyone else on the network will be able to view these 事务s, and even run your smart contracts using their own supply of the Ethereum currency.
These inter操作s take place through the official Ethereum JSON-RPC API and use Cloudflare-支持的 API 方法.

/web3/ipfs-gateway/concepts/ipfs/.title: Interplanetary File System (IPFS)
paragraphs: The Interplanetary File System (IPFS) is a distributed file storage 协议 that allows computers all over the globe to store and serve files as part of a giant peer-to-peer network.
Any computer, anywhere in the world, can download the IPFS software and start hosting and serving files.
If someone runs IPFS on their computer and uploads a file to the IPFS network, that file can be viewed and downloaded by anyone else in the world who is also 运行中 IPFS.

Every file added to IPFS is given a unique address derived from a hash of the file's content. This address is called a Content Identifier (CID) and it combines the hash of the file and a unique identifier for the hash algorithm used into a single 字符串.

IPFS currently uses SHA-256 by 默认, which produces a 256 bit (32 byte) output, and that output is encoded with Base58. Base58 is a binary-to-text encoding scheme 源ally developed for Bitcoin and has the advantage that letters that might be mistaken for each other in certain fonts (like zero and the capital letter O) are not included.
A CID will typically look something like .
However, the same hash could be encoded with Base32 or other 支持的 hash algorithms including SHA-3 and BLAKE2).
IPFS is fundamentally a [Distributed Hash 表
(DHT)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distributed_hash_表) which maps from CIDs to people who have the content addressed by that CID. The hash
表 is distributed because no single node in the network holds the entire
表. Instead, each node stores a subset of the hash 表, as well as
信息rmation about which nodes are storing other relevant sections.
When someone talks about 'uploading' content to IPFS, what they really mean

(usually) is that they are announcing to the network that they have some content by adding an entry to the DHT that maps from CID to their IP address. Some正文 else who wants to download their data would look up the CID in the DHT, find the person's IP address, and download the data directly from them.

The speed and reliability advantages of IPFS come from the fact that many people can upload the same data, and then downloads will be sp读取 between all of them. If any one of them goes offline or decides to stop hosting the data, the others can pick up the slack.
You can upload more than just individual files. For 示例, consider a folder called , which has exactly one file, , containing the 字符串 .
If that folder were uploaded with the command , both the folder and the file it contains would have their own CID. In this case, the folder would have the CID  while the file would have the CID .
You could then access the file in two ways:
请求ing the file directly:
请求ing the file by name, from the directory:
While the CID of a file will only change if the file itself changes, the CID of a directory changes any time any of the files in it change, or if any files are added/removed.
Directories make it possible to address an entire static website with a single CID and access different pages of the website by 请求ing different files in the directory.
For help with additional concepts, refer to the IPFS 文档.

Run machine learning models, powered by 无服务器 GPUs, on Cloudflare's 全球网络.

Workers AI allows you to run AI models in a 无服务器 way, without having to worry about scaling, maintaining, or paying for unused infrastructure. You can invoke models 运行中 on GPUs on Cloudflare's network from your own code — from Workers, Pages, or anywhere via the Cloudflare API.
Workers AI gives you access to:

/workers-ai/platform/pricing/.title: 定价
paragraphs: :::注意
Workers AI has 已更新 定价 to be more granular, with per-model unit-based 定价 presented, but still billing in neurons in the back end.
Workers AI is included in both the Free and Paid Workers plans and is priced at $0.011 per 1,000 Neurons.

Our free allocation allows anyone to use a total of 10,000 Neurons per day at no charge. To use more than 10,000 Neurons per day, you need to 注册 for the Workers Paid plan. On Workers Paid, you will be charged at $0.011 / 1,000 Neurons for any usage above the free allocation of 10,000 Neurons per day.
You can 监控 your Neuron usage in the Cloudflare Workers AI 控制面板.
All 限制 reset daily at 00:00 UTC. If you exceed any one of the above 限制, further operations will fail with an 错误.

Neurons are our way of measuring AI outputs across different models, representing the GPU compute needed to perform your 请求. Our 无服务器 model allows you to pay only for what you use without having to worry about renting, managing, or scaling GPUs.
The Price in 令牌s column is equivalent to the Price in Neurons column - the different units are displayed so you can easily compare and understand 定价.

paragraphs: VPC Service 绑定 provide a convenient API for accessing VPC Services from your Worker. Each 绑定 represents a connection to a service in your private network through a Cloudflare Tunnel.
Each 请求 made on the 绑定 will 路由 to the specific service that was configured for the VPC Service, while restricting access to the rest of your private network.
:::注意
Workers VPC is currently in 测试版. 特性 and APIs may change before general 可用性. While in 测试版, Workers VPC is available for free to all Workers plans.
A VPC Service 绑定 is accessed via the  parameter in your Worker's fetch 处理程序. It provides a  method for making HTTP 请求s to your private service.
:::注意[必需 角色]
To bind a VPC Service in a Worker, your user needs  (or ). For role definitions, refer to 角色.
Makes an HTTP 请求 to the private service through the configured tunnel.
The VPC Service 配置s will always be used to connect and 路由 请求s to your services in external networks, even if a different URL or host is present in the actual  operation of the Worker code.

The host provided in the  operation is not used to 路由 请求s, and instead only populates the  字段 for a HTTP 请求 that can be parsed by the server and used for Server Name Indication (SNI), when the  scheme is specified.
The port provided in the  operation is ignored — the port specified in the VPC Service 配置 will be used.
(字符串 | URL | 请求) - The URL to fetch. This must be an absolute URL including 协议, host, and path (for 示例, )
(可选 请求Init) - Standard fetch options including:
- HTTP method (获取, POST, PUT, 删除, etc.)
- 请求 头部
- 请求 正文
- AbortSignal for 请求 cancellation
:::注意[Absolute URLs 必需]
VPC Service fetch 请求s must use absolute URLs including the 协议 (/), host, and path. Relative paths are not 支持的.
Returns a  that resolves to a standard Fetch API 响应 对象.

/workers-vpc/configuration/vpc-services/.title: VPC Services
paragraphs: VPC Services are the core 构建ing block of Workers VPC. They represent specific 资源 in your private network that Workers can access through Cloudflare Tunnel.
You can use 绑定 to connect to VPC Services from Workers. Every 请求 made to a VPC Service using its  函数 will be securely 路由d to the configured service in the private network.
VPC Services enforce that 请求s are 路由d to their intended service without exposing the entire network, securing your workloads and pr事件ing server-side 请求 forgery (SSRF).
Members must possess Connectivity Directory Bind role to bind to existing VPC Services from Workers. Creating VPC Services requires members to possess the Connectivity Directory Admin role.
:::注意
Workers VPC is currently in 测试版. 特性 and APIs may change before general 可用性. While in 测试版, Workers VPC is available for free to all Workers plans.
A VPC Service consists of:
**类型**: Currently only  is 支持的 (支持 for  即将推出)
**Tunnel ID**: The Cloudflare Tunnel that provides network connectivity
**主机名 or IPv4/IPv6 addresses**: The 主机名, or IPv4 and/or IPv6 addresses to use to 路由 to your service from the tunnel in your private network
**Ports**: HTTP and/or HTTPS port 配置 (可选, 默认s to 80/443)
**解析器 IPs**: 可选ly, a specific 解析器 IP can be provided — when not provided,  will direct DNS traffic to the currently configured 默认 system 解析器.

请求s are encrypted in flight until they reach your network via a tunnel, regardless of the scheme used in the URL provided to . If the  scheme is used, a plaintext connection is established to the service from the tunnel.

The  scheme can be used for an encrypted connection within your network, between the tunnel and your service. When the  scheme is specified, a 主机名 provided to the  operation is utilized as the Server Name Indication (SNI) 值.
VPC Services 默认 to allowing both  and  schemes to be used. You can provide 值s for only one of  or  to enforce the use of a particular scheme.
When Workers VPC is unable to establish a connection to your service,  will throw an exception.
The VPC Service 配置 host and port(s) will always be used to connect and 路由 请求s to your services, even if a different host or port is present in the URL provided to the  operation in the Worker code.
The host provided in the  operation is not used to 路由 请求s, and instead only populates the  字段 for a HTTP 请求, or  and the Server Name Indication (SNI) 值 presented to your service for a HTTPS 请求.
The port provided in the  operation is ignored — the port specified in the VPC Service 配置 for the provided scheme will be used.
When using the  scheme, the tunnel verifies the TLS 证书 presented by your 源 service. Workers VPC trusts the following 证书 类型s:
**Publicly trusted 证书s** — 证书s issued by well-known public 证书 authorities (for 示例, Let's Encrypt, DigiCert).

**Cloudflare 源 CA 证书s** — Free 证书s issued by Cloudflare that encrypt traffic between Cloudflare and your 源. 源 CA 证书s are not trusted by browsers, but are trusted by Workers VPC when connecting to your private services.
If your 源 service presents a 证书 that is not issued by a publicly trusted CA or by Cloudflare 源 CA, the TLS 握手 will fail and  will throw an exception.

The following is an 示例 of a VPC Service for a service using custom HTTP and HTTPS ports, and both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses. These 配置s represent the expected contract of the REST API for creating a VPC Service, a 类型 of service within the broader connectivity directory.
The following is an 示例 of a VPC Service for a service using custom HTTP and HTTPS ports as well, using a 主机名. 注意 that since we are using a 主机名, we must provide our service with a  that 可选ly has .
Once you have 创建d a VPC Service, you can bind it to your Worker:

This 指南 will walk you through creating your first Workers VPC Service, allowing your Worker to access 资源 in your private network.

You will 创建 a Workers application, 创建 a Tunnel in your private network to connect it to Cloudflare, and then configure VPC Services for the services on your private network you want to access from Workers.
:::注意
Workers VPC is currently in 测试版. 特性 and APIs may change before general 可用性. While in 测试版, Workers VPC is available for free to all Workers plans.
开始之前, ensure you have completed the following:
Additionally, you will need:
Access to a private network (your local network, AWS VPC, Azure VNet, GCP VPC, or on-premise networks)

Or, the **Connectivity Directory Admin** role to 创建 VPC Services, and bind to them from Workers.

Navigate to your project directory:

A Cloudflare Tunnel 创建s a secure connection from your private network to Cloudflare. This tunnel will allow Workers to securely access your private 资源. You can 创建 the tunnel on a virtual machine or container in your external cloud, or even on your local desktop for the sake of this tutorial.
Navigate to the Workers VPC 控制面板 and select the **隧道s** tab.
Select **创建** to 创建 a 新功能 tunnel.
Enter a name for your tunnel (for 示例, ) and select **Save tunnel**.
Choose your operating system and architecture. The 控制面板 will provide specific 安装 instructions for your environment.
Follow the provided commands to download and 安装 , and execute the service 安装 command with your unique 令牌.
The 控制面板 will confirm when your tunnel is 成功fully connected.

Once your tunnel is connected, you will need to ensure it can access the services that you want your Workers to have access to. The tunnel should be 安装ed on a machine that can reach the internal 资源 you want to expose to Workers VPC. In external clouds, this may mean configuring Access-Control-列表s, 安全 Groups, or VPC 防火墙 规则 to ensure that the tunnel can access the desired services.
This 指南 provides a quick 设置 for Workers VPC.
For comprehensive tunnel 配置, 监控, and management, refer to the full Cloudflare Tunnel 文档.
Now that your tunnel is 运行中, 创建 a VPC Service that Workers can use to access your internal 资源:
Navigate to the Workers VPC 控制面板 and select the **VPC Services** tab.
Select **创建** to 创建 a 新功能 VPC Service.
Enter a **Service name** for your VPC Service (for 示例, ).
Select your tunnel from the **Tunnel** dropdown, or select **创建 Tunnel** if you need to 创建 a 新功能 one.
Enter the **Host or IP address** of your internal service (for 示例, , , or ).
Configure **Ports**. Select either:
**Use 默认 ports** for standard HTTP (80) and HTTPS (443)
**Provide port 值s** to specify custom HTTP and HTTPS ports
Configure **DNS 解析器**. Select either:
**Use tunnel as 解析器** to use the tunnel's built-in DNS resolution
**Custom 解析器** and enter your DNS 解析器 IP (for 示例, )
Select **创建 service** to 创建 your VPC Service.
The 控制面板 will display your 新功能 VPC Service with a unique Service ID. Save this Service ID for the next 步骤.

/workers-vpc/.title: Cloudflare Workers VPC
/workers-vpc/.description: Securely connect your private cloud to Cloudflare to 构建 cross-cloud apps.
headings: Access private APIs from Workers applications

paragraphs: Description,
Plan,

Securely connect your private cloud to Cloudflare to 构建 cross-cloud apps.
Workers VPC allows you to connect your Workers to your private APIs and services in external clouds (AWS, Azure, GCP, on-premise, etc.) that are not accessible from the public Internet.

With Workers VPC, you can configure a Cloudflare Tunnel to establish secure, private connections from your private networks to Cloudflare. Then, you can configure a VPC Service for each service in the external private network you need to connect to, and use VPC Service 绑定 to connect from Workers.
:::注意
Workers VPC is currently in 测试版. 特性 and APIs may change before general 可用性. While in 测试版, Workers VPC is available for free to all Workers plans.

部署 APIs or full-stack applications to Workers that connect to private 身份验证 services, CMS systems, internals APIs, and more. Your Workers applications run globally with optimized access to the backend services of your private network.
路由 请求s to internal microservices in your private network based on URL paths. Centralize access control and load balancing for mul提示le private services on Workers.
构建 employee-facing applications and MCP servers that aggregate data from mul提示le private services. 创建 unified 控制面板s, admin panels, and internal 工具 without exposing backend systems.
构建 无服务器 applications and 部署 instantly across the globe for exceptional 性能, reliability, and scale.
Connect to PostgreSQL and MySQL 数据库s from Workers with connection pooling and 缓存 built-in, available to all Workers plans.

/workers/configuration/cron-triggers/.description: Enable your Worker to be executed on a schedule.
headings: 1. Define a 定时 事件 列表ener
Via the Wrangler 配置 file

Via the Wrangler 配置 file
paragraphs: Render,
WranglerConfig,
TabItem,
Tabs,
DashButton,
} from "~/components";
Cron 触发器s allow users to map a cron 表达式 to a Worker using a  处理程序 that enables Workers to be executed on a schedule.

Cron 触发器s are ideal for 运行中 periodic 作业s, such as for maintenance or calling third-party APIs to collect up-to-date data. Workers 定时 by Cron 触发器s will run on underutilized machines to make the best use of Cloudflare's capacity and 路由 traffic efficiently.
:::注意
Cron 触发器s can also be combined with 工作流s to 触发器 multi-步骤, long-运行中 任务s. You can bind to a 工作流 directly from your Cron 触发器 to execute a 工作流 on a schedule.
Cron 触发器s execute on UTC time.
To respond to a Cron 触发器, you must add a  处理程序 to your Worker.
Refer to the following additional 示例 to write your code:
Setting Cron 触发器s
Mul提示le Cron 触发器s
:::注意[Cron 触发器 changes take time to propagate.]
Changes such as adding a 新功能 Cron 触发器, updating an old Cron 触发器, or deleting a Cron 触发器 may take several minutes (up to 15 minutes) to propagate to the Cloudflare 全球网络.
After you have 已更新 your Worker code to include a  事件, you must 更新 your Worker project 配置.
If a Worker is managed with Wrangler, Cron 触发器s should be exclusively managed through the Wrangler 配置 file.
Refer to the 示例 below for a Cron 触发器s 配置:
You also can set a different Cron 触发器 for each environment in your Wrangler 配置 file. You need to put the  数组 under your chosen environment. For 示例:
To add Cron 触发器s in the Cloudflare 控制面板:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Workers & Pages** page.
In **概述**, select your Worker > **设置** > **触发器s** > **Cron 触发器s**.
Cloudflare 支持s cron 表达式s with five 字段s, along with most Quartz scheduler-like cron syntax 扩展:
Days of the week go from 1 = Sunday to 7 = Saturday, which is different on some other cron systems (where 0 = Sunday and 6 = Saturday).
To avoid ambiguity you may prefer to use the three-letter abbreviations (e.g.  rather than 1).
Some common time intervals that may be useful for setting up your Cron 触发器:
At every minute
At every 30th minute
On the 45th minute of every hour
17:00 (UTC) on Sunday
07:10 (UTC) on weekdays
15:00 (UTC) on first day of the month
18:00 (UTC) on the last Friday of the month
23:59 (UTC) on the last weekday of the month
测试 Cron 触发器s using Wrangler with [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#dev), or using the Cloudflare Vite plugin. This will expose a  路由 which can be used to 测试 using a HTTP 请求.
To simulate different cron patterns, a  查询 parameter can be passed in.
可选ly, you can also pass a  查询 parameter to override  in your 定时 事件 列表ener.
To view the 执行 history of Cron 触发器s, view Cron 事件:
In **概述**, select your **Worker**.
Select **设置**.
Under **触发器 事件**, select **View 事件**.

Cron 事件 stores the 100 most recent 调用s of the Cron 定时 事件. Workers 日志 also 记录s 调用 日志 for the Cron 触发器 with a longer retention period and a 过滤器 & 查询 interface. If you are interested in an API to access Cron 事件, use Cloudflare's GraphQL 分析 API.
It can take up to 30 minutes before 事件 are displayed in Past Cron 事件 when creating a 新功能 Worker or changing a Worker's name.
Refer to 指标 and 分析 for more 信息rmation.
To 删除 a Cron 触发器 on a 部署ed Worker via the 控制面板:
Select your Worker.
Go to **触发器s** > select the three dot icon next to the Cron 触发器 you want to remove > **删除**.
When 部署ing a Worker with Wrangler any previous Cron 触发器s are replaced with those specified in the  数组.
If the  property is an empty 数组 then all the Cron 触发器s are removed.
If the  or  property are  then the currently 部署 Cron 触发器s are left in-place.
Refer to 限制 to track the maximum 数字 of Cron 触发器s per Worker.

With Green Compute 已启用, your Cron 触发器s will only run on Cloudflare points of presence that are located in 数据中心 that are powered purely by re新功能able energy. Organizations may claim that they are powered by 100 percent re新功能able energy if they have procured sufficient re新功能able energy to 账户 for their overall energy use.

Re新功能able energy can be purchased in a 数字 of ways, including through on-site generation (wind turbines, solar panels), directly from re新功能able energy 生产者s through contractual agreements called Power Purchase Agreements (PPA), or in the form of Re新功能able Energy Credits (REC, IRECs, GoOs) from an energy credit market.
Green Compute can be configured at the 账户 level:
In the **账户 details** section, find **Compute Setting**.
Select **Change**.
Select **Green Compute**.
Select **Confirm**.
