![部署 to Workers](https://dash.cloudflare.com/?to=/:账户/workers-and-pages/创建/部署-to-workers&repository=https://github.com/cloudflare/templates/tree/main/astro-blog-starter-template)
Astro is a JavaScript web framework designed for creating websites that display large amounts of content (such as b日志, 文档 sites, or online stores).

Astro emphasizes 性能 through minimal client-side JavaScript - by 默认, it renders as much content as possible at 构建 time, or on-demand on the "server" - this can be a Cloudflare Worker. “Islands” of JavaScript are added only where interactivity or personalization is needed.
Astro is also framework-agnostic, and 支持s every major UI framework, including React, Preact, Svelte, Vue, SolidJS, via its official 集成.
**创建 a 新功能 project with the 创建-cloudflare CLI (C3).**

When you run this command, C3 创建s a 新功能 project directory, initiates Astro's official 设置 tool, and configures the project for Cloudflare. It then offers the option to instantly 部署 your application to Cloudflare.
**Develop locally.**
After creating your project, run the following command in your project directory to start a local development server.
**部署 your project.**

You can 部署 your project to a  子域名 or a 自定义域名 from your local machine or any CI/CD system (including Workers 构建s). Use the following command to 构建 and 部署. If you're using a CI service, be sure to 更新 your "部署 command" accordingly.
:::提示[Automatic 配置]
Run  in a project without a Wrangler 配置 file and Wrangler will automatically detect Astro, generate the necessary 配置, and 部署 your project.
了解更多 about automatic project 配置.
If you prefer to configure your project manually, follow the 步骤s below.
If your Astro project is entirely pre-rendered, follow these 步骤s:
**Add a Wrangler 配置 file**
In your project root, 创建 a Wrangler 配置 file with the following content:

The 键 part of this config is the  字段, which tells Wrangler where to find your static assets. In this case, we're telling Wrangler to look in the  directory. If your assets are in a different directory, 更新 the  值 accordingly.
读取 about other asset 配置 options.
Also 注意 how there's no  字段 in this config - this is because you're only serving static assets, so no Worker code is needed for on demand rendering/SSR.
**构建 and 部署 your project**
If your Astro project uses on demand rendering (also known as SSR), follow these 步骤s:
**安装 the Astro Cloudflare adapter**

This command 安装s the Cloudflare adapter and makes the appropriate changes to your  file in one 步骤. By 默认, this sets the 构建 output 配置 to , which server renders all your pages by 默认. If there are certain pages that don't need on demand rendering/SSR, for 示例 static pages like a privacy policy, you should set  for that page or 路由 to pre-render it. You can 阅读更多 about the adapter 配置 options in the Astro docs.
**Add a  file**
创建 a  file in your  folder, and add the following lines to it:
The 键 parts of this config are:
points to the entry point of your Worker script. This is generated by the Astro adapter, and is what powers your server-rendered pages.
tells Wrangler where to find your static assets. In this case, we're telling Wrangler to look in the  directory. If your assets are in a different directory, 更新 the  值 accordingly.
阅读更多 about Wrangler 配置 options and asset 配置 options.
:::注意
You cannot use 绑定 if you're using Astro to generate a purely static site.

With 绑定, your Astro application can be fully integrated with the Cloudflare Developer 平台, giving you access to compute, storage, AI and more. Refer to the 绑定 概述 for more 信息rmation on what's available and how to configure them.
The Astro docs provide 信息rmation about how you can access them in your .

Astro's 会话s API allows you to store user data between 请求s, such as user p参考s, shopping carts, or 身份验证 credentials. When using the Cloudflare adapter, Astro automatically configures Workers KV for 会话 storage.
Wrangler automatically provisions a KV 命名空间 named  when you 部署, so no manual 设置 is 必需.
You can customize the KV 绑定 name with the [](https://docs.astro.构建/en/指南/集成-指南/cloudflare/#会话kv绑定name) adapter option if you want to use a different 绑定 name.
To serve a custom 404 page for your Astro site, add  to your Wrangler 配置:
This tells Cloudflare to serve your custom 404 page (for 示例, ) when a 路由 is not found. 阅读更多 about static asset 路由 behavior.

The Astro Cloudflare adapter sets the 构建 output 配置 to , which means all pages are rendered on-demand in your Cloudflare Worker. If there are certain pages that _don't_ need on demand rendering/SSR, for 示例 static pages such as a privacy policy, you should set  for that page or 路由 to pre-render it. You can 阅读更多 about on-demand rendering in the Astro docs.

If you want to use Astro as a static site generator, you do not need the Astro Cloudflare adapter. Astro will pre-render all pages at 构建 time by 默认, and you can simply upload those static assets to be served by Cloudflare.
Astro 5.x requires Node.js 18.17.1 or higher. Astro 6 (currently in 测试版) requires Node.js 22 or higher. If you're using Workers 构建s, ensure your 构建 environment meets these 要求.

/workers/configuration/placement/.title: Placement
/workers/configuration/placement/.description: Control where your Worker runs to reduce 延迟.
headings: Configure in the 控制面板
Review 请求 duration 分析
示例: Edge 身份验证 with a placed back-end
paragraphs: By 默认, Workers and Pages 函数s run in a 数据中心 closest to where the 请求 was received. If your Worker makes 请求s to back-end infrastructure such as 数据库s or APIs, it may be more performant to run that Worker closer to your back-end than the end user.

Placement can reduce the overall 延迟 of a Worker 请求 by minimizing roundtrip 延迟 of 请求s between your Worker and back-end services. You can achieve single-digit millisecond 延迟 to 数据库s, APIs, and other services 运行中 in legacy cloud infrastructure.
Consider a user in Sydney, Australia accessing an application 运行中 on Workers. This application makes mul提示le round trips to a 数据库 in Frankfurt, Germany.
The 延迟 from mul提示le round trips between Sydney and Frankfurt adds up. By placing the Worker near the 数据库, Cloudflare reduces the total 请求 duration.
Smart Placement automatically analyzes your Worker's traffic patterns and places it in an optimal location. Use Smart Placement when:
Your Worker connects to mul提示le back-end services
You do not know the exact location of your infrastructure
Your back-end services are distributed or replicated
Smart Placement is 已启用 on a per-Worker basis. Once 已启用, it analyzes the 请求 duration of the Worker in different Cloudflare locations on a regular basis.

For each candidate location, Smart Placement considers the Worker's 性能 and the network 延迟 added by forwarding the 请求. If a candidate location is significantly faster, the 请求 is forwarded there. Otherwise, the Worker runs in the 默认 location closest to the 请求.
Smart Placement only considers locations where the Worker has previously run. It cannot place your Worker in a location that does not normally receive traffic.
Smart Placement only affects the 执行 of fetch 事件 处理程序s. It does not affect RPC 方法 or named entrypoints.
Workers without a fetch 事件 处理程序 are ignored by Smart Placement.
Static assets are always served from the location nearest to the incoming 请求. If your code retrieves assets via the static assets 绑定, assets are served from the location where your Worker runs.
Smart Placement is available on all Workers plans.
Add the following to your Wrangler 配置 file:
Smart Placement may take up to 15 minutes to analyze your Worker after 部署.
Go to **Workers & Pages**.
Select your Worker.
Go to **设置** > **General**.
Under **Placement**, select **Smart**.
Smart Placement requires consistent traffic to the Worker from mul提示le locations to make a placement decision. The analysis process may take up to 15 minutes.
查询 your Worker's placement status through the Workers API:
Possible placement states:

Once Smart Placement is 已启用, data about 请求 duration is collected. 请求 duration is measured at the 数据中心 closest to the end user. By 默认, 1% of 请求s are not 路由d with Smart Placement to serve as a baseline for comparison.
View your Worker's 请求 duration 分析 to measure the impact of Smart Placement.
Cloudflare adds a  头部 to all 请求s when placement is 已启用. Use this 头部 to check whether a 请求 was 路由d with Smart Placement and where the Worker processed the 请求.
The 头部 值 includes a placement 类型 and an airport code indicating the 数据中心 location:
— The 请求 was 路由d using Smart Placement to a 数据中心 near London.
— The 请求 was not 路由d using Smart Placement. The Worker ran in the 默认 location near 新功能ark.
:::注意
The  头部 may be removed before Smart Placement exits 测试版.
Placement Hints let you explicitly specify where your Worker runs. Use Placement Hints when:
You know the exact location of your back-end infrastructure
Your Worker connects to a single 数据库, API, or service
Your infrastructure is single-homed (not replicated or anycasted)
示例 include a primary 数据库, a virtual machine, or a Kubernetes cluster in a specific region. Reducing round-trip 延迟 from 20 to 30 milliseconds per 查询 to 1 to 3 milliseconds improves 响应 times.
:::注意

Workers run on Cloudflare's 全球网络, not inside cloud provider regions. Placement Hints run your Worker in the 数据中心 with the lowest 延迟 to your specified cloud region. At extremely high 请求 volumes (hundreds of thousands of 请求s per second or more), Cloudflare may run instances across a more distributed area to balance traffic.
If your infrastructure runs in AWS, GCP, or Azure, set the  property using the format :

Cloudflare maps your specified cloud region to the 数据中心 with the lowest 延迟 to that region. Cloudflare automatically adjusts placement to 账户 for network maintenance or changes, so you do not need to specify failover regions.

If your infrastructure is not in a major cloud provider, you can specify an 端点 for Cloudflare to probe. Cloudflare will triangulate the position of your external host and place Workers in a nearby region.
Host-based placement is 实验性.
Set  to identify a layer 4 service. Cloudflare uses TCP CONNECT checks to measure 延迟 and selects the best 数据中心.
Set  to identify a layer 7 service. Cloudflare uses HTTP HEAD checks to measure 延迟 and selects the best 数据中心.

Probes are sent from public IP ranges, not Cloudflare IP ranges. Cloudflare rechecks service location at regular intervals. These probes locate single-homed 资源 and do not work correctly for broadcast, anycast, multicast, or replicated 资源.
Placement Hints 支持 Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud 平台 (GCP), and Microsoft Azure region identifiers:
For a full 列表 of region codes, refer to AWS regions, GCP regions, or Azure regions.
Workers placement behaves in similar fashion when either Smart Placement or Placement Hints are used. The following behavior applies to both.
The following limitations apply to both Smart Placement and Placement Hints:
Placement only affects the 执行 of fetch 事件 处理程序s. It does not affect RPC 方法 or named entrypoints.
Workers without a fetch 事件 处理程序 are ignored by placement.
Cloudflare adds a  头部 to all 请求s when placement is 已启用. Use this 头部 to check whether a 请求 was 路由d with placement and where the Worker processed the 请求.

If you are 构建ing full-stack applications on Workers, split your edge logic (身份验证, 路由) and back-end logic (数据库 queries, API calls) into separate Workers. Use Service 绑定 to connect them with 类型-safe RPC.
Enable placement on your back-end Worker to invoke it close to your 数据库, while the edge Worker handles 身份验证 close to the user.
This 示例 shows two Workers:
— runs at the edge (no placement), handles 身份验证
— placed near your 数据库, handles data queries
The  runs at the edge to reject unauthorized 请求s quickly. Authenticated 请求s are forwarded via RPC to , which runs near your 数据库 for fast queries.
Durable Objects provide automatic placement without 配置. Queries to a Durable 对象's embedded SQLite 数据库 are effectively zero-延迟 because compute runs in the same process as the data.
Do as much work as possible within the Durable 对象 and return a composite result, rather than making mul提示le round-trips from your Worker:

/workers/.title: Cloudflare Workers
paragraphs: A 无服务器 平台 for 构建ing, 部署ing, and scaling apps across
Cloudflare's 全球网络 with a
single command — no infrastructure to manage, no complex 配置
With Cloudflare Workers, you can expect to:
Deliver fast 性能 with high reliability anywhere in the world
构建 full-stack apps with your framework of choice, including React, Vue, Svelte, Next, Astro, React 路由r, and more
Use your preferred language, including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Rust, and more
Gain deep visibility and insight with built-in 可观测性

/workers/languages/javascript/.title: JavaScript

headings: 1. 创建 a 新功能 project with Wrangler
Implements convenient 路由 API to serve mul提示le paths from one Worker. Refer to the  示例 in the  GitHub repository.
paragraphs: Cloudflare Workers provides 支持 for Rust via the  crate, which makes 运行时 APIs and 绑定 to developer 平台 products, such as Workers KV, R2, and Queues, available directly from your Rust code.
By following this 指南, you will learn how to 构建 a Worker entirely in the Rust programming language.
Before starting this 指南, make sure you have:
A recent version of [](https://rustup.rs/)
[](https://docs.npmjs.com/获取ting-started)
The Rust  toolchain:
And  sub-command by 运行中:
Open a terminal window, and run the following command to generate a Worker project template in Rust:
Your project will be 创建d in a 新功能 directory that you named, in which you will find the following files and folders:
- The standard project 配置 file for Rust's [](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/) package manager. The template pre-populates some best-practice 设置 for 构建ing for Wasm on Workers.
- Wrangler 配置, pre-populated with a custom 构建 command to invoke  (Refer to Wrangler Bundling).
- Rust source directory, pre-populated with Hello World Worker.

After you have 创建d your first Worker, run the [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#dev) command to start a local server for developing your Worker. This will allow you to 测试 your Worker in development.
If you have not used Wrangler before, it will try to open your web browser to login with your Cloudflare 账户.
:::注意
If you have issues with this 步骤 or you do not have access to a browser interface, refer to the [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#login) 文档 for more 信息rmation.
Go to http://localhost:8787 to review your Worker 运行中. Any changes you make to your code will 触发器 a re构建, and reloading the page will show you the up-to-date output of your Worker.
With your 新功能 project generated, write your Worker code. Find the entrypoint to your Worker in :
There is some counterintuitive behavior going on here:
provides an  macro which expects a 处理程序 函数 signature identical to those seen in JavaScript Workers.
is not generally 支持的 by Wasm, but you are able to use  in a  project (refer to [](/workers/languages/rust/#async-wasm-bindgen-futures)).
provides a 运行时 API which closely matches Worker's JavaScript API, and enables integration with Worker's 平台 特性. For detailed 文档 of the API, refer to [](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/).
This macro allows you to define entrypoints to your Worker. The  macro 支持s the following 事件:
- Invoked by an incoming HTTP 请求.
- Invoked by [](/workers/配置/cron-触发器s/).
- Invoked by incoming 消息 batches from Queues (Requires  特性 in , refer to the  GitHub repository and  特性 flag).
- Invoked when the Worker is first launched (such as, to 安装 panic hooks).
The  处理程序 provides three arguments which match the JavaScript API:
**[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.请求.html)**
An 对象 representing the incoming 请求. This includes 方法 for accessing 头部, method, path, Cloudflare properties, and 正文 (with 支持 for asynchronous 流ing and JSON deserialization with Serde).
**[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.Env.html)**
Provides access to Worker 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.密钥.html) - 密钥 值 configured in Cloudflare 控制面板 or using .
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/类型.Var.html) - 环境变量 defined in .
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/kv/struct.KvStore.html) - Workers KV 命名空间 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/durable/struct.对象命名空间.html) - Durable 对象 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.Fetcher.html) - Service 绑定 to another Worker.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.存储桶.html) - R2 存储桶 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/d1/struct.D1数据库.html) - D1 数据库 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.队列.html) - Queues 生产者 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.Ai.html) - Workers AI 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.Hyperdrive.html) - Hyperdrive 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.分析EngineDataset.html) - 分析 Engine 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.DynamicDispatcher.html) - Dynamic Dispatch 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.密钥tore.html) - 密钥 Store 绑定.
[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.RateLimiter.html) - 速率限制 绑定.
**[](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.Context.html)**
Provides access to [](/workers/运行时-apis/context/#waituntil) (deferred asynchronous 任务s) and [](/workers/运行时-apis/context/#passthroughonexception) (fail open) 函数ality.

The  处理程序 expects a [](https://docs.rs/Worker/la测试/Worker/struct.响应.html) return 类型, which includes 支持 for 流ing 响应s to the client asynchronously. This is also the return 类型 of any sub请求s made from your Worker. There are 方法 for accessing status code and 头部, as well as 流ing the 正文 asynchronously or deserializing from JSON using Serde.
Implements convenient 路由 API to serve mul提示le paths from one Worker. Refer to the  示例 in the  GitHub repository.

With your project configured, you can now 部署 your Worker, to a  子域名, or a 自定义域名, if you have one configured. If you have not configured any 子域名 or 域名, Wrangler will prompt you during the 部署 process to set one up.
Preview your Worker at .
When pushing to your  子域名 for the first time, you may see  错误 while DNS is propagating. These 错误 should resolve themselves after a minute or so.
After completing these 步骤s, you will have a basic Rust-based Worker 部署ed. From here, you can add crate

依赖 and write code in Rust to implement your Worker application. If you would like to know more about the inner workings of how Rust compiled to Wasm is 支持的 by Workers, the next section outlines the 库 and 工具 involved.
Wasm Workers are invoked from a JavaScript entrypoint script which is 创建d automatically for you when using .
To access 平台 特性 such as 绑定, Wasm Workers must be able to access 方法 from the JavaScript 运行时 API.

This interoperability is achieved using [](https://rustwasm.github.io/wasm-bindgen/), which provides the glue code needed to import 运行时 APIs to, and export 事件 处理程序s from, the Wasm module.  also provides [](https://docs.rs/js-sys/la测试/js_sys/), which implements 类型s for interacting with JavaScript 对象s. In practice, this is an implementation detail, as 's API handles conversion to and from JavaScript 对象s, and inter操作 with imported JavaScript 运行时 APIs for you.
If you are using  without  / , then you will need to patch the JavaScript that it emits. This is because when you import a  file in Workers, you 获取 a  instead of a  for 性能 and 安全 reasons.
To patch the JavaScript that  emits:
Run  as you normally would.
Patch the JavaScript file that it produces (the following code block assumes the file is called ):
In your Worker entrypoint, import the 函数 and use it directly:
[](https://rustwasm.github.io/wasm-bindgen/api/wasm_bindgen_futures/) (part of the  project) provides interoperability between Rust
Futures and JavaScript Promises.  invokes the entire 事件 处理程序 函数 using , meaning that you can program using async Rust, which is turned
into a single JavaScript Promise and run on the JavaScript 事件 loop. Calls to imported JavaScript 运行时 APIs are automatically converted to Rust Futures that can be invoked from async Rust 函数s.
To run the resulting Wasm binary on Workers,  includes a 构建 tool called [](https://github.com/cloudflare/workers-rs/tree/main/Worker-构建) which:
创建s a JavaScript entrypoint script that properly invokes the module using 's JavaScript API.
Invokes  to minify and bundle the JavaScript code.
Outputs a directory structure that Wrangler can use to bundle and 部署 the final Worker.
is invoked by 默认 in the template project using a custom 构建 command specified in the  file.
Unoptimized Rust Wasm binaries can be large and may exceed Worker bundle size 限制 or experience long startup times. The template project pre-configures several useful size 优化s in your  file:
Finally,  automatically invokes [](https://github.com/brson/wasm-opt-rs) to further optimize binary size before upload.

/workers/observability/metrics-and-analy.title: 指标 and 分析
/workers/observability/metrics-and-analy.description: Diagnose issues with Workers 指标, and review 请求 data for a
headings: Custom 分析 with 分析 Engine
paragraphs: There are two graphical sources of 信息rmation about your Workers traffic at a given time: Workers 指标 and 区域-based Workers 分析.

Workers 指标 can help you diagnose issues and understand your Workers' workloads by showing 性能 and usage of your Workers. If your Worker runs on a 路由 on a 区域, or on a few 区域s, Workers 指标 will show how much traffic your Worker is handling on a per-区域 basis, and how many 请求s your site is 获取ting.
区域 分析 show how much traffic all Workers assigned to a 区域 are handling.
Workers 指标 aggregate 请求 data for an individual Worker (if your Worker is 运行中 across mul提示le 域名s, and on , 指标 will aggregate 请求s across them). To view your Worker's 指标:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Workers & Pages** page.
In **概述**, select your Worker to view its 指标.
There are two 指标 that can help you understand the health of your Worker in a given moment: 请求s 成功 and 错误 指标, and 调用 statuses.
The first graph shows historical 请求 counts from the Workers 运行时 broken down into 成功ful 请求s, 错误ed 请求s, and sub请求s.
**Total**: All incoming 请求s registered by a Worker. 请求s blocked by WAF or other 安全 特性 will not count.
**成功**: 请求s that returned a 成功 or Client Disconnected 调用 status.
**错误**: 请求s that returned a Script Threw Exception, Exceeded 资源, or Internal 错误 调用 status — refer to 调用 Statuses for a breakdown of where your 错误 are coming from.

请求 traffic data may display a drop off near the last few minutes displayed in the graph for time ranges less than six hours. This does not reflect a drop in traffic, but a slight delay in aggregation and 指标 delivery.
Sub请求s are 请求s 触发器ed by calling  from within a Worker. A sub请求 that throws an uncaught 错误 will not be counted.
**Total**: All sub请求s 触发器ed by calling  from within a Worker.
**Cached**: The 数字 of cached 响应s returned.
**Uncached**: The 数字 of uncached 响应s returned.
Wall time  represents
the elapsed time in milliseconds between the start of a Worker 调用, and
when the Workers 运行时 determines that no more JavaScript needs to run.
Specifically, wall time per 执行 chart measures the wall time that the
JavaScript context remained open — including time spent waiting on I/O, and time
spent executing in your Worker's
[](/workers/运行时-apis/context/#waituntil) 处理程序. Wall time is
not the same as the time it takes your Worker to send the final byte of a
响应 back to the client - wall time can be higher, if 任务s within
are still 运行中 after the 响应 has been sent, or it can be
lower. For 示例, when returning a 响应 with a large 正文, the Workers
运行时 can, in some cases, determine that no more JavaScript needs to run, and
closes the JavaScript context before all the bytes have passed through and been
sent.
The Wall Time per 执行 chart shows historical wall time data broken down into relevant quantiles using reservoir sampling. 了解更多 about interpreting quantiles.

The CPU Time per 执行 chart shows historical CPU time data broken down into relevant quantiles using reservoir sampling. 了解更多 about interpreting quantiles. In some cases, higher quantiles may appear to exceed CPU time 限制 without generating 调用 错误 because of a mechanism in the Workers 运行时 that allows rollover CPU time for 请求s below the CPU limit.

The Duration per 请求 chart shows historical duration per Worker 调用. The data is broken down into relevant quantiles, similar to the CPU time chart. 了解更多 about interpreting quantiles. Understanding duration on your Worker is especially useful when you are intending to do a significant amount of computation on the Worker itself.
To review 调用 statuses:
Select your Worker.
Find the **Summary** graph in **指标**.
Select **错误**.

Worker 调用 statuses indicate whether a Worker executed 成功fully or 已失败 to generate a 响应 in the Workers 运行时. 调用 statuses differ from HTTP 状态码. In some cases, a Worker 调用 succeeds but does not generate a 成功ful HTTP status because of another 错误 encountered outside of the Workers 运行时. Some 调用 statuses result in a Workers 错误 code being returned to the client.
¹ The Exceeded 资源 status may appear when the Worker exceeds a 运行时 limit. The most common cause is excessive CPU time, but is also caused by a Worker exceeding startup time or free tier 限制.

² The Internal 错误 status may appear when the Workers 运行时 fails to process a 请求 due to an internal failure in our system. These 错误 are not caused by any issue with the Worker code nor any resource limit. While 请求s with Internal 错误 status are rare, some may appear during normal operation. These 请求s are not counted towards usage for billing purposes. If you notice an elevated rate of 请求s with Internal 错误 status, review www.cloudflarestatus.com.
To further investigate exceptions, use [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#tail).

The 请求 duration chart shows how long it took your Worker to respond to 请求s, including code 执行 and time spent waiting on I/O. The 请求 duration chart is currently only available when your Worker has Smart Placement 已启用.
In contrast to 执行 duration, which measures only the time a Worker is 活跃, 请求 duration measures from the time a 请求 comes into a 数据中心 until a 响应 is delivered.

The data shows the duration for 请求s with Smart Placement 已启用 compared to those with Smart Placement 已禁用 (by 默认, 1% of 请求s are 路由d with Smart Placement 已禁用). The chart shows a histogram with duration across the x-axis and the percentage of 请求s that fall into the corresponding duration on the y-axis.
Worker 指标 can be inspected for up to three months in the past in maximum increments of one week.
区域 分析 aggregate 请求 data for all Workers assigned to any 路由 defined for a 区域.
To review 区域 指标:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the Workers 分析 page for your 区域.
区域 data can be scoped by time range within the last 30 days. The 控制面板 includes charts and 信息rmation described below.
This chart shows sub请求s — 请求s 触发器ed by calling  from within a Worker — broken down by cache status.
**Uncached**: 请求s answered directly by your 源服务器 or other servers responding to sub请求s.
**Cached**: 请求s answered by Cloudflare’s cache. As Cloudflare caches more of your content, it accelerates content delivery and reduces load on your 源.
This chart shows historical 带宽 usage for all Workers on a 区域 broken down by cache status.
This chart shows historical 请求s for all Workers on a 区域 broken down by HTTP status code.

This chart shows historical data for all Workers on a 区域 broken down by 成功ful 请求s, 已失败 请求s, and sub请求s. These 请求 类型s are categorized by HTTP status code where -level 请求s are 成功ful and  to -level 请求s are 已失败.
Worker 指标 are powered by GraphQL. 了解更多 about 查询ing our data sets in the 查询ing Workers 指标 with GraphQL tutorial.
The 指标 described above provide insight into Worker 性能 and 运行时 behavior. For custom, application-specific 分析, use Workers 分析 Engine.
分析 Engine is useful for:
**Custom business 指标** - Track 事件 specific to your application, such as signups, purchases, or 特性 usage.
**Per-customer 分析** - 记录 data with high-cardinality dimensions like customer IDs or API 键s.
**Usage-based billing** - Count API calls, compute units, or other billable 事件 per customer.
**性能 tracking** - Measure 响应 times, cache hit rates, or 错误 rates with custom dimensions.
Writes to 分析 Engine are non-blocking and do not add 延迟 to your Worker. 查询 your data using SQL through the 分析 Engine SQL API or visualize it in Grafana.
Refer to the 分析 Engine 示例 to 快速开始.

/workers/languages/typescript/.title: TypeScript
headings: 2. Generate 运行时 类型s using Wrangler
3. Make sure your  includes the generated 类型s
4. Add @类型s/node if you are using [](/workers/运行时-apis/nodejs/) (可选)
5. 更新 your scripts and CI 管道s
paragraphs: TypeScript is a first-class language on Cloudflare Workers. All APIs provided in Workers are fully 类型d, and 类型 definitions are generated directly from Workerd, the open-source Workers 运行时.
We recommend you generate 类型s for your Worker by 运行中 [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#类型s). Cloudflare also publishes 类型 definitions to GitHub and npm ().
Generate 类型s that match your Worker's 配置
Cloudflare continuously improves Workerd, the open-source Workers 运行时.
Changes in Workerd can introduce JavaScript API changes, thus changing the respective TypeScript 类型s.
This means the correct 类型s for your Worker depend on:
Your Worker's 兼容性 date.
Your Worker's 兼容性 flags.
Your Worker's 绑定, which are defined in your Wrangler 配置 file.
Any module 规则 you have specified in your Wrangler 配置 file under .

For 示例, the 运行时 will only allow you to use the [](https://nodejs.org/api/async_context.html#class-asynclocalstorage) class if you have  in your Wrangler 配置 file. This should be reflected in the 类型 definitions.
To ensure that your 类型 definitions always match your Worker's 配置, you can dynamically generate 类型s by 运行中:
See the  command docs for more details.
:::注意
This will generate a  file and (by 默认) save it to . This will include  类型s based on your Worker 绑定 _and_ 运行时 类型s based on your Worker's 兼容性 date and flags.
You should then add that file to your 's  数组. If you have the  兼容性 flag, you should also 安装 .
You can commit your 类型s file to git if you wish.

/workers/observability/logs/.title: 日志
paragraphs: 日志 are an 重要 component of a developer's toolkit to troubleshoot and diagnose application issues and maintaining system health. The Cloudflare Developer 平台 offers many 工具 to help developers manage their application's 日志.
Automatically ingest, 过滤器, and analyze 日志 emitted from Cloudflare Workers in the Cloudflare 控制面板.
Access log 事件 in near real-time. Real-time 日志 provide immediate 反馈 and visibility into the health of your Cloudflare Worker.
Tail Workers allow developers to apply custom 过滤, sampling, and 转换ation logic to telemetry data.
Send Workers Trace 事件 日志 to a 支持的 destination. Workers Logpush includes metadata about 请求s and 响应s, unstructured  消息s and any uncaught exceptions.

/workers/platform/pricing/.title: 定价
/workers/platform/pricing/.description: Workers plans and 定价 信息rmation.
paragraphs: By 默认, users have access to the Workers 免费计划. The Workers 免费计划 includes limited usage of Workers, Pages 函数s, Workers KV and Hyperdrive. 阅读更多 about the 免费计划 限制.

The Workers Paid plan includes Workers, Pages 函数s, Workers KV, Hyperdrive, and Durable Objects usage for a minimum charge of $5 USD per month for an 账户. The plan includes increased initial usage allotments, with clear charges for usage that exceeds the base plan. There are no additional charges for data transfer (egress) or 吞吐量 (带宽).
All included usage is on a monthly basis.
:::注意[Pages 函数s billing]
All Pages 函数s are billed as Workers. All 定价 and inclusions in this document apply to Pages 函数s. Refer to 函数s 定价 for more 信息rmation on Pages 函数s 定价.

Users on the Workers Paid plan have access to the Standard usage model. Workers Enterprise 账户s are billed based on the usage model specified in their contract. To switch to the Standard usage model, contact your 账户 Manager.
1  Inbound 请求s to your Worker. Cloudflare does not bill for
sub请求s you make from your Worker.
2  WebSocket connections made to a Worker are charged as a 请求,
representing the initial  connection made to establish the WebSocket.
WebSocket 消息s 路由d through a Worker do not count as 请求s.
3  请求s to static assets are free and unlimited.
A Worker that serves 15 million 请求s per month, and uses an average of 7 milliseconds (ms) of CPU time per 请求, would have the following estimated costs:

A project that serves 15 million 请求s per month, with 80% (12 million) 请求s serving static assets and the remaining invoking dynamic Worker code. The Worker uses an average of 7 milliseconds (ms) of time per 请求.
请求s to static assets are free and unlimited. This project would have the following estimated costs:
A Worker that runs on a Cron 触发器 once an hour to collect data from mul提示le APIs, process the data and 创建 a report.
720 请求s/month
3 minutes (180,000ms) of CPU time per 请求
In this scenario, the estimated monthly cost would be calculated as:
A high traffic Worker that serves 100 million 请求s per month, and uses an average of 7 milliseconds (ms) of CPU time per 请求, would have the following estimated costs:
:::注意[Custom 限制]

To pr事件 accidental runaway bills or denial-of-wallet attacks, configure the maximum amount of CPU time that can be used per 调用 by defining 限制 in your Worker's Wrangler file, or via the Cloudflare 控制面板 (Workers & Pages > Select your Worker > 设置 > CPU 限制).
If you had a Worker on the Bundled usage model prior to the 迁移 to Standard 定价 on March 1, 2024, Cloudflare has automatically added a 50 ms CPU limit on your Worker.
:::注意
Some Workers Enterprise customers maintain the ability to change usage models.
Users on the Workers Paid plan have access to the Standard usage model. However, some users may still have a legacy usage model configured.
Legacy usage models include Workers Unbound and Workers Bundled. Users are advised to move to the Workers Standard usage model.
Changing the usage model only affects billable usage, and has no technical implications.
To change your 默认 账户-wide usage model:
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Workers & Pages** page.
Find **Usage Model** on the right-side menu > **Change**.
Usage models may be changed at the individual Worker level:
In **概述**, select your Worker > **设置** > **Usage Model**.
Existing Workers will not be impacted when changing the 默认 usage model. You may change the usage model for individual Workers without affecting your 账户-wide 默认 usage model.
:::注意[Workers 日志 文档]
For more 信息rmation and 示例 of Workers 日志 billing, refer to the Workers 日志 文档.
Workers Logpush is only available on the Workers Paid plan.
1  Workers Logpush charges for 请求 日志 that reach your end
destination after applying 过滤 or sampling.
:::注意[KV 文档]
To 了解更多 about KV, refer to the KV 文档.
:::注意[Hyperdrive 文档]
To 了解更多 about Hyperdrive, refer to the Hyperdrive 文档.
:::注意[Queues billing 示例]
To 了解更多 about Queues 定价 and review billing 示例, refer to Queues 定价.
D1 is available on both the Workers Free and Workers 付费计划.
:::注意[D1 billing]
Refer to D1 定价 to 了解更多 about how D1 is billed.
:::注意[Durable Objects billing 示例]
For more 信息rmation and 示例 of Durable Objects billing, refer to Durable Objects 定价.
Vectorize is currently only available on the Workers paid plan.
R2 charges based on the total volume of data stored, along with two classes of operations on that data:
**Class A operations** which are more expensive and tend to mutate state.
**Class B operations** which tend to 读取 existing state.
There are no charges for egress 带宽.
:::注意[R2 文档]
To 了解更多 about R2 定价, including billing 示例, refer to R2 定价.
Containers are billed for every 10ms that they are 活跃ly 运行中 at the following rates, with included monthly usage as part of the $5 USD per month Workers Paid plan:

You only pay for what you use — charges start when a 请求 is sent to the container or when it is manually started. Charges stop after the container instance goes to sleep, which can happen automatically after a 超时.
Egress from Containers is priced at the following rates:
:::注意[Containers 文档]
To 了解更多 about Containers 定价, refer to Containers 定价.
请求s made from your Worker to another Worker via a Service 绑定 do not incur additional 请求 fees. This allows you to split apart 函数ality into mul提示le Workers, without incurring additional costs.
For 示例, if Worker A makes a sub请求 to Worker B via a Service 绑定, or calls an RPC method provided by Worker B via a Service 绑定, this is billed as:
One 请求 (for the initial 调用 of Worker A)
The total amount of CPU time used across both Worker A and Worker B
:::注意[Only available on Workers Standard 定价]

If your Worker is on the 已弃用 Bundled or Unbound 定价 plans, incoming 请求s from Service 绑定 are charged the same as 请求s from the Internet. In the 示例 above, you would be charged for two 请求s, one to Worker A, and one to Worker B.
Workers Paid plan is separate from any other Cloudflare plan (Free, Professional, Business) you may have. If you are an Enterprise customer, reach out to your 账户 team to confirm 定价 details.

Only 请求s that hit a Worker will count against your 限制 and your bill. Since Cloudflare Workers runs before the Cloudflare cache, the 缓存 of a 请求 still incurs costs. Refer to 限制 to review definitions and behavior after a limit is hit.

/workers/runtime-apis/bindings/.title: 绑定 (env)
/workers/runtime-apis/bindings/.description: Worker 绑定 that allow for inter操作 with other Cloudflare 资源.
paragraphs: 绑定 allow your Worker to interact with 资源 on the Cloudflare Developer 平台. 绑定 provide better 性能 and less restrictions when accessing 资源 from Workers than the REST APIs which are intended for non-Workers applications.
The following 绑定 are available today:
When you declare a 绑定 on your Worker, you grant it a specific capability, such as being able to 读取 and write files to an R2 存储桶. For 示例:

You can think of a 绑定 as a permission and an API in one piece. With 绑定, you never have to add 密钥 键s or 令牌s to your Worker in order to access 资源 on your Cloudflare 账户 — the permission is embedded within the API itself. The underlying 密钥 is never exposed to your Worker's code, and therefore can't be accidentally leaked.

When you 部署 a change to your Worker, and only change its 绑定 (i.e. you don't change the Worker's code), Cloudflare may reuse existing isolates that are al读取y 运行中 your Worker. This improves 性能 — you can change an 环境变量 or other 绑定 without unnecessarily reloading your code.

As a result, you must be careful when "polluting" global scope with derivatives of your 绑定. Anything you 创建 there might continue to exist despite making changes to any underlying 绑定. Consider an external client instance which uses a 密钥 API 键 accessed from : if you put this client instance in global scope and then make changes to the 密钥, a client instance using the 源al 值 might continue to exist. The correct approach would be to 创建 a 新功能 client instance for each 请求.
The following is a good approach:
Compared to this alternative, which might have surprising and unwanted behavior:
If you have more advanced needs, explore the AsyncLocalStorage API, which provides a mechanism for exposing 值s down to child 执行 处理程序s.
绑定 are located on the  对象, which can be accessed in several ways:
It is an argument to entrypoint 处理程序s such as [](/workers/运行时-apis/fetch/):
It is as class property on WorkerEntrypoint,
Durable对象, and 工作流:
It can be imported from :
Importing  from  is useful when you need to access a 绑定
such as 密钥 or 环境变量
in top-level global scope. For 示例, to initialize an API client:
Workers do not allow I/O from outside a 请求 context. This means that even
though  is accessible from the top-level scope, you will not be able to access
every 绑定's 方法.
For instance, 环境变量 and 密钥 are accessible, and you are able to
call  to 获取 a Durable 对象 stub in the
top-level context. However, calling 方法 on the Durable 对象 stub, making calls to a KV store,
and calling to other Workers will not work.
Additionally, importing  from  lets you avoid passing
as an argument through many 函数 calls if you need to access a 绑定 from a deeply-nested
函数. This can be helpful in a complex codebase.
:::注意
While using  from  may be simpler to write than passing it
through a series of 函数 calls, passing  as an argument is a helpful pattern
for dependency injection and 测试.
The  函数 provides a mechanism for overriding 值s of .
Imagine a user has defined the 环境变量
"NAME" to be "Alice" in their Wrangler 配置 file and 部署ed a Worker. By 默认, logging
would print "Alice". Using the  函数, you can override the 值 of
"NAME".
This can be useful when 测试 code that relies on an imported  对象.

/workers/runtime-apis/webassembly/.title: WebAssembly (Wasm)
/workers/runtime-apis/webassembly/.description: Execute code written in a language other than JavaScript or write
paragraphs: WebAssembly (abbreviated Wasm) allows you to compile languages like Rust, Go, or C to a binary format that can run in a wide variety of environments, including web browsers, Cloudflare Workers, and other WebAssembly 运行时s.
You can use WebAssembly to:
Execute code written in a language other than JavaScript, via .
:::注意
only 支持s pre-compiled 模块 as documented in the web-standards 文档.
Write an entire Cloudflare Worker in Rust, using 绑定 that make Workers' JavaScript APIs available directly from your Rust code.
Most programming languages can be compiled to Wasm, although 支持 varies across languages and compilers. 指南 are available for the following languages:
WebAssembly is a rapidly evolving set of standards, with many proposed APIs which are in various stages of development. In general, Workers 支持s the same set of 特性 that are available in Google Chrome.
SIMD is 支持的 on Workers. For more 信息rmation on using SIMD in WebAssembly, refer to Fast, parallel applications with WebAssembly SIMD.
Th读取ing is not possible in Workers. Each Worker runs in a single th读取, and the Web Worker API is not 支持的.

Compiling to WebAssembly often requires including additional 运行时 依赖. As a result, Workers that use WebAssembly are typically larger than an equivalent Worker written in JavaScript. The larger your Worker is, the longer it may take your Worker to start. Refer to Worker startup time for more 信息rmation. We recommend using 工具 like [](https://github.com/brson/wasm-opt-rs) to optimize the size of your Wasm binary.

The WebAssembly System Interface (abbreviated WASI) is a modular system interface for WebAssembly that standardizes a set of underlying system calls for networking, file system access, and more. Applications can depend on the WebAssembly System Interface to behave identically across host environments and operating systems.

WASI is an earlier and more rapidly evolving set of standards than Wasm. WASI 支持 is 实验性 on Cloudflare Workers, with only some syscalls implemented. Refer to our open source implementation of WASI, and blog post about WASI on Workers demonstrating its use.

WebAssembly on Cloudflare Workers

/workers/static-assets/.description: 创建 full-stack applications 部署ed to Cloudflare Workers.

You can upload static assets (HTML, CSS, images and other files) as part of your Worker, and Cloudflare will handle 缓存 and serving them to web browsers.
Start from CLI - Scaffold a React SPA with an API Worker, and use the Cloudflare Vite plugin.
Or just 部署 to Cloudflare
![部署 to Workers](https://dash.cloudflare.com/?to=/:账户/workers-and-pages/创建/部署-to-workers&repository=https://github.com/cloudflare/templates/tree/main/vite-react-template)
了解更多 about 支持的 框架 on Workers.

When you 部署 your project, Cloudflare 部署s both your Worker code and your static assets in a single operation. This 部署 operates as a tightly integrated "unit" 运行中 across Cloudflare's network, combining static file hosting, custom logic, and global 缓存.

The assets directory specified in your Wrangler 配置 file is central to this design. During 部署, Wrangler automatically uploads the files from this directory to Cloudflare's infrastructure. Once 部署ed, 请求s for these assets are 路由d efficiently to locations closest to your users.
:::注意
If you are using the Cloudflare Vite plugin, you do not need to specify . For more 信息rmation about using static assets with the Vite plugin, refer to the plugin 文档.
By adding an assets 绑定, you can directly fetch and serve assets within your Worker code.

By 默认, if a 请求ed URL matches a file in the static assets directory, that file will be served — without invoking Worker code. If no matching asset is found and a Worker script is present, the 请求 will be processed by the Worker. The Worker can return a 响应 or choose to defer again to static assets by using the assets 绑定 (e.g. ). If no Worker script is present, a  响应 is returned.
The 默认 behavior for 请求s which don't match a static asset can be changed by setting the  option under  in your Wrangler 配置 file:

[](/workers/static-assets/路由/single-page-application/): Sets your application to return a  响应 with  for 请求s which don't match a static asset. Use this if you have a Single Page Application. We recommend 对ing this with selective 路由 using  for advanced 路由 control.
[](/workers/static-assets/路由/static-site-generation/#custom-404-pages): Sets your application to return a  响应 with the nearest  for 请求s which don't match a static asset.

If you want the Worker code to execute before serving assets, you can use the  option. This can be set to  to invoke the Worker script for all 请求s, or configured as an 数组 of 路由 patterns for selective Worker-script-first 路由:
Invoking your Worker script on specific paths:
For a more advanced pattern, refer to SPA shell with bootstrap data, which uses HTML重写r to inject prefetched API data into the HTML stream.
Cloudflare provides automatic 缓存 for static assets across its network, ensuring fast delivery to users worldwide. When a static asset is 请求ed, it is automatically cached for future 请求s.
**First 请求:** When an asset is 请求ed for the first time, it is fetched from storage and cached at the nearest Cloudflare location.

**Subsequent 请求s:** If a 请求 for the same asset reaches a 数据中心 that does not have it cached, Cloudflare's tiered 缓存 system allows it to be retrieved from a nearby cache rather than going back to storage. This improves cache hit ratio, reduces 延迟, and reduces unnecessary 源 fetches.

/workflows/build/trigger-workflows/.title: 触发器 工作流s
headings: 触发器 a 工作流 from another 工作流
paragraphs: You can 触发器 工作流s both programmatically and via the 工作流s APIs, including:
With Workers via HTTP 请求s in a  处理程序, or 绑定 from a  or  处理程序
Using the 工作流s REST API
Via the wrangler CLI in your terminal

You can interact with 工作流s programmatically from any Worker script by creating a 绑定 to a 工作流. A Worker can bind to mul提示le 工作流s, including 工作流s defined in other Workers projects (scripts) within your 账户.
You can interact with a 工作流:
Directly over HTTP via the [](/workers/运行时-apis/处理程序s/fetch/) 处理程序
From a 队列 消费者 inside a  处理程序
From a Cron 触发器 inside a  处理程序
Within a Durable 对象
:::注意
新功能 to 工作流s? Start with the 工作流s tutorial to 部署 your first 工作流 and familiarize yourself with 工作流s concepts.
To bind to a 工作流 from your Workers code, you need to define a 绑定 to a specific 工作流. For 示例, to bind to the 工作流 defined in the 快速开始 指南, you would configure the Wrangler 配置 file with the below:
The  line defines the JavaScript 变量 that our 工作流 方法 are accessible on, including  (which 触发器s a 新功能 instance) or  (which returns the status of an existing instance).
The following 示例 shows how you can manage 工作流s from within a Worker, including:
Retrieving the status of an existing 工作流 instance by its ID
Creating (触发器ing) a 新功能 工作流 instance
Returning the status of a given instance ID

You can inspect the status of any 运行中 工作流 instance by calling  against a specific instance ID. This allows you to programmatically inspect whether an instance is 队列d (waiting to be 定时), 活跃ly 运行中, paused, or 错误ed.
The possible 值s of status are as follows:
You can explicitly pause a 工作流 instance (and later resume it) by calling  against a specific instance ID.
You can resume a paused 工作流 instance by calling  against a specific instance ID.
Calling  on an instance that is not currently paused will have no effect.
:::注意
If you have reached the maximum concurrent instances for your 工作流, resuming an instance may not restart it immediately. The instance will be 队列d until a 并发 slot becomes available.
You can stop/terminate a 工作流 instance by calling  against a specific instance ID.
Once 已停止/terminated, the 工作流 instance _cannot_ be resumed.
Restarting an instance will immediately cancel any in-progress 步骤s, erase any intermediate state, and treat the 工作流 as if it was run for the first time.

You can 创建 a 新功能 工作流 instance from within a 步骤 of another 工作流. The parent 工作流 will not block waiting for the child 工作流 to complete — it continues 执行 immediately after the child instance is 成功fully 创建d.
If the child 工作流 fails to start, the 步骤 will fail and be retried according to your 重试 配置. Once the child instance is 成功fully 创建d, it runs independently from the parent.
Refer to the 工作流s REST API 文档.
Refer to the CLI 快速开始 to 了解更多 about how to manage and 触发器 工作流s via the command-line.

When a 工作流 is 触发器ed, it can receive an 可选 事件. This 事件 can include data that your 工作流 can act on, including 请求 details, user data fetched from your 数据库 (such as D1 or KV) or from a webhook, or 消息s from a 队列 消费者.

事件 are a powerful part of a 工作流, as you often want a 工作流 to act on data. Because a given 工作流 instance executes durably, 事件 are a useful way to provide a 工作流 with data that should be immu表 (not changing) and/or represents data the 工作流 needs to operate on at that point in time.
You can pass 参数 to a 工作流 in three ways:
As an 可选 argument to the  method on a 工作流 绑定 when 触发器ing a 工作流 from a Worker.
Via the  flag when using the  CLI to 触发器 a 工作流.

Via the  API, which allows a 工作流 instance to wait for an 事件 (and 可选 data) to be received _while it is 运行中_. 工作流 instances can be sent 事件 from external services over HTTP or via the Workers API for 工作流s.
You can pass any JSON-serializable 对象 as a parameter.
:::注意

A  and its associated  property are effectively _immu表_: any changes to an 事件 are not persisted across the 步骤s of a 工作流. This includes both cases when a 工作流 is progressing normally, and in cases where a 工作流 has to be restarted due to a failure.
Store state durably by returning it from your  callbacks.

工作流s allow you to 构建 durable, multi-步骤 applications using the Workers 平台. A 工作流 can automatically 重试, persist state, run for hours or days, and coordinate between third-party APIs.
You can 构建 工作流s to post-process file uploads to R2 对象存储, automate generation of Workers AI embeddings into a Vectorize vector 数据库, or to 触发器 user lifecycle emails using Email Service.
:::注意
The term "Durable 执行" is widely used to describe this programming model.
"Durable" describes the ability of the program to implicitly persist state without you having to manually write to an external store or serialize program state.
In this 指南, you will 创建 and 部署 a 工作流 that fetches data, pauses, and processes results.
If you want to skip the 步骤s and pull down the complete 工作流 we are 构建ing in this 指南, run:
Use this option if you are familiar with Cloudflare Workers or want to explore the code first and learn the details later.
Follow the 步骤s below to learn how to 构建 a 工作流 from scratch.

The [](/工作流s/构建/workers-api/#步骤) 对象 is the core of the 工作流s API. It provides 方法 to define durable 步骤s in your 工作流:

- Executes code and persists the result. If the 工作流 is interrupted or retried, it resumes from the last 成功ful 步骤 rather than re-运行中 completed work. The callback returns serializable data, including  for large binary output in JavaScript 工作流s.
- Pauses the 工作流 for a duration (for 示例, , ).
If you return a stream, return a fresh, unlocked . BYOB 流s and BYOB 读取ers are not 支持的.
You can pass a 重试 配置 to  to customize how failures are handled. See the full 步骤 API for stream 要求, 限制, and additional 方法 like  and .

When deciding whether to break code into separate 步骤s, ask yourself: "Do I want all of this code to run again if just one part fails?" Separate 步骤s are ideal for operations like calling external APIs, 查询ing 数据库s, or 读取ing files from storage - if a later 步骤 fails, your 工作流 can 重试 from that point using data al读取y fetched, avoiding redundant API calls or 数据库 queries.
For more guidance on how to define your 工作流 logic, refer to 规则 of 工作流s.

/workflows/build/sleeping-and-retrying/.title: Sleeping and 重试ing
headings: Force a 工作流 instance to fail
paragraphs: This 指南 details how to sleep a 工作流 and/or configure 重试 for a 工作流 步骤.
You can set a 工作流 to sleep as an explicit 步骤, which can be useful when you want a 工作流 to wait, schedule work ahead, or pause until an input or other external state is 读取y.
:::注意
A 工作流 instance that is resuming from sleep will take priority over 新功能ly 定时 (队列d) instances. This helps ensure that older 工作流 instances can run to completion and are not blocked by 新功能er instances.
Use  to have a 工作流 sleep for a relative period of time:
The second argument to  accepts both  (milliseconds) or a human-读取able format, such as "1 minute" or "26 hours". The accepted units for  when used this way are as follows:
Use  to have a 工作流 sleep to a specific : this can be useful when you have a timestamp from another system or want to "schedule" work to occur at a specific time (e.g. Sunday, 9AM UTC).
You can also provide a UNIX timestamp (milliseconds since the UNIX epoch) directly to .
Each call to  in a 工作流 accepts an 可选 , which allows you define the 重试 behaviour for that 步骤.
If you do not provide your own 重试 配置, 工作流s applies the following 默认s:
When providing your own , you can configure:
The total 数字 of attempts to make for a 步骤 (accepts  for unlimited 重试)
The delay between attempts (accepts both  (ms) or a human-读取able format)
What backoff algorithm to apply between each attempt: any of , , or
When to 超时 (in duration) before considering the 步骤 as 已失败 (including during a 重试 attempt, as the 超时 is set per attempt)
For 示例, to limit a 步骤 to 10 重试 and have it apply an exponential delay (starting at 10 seconds) between each attempt, you would pass the following 配置 as an 可选 对象 to :
工作流 步骤 callbacks receive a context 对象 containing the current attempt 数字 (1-索引ed). This allows you to access which 重试 attempt is currently executing.
You can also force a 工作流 instance to fail and _not_ 重试 by throwing a  from within the 步骤.
This can be useful when you detect a terminal (permanent) 错误 from an 上游 system (such as an 身份验证 failure) or other 错误 where 重试ing would not help.
The 工作流 instance itself will fail immediately, no further 步骤s will be invoked, and the 工作流 will not be retried.
Any uncaught exceptions that propagate to the top level, or any 步骤s that reach their 重试 limit, will cause the 工作流 to end 执行 in an  state.
If you want to avoid this, you can catch exceptions emitted by a . This can be useful if you need to 触发器 clean-up 任务s or have 条件al logic that 触发器s additional 步骤s.
To allow the 工作流 to continue its 执行, surround the intended 步骤s that are allowed to fail with a  block.

工作流s exposes an API directly to your Workers scripts via the 绑定 concept. 绑定 allow you to securely call a 工作流 without having to manage API 键s or clients.
You can bind to a 工作流 by defining a  绑定 within your Wrangler 配置.
For 示例, to bind to a 工作流 called  and to make it available on the  变量 to your Worker script, you would configure the following 字段s within the  绑定 definition:

Visit the 文档 on calling 工作流s from Pages for 示例.

You can also bind to a 工作流 that is defined in a different Worker script from the script your 工作流 definition is in. To do this, provide the  键 with the name of the script to the  绑定 definition in your Wrangler 配置.

/workflows/.title: Cloudflare 工作流s
paragraphs: 构建 durable multi-步骤 applications on Cloudflare Workers with 工作流s.
With 工作流s, you can 构建 applications that chain to获取her mul提示le 步骤s, automatically 重试 已失败 任务s,
and persist state for minutes, hours, or even weeks - with no infrastructure to manage.
Use 工作流s to 构建 reliable AI applications, process data 管道s, manage user lifecycle with automated emails and trial expirations, and implement human-in-the-loop approval systems.
工作流s give you:
Durable multi-步骤 执行 without 超时s
The ability to pause for external 事件 or approvals
Automatic 重试 and 错误处理
Built-in 可观测性 and 调试
An image processing 工作流 that fetches from R2, generates an AI description, waits for approval, then publishes:
快速开始
Browse the 示例
Break complex operations into durable 步骤s with automatic 重试 and 错误处理.
Pause 工作流s for seconds, hours, or days with  and .
Wait for Webhook, user input, or external system 响应s before continuing 执行.
触发器, pause, resume, and terminate 工作流 instances programmatically or via API.
构建 无服务器 applications and 部署 instantly across the globe for exceptional 性能, reliability, and scale.
部署 dynamic front-end applications in 记录 time.

/workflows/reference/pricing/.title: 定价
headings: Are there additional costs for 工作流s?
Are 工作流s available on the Workers 免费计划?
What is a 工作流 调用?
How do 工作流s show up on my bill?
Are there any 限制 to 工作流s?
paragraphs: :::注意
工作流s is included in both the Free and Paid Workers plans.
工作流s 定价 is identical to Workers Standard 定价 and are billed on three dimensions:
**CPU time**: the total amount of compute (measured in milliseconds) consumed by a given 工作流.
**请求s** (调用s): the 数字 of 工作流 调用s. Sub请求s made from a 工作流 do not incur additional 请求 costs.
**Storage**: the total amount of storage (measured in GB) persisted by your 工作流s.
A 工作流 that is waiting on a 响应 to an API call, paused as a result of calling , or otherwise idle, does not incur CPU time.
:::注意[CPU 限制]
You can increase the CPU limit available to your 工作流 instances up to 5 minutes per 工作流 by setting the  property in your Wrangler 配置.
Storage billing for 工作流s will go live on September 15th, 2025.

Storage is billed using gigabyte-month (GB-month) as the billing metric, identical to Durable Objects SQL storage. A GB-month is calculated by averaging the peak storage per day over a billing period (30 days).
Storage is calculated across all instances, and includes 运行中, 错误ed, sleeping and completed instances.
By 默认, instance state is retained for 3 days on the 免费计划 and 7 days on the Paid plan.
When creating a 工作流 instance, you can set a shorter state retention period if you do not need to retain state for 错误ed or completed 工作流s.
Deleting instances via the Workers API, Wrangler CLI, REST API, or 控制面板 will free up storage. 注意 that it may take a few minutes for storage 限制 to 更新.
An instance that attempts to store state when your have reached the storage limit on the 免费计划 will cause an 错误 to be thrown.
Frequently asked questions 相关 to 工作流s 定价:
No. 工作流s are priced based on the same compute (CPU time), 请求s (调用s) as Workers, as well as storage (state from a 工作流).
Yes.
A 工作流 调用 is when you 触发器 a 新功能 工作流 instance: for 示例, via the Workers API, wrangler CLI, or REST API. 步骤s within a 工作流 are not 调用s.
工作流s are billed as Workers, and share the same CPU time and 请求 SKUs.
Refer to the published 限制 文档.

/workflows/reference/limits/.title: 限制
headings: instances do not count towards instance 并发 限制
Increasing 工作流 步骤 限制
Increasing 工作流 CPU 限制
Increasing 工作流 sub请求 限制
paragraphs: 限制 that apply to authoring, 部署ing, and 运行中 工作流s are detailed below.
Many 限制 are inherited from those applied to Workers scripts and as documented in the Workers 限制 文档.
:::注意
工作流s cannot be 部署ed to Workers for 平台s 命名空间s, as 工作流s do not 支持 Workers for 平台s.
[^2]: 工作流 instance state and 日志 will be retained for 3 days on the Workers 免费计划 and for 30 days on the Workers Paid plan.
[^3]: A 工作流 instance can run forever, as long as each 步骤 does not take more than the CPU time limit and the maximum 数字 of 步骤s per 工作流 is not reached.
[^4]: Match pattern: _\_
[^5]:  does not count towards the maximum 步骤s limit
[^6]: 工作流s will return a HTTP 429 rate limited 错误 if you exceed the rate of 新功能 工作流 instance creation.
[^7]: Only instances with a  state count towards the 并发 限制. Instances in the  state are excluded from these 限制.
[^8]: Each instance 创建d or restarted counts towards this limit
[^9]: Applies to non-stream  return 值s. In JavaScript 工作流s,  is also a 支持的 serializable return 类型 for larger binary output.
[^10]: This total includes persisted bytes from 流ed 步骤 outputs returned from JavaScript  calls.

In JavaScript 工作流s, if you need to persist large binary output from a 步骤, return a . 流ed outputs still count toward the per-instance storage limit, so store very large or long-lived artifacts in external storage such as R2 and return a 参考 when appropriate.

Instances that are in a  state — either sleeping via , waiting for a 重试, or waiting for an 事件 via  — do not count towards 并发 限制. This means you can have millions of 工作流 instances sleeping or waiting for 事件 simultaneously, as only 活跃ly  instances count toward the 10,000 concurrent instance limit.
However, if there are 10,000 concurrent instances 活跃ly 运行中, an instance that has been in a  state will be 队列d instead of resuming immediately.
When an instance transitions from  to , other  instances will be 定时 (usually the oldest 队列d instance, on a best-effort basis). This state transition may not occur if the wait duration is very short.
For 示例, consider a 工作流 that does some work, waits for 30 days, and then continues with more work:
While a given 工作流 instance is waiting for 30 days, it will transition to the  state, allowing other  instances to run if 并发 限制 are reached.
Each 工作流 instance 支持s 10,000 步骤s by 默认, but this can be increased up to 25,000 步骤s in your Wrangler 配置. Refer to 工作流 步骤 限制 for more 信息rmation.

工作流s are Worker scripts, and share the same per 调用 CPU 限制 as any Workers do. 注意 that CPU time is 活跃 processing time: not time spent waiting on network 请求s, storage calls, or other general I/O, which don't count towards your CPU time or 工作流s compute consumption.
If your 工作流 exceeds its CPU time limit, it will throw the following 错误:
This will appear as  in [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#tail) outcomes and as  in Workers 指标.
By 默认, the maximum CPU time per 工作流 调用 is set to 30 seconds, but can be increased for all 调用s associated with a 工作流 definition by setting  in your Wrangler 配置:
To 了解更多 about CPU time and 限制, review the Workers 文档.

A sub请求 is any 请求 that a 工作流 makes to either Internet 资源 using the Fetch API or 请求s to other Cloudflare services like R2, KV, or D1. Because 工作流s are long-运行中 and often make many calls to external services or Cloudflare APIs, they can exceed the 默认 sub请求 limit.
If your 工作流 exceeds its sub请求 limit, it will throw the following 错误:
This will appear as  in Workers 指标 and as  in [](/workers/wrangler/commands/general/#tail) outcomes.
By 默认, the maximum 数字 of sub请求s per 工作流 instance is 10,000 on Workers 付费计划, but this can be increased up to 10 million by setting  in your Wrangler 配置:
Workers on the 免费计划 remain limited to 50 external sub请求s and 1,000 sub请求s to Cloudflare services per 调用.
To 了解更多 about sub请求 限制, review the Workers 文档.

/zaraz/advanced/load-custom-managed-comp.title: Custom Managed Components
headings: 部署 a Managed Component to Cloudflare
Configure a Managed Component in Cloudflare
paragraphs: Zaraz 支持s loading custom third-party 工具 using Managed Components. These can be Managed Components that you have developed yourself or that were developed by others. Using Custom Managed Components with Zaraz is done by converting them into a Cloudflare Worker 运行中 in your 账户.
If you are 新功能 to Managed Components, we recommend you 快速开始 with creating your own Managed Component or check out our demo Managed Component.
:::注意

If your Managed Component requires any 构建ing, transpiling, or bundling, you must complete those 步骤s before you can 部署 it. For 示例, this is 必需 for components written in TypeScript and is usually done by 运行中  or an equivalent.
To 快速开始, you need have a JavaScript file 读取y for 部署, that exports the 默认 Managed Component 函数 for your Managed Component.
In this 指南, we will use a simple 示例 of a Custom Managed Component that counts user visits and 日志 this data in the console:
Open a terminal in your Managed Component’s root directory.
From there, run , which will 部署 the Managed Component to a specialized Cloudflare Worker. Change the path to your . You can also rename the Component.
Your Managed Component should now be visible on your 账户 as a Cloudflare Worker prefixed with .
As with regular 工具, it is recommended that you 创建 the 触发器s you need first, if the Custom Managed Component you are adding needs to start 操作s using firing 触发器s different from the 默认  触发器.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Tag 设置** page.
Select **工具 配置** > **Third-party 工具**.
Select **Add 新功能 tool** and choose **Custom Managed Component** from the 工具 library page. Select **Continue** to confirm your selection.
In **Select Custom MC**, choose a Custom Managed Component that you have 部署ed to your 账户, such as . Select **Continue**.
In **权限**, select the 权限 you want to grant the Custom Managed Component. If you run an untrusted Managed Component, pay close attention to what 权限 you are granting. Select **Continue**.
In **设置**, configure the 设置 for your 新功能 tool. The 信息rmation you need to enter will depend on the code of the Managed Component. You can add 设置 and 默认 字段s, as well as use 变量s you have previously 设置.
Select **Save**.

While your tool is now configured, it does not have any 操作s associated with it yet. Adding 新功能 操作s will tell Zaraz when to contact your Managed Component, and what 信息rmation to send to it. When adding 操作s, make sure to verify the 操作 类型 you are using. The 类型s  and  are most commonly used, but you can add any 操作 类型 to match the 事件 列表eners your Managed Component is using. Learn how to 创建 additional 操作s.
If your Managed Component 列表ens to  事件, toggle E-commerce tracking in the Managed Component 设置 page.
As of now, Custom Managed Components do not 支持 the use of the following 方法 yet:

/zaraz/get-started/.title: 快速开始
headings: Add a third-party tool to your website
事件, 触发器s and 操作s
paragraphs: Before being able to use Zaraz, it is recommended that you 代理 your website through Cloudflare. Refer to 设置 Cloudflare for more 信息rmation. If you do not want to 代理 your website through Cloudflare, refer to Use Zaraz on 域名s not proxied by Cloudflare.
You can add 新功能 third-party 工具 and load them into your website through the Cloudflare 控制面板.
In the Cloudflare 控制面板, go to the **Tag 设置** page.
If you have al读取y added a tool before, select **Third-party 工具** and click on **Add 新功能 tool**.
Choose a tool from the 工具 catalog. Select **Continue** to confirm your selection.

In **设置**, configure the 设置 for your 新功能 tool. The 信息rmation you need to enter will depend on the tool you choose. If you want to use any dynamic properties or 变量s, select the  登录 the drop-down menu next to the relevant 字段.
In **操作s** 设置 the 操作s for your 新功能 tool. You should be able to select Pageviews, 事件 or E-Commerce [^1].
Select **Save**.
[^1]: Some 工具 do not 支持的 Automatic 操作s, see the section about Custom 操作s if the tool you are adding doesn't present Automatic 操作s.

Zaraz relies on 事件, 触发器s and 操作s to determine when to load the 工具 you need in your website, and what 操作 they need to perform. The way you configure Zaraz and where you start largely depend on the tool you wish to use. When using a tool that 支持s Automatic 操作s, this process is largely done for you. If the tool you are adding doesn't 支持 Automatic 操作s, 阅读更多 about configuring Custom 操作s.
When using Automatic 操作s, the available 操作s are as follows:
**Pageviews** - for tracking every pageview on your website
**事件** - For tracking calls using the  Web API
**E-commerce** - For tracking calls to  Web API
If you need to programmatically start 操作s in your 工具, Cloudflare Zaraz provides a unified Web API to send 事件 to Zaraz, and from there, to third-party 工具. This Web API includes the ,  and  方法.

The Track method allows you to track custom 事件 and 操作s on your website that might happen in real time. The Set method is an easy shortcut to define a 变量 once and have it sent with every future Track call. E-commerce is a unified method for sending e-commerce 相关 data to mul提示le 工具 without needing to configure 触发器s and 事件. Refer to Web API for more 信息rmation.

If you suspect that something is not working the way it should, or if you want to verify the operation of 工具 on your website, 阅读更多 about 调试 Mode and Zaraz 监控. Also, check the 常见问题 page to see if your question was al读取y answered there.
Users and companies have developed 插件 that make using Zaraz easier on specific 平台s. We recommend checking out these 插件 if you are using one of these 平台s.
Beetle Tracking - Integrate Zaraz with your WordPress WooCommerce website to track e-commerce 事件 with zero 配置. Beetle Tracking also 支持s consent management and other advanced 特性.

/zaraz/consent-management/.title: Consent management
headings: 重要 things to 注意
paragraphs: Zaraz provides a Consent Management 平台 (CMP) to help you address and manage 必需 consents under the European General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Directive on privacy and electronic communications. This consent 平台 lets you easily 创建 a consent modal for your website based on the 工具 you have configured. With Zaraz CMP, you can make sure Zaraz only loads 工具 under the umbrella of the specific purposes your users have agreed to.
The consent modal added to your website is concise and gives your users an easy way to opt-in to any purposes of data processing your 工具 need.

The Zaraz Consent Management 平台 (CMP) has a Purposes section. This is where you will have to 创建 purposes for the third-party 工具 your website uses. To better understand the terms involved in dealing with personal data, refer to these definitions:

**Purpose**: The reason you are loading a given tool on your website, such as to track conversions or improve your website’s layout based on behavior tracking. One purpose can be assigned to many 工具, but one tool can be assigned only to one purpose.
**Consent**: An affirmative 操作 that the user makes, 必需 to store and access Cookies (or other persistent data, like ) on the users’ computer/browser.
:::注意
All 工具 use consent as a legal basis. This is due to the fact that they all use Cookies that are not strictly necessary for the website’s correct operation. Due to this, all purposes are opt-in.

When you add a 新功能 tool to your website, Zaraz does not assign any purpose to it. This means that this tool will skip consent by 默认. Remember to check the Consent Management 设置 every time you 设置 a 新功能 tool. This helps ensure you avoid a situation where your tool is 触发器ed before the user gives consent.
The user’s consent p参考s are stored within a first-party Cookie. This Cookie is a JSON file that maps the purposes’ ID to a //missing 值:
值: The user gave consent.
值: The user refused consent.
Missing 值: The user has not made a choice yet.
:::注意[重要]
Cloudflare cannot recommend nor assign by 默认 any specific purpose for your 工具. It is your responsibility to properly assign 工具 to purposes if you need to comply with GDPR.
Purposes that have no 工具 assigned will not show up in the CMP modal.
If a tool is assigned to a purpose, it will not run unless the user gives consent for the purpose the tool is assigned for.

Once your website loads for a given user for the first time, all the 触发器s you have configured for 工具 that are waiting for consent are cached in the browser. Then, they will be fired when/if the user gives consent, so they are not lost.

If the user visits your website for the first time, the consent modal will automatically show up. This also happens if the user has previously visited your website, but in the meantime you have 已启用 CMP.
On subsequent visits, the modal will not show up. You can make the modal show up by calling the 函数  — for 示例, by 绑定 it to a button.

/zaraz/web-api/.title: Web API
paragraphs: Zaraz provides a client-side web API that you can use anywhere inside the  tag of a page.

This API allows you to send 事件 and data to Zaraz, that you can later use when creating your 触发器s. Using the API lets you tailor the behavior of Zaraz to your needs: You can launch 工具 only when you need them, or send 信息rmation you care about that is not otherwise automatically collected from your site.
